EDCS-08200856.summary: An epitaph for Octavia Tyche, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08200857.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of a young child named Gaius Oppius Agatho.

EDCS-08200858.translation: Publius Pacilius Agathopus lived 55 years; here he lies, [for] Pacilia, daughter of Gnaeus.
EDCS-08200858.summary: A tombstone for Publius Pacilius Agathopus, likely related to Pacilia, daughter of Gnaeus.

EDCS-08200859.translation: Publius Pacilius Silvanus, freedman of Publius, lived 60 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200859.summary: A tombstone for Publius Pacilius Silvanus, a freedman, indicating his age and burial.

EDCS-08200860.translation: Arescusa, freedwoman of a woman, is placed here.
EDCS-08200860.summary: A tombstone for Arescusa, a freedwoman, stating her burial.

EDCS-08200861.translation: [?] 25 years; Gaius Papirius Cedonius [buried] here for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08200861.summary: An epitaph dedicated by Gaius Papirius Cedonius to his wife, including her age.

EDCS-08200862.translation: Phoebus, son of the farm manageress Procla, servant of the Cerellian family, lived 19 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200862.summary: A tombstone for Phoebus, son of a farm manageress and servant, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-08200863.summary: A simple inscription with two names, possibly a greeting or a very brief memorial.

EDCS-08200864.translation: Plaetoria Helpis, freedwoman of Publius, lived 90 years; here she lies.
EDCS-08200864.summary: A tombstone for Plaetoria Helpis, a freedwoman, noting her advanced age and burial.

EDCS-08200865.translation: Lucius Plinius Peregrinus, freedman of Lucius, lived 30 years; here he lies.
EDCS-08200865.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Plinius Peregrinus, a freedman, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-08200866.translation: Plinia Prima, freedwoman of Lucius, lived 45 years; here she lies.
EDCS-08200866.summary: A tombstone for Plinia Prima, a freedwoman, noting her age and burial.

EDCS-08200869.translation: Marcus Pomponius Violus, son of Marcus, aged 18, lies here.
EDCS-08200869.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Pomponius Violus, an 18-year-old, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200870.translation: Pomponia Hilara, freedwoman of Caius, lies here.
EDCS-08200870.summary: A tombstone for Pomponia Hilara, a freedwoman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200871.translation: Pomponia Hitalia lived 50 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200871.summary: A tombstone for Pomponia Hitalia, who lived to 50, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200872.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pyramus pater posuit Secundae filiae suae vixit annos II\n
EDCS-08200872.translation: To the departed spirits. Pyramus erected this for his daughter Secunda, who lived 2 years.
EDCS-08200872.summary: A funerary dedication by Pyramus for his young daughter Secunda, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200873.translation: Restitutus, slave of Vigilia Secunda, lived 23 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200873.summary: A tombstone for Restitutus, a slave of Vigilia Secunda, aged 23, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200874.translation: I was called Romanus by name, whom the departed spirits hastened to snatch away prematurely; who caused bitter grief by my untimely death. I lived happily for [about] 3 years, for [my parents grieved intensely].
EDCS-08200874.summary: A tombstone for a child named Romanus who died young, lamenting his premature death and the grief of his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200875.translation: Rutilia Ampliata, daughter of Lucius, lived 52 years, lies here, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08200875.summary: A tombstone for Rutilia Ampliata, who lived 52 years, honored by a decree of the local council (decurions), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200876.translation: Rutilia Homilia lived 25 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200876.summary: A tombstone for Rutilia Homilia, who lived 25 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200877.translation: To the departed spirits. Saturninus, slave of Laenilla Eutacte, lived 45 years. Her slave Panthia made this for her deserving husband and her sweetest daughter. They lie here.
EDCS-08200877.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a slave named Panthia to her husband Saturninus and daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200878.translation: For Saturnio, slave of Gabinius, lived 38 years, lies here.
EDCS-08200878.summary: A tombstone for Saturnio, a slave of Gabinius, aged 38, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200879.translation: To the deserving Secundilla, his wife, from her own funds. She lived 45 years.
EDCS-08200879.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Secundilla, who lived 45 years, with her wife providing for the monument.

EDCS-08200880.translation: Secundus Maes lived 7 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-08200880.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Secundus Maes, who lived 7 years.

EDCS-08200881.translation: To the gods, the spirits. Gaius Geminius Macedo lived 36 years. Here he lies. Gaius Geminius Secundus placed this for the deserving.
EDCS-08200881.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Gaius Geminius Macedo, who lived 36 years, and was set up by Gaius Geminius Secundus.

EDCS-08200882.translation: To the gods, the spirits. Sergius Eutuches lived 35 years. Here he lies. Sergia Givome lived 45 years.
EDCS-08200882.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Sergius Eutuches, who lived 35 years, and Sergia Givome, who lived 45 years.

EDCS-08200883.translation: Sergia Secunda and Hermais lived 1 year and 3 months. Here she lies. Gabinia Brundisina lived 40 years.
EDCS-08200883.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Sergia Secunda and Hermais, who lived 1 year and 3 months, and Gabinia Brundisina, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-08200884.translation: Sergia Veneria, freedwoman of Gaius, lies here.
EDCS-08200884.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Sergia Veneria, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08200885.translation: To the gods, the spirits. Decimus Servilius Primus lived 27 years. Here he lies. Servilia Tertulla.
EDCS-08200885.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Decimus Servilius Primus, who lived 27 years, with Servilia Tertulla also mentioned.

EDCS-08200887.translation: To the gods, the spirits. Sextia Thais lived 52 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-08200887.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Sextia Thais, who lived 52 years.

EDCS-08200888.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Publius Silius and Andro.

EDCS-08200890.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Statia Optata, who lived 9 years.

EDCS-08200891.translation: To the shades of the departed. Statilia Glyce lived 32 years and 2 months; she lies here.
EDCS-08200891.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Statilia Glyce.

EDCS-08200892.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Stlaccia Floria.

EDCS-08200893.translation: [...]cia Sumpherusa lived [..]5 years; she lies here.
EDCS-08200893.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Sumpherusa.

EDCS-08200894.translation: Cerdo, freedman of Numerius Terraeus (a woman), lived 50 years; he lies here. Poemne, freedwoman of the same woman, lived 60 years.
EDCS-08200894.summary: A funerary epitaph for two individuals, Cerdo and Poemne, who were freedpersons of a woman named Numerius Terraeus.

EDCS-08200895.translation: To the shades of the departed. Numerius Terraeus Dyrrachinus lived 23 years; he lies here. Numerius Terraeus Dyrrachinus, his father, set [this up] for him as deserving.
EDCS-08200895.summary: A funerary epitaph for Numerius Terraeus Dyrrachinus, with his father setting up the monument.

EDCS-08200896.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Terraea Nerotis, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08200897.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Theoris.

EDCS-08200898.translation: [...]N[...] lies here? Tutoria Optata lived 2[..] years; she lies here.
EDCS-08200898.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Tutoria Optata.

EDCS-08200899.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tutoria Rufa.

EDCS-08200900.translation: Veratia Aphrodisia, freedwoman of Marcus, lies here.
EDCS-08200900.summary: A funerary epitaph for Veratia Aphrodisia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08200901.translation: Lucius Vibius Pansa, son of Lucius, lived 80 years, is buried here.
EDCS-08200901.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Vibius Pansa, recording his name, parentage, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-08200902.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia Aphrodisia, indicating her burial.

EDCS-08200903.translation: Vibia Secunda lived 30 years, is buried here.
EDCS-08200903.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia Secunda, recording her name, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-08200904.translation: To the divine shades. Assius Carpophorus, her husband, and Calventius Maturus erected this to Ulpia Prima, who lived 30 years, a deserving wife, buried here.
EDCS-08200904.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Assius Carpophorus and Calventius Maturus for Ulpia Prima, recording her age at death and their relationship.

EDCS-08200905.translation: Lucius Volcacius Tullius is buried here, lived 80 years.
EDCS-08200905.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Volcacius Tullius, recording his name, burial, and age at death.

EDCS-08200906.translation: Lucius Volumnius Chrestus, freedman of Lucius, lived 80 years, is buried here.
EDCS-08200906.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Volumnius Chrestus, recording his name, manumission, age at death, and burial.

EDCS-08200907.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Volumnia Primigenia, recording her name and age at death.

EDCS-08200909.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Simia mater bene merenti fecit et sibi vixit annos XXX\n
EDCS-08200909.translation: To the divine shades. Simia erected this for her deserving mother and for herself; she lived 30 years.
EDCS-08200909.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Simia for her mother and herself, recording the mother's age at death.

EDCS-08200916.translation: To the divine shades. His sons erected this for Lucius Audius Philogenes, freedman of Lucius, Mercurialis and Augustalis, who lived 65 years, to their most devoted father.
EDCS-08200916.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by the sons for their father, Lucius Audius Philogenes, recording his freedman status, affiliations, age at death, and relationship.

EDCS-08200917.translation: To the divine shades. Clodius Iustus to his deserving foster child.
EDCS-08200917.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Clodius Iustus for his foster child, Cerdippus, recording their relationship.

EDCS-08200918.translation: To the departed spirits. Servius Cornelius Silvanus, freedman of Servius, lived 46 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200918.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Servius Cornelius Silvanus, who died at the age of 46.

EDCS-08200919.translation: To the departed spirits. Quene Mumiaes Laenillaes, a servant, lived 55 years. Aster set this up for her, deserving well.
EDCS-08200919.summary: A funerary epitaph for the freedwoman Quene Mumiaes Laenillaes, set up by Aster.

EDCS-08200922.translation: Lucius Clodius Pius Marianus, son of Lucius, prefect of the craftsmen, lived 8 years, 6 months, and 6 days. For him, a statue and [text missing] were dedicated and given. He is buried here. Marianus and Pia for their sweetest son.
EDCS-08200922.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy, Lucius Clodius Pius Marianus, who died at age 8, with a monument erected by his parents.

EDCS-08200923.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Gerellanus Asyncritus, freedman of Publius, an Augustalis, lived 28 years. He is buried here. Publius Gerellanus Asyncritus, his father, for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-08200923.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Gerellanus Asyncritus, who died at 28, erected by his father.

EDCS-08200924.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly related to a haruspex named Satrio Crea[---]io.

EDCS-08200925.translation: Aste, servant of our Caesar, lived 55 years. Silvanus made this for his deserving nurse. She is buried here.
EDCS-08200925.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aste, a servant of Caesar, erected by her nurse Silvanus.

EDCS-08200926.translation: To the departed spirits. Cocceia Prima, daughter of Marcus, lived 20 years. She is buried here. Marcus Cocceius Lucifer for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-08200926.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cocceia Prima, who died at 20, erected by her father.

EDCS-08200927.translation: To Flavia Ampelis, for her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08200927.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Ampelis, dedicated by her spouse.

EDCS-08200928.translation: To the departed spirits. Geminius Silvanus lived 40 years. Auge made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-08200928.summary: A funerary epitaph for Geminius Silvanus, who died at 40, erected by his wife Auge.

EDCS-08200929.translation: To the departed spirits. Secundilla, freedwoman of Servius, Arrun[---] Rufi[---], mother, set up [this]. She lived 2 years.
EDCS-08200929.summary: A funerary epitaph for Secundilla, who died at age 2, erected by her mother.

EDCS-08200930.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextia Terpsa, freedwoman of Publius, lived 50 years.
EDCS-08200930.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextia Terpsa, a freedwoman, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-08200933.translation: To Faustina Augusta, by public decree of the town councillors.
EDCS-08200933.summary: An honorific inscription dedicated to Faustina Augusta, decreed by local councillors.

EDCS-08200934.translation: To Lucius Junius Moderatus Columella, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, a military tribune in the VI Ferrata legion.
EDCS-08200934.summary: An inscription honoring Lucius Junius Moderatus Columella, a military tribune.

EDCS-08200936.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Calpurnius Cratistus lived 6 years and 40 days; he lies here.
EDCS-08200936.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Calpurnius Cratistus, recording his age and burial location.

EDCS-08200937.translation: Calpurnia Cratista lived 50 years; she lies here.
EDCS-08200937.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Cratista, recording her age and burial location.

EDCS-08200938.translation: Gnaeus Nearchus Nepos Fabianus, at his own expense.
EDCS-08200938.summary: An inscription indicating a dedication or monument funded by Gnaeus Nearchus Nepos Fabianus.

EDCS-08200939.translation: To the departed spirits. Crusis of the Tittinii lived 60 years; she lies here. Pardalas, grandson of Kaninius Proclus, lived 10 years; he lies here. Marcus Kaninius Euhetes made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08200939.summary: A complex funerary monument with inscriptions for at least two individuals, including a wife and her grandson.

EDCS-08200940.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Festus lived 55 years. Lupula made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-08200940.summary: A funerary epitaph where Lupula commemorates her husband, Festus.

EDCS-08200941.translation: To Gaius Julius Ambrosius. Decimus Lucretius Iustus, his son.
EDCS-08200941.summary: An inscription likely commemorating Gaius Julius Ambrosius, erected by his son Decimus Lucretius Iustus.

EDCS-08200942.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Filemation lived 19 years; she lies here.
EDCS-08200942.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Filemation, recording her age and burial location.

EDCS-08200943.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Laenia Primigenia lived 55 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-08200943.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Laenia Primigenia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-08200944.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Belonging to] Sex(tus) Licinius Priscus.
EDCS-08200944.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Sex(tus) Licinius Priscus.

EDCS-08200945.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Memmius Dionysius lived 60 years; he is buried here. Iulia Maria made [this] for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-08200945.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Gaius Memmius Dionysius and states that his wife, Iulia Maria, erected the monument.

EDCS-08200946.translation: Memmia Secundina lived 28 years; she is buried here. Memmius Saenanius made [this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08200946.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Memmia Secundina and states that her husband, Memmius Saenanius, erected the monument.

EDCS-08200947.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Messia Roda lived 40 years; she is buried here. Cornelius Fortunatus made [this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08200947.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Messia Roda and states that her husband, Cornelius Fortunatus, erected the monument.

EDCS-08200948.translation: To Titia Apula, daughter of Publius, the most devoted daughter. Her parents [erected this].
EDCS-08200948.summary: This epitaph was erected by parents for their devoted daughter, Titia Apula.

EDCS-08200949.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Belonging to] Claudius, the younger, son of Aulus Titinius.
EDCS-08200949.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius, the younger, son of Aulus Titinius.

EDCS-08200950.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Belonging to] Aulus Titinius Fructus.
EDCS-08200950.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Titinius Fructus.

EDCS-08200951.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. [Belonging to] Claudius, son of Aulus Titinius.
EDCS-08200951.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius, son of Aulus Titinius.

EDCS-08200952.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and age of Titinia Daphne.

EDCS-08200953.translation: Faustus, the freedman, made this for Decimus Veturius Anterus.
EDCS-08200953.summary: A tombstone erected by a freedman for his former master.

EDCS-08200954.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Vicilia Titinia Procula.
EDCS-08200954.summary: A funerary dedication to Vicilia Titinia Procula.

EDCS-08200962.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Aud(ianus?) Verus, aedile with magisterial authority, lived 37 years. He is buried here. Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Helvius Verus, aedile with magisterial authority, lived 25 years. Helvia Hasphasia [erected this] for her well-deserving husband and son.
EDCS-08200962.summary: A funerary monument commemorating two individuals, likely father and son, both aediles with magisterial authority, with the latter part dedicated by a wife and mother.

EDCS-08200969.translation: Sextus Numitorius Epaphra, aged 40, is buried here.
EDCS-08200969.summary: A tombstone for Sextus Numitorius Epaphra, indicating his age and burial.

EDCS-08200973.translation: Flavia Philoma, freedwoman of Augustus, lived 31 years and 6 months. Her father Daphnus placed this for his most dutiful and sweetest daughter.
EDCS-08200973.summary: A tombstone erected by a father for his deceased daughter, Flavia Philoma.

EDCS-08200974.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Decimus Apertius Secundus, veteran of the Second Legion Traiana Fortis, lived 50 years. Iulia Varia made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-08200974.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a wife to her husband, a veteran of the Legio II Traiana Fortis.

EDCS-08200975.translation: The freedman of the woman, Publius Gellius Somus, is buried here.
EDCS-08200975.summary: A tombstone indicating the burial of Publius Gellius Somus, a freedman.

EDCS-08200976.translation: For Lautinia Panthæra, freedwoman of the woman, and her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-08200976.summary: A funerary dedication for Lautinia Panthæra and her husband.

EDCS-08200977.translation: [---]OSSVCID[---] for Lucius Licinius Macro, for Antonia, daughter of Publius, Ruf[---], for Aulus Bimius Flaccus, son of Flaccus [---]
EDCS-08200977.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording multiple individuals, possibly related to familial or social connections, with names and filiations.

EDCS-08200982.translation: [---] Petronius Strabo, son of Lucius, [son of?] Claudia, holds the office of quattuorvir with aedilic power for the third time, and as quattuorvir with magisterial authority, and [---] for Petronia Zosima, [daughter of?] Mnasias. Petronia Zosima made this.
EDCS-08200982.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Petronia Zosima, likely to her husband and possibly a father, who held significant magistracies.

EDCS-08200983.translation: Publius Publicius Adiutor, son of Publius, of the Claudia tribe, aedile twice and a duovir for judging, lived 35 years, is buried here. A wicked mother made this for her son.
EDCS-08200983.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Publicius Adiutor, detailing his civic offices and noting that his mother erected it.

EDCS-08200984.translation: Symphoros of Antioch, a freedman of the emperor, from Tralles, lived 85 years.
EDCS-08200984.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Symphoros, identified as being from Tralles and associated with the imperial household.

EDCS-08200988.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Appalena Ammaru lived 60 years. Her daughter made this for her mother.
EDCS-08200988.summary: This tombstone honors Appalena Ammaru, noting her age and that her daughter erected it.

EDCS-08200990.translation: Atilia Erotis lived 22 years, is buried here.
EDCS-08200990.summary: This epitaph records the death of Atilia Erotis and her age.

EDCS-08200992.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caelidia Nomia lived 35 years, is buried here. Her father made this.
EDCS-08200992.summary: This tombstone is for Caelidia Nomia, who lived 35 years, and was erected by her father.

EDCS-08200994.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Deccius Faustus lived 55 years. His son made this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-08200994.summary: This tombstone honors Gaius Deccius Faustus, who lived 55 years, and was erected by his son.

EDCS-08200995.translation: For Gaius Licinius Favor, lived 73 years. Gaius Licinius Iustus [erected this].
EDCS-08200995.summary: This epitaph records the lifespan of Gaius Licinius Favor and indicates it was erected by Gaius Licinius Iustus.

EDCS-08200998.summary: This incomplete epitaph records the name Numerius Marianus and mentions his lifespan.

EDCS-08200999.translation: Titus Sextilius Felix lived 64 years, is buried here. His freedmen [erected this] for their well-deserving patron.
EDCS-08200999.summary: This tombstone is for Titus Sextilius Felix, who lived 64 years, and was erected by his freedmen.

EDCS-08201001.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Tyche lived 80 years. Her wife made this for her excellent, well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08201001.summary: This tombstone honors Ulpia Tyche, who lived 80 years, and was erected by her wife.

EDCS-08201006.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Petiliae Quinti filiae Secundinae sacerdoti Minervae vixit nnos VIIII menses VII dies XVIII ob infatigabilem pietatem eius Messia Dorcas mater infelicissima filiae dulcissimae bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-08201006.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Secundina, priestess of Minerva, daughter of Quintus, who lived 9 years, 7 months, and 18 days. For her tireless devotion, her most unhappy mother Messia Dorcas made this for her most beloved and deserving daughter.
EDCS-08201006.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Secundina, dedicated by her mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201007.translation: Marcus Fulvius Triumphus, freedman of a woman. Postumia Prima, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-08201007.summary: A funerary epitaph listing two individuals, likely a couple, possibly indicating their manumission status and patron relationships, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201008.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan the Parthian, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 15th time, consul for the 3rd time, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201008.summary: A dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely for a public building or monument, indicating his titles and consulships, mid-to-late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08201009.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Claudia Panthia, most rare. Peticius Dionysius, her son, made this for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-08201009.summary: A funerary epitaph for Claudia Panthia, dedicated by her son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201010.translation: Gaius Titius Probus erected this for his pupil Titus Septimus, who lived for six years.
EDCS-08201010.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Titus Septimus, dedicated by his teacher or guardian Gaius Titius Probus, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201011.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, proconsul, by the decurions and Augustales from collected funds.
EDCS-08201011.summary: A dedication to Emperor Gordian III, likely for a public building or monument, sponsored by local officials, mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08201012.translation: Caelonia Saturnina to Gaius Pompilius, freedman of Caesar, her comrade in arms.
EDCS-08201012.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Pompilius, dedicated by his comrade Caelonia Saturnina, likely indicating a shared military service or barrack life, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201013.translation: Acte, freedwoman, made this for her well-deserving patron Gaius Marcius Severus, who lived 55 years.
EDCS-08201013.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Marcius Severus, dedicated by his freedwoman Acte, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201014.translation: To Silvanus, servant of Caesar. He lived 40 years. He lies here.
EDCS-08201014.summary: A funerary epitaph for Silvanus, a servant of Caesar, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201016.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Constantine, Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, consul [?], emperor [?], father of the fatherland, proconsul, [?]
EDCS-08201016.summary: A dedication to Emperor Constantine I, likely for a public building or monument, recording some of his titles, early 4th century CE.

EDCS-08201018.translation: To Heracles, freedman of Gaius Julius Saturninus, an Augustalis, for himself and for his son Gaius Julius Salpinus and for Julia Soteria, freedwoman, Thesmo.
EDCS-08201018.summary: A funerary monument set up by Heracles for himself, his son, and a freedwoman, identified by his patron's name.

EDCS-08201020.translation: Philodespotos, deputy to Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus' slave, erected this for Zosimena, his fellow slave and wool-worker.
EDCS-08201020.summary: A funerary monument erected by Philodespotos, a deputy slave, for his fellow slave Zosimena, who worked in the wool trade.

EDCS-08201021.translation: To Rhodanus, slave and agent of Salvia Recepta.
EDCS-08201021.summary: A funerary dedication for Rhodanus, a slave and agent, identifying his owner.

EDCS-08201022.translation: To the divine shades. Publius Voconius Rufus for his well-deserving father, Publius Voconius Victor.
EDCS-08201022.summary: A funerary monument set up by Publius Voconius Rufus for his father, Publius Voconius Victor.

EDCS-08201025.translation: Sacred to Vesta. Publius Titius, son of Lucius, and Publius Curtius Salasus, son of Publius, four-men officials, from the proceeds of gladiatorial games, by decree of the senate.
EDCS-08201025.summary: A dedication to Vesta by two officials, funded by gladiatorial games, as decreed by the senate.

EDCS-08201026.translation: Sacred to Vortumnus. Publius Curtius Salasus, son of Publius, and Publius Titius, son of Lucius, four-men officials, from the proceeds of gladiatorial games, by decree of the senate.
EDCS-08201026.summary: A dedication to Vortumnus by two officials, funded by gladiatorial games, as decreed by the senate.

EDCS-08201028.translation: To our lords, the August Caesar Constantine and his sons Caesars. Volusius Venustus, a most distinguished man, governor of Apulia and Calabria, set up statues for the adornment of the portico out of love for his homeland, a site granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201028.summary: A dedication by Volusius Venustus, governor, for public adornment, honoring the emperor and his sons, funded by his love for his homeland.

EDCS-08201029.translation: To Annia Rufina, daughter of Quintus, from Publius Cassius Dexter, one of the seven officials responsible for feasts, because of the merits of her grandfather Rufus and her mother Albina, by unanimous consent.
EDCS-08201029.summary: A dedication to Annia Rufina, honoring her grandfather and mother, by Publius Cassius Dexter, a member of a religious college.

EDCS-08201030.translation: To Quintus Articuleius Regulus, praetor, proconsul, legate of Emperor Caesar Augustus, father of the country, our patron.
EDCS-08201030.summary: A public dedication honoring Quintus Articuleius Regulus for his distinguished career and service as patron.

EDCS-08201031.translation: To Lucius Silanus, son of Marcus, grandson of Decimus, praetor, augur.
EDCS-08201031.summary: A dedication to Lucius Silanus, honoring his praetorship and role as an augur.

EDCS-08201033.translation: To Marcus Antonius Vitellianus, a distinguished man, patron of the colony, prefect of the district of Apulia, Calabria, Lucania, and the Bruttii, on account of his outstanding devotion to the fatherland and citizens and his singular diligence in preserving the peace of the region, at the request of the people, by decree of the decurions, publicly honored.
EDCS-08201033.summary: This inscription records a public honor bestowed by the decurions of Canusium upon Marcus Antonius Vitellianus for his service as prefect of southern Italy, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08201035.translation: [---] third, son of Gaius, of the Oufentina tribe, made [---] [---]dia, 10 years old [---] [---]dia, freedwoman of Lucius, Statia [---] [---]ius, son of Publius, of the Oufentina tribe, Gar[---] military tribune [---]dia, freedwoman of Lucius, Hilara [---]tius, son of Gaius, of the Oufentina tribe, Silo.
EDCS-08201035.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to record names and familial relationships, possibly related to a funerary monument or a list of citizens, originating from the Oufentina tribe, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201037.translation: During the consulship of Lucius Marius Maximus II and Lucius Roscius Aelianus, Marcus Antonius Priscus and Lucius Annius Secundus, quinquennial duoviri, saw to the engraving of the names of the decurions on bronze. // Clarissimi viri patrons. // Appius Claudius Iulianus, Titus Lorenius Celsus, Marcus Aedinius Iulianus, Lucius Didius Marinus, Lucius Domitius Honoratus, Marcus Antonius Balbus, Marcus Statius Longinus, Lucius Pontius Verus, Gaius Betitius Pius, Gaius Gavivius Maximus, Gaius Furius Octavianus, Lucius Bruttius Praesens, Gaius Bruttius Crispinus, [[Gaius Petronius Magnus]], Gaius Iunius Numidianus, Marcus Papirius Candidus, Lucius Caecilius Maximus, Quintus Caelius Flavianus, Lucius Lucilius Priscilianus, Lucius Pontius Bassus, Lucius Pontius Mauricius, Marcus Antonius Crispinus, Tiberius Iulius Licinianus, Gaius Sulpicius Arrenianus, Gaius Licinius Licinianus, Lucius Valerius Turbo, Lucius Flavius Lucilianus, Publius Marcius Maximillianus, Marcus Statius Patruinus, Marcus Statius Longinus the younger, Marcus Valerius Turbo the younger. // Roman equestrians patrons. // Publius Gerellanus Modestus, Titus Ligerius Postuminus, Titus Munatius Felix, Titus Flavius Crocalianus, Gaius Galbius Soterianus, Titus Aelius Rufus, Titus Aelius Flavianus, Quintus Coelius Sabinianus. // Quinquennales. // Titus Ligerius Postuminus, Titus Annaeus Rufus, Lucius Abuccius Proculus, Titus Aelius Rufus, Titus Aelius Flavianus, Marcus Antonius Priscus, Lucius Annius Secundus. // Elected among the quinquennales. // Gaius Galbius Soterianus, Lucius Abbucius Iulianus, Gaius Silius Antius, Publius Aelius Victorinus. // Duoviri. // Aulus Caesellius Proculus (II), Lucius Faenius Merops (II), Lucius Abuccius Maximianus, Quintus Iunius Alexander (II), Marcus Aemilius Marcellus, Gaius Iulius Hospitalis, Lucius Marcius Fortunatianus, Gaius Fulvius Satyrus, Publius Libuscidius Victorinus, Quintus Fabius Felicissimus, Titus Aelius Antonius, Lucius Herennius Arescunianus, Titus Curius Salvianus, Aulus Kanuleius Felicissimus, Publius Sergius Bassaeus, Publius Graecidius Firmus, Marcus Athanius Felicissimus, Gaius Vibius Octavianus, Tiberius Claudius Onesimianus, Lucius Annius Pius, Quintus Iunius Onesiphorus, Gaius Lucretius Venustus, Aulus Fabius Cassianus, Lucius Triccius Apollinaris, Marcus Apronius Primus, Publius Esquilius Silvanus, Quintus Iunius Rusticus, Publius Clodius Dasimianus, Lucius Abuccius Felicinaus. // Aediles. // Titus Flavius Crocalianus, Gaius Ennius Marcianus, Sextus Tedius Priscus, Publius Graecidius Iustus, Tiberius Claudius Candidus, Marcus Servilius Helius, Titus Artorius Minervalis, Lucius Herennius Crescens, Titus Flavius Marinus, Lucius Clatius Secundinus, Lucius Abuccius Euryalus, Publius Marcius Carpophorus, Lucius Dasimius Priscus, Quintus Fabius Tialamus, Tiberius Claudius Eutychianus, Marcus Sempronius Sabinianus, Gaius Ennius Priscianus, Lucius Faenius Merops the younger, Marcus Antonius Vindex. // Quaestors. // Lucius Ceius Asclepiodotianus, Lucius Abbucius Laberianus, Decimus Balonius Felix, Titus Flavius Iustus, Titus Flavius Quintio, Marcus Saufeius Constans, Marcus Marcius Ianuarius, Publius Sergius Augurinus, Marcus Aurelius Acrisius. // Pedanei. // Quintus Fabius Fabianus, Lucius Vibius Iuventianus, Publius Graecidius Vestinus, Publius Carinatius Agathangelus, Gaius Terentius Priscinus, Gaius Pomponius Cupitus, Gaius Peticius Dionysius, Sextus Calpurnius Aemilianus, Lucius Novius Alticus, Gaius Vibius Saturninus, Publius Pacilius Chrysomallus, Titus Pomponius Felix, Marcus Aurelius Maximus, Decimus Agrius Pietas, Quintus Iunius Silvanus, Aulus Kanuleius Onesimianus, Titus Pompeius Vitalis, Gaius Fufidius Rufus, Titus Pompeius Alexander, Gaius Lucretius Venustus the younger, Gaius Iulius Stachys, Marcus Athanius Felix, Lucius Herennius Celsus, Decimus Satrenius Satrenianus, Titus Pompeius Attalus, Publius Esquilius Silvanus, Tiberius Claudius Onesimianus the younger, Quintus Iunius Musogenes, Publius Rutilius Tertullinus, Tiberius Claudius Verus, Marcus Ulpius Anthimus, Publius Publicius Maximus. // Praetextati. // Titus Flavius Frontinus, Gaius Iulius Hospitalis the younger, Lucius Abuccius Proculus the younger, Marcus Aurelius Marullus, Titus Aelius Nectareus, Lucius Eggius Maximus, Gaius Vibius Marcellus, Publius Publicius Maximus the younger, Lucius Annius Rufus, Lucius Triccius Apollinaris the younger, Marcus Aurelius Iulius, Marcus Aurelius Agrippinus, Lucius Attius Ianuarius, Gaius Galbius Atticillianus, Gaius Vibius Faustinus, Titus Pompeius Asclepius, Lucius Timinius Ponticus, Marcus Aurelius Valens, Gaius Galbius Amandus, Marcus Servilius Marcellus, Marcus Gavius Rufus, Lucius Dasimius Iustus, Quintus Iunius Trophimianus, Titus Flavius Silvinus, Tiberius Claudius Fortunatus.
EDCS-08201037.summary: This extensive inscription, dated to 210 CE, lists the magistrates and patrons of the Roman colony of Canusium, compiled by the quinquennial duoviri Marcus Antonius Priscus and Lucius Annius Secundus.

EDCS-08201038.translation: To Athenas, for Lucius Annius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe, a distinguished man in all aspects of his life, patron of this colony; when the people had requested him from the judges in his equestrian rank, content with the honor of a standing statue, he returned thanks for their willingness.
EDCS-08201038.summary: This inscription records the bestowal of a statue in honor of Lucius Annius Rufus, a patron of the colony, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201039.translation: To Lucius Annius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Oufentina tribe, patron, for his outstanding and incomparable generosity, by unanimous consent.
EDCS-08201039.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Annius Rufus, a patron of the colony, honoring his generosity, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201040.translation: For Baebia Arbuscula, duovir, aedile, quaestor, and for Nice, her mother.
EDCS-08201040.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records funerary dedications to a woman named Baebia Arbuscula and her mother, possibly in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201041.translation: To Sextus Mutronius, son of Sextus, [---] duovir, [---] of the people of the municipium of Canusium, because he [---] the annona [---]
EDCS-08201041.summary: This fragmentary inscription records Sextus Mutronius, a duovir of the municipium of Canusium, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201043.translation: By decree of the decurions of the colony Aurelia Augusta Pia Canusium, to Aurelius Apolaustus, freedman of Augustus, a pantomime, Augustalis, and hierophant of his time.
EDCS-08201043.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument by the decurions of Canusium to Aurelius Apolaustus, an Augustalis and pantomime, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201044.translation: To Apronia Iunice, freedwoman of Marcus, Marcus Apronius Euangelus, an Augustalis, [dedicated this to his] dearest foster-child; place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201044.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Apronius Euangelus, an Augustalis, to his foster-child Apronia Iunice, with the place granted by the decurions, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201046.translation: To Aulus Dasimius Sodalus, freedman of Aulus, an Augustalis; to Mucius Maxima; to Sextus Mucius Maximus, an Augustalis; to Albia Certa, freedwoman of Gaius; to Clodia Iucunda, nurse; from the will of Mucius Maxima.
EDCS-08201046.summary: This funerary inscription, originating from a will, dedicates monuments to several individuals, including two Augustales and a nurse, in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201047.translation: To Mariae Heuremae Villani. Publius Libuscidius Comus, freedman of the emperor and an argentarius, [dedicated this] to his wife Mariae Trypherae, to Lyde his freedwoman, and to Cosmus, freedman of the emperor.
EDCS-08201047.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Publius Libuscidius Comus to his wife, freedwoman, and another freedman, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08201048.translation: Sextus Sotidius Primus, freedman of the emperor. Flavia Duta, his mother. Tyrannus.
EDCS-08201048.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication from Sextus Sotidius Primus, an imperial freedman, to his mother Flavia Duta, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08201049.translation: Publius Valerius Pomarius, freedman of Publius, freedman of the emperor, [dedicated this] to Publius Valerius Philocalus, freedman of Publius, freedman of the emperor, and to Relatus, freedman of Publius, freedman of the emperor.
EDCS-08201049.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication made by Publius Valerius Pomarius to two other individuals with similar names and status, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08201050.translation: Caius Vellaeus Ursio, freedman of Caius, freedman of the emperor, for himself and for his daughter Tutorina, for his wife Trebellia Felicula, for Trebellia Arescusae, and Trebellia Felicula for herself, and for my freedmen and freedwomen, made this monument with a circuit of 15 feet and a right of way.
EDCS-08201050.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Caius Vellaeus Ursio establishes a monument and a right of way for himself, his family, and his freedpersons, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08201051.translation: Aulus Arrius Philemo, freedman of Aulus. Liburnus. Livia Flora, freedwoman of Lucius. Livia Haline, freedwoman of Aulus (and a woman). [They] will not follow the heir.
EDCS-08201051.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial plot, stating that the deceased (Philemo, Flora, and Haline) are not to be subject to the inheritance claims of an heir, possibly from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08201052.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Ignatia Afra made [this] for her well-deserving father, Lucius Apronius Africanus.
EDCS-08201052.summary: This funerary inscription records Ignatia Afra dedicating a monument to her father, Lucius Apronius Africanus, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08201053.summary: This fragmentary inscription simply records the name of a slave, Aquillia, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201054.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Genetlius, her husband, made [this] for his well-deserving wife, Arruntia Apollonia.
EDCS-08201054.summary: This funerary inscription records Genetlius dedicating a monument to his wife, Arruntia Apollonia, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08201055.translation: Titus Artorius Faustus, freedman of Titus, [dedicated this] to Raia Iucunda, freedwoman of Quintus; to Quintus Raio Epaphroditus, freedman of Quintus; to Quintus Raio Felix Lupus, freedman of the woman; to Raia Tyche, freedwoman of Titus; to Quintus Raio Faustus, freedman of the woman; to Titus Artorius Fortunatus, freedman of Titus; and to Titus Artorius Rusticus, freedman of Titus.
EDCS-08201055.summary: This extensive funerary inscription records Titus Artorius Faustus dedicating a monument to a large group of relatives and freedpersons, likely dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08201056.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Attius Eutyches, her husband, made [this] for his incomparable wife, Attia Artemisia.
EDCS-08201056.summary: This funerary inscription records Lucius Attius Eutyches dedicating a monument to his wife, Attia Artemisia, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08201057.translation: Publius Avillius Zosimus, freedman of Publius, and Publius Avillius Mato, son of Publius, lived 20 years. Campila Rufilla, freedwoman of Campilus, [erected this].
EDCS-08201057.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating the deceased Publius Avillius Zosimus and Publius Avillius Mato, possibly related, and Campila Rufilla, likely the dedicator.

EDCS-08201060.translation: Aulus Baebius Faustus, freedman of Aulus, made this at his own expense for himself, for his patron Aulus Baebius Suro, freedman of Aulus, and for Baebia Felicula, freedwoman of Aulus, and for Aulus Baebius Fortunatus, freedman of Aulus.
EDCS-08201060.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aulus Baebius Faustus for himself and members of his family and patron.

EDCS-08201061.translation: To Publius Baebius Felix and Verronia Zosima.
EDCS-08201061.summary: This is a fragmented dedication possibly for Publius Baebius Felix and Verronia Zosima.

EDCS-08201062.translation: Publius Baebius Paratus, freedman of Publius, for himself and Baebia Auxesis, freedwoman of Publius, Baebia Mellusa, freedwoman of the Publii, Lucius Maecius Theophilus, freedman of Lucius, Tiberius Claudius Primigenius, Baebia Tyche, freedwoman of Publius, Philetus, Melissus, Semne, and Abascanthus.
EDCS-08201062.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication made by Publius Baebius Paratus for himself and a considerable number of individuals, possibly family and freedmen.

EDCS-08201063.translation: Publius Barbatius Philotaerus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-08201063.summary: This is likely a tombstone or dedication naming Publius Barbatius Philotaerus, a freedman.

EDCS-08201064.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Nestor, his comrade, made this for Bassilla, his deserving wife, with whom he lived for 9 years.
EDCS-08201064.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Nestor to his wife Bassilla, indicating their 9-year marriage.

EDCS-08201066.translation: To Aulus Canuleius Valens, son of Aulus.
EDCS-08201066.summary: This is an dedication, possibly funerary, to Aulus Canuleius Valens.

EDCS-08201067.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Clatius Phyllinus and Baebia Eutychia made this for their most dutiful daughter Clatia Selene, who lived 22 years, 8 months, and 8 days; for themselves and their own.
EDCS-08201067.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Clatius Phyllinus and Baebia Eutychia for their daughter Clatia Selene, and also for themselves and other family members.

EDCS-08201068.translation: To the departed spirits. Syntyche made this for Tiberius Claudius Thraso, the deserving.
EDCS-08201068.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Syntyche to Tiberius Claudius Thraso.

EDCS-08201070.translation: Lucius Critonius Felix, freedman of Lucius, [made this] only for Critonia Rufilla, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-08201070.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Lucius Critonius Felix for Critonia Rufilla, and possibly for himself.

EDCS-08201071.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Curtius Argyrus made this for his most dutiful and well-deserving wife, Maritima.
EDCS-08201071.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201073.translation: Dasimia Sy[lla], freedwoman of Aulus, made this for herself, for her son Aulus Dasimius Primigenius, freedman of Aulus, for her son Fausto[s], freedman of Hostilius, and for their son Cosmus, for her sons; the mother made this for her sons.
EDCS-08201073.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother for herself and her sons, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201074.translation: Titus Apronius Demetrius, freedman of Titus; Calvisia Fausta, freedwoman of Lucius; Lucius Calvisius Aeschinus, freedman of Lucius; Calvisia Viola, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-08201074.summary: A list of names, likely freedpersons and their patrons, perhaps related to a legal or familial association, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201076.translation: To the departed spirits. Euscemus [made this] for Euscemus, his most dutiful son, who lived for 5 years, 10 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-08201076.summary: A funerary dedication by a father for his young son, including his age at death, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201077.translation: To the memory of Merops Faenius, son of Lucius, made this for himself while alive, for Merope, daughter of Faenia, and for Theodote, wife of Galbius, and for his grandson Theodotus, son of Artorius.
EDCS-08201077.summary: A funerary dedication by a man for himself, his wife, and grandson, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201078.translation: [...]ssidius Felix, a weaver, for himself and for [...]ssidius Pamphilus, [his] father, buried here.
EDCS-08201078.summary: A funerary dedication by a weaver for himself and his father, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201079.translation: Gaius Flavius Tu[tus] for himself and for Vavidia Prima and Gaius Vavidius Secundus.
EDCS-08201079.summary: A dedication by Gaius Flavius Tutus for himself and two other individuals, possibly family members or freedpersons, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201080.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred. The husband made this for his well-deserving wife, Flavia Hyginia.
EDCS-08201080.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201081.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Galbius Restutus made this for his father Galbius Sosius, for himself, and for his descendants, freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-08201081.summary: A funerary dedication by a son for his father, and for himself and his descendants, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201082.translation: Mallia Symferusa made this for her deserving husband, Lucius Herennius Primus.
EDCS-08201082.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her husband, dated to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201083.translation: To the departed spirits. To my sweetest sister Ias Ianuaria, who deserved well, [I] made this; she lived 17 years, 3 months, 11 days. I hoped for myself from you, now I have made [this] for you.
EDCS-08201083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by an unknown individual to their beloved sister, Ias Ianuaria, who died at a young age.

EDCS-08201084.translation: Lucius Lepidius Euetes made this for himself, and for his mother Titinia Lyra, and for all of mine.
EDCS-08201084.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Lepidius Euetes for himself, his mother, and his household.

EDCS-08201086.translation: To the departed spirits. To Titus Libuscidius Faustinus, a deserving son, Carpus the father made this with Chelidon, the mother; he lived 10 months.
EDCS-08201086.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Carpus and Chelidon for their son, Titus Libuscidius Faustinus, who died in infancy.

EDCS-08201088.translation: To the departed spirits. To Titus Livius Eutychetes, his wife Baebia Daphne and his son Livius Tertius made this, deserving of good.
EDCS-08201088.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by the wife and son of Titus Livius Eutychetes to his memory.

EDCS-08201091.translation: Minucia Euclesium, freedwoman of Publius, for herself and for Publius Minucius Mane, freedman of Publius, her husband, and for Minucia Certa, freedwoman of a woman, and for Minucia Erotis, freedwoman of a woman; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-08201091.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Minucia Euclesium, who also set it up for her husband, and two other freedwomen, establishing that the monument is not to be inherited.

EDCS-08201092.translation: [Possibly related to] Murdius, freedman of Murdia, Pharnaces. Aninia, daughter of Publius, this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-08201092.summary: This funerary epitaph records Pharnaces, a freedman, and states that the monument will not pass to an heir.

EDCS-08201093.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred. Claudia Nomas placed this for her deserving husband, Marcus Numisius Euhelpistus, and for herself and her family.
EDCS-08201093.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by Claudia Nomas for her husband Marcus Numisius Euhelpistus and for her family.

EDCS-08201094.translation: To the departed spirits. Sacred. Nummia Varia, a most distinguished woman, [erected this] for her deserving freedwoman, Nummia Aurha.
EDCS-08201094.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Nummia Varia for her deserving freedwoman, Nummia Aurha.

EDCS-08201096.translation: Lucius Postumulenus Nicephorus made this for himself, and for his wife Nonia Verecunda, and for his daughter Sotidua Maxima, daughter of Lucius, and for their descendants.
EDCS-08201096.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by Lucius Postumulenus Nicephorus for himself, his wife, his daughter, and their descendants.

EDCS-08201097.translation: Lucius Seius Eros, Lucius Seius Urbanus, Sulpicia Chreste, Maria Tertia.
EDCS-08201097.summary: This inscription lists four names and likely represents individuals associated with a funerary monument or a shared burial plot.

EDCS-08201098.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Servilia Iunx made this for her most beloved daughter, Ianuaria, who lived 7 years and 11 months.
EDCS-08201098.summary: A tombstone set up by Servilia Iunx for her daughter Ianuaria, who died at a young age.

EDCS-08201099.translation: To the departed spirits. To Themis, who lived 26 years and 21 days. For Eutychius, to a well-deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-08201099.summary: A funerary inscription for Themis, who died at age 26, and for Eutychius, a fellow slave.

EDCS-08201102.translation: Gaius Vavillius [---]dus for himself and for Quintus Martius Hilarion, Vavidia Chysris, Vavidia Corinthas, Quintus Martius Sustinendus.
EDCS-08201102.summary: An inscription recording the dedication of a monument for several individuals, possibly a family group, by Gaius Vavillius [---]dus.

EDCS-08201103.translation: Gaius Vavidius Menophilus, freedman of Gaius. Stabilio, freedman of Gaius, is buried here.
EDCS-08201103.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Vavidius Menophilus and Gaius Vavidius Stabilio, both freedmen of a Gaius.

EDCS-08201105.translation: Gaius Vellaeus Albanus for himself and for Cluatia Themis, freedwoman of Gnaeus, Tagullia Procne, freedwoman of the mulier (woman), and for Sextus Pulfernnus Rufus. No one else is permitted in this monument except those whose names are written.
EDCS-08201105.summary: An inscription establishing rules for burial in a monument, intended for Gaius Vellaeus Albanus, Cluatia Themis, Tagullia Procne, and Sextus Pulfernnus Rufus.

EDCS-08201107.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Verronius Aquila, an excellent man.
EDCS-08201107.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Verronius Aquila, described as an excellent man.

EDCS-08201108.translation: Tiberius Vettius Philargyrus, freedman of Tiberius. Graecidia Geuma, freedwoman of Aulus. Aulus Graecidius Gratus and Gamus. This monument will not follow an heir.
EDCS-08201108.summary: A funerary monument inscription for Tiberius Vettius Philargyrus and others, stating that the monument will not pass to an heir.

EDCS-08201114.translation: To Gaius Calvisius Sabinus, consul and patron.
EDCS-08201114.summary: An inscription dedicating a monument or honoring Gaius Calvisius Sabinus, who held the consulship.

EDCS-08201115.translation: To Sextus Dasimius Primus, son of Aulus, of the Oufentina tribe, who held the office of a duovir twice, aedile, duovir with judicial power, and quaestor; erected publicly by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201115.summary: A public dedication to Sextus Dasimius Primus, honoring his various magistracies, commissioned by the town council.

EDCS-08201117.translation: Numerius Ussaeus, son of Sextus, and Titus Salisius, son of Titus, as duovirs with judicial power, took care that a statue be erected to Minerva from sacred offerings.
EDCS-08201117.summary: An inscription recording the erection of a statue to Minerva by two duovirs with judicial power, funded by sacred donations.

EDCS-08201118.translation: Maxima, fellow slave, and Epaphra, fellow slave, set this up for Liberalis, the well-deserving, and for themselves.
EDCS-08201118.summary: A funerary monument set up by slaves for a fellow slave, possibly dating to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-08201123.translation: Gnaeus Trebatius Logus, Quintus Metilius Mario, and Marcus Rossius Ampliatus dedicate this sacred offering to the Mother of the Idæan Gods.
EDCS-08201123.summary: A dedication to the Mother of the Idæan Gods made by three individuals, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-08201124.translation: Sagaris, his agent, set this up for Mercury the Unconquered, for our safety.
EDCS-08201124.summary: An inscription dedicating an offering to Mercury for the safety of an individual named Praesens and his agent, Sagaris, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201131.summary: Fragmentary inscription mentioning a former proconsul named Eppius, likely from a Roman provincial context.

EDCS-08201133.translation: For Gaius Flavius Faustinus, son of Gaius, of the Horatia tribe, decurion of the colony, who lived 27 years, 9 months, and 13 days, and served in the 9th Praetorian cohort. His mother, Burbatia Ursilla, daughter of Publius, for her excellent son.
EDCS-08201133.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Flavius Faustinus, a decurion who served in the Praetorian Guard, set up by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201134.translation: Philargyrus, freedman, for Gaius Oppius, son of Titus, of the Horatia tribe, optio of the first cohort of the 12th legion.
EDCS-08201134.summary: An inscription likely honouring Gaius Oppius, an optio in the 12th Legion, set up by his freedman Philargyrus.

EDCS-08201140.translation: Publius Ennius Maximus, son of Publius, of the Horatia tribe, and Ennia Maxima, daughter of Publius, of the Horatia tribe, had this set up for themselves, for their mother Voltia Secunda, daughter of Caius. Publius Ennius Basso, son of Publius, of the Horatia tribe, aedile twice, duovir with judicial authority twice, quinquennial duovir, pontiff, quaestor, curator of public funds, judge from the five centuries. [And for their grandfather and father, --- Ennius ---]
EDCS-08201140.summary: A funerary monument honouring multiple members of the Ennius family, including those who held significant civic and religious offices, set up by later generations, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201141.translation: Gaius Flaminius Gallus, son of Gaius, as aedile, paved the road and the sidewalk for the honour of his office, one hundred paces, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201141.summary: A public works inscription recording the paving of a road and sidewalk by the aedile Gaius Flaminius Gallus, funded by public decree, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201143.translation: Marcus Muttienus, son of Lucius, and Gaius Vibius, son of Lucius, duovirs with judicial authority, saw to the repair of the statues by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201143.summary: An inscription recording the repair of statues undertaken by two duovirs, acting on a decree of the local council, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201144.translation: Marcus Muttienus, son of Lucius; Gaius Vibius, son of Lucius; Marcus Messius, son of Titus; Marcus Camillius, son of Lucius, for the honour.
EDCS-08201144.summary: A fragmentary inscription listing several individuals, possibly officials or magistrates, who undertook an action related to an honour, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201145.translation: Lucius Glitius, son of Publius, aedile and duovir, supervised the repair of the wall by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201145.summary: This inscription records the supervision of a wall repair by a local magistrate.

EDCS-08201146.translation: Lucius Paccius Priscus, aedile, duovir, and quaestor, curator of the Catinian games, [dedicated this to] Maximus Claudius.
EDCS-08201146.summary: This inscription records the public career and possibly a dedication by Lucius Paccius Priscus.

EDCS-08201147.translation: Quintus Raius Broccus, son of Statius, duovir, [supervised this] by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201147.summary: This inscription records a public work supervised by Quintus Raius Broccus, a local magistrate.

EDCS-08201152.translation: Quintus Plotus, son of Publius, and Marcus Valerius, son of [---], duovirs, and Publius Vettius, son of Marcus, and Lucius Caetronius, son of Aulus, aediles, [dedicated this ---].
EDCS-08201152.summary: This inscription lists several local magistrates, including duovirs and aediles, possibly dedicating a monument.

EDCS-08201158.inscription_type: tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-08201158.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. The worshippers of Liber from the sanctuary [dedicated this to] Gaius Iulius Entellus, [for ---] seven [years?].
EDCS-08201158.summary: This is a funerary inscription or dedication by the cultists of Liber to Gaius Iulius Entellus.

EDCS-08201160.translation: To Lucius Livius Probus, freedman of Lucius, an Augustalis; to Livia Himerte, freedwoman of Lucius, his wife; to Lucius Livius Silvester, his father; to Lucius Livius Silvester, his son; to Livia Laid[is?]; to Livia Sabina, freedpersons.
EDCS-08201160.summary: This is a funerary inscription for members of the Livius family and their freedpersons, including an Augustalis.

EDCS-08201161.translation: To Gaius Marius Suavius, Augustalis and quinquennalis, [dedicated this] to the Venusines.
EDCS-08201161.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Gaius Marius Suavius, a local official, possibly to the local community or deities.

EDCS-08201162.translation: To Primus, freedman of the woman Oppia, an Augustalis, [dedicated this ---].
EDCS-08201162.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Primus, a freedman and Augustalis.

EDCS-08201163.translation: Chryseros(?), Augustalis(?), [dedicated this to ---]lia, son [---], [for ---] four [years?].
EDCS-08201163.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication by an Augustalis and his son, possibly related to a tenure or service.

EDCS-08201166.translation: Lucilia Expectata, freedwoman of Marcus, [placed this for] Quintus Baebius Cladus, the physician.
EDCS-08201166.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucilia Expectata for the physician Quintus Baebius Cladus.

EDCS-08201168.translation: To Quintus Dellius Optatus, son of Quintus, a merchant of Ravenna. Priscus, his freedman, [erected this].
EDCS-08201168.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman for his deceased patron, a merchant from Ravenna.

EDCS-08201169.translation: Gaius Egnatius Entimus, physician, [erected this] for himself and for Egnatia Firma, his cohabitant, and for his most dutiful son Gaius Egnatius Entimus.
EDCS-08201169.summary: A funerary dedication by a physician for himself and his family members.

EDCS-08201171.translation: To Verecundus, son of Trop(h)imus Pyramis, supervisor and deputy of the colonists. He lived 2 years and 6 months. Mansueta, his grandmother, [erected this].
EDCS-08201171.summary: A funerary dedication for a child who served as a supervisor and deputy, set up by his grandmother.

EDCS-08201173.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication to a woman named Aelia.

EDCS-08201225.translation: [---] Herennia, freedwoman of the woman, and Herennia, freedwoman of the woman, [---] set [this] up.
EDCS-08201225.summary: A funerary dedication by two freedwomen, names incomplete.

EDCS-08201226.translation: Herminia Zosima, freedwoman of Marcus, lies here. She was 22 years old. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the ground.
EDCS-08201226.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial details and age of a freedwoman named Herminia Zosima.

EDCS-08201229.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary dedication or ownership marker for Iunia Ithace.

EDCS-08201230.translation: Gaius Lanius, son of Gaius, [and his] son. 14 feet in front, 15 feet in the ground.
EDCS-08201230.summary: A funerary boundary marker specifying the dimensions of a plot, set up by a father and son named Gaius Lanius.

EDCS-08201231.translation: [---] Lucullus, freedman of Gaius Api[---]. Lucullus [---]. Arche[---]. Publius Bae<b=P>ius [---]. Cosm[---]
EDCS-08201231.summary: A heavily damaged inscription with multiple names, possibly a funerary or dedication context.

EDCS-08201234.translation: To the divine shades of Marcia [---]atellia Antonia Beronice. Her mother set [this] up for her most dutiful and deserving daughter.
EDCS-08201234.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother for her deserving daughter, with partially preserved names.

EDCS-08201235.translation: Marcia Moschis for herself and for her freedman Fortunatus.
EDCS-08201235.summary: An epitaph recording a dedication by Marcia Moschis for herself and her freedman, Fortunatus.

EDCS-08201236.translation: ... Mario ... for his freedman Pyramus, and the freedwoman Ampliata, wife of ... to her husband deserving of good memory, and for herself and ... placed.
EDCS-08201236.summary: A fragmentary epitaph for a freedman named Pyramus and a freedwoman named Ampliata, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE, recording a dedication to a husband and for themselves.

EDCS-08201239.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly naming a person ending in -maxsimusor.

EDCS-08201240.translation: Lucius Messius Onesiphorus, freedman of Lucius, and Alphia Redempta, for themselves and their most dutiful son, placed (this).
EDCS-08201240.summary: An epitaph from the 1st or 2nd century CE, recording a dedication by Lucius Messius Onesiphorus and Alphia Redempta for themselves and their son.

EDCS-08201241.translation: Gaius Murrasius Epicadus, freedman of Gaius, and Caecilia Prim[--], freedwoman of Lucius, aged 11 years, and Caecilia Salvi[--], freedwoman of Lucius, placed (this) on the front 12 feet and in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-08201241.summary: A funerary epigram for Caecilia Prim[--] (aged 11) and Caecilia Salvi[--], likely from the late 1st century BCE to early 2nd century CE, with measurements for the plot.

EDCS-08201244.translation: The place of Quintus Numpidius Sabinus, son of Quintus, on the front ...
EDCS-08201244.summary: A boundary marker or land sale inscription indicating ownership of a plot by Quintus Numpidius Sabinus.

EDCS-08201245.translation: ... of Octavius Niger ... slave ... unfortunate ... Communis and ... Nice, most dutiful, and for themselves placed.
EDCS-08201245.summary: A fragmentary epitaph, likely from the 2nd century CE, possibly a dedication for a slave of Octavius Niger, with dedications by Communis and Nice for themselves.

EDCS-08201247.translation: To Ovia Pyrallis. Apollonius, her father, placed (this).
EDCS-08201247.summary: A funerary dedication by Apollonius for his daughter Ovia Pyrallis.

EDCS-08201248.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. The heirs of Marcus Pacuvius Hermadion placed (this).
EDCS-08201248.summary: A tomb inscription from the 1st to 3rd century CE, recording a dedication by the heirs of Marcus Pacuvius Hermadion.

EDCS-08201249.translation: To Papiria Patronae. Facelus and Papirius (dedicated this).
EDCS-08201249.summary: A funerary dedication for Papiria Patronae by Facelus and Papirius.

EDCS-08201250.translation: Pescennia, daughter of Aulus, [purchased a plot] 12 feet in front.
EDCS-08201250.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land purchased by Pescennia.

EDCS-08201251.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gnaeus Petronius Isidorus, a most innocent man, who lived 56 years. Gnaeus Petronius Isidorus, his son, erected this for his dearest and truly most devoted father, who deserved well.
EDCS-08201251.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gnaeus Petronius Isidorus and was erected by his son.

EDCS-08201255.translation: A plot belonging to Lucius Pontius Phileros, freedman of Lucius, and to Tertia, freedwoman of Lucius, daughter of Lucius Pontius. Situated in the field, 13 feet [in extent].
EDCS-08201255.summary: This inscription defines the dimensions of a plot of land owned by Lucius Pontius Phileros and Tertia.

EDCS-08201256.translation: For Primitivos and Primigenius, slaves of Aufidius Philargyrus.
EDCS-08201256.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to two slaves, Primitivos and Primigenius.

EDCS-08201257.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Proculus, a most sweet child, who lived 6 years and 2 months. Gemellinus and Victoria erected this for their dearest son, who deserved well.
EDCS-08201257.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Proculus, a child, and was erected by his parents, Gemellinus and Victoria.

EDCS-08201258.translation: Quintus Publicius Demosthenes is buried here. In front, 12 feet; in the field, 14 feet.
EDCS-08201258.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Quintus Publicius Demosthenes and the dimensions of his plot.

EDCS-08201259.translation: [To] Rabiria Modesta, who lived 5 years [illegible]. Cornelius [illegible] and Rabiria [illegible] erected [this] for [their] daughter.
EDCS-08201259.summary: This damaged funerary inscription records the death of Rabiria Modesta, likely a child, and was erected by her parents.

EDCS-08201260.translation: Raevidia Optata, freedwoman of Raevidia, for herself and for her mother Raevidia Iucunda. In front, 12 feet; in the field, 12 feet.
EDCS-08201260.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot purchased by Raevidia Optata for herself and her mother.

EDCS-08201261.translation: For Roscius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Pomptina tribe. In front, 12 feet.
EDCS-08201261.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot belonging to Roscius Rufus.

EDCS-08201262.translation: Publius Rufrenus Spectatus for himself and for his wife Cassia Prima, and for his mother-in-law Cassia Silvia.
EDCS-08201262.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication by Publius Rufrenus Spectatus for himself, his wife, and his mother-in-law.

EDCS-08201264.translation: [Sat]rena Calene placed this for herself and for [Caius] Antistius Buccio.
EDCS-08201264.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Satrena Calene for herself and Caius Antistius Buccio.

EDCS-08201265.translation: Grapte, servant of our Caesar, placed this for her well-deserving husband Satrio Isargyrus and for herself.
EDCS-08201265.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Grapte, a servant of the emperor, for her husband and herself.

EDCS-08201266.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Verecunda placed this for her well-deserving wife Appia Rufa, servant, and for herself.
EDCS-08201266.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Verecunda for her wife Appia Rufa and herself.

EDCS-08201267.translation: For their bones, Numerius Scalacius Stabilius and Numerius Scalacius Clemens. Fate...
EDCS-08201267.summary: This inscription appears to mark the burial place of Numerius Scalacius Stabilius and Numerius Scalacius Clemens, possibly indicating their remains.

EDCS-08201268.translation: Lucius Scutarius Andrea and Marcus Turellius Diomedes, freedman of Marcus, lie here, 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-08201268.summary: This funerary inscription marks a plot of land measuring 12x12 feet, belonging to Lucius Scutarius Andrea and Marcus Turellius Diomedes.

EDCS-08201269.translation: Publius Servius Census placed this, 12 feet in the field and 12 feet in front.
EDCS-08201269.summary: This inscription marks a plot of land measuring 12x12 feet, dedicated by Publius Servius Census.

EDCS-08201271.translation: Sibilisia Tacita, daughter of Caius, [placed this] for herself and for Caius Statius Iaso, son of Caius, her husband.
EDCS-08201271.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Sibilisia Tacita for herself and her husband, Caius Statius Iaso.

EDCS-08201272.translation: Alphius placed this for Silva Betita Pii, his incomparable fellow servant, and for himself.
EDCS-08201272.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Alphius for his fellow servant Silva Betita Pii and himself.

EDCS-08201275.translation: Statilia Prima, freedwoman of Auctus, [this plot measures] 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-08201275.summary: This inscription marks a plot of land measuring 12x12 feet, belonging to Statilia Prima, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08201279.translation: Caius Egnatius Pudens placed this for Marcus Ampliatus and Badia Medusa, his friend and sister.
EDCS-08201279.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Caius Egnatius Pudens for his friend Marcus Ampliatus and his sister Badia Medusa.

EDCS-08201282.translation: To Thesmos, son of Volusius Lepidus (?). His mother [3]ta placed this monument with dimensions of 12 feet in front and 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-08201282.summary: A funerary monument marking a plot of land, likely for Thesmos, with dimensions specified.

EDCS-08201288.translation: Tullia Hilara, freedwoman of Marcus, [is buried here]; her plot is 12 feet in front and 10 feet in depth.
EDCS-08201288.summary: A funerary inscription for Tullia Hilara, a freedwoman, specifying the dimensions of her burial plot.

EDCS-08201290.translation: Valentinus and Capriola set this up for their son Valentino, slave of Acuvia Liciniana, who lived 59 years, 6 months, and 15 days, and for themselves.
EDCS-08201290.summary: A family funerary monument set up by Valentinus and Capriola for their son Valentino and for themselves.

EDCS-08201291.translation: Lucius Valerius Apronianus purchased this for his wife Innevia Gaia Dom[---], with whom he lived 11 years, and placed it as a mark of well-deserved affection.
EDCS-08201291.summary: A funerary monument purchased by Lucius Valerius Apronianus for his wife Innevia Gaia Dom[---], indicating the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-08201293.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Veidius Aphrodisius lived 10 years and 4 days. His mother Aphrodosia placed this.
EDCS-08201293.summary: A funerary monument for Veidius Aphrodisius, who died young, placed by his mother Aphrodosia.

EDCS-08201294.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Venellia Primitiva placed this for her well-deserving friend Eutrapelus.
EDCS-08201294.summary: A funerary monument placed by Venellia Primitiva for her friend Eutrapelus.

EDCS-08201295.translation: [---]dius Verres, son of Publius, of the Horatia tribe, [erected this] in accordance with his will.
EDCS-08201295.summary: An inscription indicating that Verres, son of Publius, erected a monument as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-08201296.translation: Gaius Vetius Pylaes for himself and for Ferrena Rufilla, freedwoman of Titus, his wife; 12 feet in front and 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-08201296.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Vetius Pylaes and his wife Ferrena Rufilla, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-08201298.translation: Quintus Hermias, freedman of Quintus, [plot dimensions] 12 feet in front and 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-08201298.summary: A funerary monument for Quintus Hermias, a freedman, specifying the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-08201299.translation: Lucius Onesimus, freedman of Lucius, for himself [erected this].
EDCS-08201299.summary: A funerary inscription indicating that Lucius Onesimus, a freedman, set up a monument for himself.

EDCS-08201300.translation: Marcus Vibius Pamphylus, freedman of Marcus, is buried here. His freedman, Marcus Vibius Pamphylus, set this up for himself and his family. Frontage 12 feet, depth 12 feet.
EDCS-08201300.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Vibius Pamphylus, set up by his freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201301.translation: Menatia Valentina and Aibarus, his parents, set this up for Gaius Virginus Aibarus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-08201301.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Virginus Aibarus, set up by his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201302.translation: Viselia Sofanisis, daughter of Quintus. Frontage 12 feet, depth 12 feet.
EDCS-08201302.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating plot dimensions for Viselia Sofanisis, daughter of Quintus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201313.translation: Sextus Fabius. Frontage 12 feet, depth 14 feet.
EDCS-08201313.summary: A funerary epitaph indicating plot dimensions for Sextus Fabius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201349.translation: Iulia Firmilla set this up for her unfortunate son, a soldier of the praetorian cohort IX, an equestrian, who served for 13 years and lived for 31 years, and for herself.
EDCS-08201349.summary: A funerary epitaph for a praetorian soldier, set up by his mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201350.translation: Lucius Ceternius, son of Annius, of the Horatia tribe, a IIvir with curule authority, is buried here.
EDCS-08201350.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Ceternius, who held the office of IIvir, likely dating to the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-08201351.translation: To Gaius Egnatius Maro, son of Gaius, of the Horatia tribe, priest of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, IIvir quinquennalis, and three times prefect of the artisans; and to Egnatia Marulla, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-08201351.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating several public offices held by Gaius Egnatius Maro, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201352.translation: Aurelia Donata lived for 55 years. Her son Beryllus set this up for his well-deserving mother.
EDCS-08201352.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Donata, set up by her son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201353.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cassia lived for 22 years, 3 months, and 29 days. Ianuarius made this for his well-deserving companion and for himself.
EDCS-08201353.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cassia, set up by Ianuarius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201354.translation: Anicia Venusta, freedwoman of Quintus, had this made for her daughter Equitia Tertulla, daughter of Marcus, and for her grandson Quintus Stallius Clementi, son of Quintus.
EDCS-08201354.summary: A funerary epitaph commissioned by Anicia Venusta for her daughter and grandson, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201358.translation: Petronia Constantiana, daughter of Publius, lies here, an incomparable woman who lived 55 years, of which she was with her husband Savonius Lemnus for 30 years.
EDCS-08201358.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Petronia Constantiana, recording her lifespan and her marriage duration.

EDCS-08201364.translation: To Publius Fundanius Priscus, son of Publius, of the Papiria tribe, patron of the municipality, who fulfilled all offices and burdens, patron of the citizenry of Ausculum, who, since he often provided many and the greatest benefits to the state, also introduced a new fountain at his own expense with great expenditure for its construction and reformed the citizens and the homeland, the capulatores [dedicate this] to their most outstanding patron, a place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201364.summary: This is a dedication honoring Publius Fundanius Priscus, a patron of Ausculum, for his public benefactions, particularly the construction of a fountain, and was erected by the capulatores by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-08201384.translation: Aulus Hymnus, freedman of Aulus, and Canuleia Ficele, his wife.
EDCS-08201384.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Hymnus and his wife Canuleia Ficele.

EDCS-08201385.translation: Lucius Publilius Patruinus, a most distinguished man, [dedicated] a temple with an altar to Diana.
EDCS-08201385.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple and altar to Diana by Lucius Publilius Patruinus, a man of consular rank.

EDCS-08201387.translation: To Lucius Publilius Celsus Patruinus, consular man, curator of the state, the town council and people of Canusium [dedicate this] to their patron for his singular justice, restraint, and honor.
EDCS-08201387.summary: This is a dedication by the citizens of Canusium to their patron, Lucius Publilius Celsus Patruinus, honoring his justice and integrity.

EDCS-08201388.translation: Gaius Dasimius, son of Gaius, aedile, praetor, and Busia Sabuli, daughter of Dasimius.
EDCS-08201388.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Dasimius, who held the offices of aedile and praetor, and his wife Busia Sabuli.

EDCS-08201389.text_edition: \n Divo Severo Augusto publice decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08201389.translation: To the Deified Severus Augustus, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201389.summary: This inscription records a public decree by the decurions to honor the deified Emperor Severus Augustus.

EDCS-08201391.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Galerio Valerio Maximino nobilissimo Caesari Ulpius Alenus vir perfectissimus corrector Apuliae et Calabriae devotus numini maiestatique eius Dedicata V Nonas Martias Aemiliano II et Aquilino consulibus\n
EDCS-08201391.translation: To our lord Galerius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesar, Ulpius Alenus, a most perfect man, corrector of Apulia and Calabria, devoted to his divine majesty, dedicated on the 5th day before the Nones of March in the consulship of Aemilianus II and Aquilinus.
