EDCS-08900229.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and burial of Birria Cognita, a wet-nurse.

EDCS-08900230.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Prigimenius sic Felicitati suae conservae fecit con sic qua vixit nnos XX\n
EDCS-08900230.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Prigimenius made this for his preserved slave and companion, with whom he lived 20 years.
EDCS-08900230.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Prigimenius to his deceased slave and companion, Felicitas.

EDCS-08900231.text_edition: \n Maecia liberta Prima hic sita est\n
EDCS-08900231.translation: Maecia Prima, the freedwoman, is buried here.
EDCS-08900231.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Maecia Prima, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08900232.text_edition: \n Aulus Maianius Speratus vixit annos XVIIII\n
EDCS-08900232.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Aulus Maianius Speratus.

EDCS-08900233.text_edition: \n Dilices sic vixit annos vix L hic situs\n
EDCS-08900233.translation: Dilices lived about 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-08900233.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial of Dilices.

EDCS-08900234.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sosiusa vixit nnos LII serba sic hic sita\n
EDCS-08900234.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Sosiusa, a slave, lived 52 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-08900234.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan and burial of Sosiusa, a slave.

EDCS-08900237.translation: Gaius Atilius Phileros is buried here.
EDCS-08900237.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Gaius Atilius Phileros.

EDCS-08900238.translation: A pot for Lucius Aemilius Eutyches.
EDCS-08900238.summary: This inscription on a pot records its owner, Lucius Aemilius Eutyches.

EDCS-08900240.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or ownership mark for Caesia Chreste.

EDCS-08900241.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Asellius N vixit annos LXXX Alexianus \n
EDCS-08900241.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Asellius N. lived 80 years. Alexianus.
EDCS-08900241.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Asellius N. and is associated with Alexianus.

EDCS-08900242.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodia Iunix vixit annos L hic sita est Hostius Laetus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-08900242.translation: To the departed spirits. Clodia Iunix lived 50 years and is buried here. Hostius Laetus placed this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-08900242.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia Iunix, erected by her husband Hostius Laetus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900243.text_edition: \n Ophelis vixit annos XXXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-08900243.translation: Ophelis lived 40 years and is buried here.
EDCS-08900243.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ophelis, a woman who died at the age of 40, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900244.text_edition: \n Marcus Caprius Aglaus vixit annos LV\n
EDCS-08900244.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Caprius Aglaus, who died at the age of 55, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900245.text_edition: \n Theodoe vixit annos X hic sita est Honoratus pater filiae\n
EDCS-08900245.translation: Theodoe lived 10 years and is buried here. Her father Honoratus placed this for his daughter.
EDCS-08900245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Theodoe, a young girl who died at the age of 10, erected by her father Honoratus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900246.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Germana serba sic Apolloni vixit annos V hic sita est Germanus pater filiae dulcissima<e=I> \n
EDCS-08900246.translation: To the departed spirits. Germana, a slave of Apollonius, lived 5 years and is buried here. Her father Germanus placed this for his most sweet daughter.
EDCS-08900246.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Germana, a slave, who died at the age of 5, erected by her father Germanus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900247.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Delphices Sextus Aveius Longinus uxori karissimae fecit\n
EDCS-08900247.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextus Aveius Longinus made this for his dearest wife, Aelia Delphices.
EDCS-08900247.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Delphices, erected by her husband Sextus Aveius Longinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900249.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hermadio Augustorum nnostrorum servus vixit annos XXXV hic situs est Beneria sic fratri dulcissymo sic faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08900249.translation: To the departed spirits. Hermadius, a slave of our Emperors, lived 35 years and is buried here. Beneria arranged for this to be made for her most sweet brother.
EDCS-08900249.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hermadius, a slave of the emperors, erected by his sister Beneria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900250.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Papas Iusti servus vixit annos LX bene merenti conserva Callipolis fecit hic situs est\n
EDCS-08900250.translation: To the departed spirits. Papas Iusti, a slave, lived 60 years. His deserving freedwoman Callipolis made this for him. He is buried here.
EDCS-08900250.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Papas Iusti, a slave, erected by his freedwoman Callipolis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900251.text_edition: \n Salvia Iusti serva vixit annis hic sita est SCconservus bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-08900251.translation: Salvia, a slave of Iustus, lived [number of years missing] years and is buried here. Her fellow slave arranged for this to be made for her deserving [self].
EDCS-08900251.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Salvia, a slave, erected by a fellow slave, with the duration of her life missing, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900252.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felix, who lived 51 years, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900253.text_edition: \n Spaectaetus vixit annos III hic situs Spaictata sic vixit annos IX hic sita EVSNSVI bene maerentibus sic\n
EDCS-08900253.translation: Spaectaetus lived 3 years, here he lies. Spictata lived 9 years, here she lies. EVSNSVI, to those who grieve well.
EDCS-08900253.summary: This is a funerary inscription for two individuals, Spaectaetus and Spictata, likely children given their ages and the phrase 'to those who grieve well'.

EDCS-08900254.text_edition: \n Marcus Iuventius Maesius veteranus legionis IIII Scythicae militavit nnos XXX vixit annos LX hic situs est\n
EDCS-08900254.translation: Marcus Iuventius Maesius, a veteran of the IV Scythica legion, served for 30 years, lived for 60 years, here he lies.
EDCS-08900254.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Iuventius Maesius, a veteran of the IV Scythica legion, detailing his military service and lifespan.

EDCS-08900257.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Constanti filio Flavio Valerio Constantino Pio Felici Invicto Augusto consuli III imperatori VIIII patri patriae proconsuli \n
EDCS-08900257.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Constantius, Flavius Valerius Constantinus, pious, fortunate, unconquered Augustus, consul for the third time, imperator for the ninth time, father of the fatherland, proconsul.
EDCS-08900257.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Emperor Constantine the Great, listing his titles and consulships, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-08900892.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flegusa Anni Veri serva vixit annos XXXX hic sita Euhelpistus conservae merenti\n
EDCS-08900892.translation: To the departed spirits. Flegusa, slave of Annius Verus, lived 40 years, here she lies. Euhelpistus, to his deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-08900892.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flegusa, a slave of Annius Verus, erected by her fellow slave Euhelpistus.

EDCS-08900894.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Apriculus hic situs est vixit annos VI\n
EDCS-08900894.translation: To the departed spirits. Apriculus, here he lies, lived 6 years.
EDCS-08900894.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Apriculus, a child who died at the age of six.

EDCS-09001233.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sollerti Cai Umbri Cai filii Stellatina Concordi Secundi adlecti in ordinem decurionum Concordia Prima mater bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09001233.translation: To the departed spirits, sacred to Sollertia. Concordia Prima, mother, made this for her deserving daughter, the wife of Caius Umbrius, son of Caius, of the Stellatina tribe, appointed to the order of decurions.
EDCS-09001233.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Concordia Prima to her daughter Sollertia, who was the wife of Caius Umbrius and held a position in local government.

EDCS-09001239.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tiberi Claudi Tildyi sic Augustalis vixit annis XXXIII mense I dies sic XXIIII parentes filio piissimo\n
EDCS-09001239.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Tiberius Claudius Tildius, also called Augustalis, parents for their most dutiful son, lived 33 years, 1 month, and 24 days.
EDCS-09001239.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Tildius, erected by his parents, stating his age at death and an additional cognomen.

EDCS-09001240.text_edition: \n Halinus et ni uxori arbitratu uxorisque \n
EDCS-09001240.translation: Halina and to his wife by the wife's decree.
EDCS-09001240.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a funerary inscription, mentioning Halina and his wife, with the latter having some agency in its erection.

EDCS-09001244.text_edition: \n Caius Salvius Occiae libertus Zosimus Augustalis quinquennalis hic situs suadet quae fecit et ipse unguere ama pota fruere utere nosce miser te thensaurum sic heredis vescere avare tui Salvia Lasciva \n
EDCS-09001244.translation: Gaius Salvius Zosimus, freedman of Occia, Augustalis for five years, here he lies. He advises: 'Love to anoint, drink, enjoy, use, know the miser, you of your heir's treasure; feed greedily.' Salvia Lasciva.
EDCS-09001244.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Salvius Zosimus, a freedman and local official, which includes a somewhat didactic and peculiar epitaph, possibly intended by his heir Salvia Lasciva.

EDCS-09001245.text_edition: \n Lucifero Rufines sic servo colono Daphne conserva Salvius filius bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-09001245.translation: To Luciferous, the freedman, the farmer, and Daphne, the slave woman. Salvius, son, placed [this] for his deserving [parents/benefactors].
EDCS-09001245.summary: This funerary inscription, likely erected by Salvius, honors Luciferous, a freedman farmer, and Daphne, a slave woman.

EDCS-09001246.text_edition: \n Titus Petronius Cai filius Horatia faber\n
EDCS-09001246.translation: Titus Petronius, son of Caius, of the Horatia tribe, craftsman.
EDCS-09001246.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Petronius, identifying his name, father, tribal affiliation, and profession, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09001247.translation: To Rhodanus, freedman of Seppius Rufus, moneyer. This way.
EDCS-09001247.summary: This inscription appears to be a directional marker or a dedication by Rhodanus, a freedman and moneyer, possibly related to a public building or area, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001248.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Cornelio Primigenio Cornelia Chrysodice coniugi bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09001248.translation: Cornelia Chrysodice erected this for her well-deserving husband, Cnaeus Cornelius Primigenius.
EDCS-09001248.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Cornelia Chrysodice for her husband, Cnaeus Cornelius Primigenius, indicating a relationship of devotion and remembrance, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001249.text_edition: \n Caius Magious sic Cai filius Hor(atia) ---\n
EDCS-09001249.translation: Caius Magious, son of Caius, of the Horatia tribe. [---]
EDCS-09001249.summary: This incomplete inscription identifies Caius Magious by name, father, and tribal affiliation, suggesting it was part of a longer dedication or epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001250.text_edition: \n Locus Octavia |(mulieris) liberta Arbuscula vivit Statia Cai filia Chila Caius Statius Cai libertus Statius in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XII\n
EDCS-09001250.translation: Octavia Arbuscula, freedwoman of the (woman) [Octavia?], lives. Statia Chila, daughter of Caius, and Caius Statius, freedman of Caius, (dedicate this?) in front 12 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-09001250.summary: This inscription seems to record property boundaries or dimensions related to Octavia Arbuscula, her freedwoman status, and potentially Statia Chila and Caius Statius, likely marking land for funerary or residential use in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001255.text_edition: \n Publius Memmius Publi libertus Diogenes Augustalis prr factus tribunal et tectum sua pecunia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09001255.translation: Publius Memmius Publi libertus Diogenes Augustalis, former praetor, undertook to have the tribunal and roof built with his own money.
EDCS-09001255.summary: This inscription records a public works project funded by Publius Memmius Diogenes Augustalis, a former praetor and freedman, concerning the construction of a tribunal and roof, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001256.summary: This fragmentary inscription names Quintus Marcius Phil-, likely the beginning of a full name, possibly for a funerary or dedicatory monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001257.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Felicla vixit annos XXXII Talassus coniugi bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09001257.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Felicla lived 32 years. Talassus erected this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-09001257.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felicla, who lived 32 years, erected by her husband Talassus, expressing love and remembrance, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001258.text_edition: \n Octavia Marci liberta Prima hic sita est vixit annis C\n
EDCS-09001258.translation: Octavia Prima, freedwoman of Marcus, is buried here. She lived 100 years.
EDCS-09001258.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Octavia Prima, a freedwoman of Marcus, stating her burial location and remarkable age of 100 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001259.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publi Aeli Augusti liberti Blasti liberti et heredes patrono bene merenti\n
EDCS-09001259.translation: The freedmen and heirs of Publius Aelius Blastus, freedman of the Emperor, (dedicate this) to their deserving patron.
EDCS-09001259.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the freedmen and heirs of Publius Aelius Blastus, a freedman of Emperor Hadrian, to their patron, indicating a relationship of loyalty and posthumous respect, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001260.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus Natalis Quinti Herenni servus vixit annos XXXV Prima coniugi mi bene merenti fili patri merenti posuerunt hic situs\n
EDCS-09001260.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Natalis, a slave of Quintus Herennius, lived 35 years. Prima placed this for her well-deserving husband and son. Here he lies.
EDCS-09001260.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Natalis, a slave, and was set up by his wife Prima, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200118.text_edition: \n Ania Comice vixit annos XXX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200118.translation: Ania Comice lived 30 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-09200118.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Ania Comice, who died at the age of 30, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200119.text_edition: \n Mercellia Titi liberta Anthis vixit annos LX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200119.translation: Mercellia Anthis, freedwoman of Titus, lived 60 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-09200119.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Mercellia Anthis, a freedwoman, who died at 60 years of age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200120.text_edition: \n Antonia Anthis vixit annis XLV hic sita est\n
EDCS-09200120.translation: Antonia Anthis lived 45 years. Here she is buried.
EDCS-09200120.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Anthis, who lived 45 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200121.text_edition: \n Numerius Tereus Proculus vixit annos XXXV hic situs\n
EDCS-09200121.translation: Numerius Tereus Proculus lived 35 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-09200121.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Numerius Tereus Proculus, who died at 35 years of age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200122.translation: Sacred to the Deities of the Underworld. Tiberius Claudius [ --- ]
EDCS-09200122.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Deities of the Underworld, likely for a Tiberius Claudius, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200123.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Titus Clodius Pistus lived 31 years. Here he lies. Ca[---] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-09200123.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Clodius Pistus, who died at 31, and mentions a dedication to his wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200124.text_edition: \n Caius Flavius Cai libertus Nicephor vixit annos XLV hic situs Iolle bene merenti coniugi fecit\n
EDCS-09200124.translation: Caius Flavius Nicephor, freedman of Caius, lived 45 years. Here he lies. He made this for his well-deserving wife, Iolle.
EDCS-09200124.summary: This funerary inscription records the life of Caius Flavius Nicephor, a freedman, who died at 45, and notes that he erected it for his wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200125.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fabia Marci liberta Vera vixit annos XX \n
EDCS-09200125.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Fabia Vera, freedwoman of Marcius, lived 20 years.
EDCS-09200125.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Deities of the Underworld for Fabia Vera, a freedwoman, who died at 20 years of age, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200126.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cnaeus Pomponius Plocamio vixit annos XIV hic situs Pomponia Plocamis vixit annos XXX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200126.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Gnaeus Pomponius Plocamius lived 14 years. Here he lies. Pomponia Plocamis lived 30 years. Here she lies.
EDCS-09200126.summary: This funerary inscription records the lives and burials of Gnaeus Pomponius Plocamius (aged 14) and Pomponia Plocamis (aged 30), likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09200127.text_edition: \n Prima Frecania Cai liberta hic sita\n
EDCS-09200127.translation: Prima Frecania, freedwoman of Caius, lies here.
EDCS-09200127.summary: This is a tombstone for Prima Frecania, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200128.translation: Epitynchanus, freedman of Titus, lived [X] years.
EDCS-09200128.summary: This is a tombstone fragment for Epitynchanus, a freedman, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200129.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcius MuIIEMONI, who lived 80 years, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200132.text_edition: \n vixit annos XXIII Marcus Aeficius Hermes sorori pientissimae\n
EDCS-09200132.translation: Marcus Aeficius Hermes lived 23 years; he made this for his most dutiful sister.
EDCS-09200132.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Aeficius Hermes, who lived 23 years, and also mentions his sister, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200134.text_edition: \n Tiberius Aulius Tiberi libertus Felicio vixit annis XXV hic situs Aulia Tiberi liberta vixit annis LXXX Gnoste mater hic sita\n
EDCS-09200134.translation: Tiberius Aulius Felicio, freedman of Tiberius, lived 25 years; he lies here. Aulia, freedwoman of Tiberius, lived 80 years; Gnoste, mother, lies here.
EDCS-09200134.summary: This is a tombstone for Tiberius Aulius Felicio and Aulia, mentioning their mother Gnoste, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200135.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Pinnius Natalis vixit annis VI Caius Pinnius Chrysanthus pater Pinnia Natalia vixit annis VI hic siti\n
EDCS-09200135.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Pinnius Natalis lived 6 years. Caius Pinnius Chrysanthus, father. Pinnia Natalia lived 6 years; they lie here.
EDCS-09200135.summary: This is a tombstone for two children, Caius Pinnius Natalis and Pinnia Natalia, and their father Caius Pinnius Chrysanthus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200136.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cornelia Primigenia vixit annos XXIII hic sita est Ursus coniugi bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09200136.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelia Primigenia lived 23 years; she lies here. Ursus made this for his well-deserving spouse.
EDCS-09200136.summary: This is a tombstone for Cornelia Primigenia, for whom her husband Ursus erected a monument, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200137.text_edition: \n Caius Sentius Cai libertus Fronto Sentia Cai liberta Malchis hic siti\n
EDCS-09200137.translation: Caius Sentius Fronto, freedman of Caius, and Sentia Malchis, freedwoman of Caius, lie here.
EDCS-09200137.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Sentius Fronto and Sentia Malchis, both freedpersons of Caius, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200138.text_edition: \n Festiba sic Auli Albi Pacati liberta vixit annos LX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200138.translation: Festiba, freedwoman of Aulus Albius Pacatus, lived 60 years; she lies here.
EDCS-09200138.summary: This is a tombstone for Festiba, a freedwoman, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200139.text_edition: \n Marcus Pulius Marci libertus Eros situs\n
EDCS-09200139.translation: Marcus Pulius Eros, freedman of Marcus, lies here.
EDCS-09200139.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Pulius Eros, a freedman, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09200140.text_edition: \n Octavia Improbata vixit annos LIII menses III hic sita\n
EDCS-09200140.translation: Octavia Improbata lived 53 years and 3 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-09200140.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Octavia Improbata, recording her age at death.

EDCS-09200141.text_edition: \n Lucius Audius Luci libertus Euhodus vixit annos XL hic situs est Argus filius eius vixit annos IIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-09200141.translation: Lucius Audius Euhodus, freedman of Lucius, lived 40 years; he is buried here. Argus, his son, lived 4 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09200141.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Audius Euhodus and his son Argus, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-09200142.text_edition: \n Octavia Suntuche sic vixit annos XXIV hic sita\n
EDCS-09200142.translation: Octavia Suntuche lived 24 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09200142.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Octavia Suntuche, recording her age at death.

EDCS-09200143.text_edition: \n Primilla Cuppi vixit nnos XII hic sita\n
EDCS-09200143.translation: Primilla Cuppi lived 12 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09200143.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Primilla Cuppi, recording her age at death.

EDCS-09200144.text_edition: \n Caius Sentius Fronto hic situs est\n
EDCS-09200144.translation: Gaius Sentius Fronto is buried here.
EDCS-09200144.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Sentius Fronto, indicating his burial place.

EDCS-09200145.text_edition: \n Mamia Amarillis vixit annos LX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200145.translation: Mamia Amarillis lived 60 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09200145.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Mamia Amarillis, recording her age at death.

EDCS-09200146.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Philodespotos, indicating his burial place.

EDCS-09200147.text_edition: \n Accia Synethea vixit annos LXXX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200147.translation: Accia Synethea lived 80 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09200147.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Accia Synethea, recording her age at death.

EDCS-09200148.text_edition: \n Annia Anni liberta Laeis vixit annos XX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200148.translation: Annia Laeis, freedwoman of Annia, lived 20 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09200148.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Annia Laeis, recording her age at death.

EDCS-09200149.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fabia Marci filia Thallusa vixit annos XXII menses VII hic sita\n
EDCS-09200149.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Fabia Thallusa, daughter of Marcus Fabius, lived 22 years and 7 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-09200149.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fabia Thallusa, daughter of Marcus Fabius, recording her age at death.

EDCS-09200150.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gerellana Sosiusa vixit annos LX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200150.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gerellana Sosiusa lived for 60 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200150.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gerellana Sosiusa, who lived to 60 years.

EDCS-09200151.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Publicius Thallus vixit annos L hic situs\n
EDCS-09200151.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Publicius Thallus lived for 50 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200151.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Publicius Thallus, who lived to 50 years.

EDCS-09200152.text_edition: \n Caius Habidius Cai libertus Megistio hic situs\n
EDCS-09200152.translation: Caius Habidius Megistio, freedman of Caius, is buried here.
EDCS-09200152.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Habidius Megistio, a freedman.

EDCS-09200153.text_edition: \n Appalius Cha<r=P>ito vixit annos LXV Aequitas coniugi bene merenti posuit \n
EDCS-09200153.translation: Aequitas placed this for her deserving husband, Appalius Charito, who lived for 65 years.
EDCS-09200153.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aequitas for her husband, Appalius Charito.

EDCS-09200154.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Octabia sic Chrestya sic vixit annos XXXV hic sita\n
EDCS-09200154.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Octabia, called Chrestya, lived for 35 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200154.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Octabia, who lived to 35 years.

EDCS-09200155.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Granius Cerdo vixit annos XXXX posuit Memmia coniugi merenti\n
EDCS-09200155.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Granius Cerdo lived for 40 years and Memmia placed this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-09200155.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Granius Cerdo, set up by his wife Memmia.

EDCS-09200156.text_edition: \n Nomius vixit annos VIII hic situs\n
EDCS-09200156.translation: Nomius lived for 8 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200156.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nomius, who lived to 8 years.

EDCS-09200157.translation: Tutoria, freedwoman of [a woman], Aprodisia, is buried here.
EDCS-09200157.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tutoria Aprodisia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-09200158.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maecia Irene vixit annos XIX hic sita Maecia Crysis vixit annos LX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200158.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maecia Irene lived for 19 years and is buried here. Maecia Crysis lived for 60 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200158.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the deaths of two women, Maecia Irene (19) and Maecia Crysis (60).

EDCS-09200159.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Seius Victor vixit annos XXIII hic situs Decimus Iulius Victor pater vixit annos LX Quintus Seius Primigenius fratri piissimo\n
EDCS-09200159.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Seius Victor lived for 23 years and is buried here. Decimus Iulius Victor the father lived for 60 years. [Dedicated by] Quintus Seius Primigenius to his most devoted brother.
EDCS-09200159.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Seius Victor (23), with mention of his father Decimus Iulius Victor (60), and dedicated by his brother Quintus Seius Primigenius.

EDCS-09200160.text_edition: \n Lucius Longinus Dorephorus vixit annos XL hic situs\n
EDCS-09200160.translation: Lucius Longinus Dorephorus lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09200160.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of Lucius Longinus Dorephorus.

EDCS-09200161.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus Marcus Laenius Cerialis vixit annos XXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09200161.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Laenius Cerialis lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09200161.summary: A tombstone dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Marcus Laenius Cerialis, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-09200162.text_edition: \n Caius Vibreius CCaiorum et |(mulieris) libertus Felix vixit annos XXIIX\n
EDCS-09200162.translation: Felix, freedman of Caius Vibreius and his wife, lived 28 years.
EDCS-09200162.summary: An epitaph recording the lifespan of Felix, a freedman, and mentioning his patrons.

EDCS-09200163.summary: An epitaph recording the lifespan of Seia Zosima.

EDCS-09200164.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Colenda vixit annos XXVIII Armiger coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09200164.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Armiger placed this for his deserving wife, Colenda, who lived 28 years.
EDCS-09200164.summary: A tombstone dedicated by Armiger to his wife Colenda, recording her age and his devotion.

EDCS-09200165.translation: Caius Egnatuleius Rufus is buried here.
EDCS-09200165.summary: A tombstone indicating the burial place of Caius Egnatuleius Rufus.

EDCS-09200166.text_edition: \n Crescens publicus arcarius vixit annos XXI hic situs\n
EDCS-09200166.translation: Crescens, a public treasurer, lived 21 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09200166.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of Crescens, identified by his public office as a treasurer.

EDCS-09200168.text_edition: \n Valeria Beronice sic vixit nnos XXXV hic sita\n
EDCS-09200168.translation: Valeria Beronice lived 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-09200168.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan and burial of Valeria Beronice.

EDCS-09200169.text_edition: \n Marcus Octavius Marci libertus Philodespotus hic situs\n
EDCS-09200169.translation: Marcus Octavius Philodespotus, freedman of Marcus, is buried here.
EDCS-09200169.summary: A tombstone identifying the burial place of Marcus Octavius Philodespotus, a freedman, and mentioning his former master.

EDCS-09200170.summary: An inscription that begins with the name Sextus Aemilius but is incomplete.

EDCS-09200171.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Octavius Speratus.

EDCS-09200172.text_edition: \n Caius Geminius |(mulieris) libertus Secundus vixit nnos L hic situs\n
EDCS-09200172.translation: Secundus, freedman of the freedwoman Caius Geminius, lived 50 years; he lies here.
EDCS-09200172.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Secundus, a freedman, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-09200173.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Silvana Publi Mummi Sisennae serva vixit annis XII Onesime n<e=I>pticulae dulcissimae fecit hic sita est \n
EDCS-09200173.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Silvana, slave of Publius Mummius Sisenna, lived 12 years; Onesime made this for her most sweet little niece, who lies here.
EDCS-09200173.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Silvana, a slave, made by her niece Onesime, who died at the age of 12.

EDCS-09200174.text_edition: \n Dynamis vixit annos XIII hic sita\n
EDCS-09200174.translation: Dynamis lived 13 years; she lies here.
EDCS-09200174.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Dynamis, who died at the age of 13.

EDCS-09200175.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Iulius Brundisinus vixit nnos XXVIII hic situs Iulia Pallas mater\n
EDCS-09200175.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Iulius Brundisinus lived 28 years; he lies here. Iulia Pallas, his mother.
EDCS-09200175.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Iulius Brundisinus, who died at the age of 28, set up by his mother.

EDCS-09200176.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Luci liberta Hygia vixit annos XXXV hic sita\n
EDCS-09200176.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Hygia, freedwoman of Lucius Iulius, lived 35 years; she lies here.
EDCS-09200176.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Hygia, a freedwoman, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-09200177.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Luci liberta Tertia vixit annos LX Publius Gerellanus Publi libertus Taurio vixit annos LXX hic siti\n
EDCS-09200177.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iulia Tertia, freedwoman of Lucius Iulius, lived 60 years. Publius Gerellanus, freedman of Publius, lived 70 years; they lie here.
EDCS-09200177.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulia Tertia and Publius Gerellanus, who died at the ages of 60 and 70 respectively.

EDCS-09200179.text_edition: \n Lucius Clodius Modesstus sic vixit annos XL hic situs\n
EDCS-09200179.translation: Lucius Clodius Modestus lived 40 years; he lies here.
EDCS-09200179.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Clodius Modestus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-09200180.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aulus Gabinius Auli libertus Hermes vixit annos XXX hic situs\n
EDCS-09200180.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aulus Gabinius Hermes, freedman of Aulus, lived 30 years; he lies here.
EDCS-09200180.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Gabinius Hermes, a freedman, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-09200181.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Nepho vixit annos LV hic situs\n
EDCS-09200181.translation: Titus Flavius Nepho lived 55 years; he lies here.
EDCS-09200181.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Nepho, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-09200183.text_edition: \n Lucius Argentarius Treptus vixit annos XLVI hic situs Nemestronia Festa vixit annos L dies X hic sita\n
EDCS-09200183.translation: Lucius Argentarius Treptus lived 46 years and is buried here. Nemestronia Festa lived 50 years and 10 days and is buried here.
EDCS-09200183.summary: This is a tombstone recording the lives and deaths of two individuals, Lucius Argentarius Treptus and Nemestronia Festa.

EDCS-09200185.text_edition: \n Clodius libertus Communes sic vixit annos VII hic situs\n
EDCS-09200185.translation: Clodius, freedman of Communes, lived 7 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200185.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death of Clodius, a freedman.

EDCS-09200186.translation: Marcus Citius and Marcus Dimosius, gladiators. In front 20 feet, in the field 20 feet.
EDCS-09200186.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot of 20x20 feet designated for two gladiators, Marcus Citius and Marcus Dimosius.

EDCS-09200187.text_edition: \n Qenarus sic publicus arcarius thermarum vixit annos \n
EDCS-09200187.translation: Qenarus, freedman of the public accountant of the baths, lived [...] years.
EDCS-09200187.summary: This is a tombstone fragment recording the life of Qenarus, a freedman and public accountant of the baths.

EDCS-09200189.text_edition: \n Lucius Iulius Proculus vixit annos LXXV hic situs\n
EDCS-09200189.translation: Lucius Iulius Proculus lived 75 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200189.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death of Lucius Iulius Proculus.

EDCS-09200190.text_edition: \n Marcus Octavius |(mulieris) libertus Felicio vixit annos XVI hic situs\n
EDCS-09200190.translation: Marcus Octavius Felicio, freedman of a woman, lived 16 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200190.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death of Marcus Octavius Felicio, a freedman.

EDCS-09200191.text_edition: \n Decimus Paprius Eros vixit annos L hic situs\n
EDCS-09200191.translation: Decimus Paprius Eros lived 50 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200191.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death of Decimus Paprius Eros.

EDCS-09200192.text_edition: \n Pudens Glani vixit annos V hic\n
EDCS-09200192.translation: Pudens Glani lived 5 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200192.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death of Pudens Glani.

EDCS-09200193.text_edition: \n Publius Saeius sic Ammonius vixit annos LVI hic situs\n
EDCS-09200193.translation: Publius Saeius Ammonius lived 56 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200193.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death of Publius Saeius Ammonius.

EDCS-09200194.text_edition: \n Seia Euphrosyne vixit annos XXX hic sita\n
EDCS-09200194.translation: Seia Euphrosyne lived 30 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09200194.summary: This is a tombstone recording the life and death of Seia Euphrosyne.

EDCS-09200195.text_edition: \n Seia Pephilemeni liberta vixit annos XX hic sita est\n
EDCS-09200195.translation: Seia Pephilemeni, freedwoman, lived for 20 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-09200195.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedwoman named Seia Pephilemeni who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-09200196.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph for Taridia Anicula.

EDCS-09200197.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Trehptus sic \n
EDCS-09200197.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Valerius Trehptus, thus.
EDCS-09200197.summary: This inscription invokes the Divine Manes and likely introduces a funerary monument for Lucius Valerius Trehptus.

EDCS-09200198.text_edition: \n Iue vixit annos LXXV Seia Agathoclea mater eius vixit annos LXXXV Pomponia Felicitas uxor Ampliati \n
EDCS-09200198.translation: Iue lived for 75 years. Seia Agathoclea, her mother, lived for 85 years. Pomponia Felicitas, wife of Ampliatus.
EDCS-09200198.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of Iue and her mother Seia Agathoclea, and mentions Pomponia Felicitas, wife of Ampliatus.

EDCS-09200199.text_edition: \n Publius Lucilius Brundisinus vixit annos XX \n
EDCS-09200199.translation: Publius Lucilius Brundisinus lived for 20 years.
EDCS-09200199.summary: This funerary epitaph records the lifespan of Publius Lucilius Brundisinus.

EDCS-09200201.text_edition: \n Homerus Iucundae servus vixit nnos VIIII et menses VIII\n
EDCS-09200201.translation: Homerus, slave of Iucunda, lived for 9 years and 8 months.
EDCS-09200201.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Homerus, a slave who died at the age of 9 years and 8 months.

EDCS-09200202.text_edition: \n Servaea |(mulieris) liberta Cleopatra vixi annos hic sita LXV sic Caius Caesius Aesopus vixi annos LXXX hic situs\n
EDCS-09200202.translation: Servaea Cleopatra, freedwoman of a woman, lived here for 65 years. Caius Caesius Aesopus lived here for 80 years.
EDCS-09200202.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages and burial of Servaea Cleopatra and Caius Caesius Aesopus.

EDCS-09200203.text_edition: \n vixit annos XXC et Albia Ampliata threpta eius vixit annos XXX hic siti\n
EDCS-09200203.translation: Lived for 20 years, and Albia Ampliata, his freedwoman, lived for 30 years. They are buried here.
EDCS-09200203.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of an unnamed man and his freedwoman Albia Ampliata.

EDCS-09200204.translation: Aulus Maianius Fortunatus, freedman of Aulus.
EDCS-09200204.summary: This inscription identifies Aulus Maianius Fortunatus as the freedman of Aulus.

EDCS-09200205.translation: Lucius Arruntius Equiatus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-09200205.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Arruntius Equiatus as the freedman of Lucius.

EDCS-09200206.translation: Urbana, freedwoman of Publius, set this up.
EDCS-09200206.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Urbana, a freedwoman, likely for herself or a patron, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200207.text_edition: \n Caius Octavius Iustus vixit annos III SSVI \n
EDCS-09200207.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Gaius Octavius Iustus, likely a tombstone, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200208.text_edition: \n vixit annos LXXXX Sextilia Vitalia vixit annos \n
EDCS-09200208.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Sextilia Vitalia, likely a tombstone, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200209.text_edition: \n Hermes Quinti Patrensis vixit annos VIII hic situs\n
EDCS-09200209.translation: Hermes of Patra lived 8 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09200209.summary: This inscription records the burial of Hermes, an 8-year-old from Patra, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200210.text_edition: \n untius vixit annos XL hic situs\n
EDCS-09200210.translation: ...untius lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09200210.summary: This inscription records the burial of an individual whose name is partially lost, who lived 40 years, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200211.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Tranquillus Agatopus vixit annos II Cercnnabio sic threpto suo bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09200211.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Tranquillus Agatopus lived 2 years. Cercnnabius placed this for his well-deserving foster-son.
EDCS-09200211.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Manes by Cercnnabius for his foster-son Lucius Tranquillus Agatopus, who died at age 2, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200212.text_edition: \n Publius Sextilius Zosimus vixit annos XXXV hic (situs) est\n
EDCS-09200212.translation: Publius Sextilius Zosimus lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09200212.summary: This inscription records the burial of Publius Sextilius Zosimus, who lived 35 years, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200213.text_edition: \n Cassius Longinus veteranus ex classe praetoria Ravennate vixit annos L hic situs A \n
EDCS-09200213.translation: Cassius Longinus, veteran from the Praetorian fleet at Ravenna, lived 50 years; he is buried here. A.
EDCS-09200213.summary: This inscription records the burial of Cassius Longinus, a 50-year-old veteran of the Ravenna fleet, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200214.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tiberius Claudius Hellespontianus vixit annos LX hic situs Iulia uxor coniugi optimo\n
EDCS-09200214.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tiberius Claudius Hellespontianus lived 60 years; he is buried here. Iulia, his wife, for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-09200214.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Tiberius Claudius Hellespontianus, who lived 60 years, dedicated by his wife Iulia, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200215.text_edition: \n Lucius Pullius Primus vixit annos XL hic situs\n
EDCS-09200215.translation: Lucius Pullius Primus lived 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09200215.summary: This inscription records the burial of Lucius Pullius Primus, who lived 40 years, date uncertain.

EDCS-09200216.text_edition: \n Atimetus Volumni servus vixit annos VII\n
EDCS-09200216.translation: Atimetus, slave of Volumnius, lived 7 years.
EDCS-09200216.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a slave named Atimetus.

EDCS-09200217.text_edition: \n cum Firmia Primitiva vixit annos XXXV hic sita\n
EDCS-09200217.translation: Lived 35 years with Firmia Primitiva, is buried here.
EDCS-09200217.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Firmia Primitiva.

EDCS-09200218.text_edition: \n Secunda Philoxai liberta vixit annos L hic sita est\n
EDCS-09200218.translation: Secunda, freedwoman of Philoxai, lived 50 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09200218.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Secunda, a freedwoman.

EDCS-09200219.text_edition: \n Pullius Clarus vixit annos XXVI hic situs est\n
EDCS-09200219.translation: Pullius Clarus lived 26 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09200219.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Pullius Clarus.

EDCS-09200221.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Pomponius Cnaei libertus Amethustus sagarius MAC Marci Aug vixit annos LXII hic situs Gerellana Peloris vixit annos LXXV uxor hic sita\n
EDCS-09200221.translation: Cnaeus Pomponius Amethustus, freedman of Gnaeus, a sagarius (maker of ornamental coverings for litters), lived 62 years, is buried here. Gerellana Peloris lived 75 years, wife, is buried here.
EDCS-09200221.summary: A double funerary epitaph for Cnaeus Pomponius Amethustus, a freedman and craftsman, and his wife Gerellana Peloris.

EDCS-09200222.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Rufinae conserbae sic bene merenti quae vixit annos XLIII Germanus conserbus sic posuit\n
EDCS-09200222.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Germanus, a fellow slave, set this up for Rufina, a fellow slave, who well deserved it and lived 43 years.
EDCS-09200222.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a fellow slave for Rufina, a fellow slave, who had died at age 43.

EDCS-09200224.text_edition: \n Caecilia |(mulieris) liberta Truphera sic hic sita est nnorum XXV\n
EDCS-09200224.translation: Truphera, freedwoman of Caecilia (a woman), is buried here. 25 years old.
EDCS-09200224.summary: A funerary epitaph for Truphera, a freedwoman, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-09200225.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Decimo Octavio Primigenio vixit annos V menses II Faustus et Quintus threpto suo carissimo\n
EDCS-09200225.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Faustus and Quintus set this up for their dearest freedman, Decimus Octavius Primigenius, who lived 5 years and 2 months.
EDCS-09200225.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy, Decimus Octavius Primigenius, who died at age 5, set up by his two patrons.

EDCS-09200226.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Eurithymus Gerellani Diadumeni vixit annos L hic situs Oenanthe \n
EDCS-09200226.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Eurithymus, son of Gerellanus Diadumenus, lived 50 years, is buried here. Oenanthe.
EDCS-09200226.summary: A funerary epitaph for Eurithymus, son of Gerellanus Diadumenus, who died at age 50.

EDCS-09200227.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lamisca vicxit sic annos XLV hic sita est filia matri bene merenti libens posuit\n
EDCS-09200227.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lamisca lived 45 years, is buried here. Daughter set this up with willing heart for a mother who well deserved it.
EDCS-09200227.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a daughter for her mother, Lamisca, who died at age 45.

EDCS-09300160.text_edition: \n Ossa Creperei Pacatiani IIIIviri iure dicundo pontificis Crepereius Tribulis filius posuit\n
EDCS-09300160.translation: The remains of Crepereius Pacatianus, one of the four officials with judicial power, son of the priest Crepereius Tribulis, erected this.
EDCS-09300160.summary: This inscription records the funerary monument erected by Crepereius Tribulis for his son, Crepereius Pacatianus, who held a high judicial office.

EDCS-09300161.text_edition: \n Publius Tarsaeus Publi filius aedilis turrim de sua pequnia faciundam coeravit eidemque probavit constat HS MMMMMMquindecim milibus\n
EDCS-09300161.translation: Publius Tarsaeus, son of Publius, aedile, arranged for a tower to be built at his own expense and approved it; it cost 6,000,000 sesterces.
EDCS-09300161.summary: This inscription records the construction of a tower by the aedile Publius Tarsaeus at his own expense, detailing the significant cost.

EDCS-09300164.text_edition: \n Caio Ateio Cai liberto Numerio Tertia liberta posuit\n
EDCS-09300164.translation: Numerius Tertia, freedwoman of Caius Ateius, erected this for him, his freedman.
EDCS-09300164.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Numerius Tertia, a freedwoman, for her former master, Caius Ateius.

EDCS-09300165.translation: Avieddia, freedwoman of Erotis, is buried here.
EDCS-09300165.summary: This inscription records the burial place of Avieddia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-09300166.text_edition: \n Quintus Avillius Quinti libertus Lucco hic situs Quintus Avillius Quinti libertus Hilarus posuit\n
EDCS-09300166.translation: Quintus Avillius Lucco, freedman of Quintus, is buried here. Quintus Avillius Hilarus, freedman of Quintus, erected this.
EDCS-09300166.summary: This inscription records the burial place of Quintus Avillius Lucco, erected by Quintus Avillius Hilarus, both freedmen of Quintus.

EDCS-09300167.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Heleno qui vixit annis VIII SANSENA pientissimo filio\n
EDCS-09300167.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Heleno, who lived 8 years, and SANSENA, his most devoted son.
EDCS-09300167.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Shades for Heleno and his devoted son, SANSENA.

EDCS-09300169.text_edition: \n Ossa Muttienae Quinti libertae Augenis vixit annos VIII dies XXXXV\n
EDCS-09300169.translation: The remains of Muttiena Augeni, freedwoman of Quintus, who lived 8 years and 45 days.
EDCS-09300169.summary: This inscription records the burial and lifespan of Muttiena Augeni, a freedwoman.

EDCS-09300170.text_edition: \n Muttiena Tertulla hic sita Aulus Muttienus Eumenes filius posuit\n
EDCS-09300170.translation: Muttiena Tertulla is buried here. Aulus Muttienus Eumenes, her son, erected this.
EDCS-09300170.summary: This inscription records the burial place of Muttiena Tertulla, erected by her son Aulus Muttienus Eumenes.

EDCS-09300171.text_edition: \n M(arco) Plotio M(arci) liberto Epaphrodito et Cluentiae |(mulieris) libertae Augeni Marcus Plotius Marci filius Placidus sibi et parentibus posuit\n
EDCS-09300171.translation: For Marcus Plotus Epaphroditus, freedman of Marcus, and for Cluentia Augeni, freedwoman of a woman. Marcus Plotus Placidus, son of Marcus, erected this for himself and his parents.
EDCS-09300171.summary: This inscription records a family burial monument erected by Marcus Plotus Placidus for himself, his parents, and two freedpersons.

EDCS-09300172.text_edition: \n Porcia Luci liberta Glycinna sita Aulus Manlius Auli libertus Princeps\n
EDCS-09300172.translation: Porcia Glycinna, freedwoman of Lucius, is buried here. Aulus Manlius Princeps, freedman of Aulus.
EDCS-09300172.summary: This inscription records the burial place of Porcia Glycinna, freedwoman of Lucius, and mentions Aulus Manlius Princeps, a freedman.

EDCS-09300173.text_edition: \n Aulo Roscio Opsequenti sic Castricia Peregrina ex testamento eius\n
EDCS-09300173.translation: To Aulus Roscius Opsequens, thus Castricia Peregrina, from his will.
EDCS-09300173.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication made by Castricia Peregrina to Aulus Roscius Opsequens according to his will.

EDCS-09300177.text_edition: \n Primigeniae Auli Cervi Primigeni et Ummidiae Secundae filiae suae fecit carissimae vixit nnos VIIII S\n
EDCS-09300177.translation: Aulus Cervius Primigenius and his daughter Ummidia Secunda made this for their dearest daughter Primigenia, who lived 9 years.
EDCS-09300177.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument set up by parents for their young daughter, Primigenia.

EDCS-09300178.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Donatae |(mulieris) libertae bene merenti vixit annos VI menses VIIII fecit Synethia patrona\n
EDCS-09300178.translation: To the Divine Shades. Synethia, the patroness, made this for Donata, her freedwoman, who deserved well, who lived 6 years and 9 months.
EDCS-09300178.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring a freedwoman, Donata, set up by her patroness Synethia.

EDCS-09300184.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Trebius libertus Philargurus situs Aleria\n
EDCS-09300184.translation: Gnaeus Trebius Philargurus, freedman, is buried here in Aleria.
EDCS-09300184.summary: This inscription records the burial of Gnaeus Trebius Philargurus, a freedman, in Aleria.

EDCS-09300185.text_edition: \n Quintus Lergenius sic Stati filius Claudia Lep situs est\n
EDCS-09300185.translation: Quintus Lergenius, son of Statius, is buried here. Claudia Lep [is responsible?].
EDCS-09300185.summary: This inscription records the burial of Quintus Lergenius, son of Statius, likely set up by Claudia Lep.

EDCS-09300186.text_edition: \n - Lollius Luci filius Claudia Libo bucinator legionis I\n
EDCS-09300186.translation: Lollius, son of Lucius, Claudia Libo, trumpeter of the I Legion.
EDCS-09300186.summary: This inscription likely records the name and military role (trumpeter of the I Legion) of Lollius, son of Lucius, possibly related to Claudia Libo.

EDCS-09300187.translation: entius, son of Marcus, quinquennial magistrate of Bareti [---].
EDCS-09300187.summary: This inscription records the name and office (quinquennial magistrate) of entius, son of Marcus, from Bareti.

EDCS-09300872.text_edition: \n Caio Torio Eutycho posuit Caius Torius Celer patronus\n
EDCS-09300872.translation: Caius Torrius Celer, the patron, placed this for Caius Torrius Eutyches.
EDCS-09300872.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a patron, Caius Torrius Celer, for his client, Caius Torrius Eutyches.

EDCS-09300874.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aulus Cascius Marcianus matri suae Casciae Capriolae pientissimae de suo fecit bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09300874.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Cascius Marcianus made this from his own resources for his mother, Cascia Capriola, who deserved well and was most dutiful.
EDCS-09300874.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication from a son, Caius Cascius Marcianus, to his mother, Cascia Capriola.

EDCS-09300880.text_edition: \n Saturninus Caesaris servus vixit annos XXXXIIII hic situs\n
EDCS-09300880.translation: Saturninus, servant of Caesar, lived 44 years, is buried here.
EDCS-09300880.summary: This inscription records the burial of Saturninus, a servant of Caesar, who lived 44 years.

EDCS-09300885.text_edition: \n Lucio Marcio Luci liberto Medimo vixit nnos XVIIII Lucio Marcio Publi filio Severo vixit nnos menses II Lucio Marcio \n
EDCS-09300885.translation: Lucius Marcius Medimus, son of Lucius Marcius, lived 19 years. Lucius Marcius Severus, son of Publius, lived 2 years and [some months]. Lucius Marcius...
EDCS-09300885.summary: This epitaph records the deaths of at least three individuals named Lucius Marcius, likely family members, noting their ages at death.

EDCS-09400183.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Allio Liberali Afinia Patricia coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09400183.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Afinia Patricia set this up for her well-deserving wife, Allia Libera.
EDCS-09400183.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Afinia Patricia to her wife, Allia Libera, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09400184.text_edition: \n Civitati Marci Alli Restituti vernae vixit nnos XX Publius Tullius Prothymus amicae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09400184.translation: Publius Tullius Prothymus set this up for his well-deserving beloved, the slave girl (verna) of the community of Marcus Allius Restitutus, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-09400184.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a slave girl, beloved by Publius Tullius Prothymus, who was owned by Marcus Allius Restitutus.

EDCS-09400185.text_edition: \n Camuria |(mulieris) liberta Fausta et Ephesia hic sita est annorum XIIX Quintus Ennius Quinti libertus Stabilio in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XII\n
EDCS-09400185.translation: The freedwoman Camuria Fausta and Ephesia are buried here; she was 18 years old. Quintus Ennius Stabilis, freedman of Quintus Ennius, [purchased this plot of] 12 feet in front and 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-09400185.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Camuria Fausta, aged 18, and provides dimensions for the burial plot purchased by Quintus Ennius Stabilis.

EDCS-09400187.text_edition: \n Lucretiae Maximae Mettia Sabina matri bene merenti et Salvidenus Saturninus contibernali sic karissimae posuerunt\n
EDCS-09400187.translation: Mettia Sabina set this up for Lucretia Maxima, her well-deserving mother, and Salvidenus Saturninus set this up for [his] dearest fellow soldier (contubernalis).
EDCS-09400187.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Mettia Sabina for her mother, Lucretia Maxima, and by Salvidenus Saturninus for his close comrade.

EDCS-09400188.text_edition: \n Ostiensi Septimina conserva bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09400188.translation: Septimina set this up for her well-deserving fellow slave (conserva) Ostiensis.
EDCS-09400188.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Septimina for her fellow slave, Ostiensis.

EDCS-09400189.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Seppiae Benivolae Quintus Seppius Fortunatus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09400189.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Seppius Fortunatus set this up for his well-deserving wife, Seppia Benivola.
EDCS-09400189.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Quintus Seppius Fortunatus for his wife, Seppia Benivola.

EDCS-09400190.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ioviano Seppies sic Rufines sic servo Capriolus et Aphro et Iovicae et sibi vixit menses XI dies XXII\n
EDCS-09400190.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Capriolus and Aphro and Iovica set this up for Iovianus, their fellow slave (servus), and for themselves; he lived 11 months and 22 days.
EDCS-09400190.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a slave named Iovianus at 11 months and 22 days old, and was erected by fellow slaves including Capriolus and Aphro.

EDCS-09400191.text_edition: \n Locus Luci Stati Luci liberti Nicandri et Anulliae |(mulieris) libertae Expectatae\n
EDCS-09400191.translation: A burial place for Lucius Stasius Nicander, freedman of Lucius Stasius, and for Anullia Expectata, freedwoman of Anullia.
EDCS-09400191.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot designated for Lucius Stasius Nicander and Anullia Expectata, both freedpersons.

EDCS-09400192.text_edition: \n Titiae Cai libertae Clarae Titia Enchiridium liberta fecit Numerio Abellio Numeri liberto Felici hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XII\n
EDCS-09400192.translation: Titia Enchiridium, freedwoman of Titia, made this monument for her freedman Numerius Abellius Felix, freedman of Numerius; it will not pass to an heir. [12] feet in front and [12] feet in depth.
EDCS-09400192.summary: This funerary monument was created by Titia Enchiridium for her freedman Numerius Abellius Felix, specifying plot dimensions and that the property is not inheritable.

EDCS-09400193.text_edition: \n Euelpistus Augusti nostri dispensator horologium praetorio Publiliano dono dedit\n
EDCS-09400193.translation: Euelpistus, administrator of our Augustus, dedicated the sundial in the Praetorian barracks as a gift.
EDCS-09400193.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a sundial by an imperial administrator.

EDCS-09400195.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Felicitati Caesarum nostrorum servae vernae coniugi incomparabili ius Augustorum \n
EDCS-09400195.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Felicitas, slave girl of our Caesars, her native wife, incomparable, servant of the Augusti.
EDCS-09400195.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Felicitas, a slave and wife, dedicated by her family.

EDCS-09400196.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Severiae Aphrodite coniugi incomparabili cum qua vixit nnos XXXVII Receptus Augusti libertus procurator posuit\n
EDCS-09400196.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Severia Aphrodite, incomparable wife, with whom he lived for 37 years. Receptus, freedman of Augustus and procurator, erected this.
EDCS-09400196.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Severia Aphrodite after 37 years of marriage and was erected by her husband, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-09401025.text_edition: \n Lucius Tullius Luci libertus Princeps et Quintus Eppius Quinti libertus Agapite merenti fecerunt sibi et Minuciae Cai libertae Cale uxori suae Thalasa sic ancilla et A Minuc Lucius Tullius Luci filius Crassus Gelli Primig vixit nnos IIII \n
EDCS-09401025.translation: Lucius Tullius, son of Lucius, known as Princeps, and Quintus Eppius, son of Quintus, known as Agapite, made this for the deserving one, for themselves and for Minucia, daughter of Caius, his wife, known as Thalasa, and also for their slave girl, and for Lucius Tullius, son of Lucius, son of Crassus; Gellia, daughter of Primus, lived for 4 years.
EDCS-09401025.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a family, including a child, possibly erected by two men and a slave.

EDCS-09401026.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Venusta vixit annis XLV mensibus V cara suis Carpophorus conserbai sic bene merenti\n
EDCS-09401026.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Venusta lived 45 years and 5 months; to her beloved by her family, Carpophorus, loyal servant, erected this for the well-deserving one.
EDCS-09401026.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Venusta after 45 years and 5 months, and was erected by her family member, Carpophorus.

EDCS-09401027.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Ianuariae infanti dulcissimae quae vixit annis IIII mensibus VII Ianuarius et Syntrophe parentes bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-09401027.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld, for Aurelia Ianuaria, most sweet infant, who lived 4 years and 7 months. Ianuarius and Syntrophe, parents, erected this for the well-deserving one.
EDCS-09401027.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young child, Aurelia Ianuaria, and was erected by her parents.

EDCS-09401028.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Titio Felicissimo vixit annos menses V dies X Lucius Titius filio pientissimo dulcissimo\n
EDCS-09401028.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld, for Lucius Titius Felicissimus, who lived 5 months and 10 days. Lucius Titius erected this for his most dutiful, most sweet son.
EDCS-09401028.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Titius Felicissimus, who lived only a few months, and was erected by his father.

EDCS-09401385.text_edition: \n Lucius Salvius Luci filius Caius Aemilius Luci filius Bassus IIviri iure dicundo silanos tubosque reficiendos ex decurionum sententia curaverunt idemque probaverunt\n
EDCS-09401385.translation: Lucius Salvius, son of Lucius, and Caius Aemilius Bassus, son of Lucius, as IIviri (magistrates with judicial power), had the sewers and pipes repaired according to the decree of the decurions, and they approved them.
EDCS-09401385.summary: This inscription records the repair of sewers and pipes by two magistrates (IIviri) in a Roman town, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-09401386.text_edition: \n Acciae Moderatae Obellius Firmus sorori bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09401386.translation: Obellius Firmus erected this for Accia Moderata, his sister, the well-deserving one.
EDCS-09401386.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a brother for his sister.

EDCS-09401387.text_edition: \n Quintus Alfius Quinti filius Lucullus Livianus\n
EDCS-09401387.translation: Quintus Alfius, son of Quintus, Lucullus Livianus.
EDCS-09401387.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name and filiation of an individual, Quintus Alfius Lucullus Livianus.

EDCS-09401389.text_edition: \n Marci Creperi Marci liberti Hilari CA et Marci Creperi Marci liberti Bassi et Creperiae Marci libertae Optatae Creperia Marci liberta Peristera hic sita\n
EDCS-09401389.translation: Creperia Peristera, freedwoman of Marcus, lies here, [as do] Marcus Creperius Hilarius, freedman of Marcus, and Marcus Creperius Bassus, freedman of Marcus, and Creperia Optata, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-09401389.summary: This is a funerary inscription for multiple freedpersons, likely a family group, indicated by shared patronymic and given names.

EDCS-09401390.text_edition: \n Marcus Creperius Theomnestus Creperiae Geminae libertae matri et Luciliae Aphroditi bene merentibus fecit\n
EDCS-09401390.translation: Marcus Creperius Theomnestus made this for his well-deserving freedwoman mother, Creperia Gemina, and for Lucilia Aphrodite.
EDCS-09401390.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Marcus Creperius Theomnestus for his freedwoman mother and another individual, Lucilia Aphrodite.

EDCS-09401391.text_edition: \n Firmiae Faustillae Callimachus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09401391.translation: Callimachus erected [this] for his well-deserving Firmia Faustilla.
EDCS-09401391.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Callimachus for Firmia Faustilla.

EDCS-09401392.text_edition: \n Quinto Herennio Epaphrae Magia Felicia et Caius Turranius Crescens filii bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-09401392.translation: Magia Felicia and Caius Turranius Crescens erected this for their well-deserving son, Quintus Herennius Epaphra.
EDCS-09401392.summary: This funerary epitaph was set up by parents for their son Quintus Herennius Epaphra.

EDCS-09401393.text_edition: \n Iulio Musteolo nnorum XV Iulia Hermione fratri karissimo et Iulio Hermeti et Valeriae Euthychiae sic parentibus piissimis posuit\n
EDCS-09401393.translation: Iulia Hermione erected this for her most beloved brother, Iulius Musteolus, aged fifteen, and for their most dutiful parents, Iulius Hermetus and Valeria Euthychia.
EDCS-09401393.summary: This funerary inscription honors a deceased brother and his parents, erected by his sister.

EDCS-09401394.text_edition: \n RIAENVAE Aerios servus cunarius conservae et sibi posuit\n
EDCS-09401394.translation: Aerios, the cook of RIAENVA, set this up for his fellow servant and for himself.
EDCS-09401394.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a slave named Aerios, for himself and a fellow slave.

EDCS-09401395.text_edition: \n Quinto Ovio Nothidis liberto Stolo Augustali Oviae Methe libertae Quinto Ovio Heraclito et Telesphoro servo\n
EDCS-09401395.translation: Stolo, freedman of Quintus Ovius Nothides, [set this up] for his Augustalis freedwoman Ovia Methe, for Quintus Ovius Heraclitus, and for the slave Telesphorus.
EDCS-09401395.summary: This funerary inscription records dedications to several individuals, including freedpersons and a slave, likely by Stolo, a freedman.

EDCS-09401396.text_edition: \n Numerius Papius Marci filius Horatia Lucius Papius Numeri filius Bassus in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XII\n
EDCS-09401396.translation: Numerius Papius Bassus, son of Marcus, of the Horatia tribe, [and] Lucius Papius Bassus, son of Numerius, [set this up specifying dimensions:] twelve feet in front, twelve feet in the field.
EDCS-09401396.summary: This inscription, likely related to property or a tomb, records the names of two individuals and the dimensions of a plot of land.

EDCS-09401397.text_edition: \n Caio Salvio Cai liberto Ursulo Publius Pomponius Saturninus amico merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09401397.translation: Publius Pomponius Saturninus erected this for his deserving friend, Caius Salvius Ursulus, freedman of Caius.
EDCS-09401397.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius Pomponius Saturninus for his friend Caius Salvius Ursulus.

EDCS-09401398.text_edition: \n Publio Sextilio Publi liberto Hygino Sextilius Ianuarius et Sextilia Hygia posuerunt\n
EDCS-09401398.translation: Sextilius Ianuarius and Sextilia Hygia erected [this] for their well-deserving Publius Sextilius Hyginus, freedman of Publius.
EDCS-09401398.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Sextilius Ianuarius and Sextilia Hygia for their freedman Publius Sextilius Hyginus.

EDCS-09401399.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Vettiae Onesimae vixit nnos XXXI Vettius Firmus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09401399.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Vettia Onesima lived for 31 years. Vettius Firmus placed this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-09401399.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of Vettia Onesima and its dedication by her husband.

EDCS-09401400.text_edition: \n Mario Felici patri Marius Rufinus et Savia Saturnina uxor bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-09401400.translation: Marius Rufinus and his wife Savia Saturnina placed this for their deserving father Marius Felix.
EDCS-09401400.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of Marius Felix and its dedication by his son and wife.

EDCS-09401405.text_edition: \n Phileto et Comice Caesaris servis Aelia Augusti liberta Philete optimis parentibus posuit\n
EDCS-09401405.translation: Aelia, freedwoman of Augustus, placed this for Philetus and Comicus, slaves of Caesar, her excellent parents.
EDCS-09401405.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of two former slaves of Caesar and its dedication by their freedwoman daughter.

EDCS-09700065.text_edition: \n Lucius Rusticelius Cosmus aedituus Neptuno sacrum\n
EDCS-09700065.translation: Lucius Rusticelius Cosmus, the temple attendant, dedicated this to Neptune.
EDCS-09700065.summary: A dedication to Neptune from an unknown date, made by Lucius Rusticelius Cosmus, a temple attendant.

EDCS-09700069.text_edition: \n Lucio Caesari Augusti filio auguri consuli designato principi iuventutis\n
EDCS-09700069.translation: To Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus, augur, consul-designate, prince of the youth.
EDCS-09700069.summary: An honorific inscription for Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus, likely from the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE, detailing his titles.

EDCS-09700070.translation: To Agrippa Julius Caesar, son of Augustus.
EDCS-09700070.summary: An honorific inscription for Agrippa Julius Caesar, son of Augustus, likely from the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-09700072.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Decimus Lucretius Phasis sacerdos Iovis vixit annos LXVII hic situs est bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09700072.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Decimus Lucretius Phasis, priest of Jupiter, lived for 67 years. He is buried here. He placed this for his deserving [relative].
EDCS-09700072.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of Decimus Lucretius Phasis, a priest of Jupiter, and its dedication.

EDCS-09700073.text_edition: \n Auctus Theophili servus faber vixit ann[os ------\n
EDCS-09700073.translation: Auctus, slave of Theophilus, a craftsman, lived for [---] years.
EDCS-09700073.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of Auctus, a slave and craftsman, with an incomplete lifespan.

EDCS-09700074.text_edition: \n Camulus Crispinillae servus gregarius vixit annos XXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09700074.translation: Camulus, slave of Crispinilla, a private soldier, lived for 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-09700074.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of Camulus, a slave and private soldier, and stating his burial location.

EDCS-09700075.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Castricia Tyche vixit annos XC hic sita est Barrutius Frhonto sic coniugi bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09700075.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Castricia Tyche lived for 90 years. She is buried here. Barrutius Frhonto made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-09700075.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording the death of Castricia Tyche at an advanced age and its dedication by her husband.

EDCS-09700077.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Domitius Syneros.
EDCS-09700077.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the departed by Marcus Domitius Syneros.

EDCS-09700078.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Genethlius vixit annos XXXV hic situs est Publius Laelius Zosimus patri bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09700078.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Genethlius lived 35 years and is buried here. Publius Laelius Zosimus made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-09700078.summary: This tombstone records the death of Genethlius, who lived 35 years, and was made by his son Publius Laelius Zosimus.

EDCS-09700079.text_edition: \n Lucius Genicius Valens vixit annos LXXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09700079.translation: Lucius Genicius Valens lived 85 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09700079.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lucius Genicius Valens, who lived 85 years.

EDCS-09700080.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iusta conserbo sic Pyrallo cum quo vixit annos XXI itemque vixit annos XXXVII bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09700080.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iusta for her companion Pyrallus, with whom she lived 21 years, and also he lived 37 years. Made for a deserving person.
EDCS-09700080.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Iusta to her deceased companion Pyrallus, commemorating their respective lifespans of 21 and 37 years.

EDCS-09700081.text_edition: \n Lupa vixit annos XXXX hic sita est Faustus velarius\n
EDCS-09700081.translation: Lupa lived 40 years and is buried here. Faustus velarius.
EDCS-09700081.summary: This tombstone records the death of Lupa, who lived 40 years, and was likely placed by Faustus velarius.

EDCS-09700082.text_edition: \n Norbana Cai filia Recepta vixit annos XXVIII hic sita est Caius Carrinas Cai libertus Aphrodisius vir de suo\n
EDCS-09700082.translation: Norbana, daughter of Caius, Recepta, lived 28 years and is buried here. Caius Carrinas Aphrodisius, freedman of Caius, erected this from his own means.
EDCS-09700082.summary: This tombstone records the death of Norbana Recepta, aged 28, and was erected by her husband Caius Carrinas Aphrodisius.

EDCS-09700083.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Oriens sarcinator vixit annos LXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-09700083.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Oriens the packer lived 70 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09700083.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the departed for Oriens the packer, who lived 70 years.

EDCS-09700084.text_edition: \n Quietus Crispinillae gregarius fecit bene merenti contubernali vixit annos XXXV\n
EDCS-09700084.translation: Quietus made this for his deserving comrade, lived 35 years.
EDCS-09700084.summary: This inscription records the death of a soldier named Quietus, who lived 35 years and was commemorated by a comrade.

EDCS-09700085.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus C argentari Allia coniugi bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09700085.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Argentus made this for his deserving wife Allia.
EDCS-09700085.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the spirits of the departed by Argentus for his wife Allia.

EDCS-09700086.text_edition: \n Successa Corneli Acasti serva vixit annis XL\n
EDCS-09700086.translation: Successa, slave of Cornelius Acastus, lived 40 years.
EDCS-09700086.summary: This tombstone records the death of Successa, a slave of Cornelius Acastus, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-09700087.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Ulpius Agathangelus Casiae Speratae bene merenti posuit veixit sic XLI hic sita\n
EDCS-09700087.translation: To the Divine Manes. Marcus Ulpius Agathangelus erected this for his well-deserving Casia Sperata, who lived 41 years. Placed here.
EDCS-09700087.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus Ulpius Agathangelus to his deceased wife Casia Sperata, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700088.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ursilla serva Caesaris vixit annis XXXX fecit Ulpius Fortunatus matri bene merenti hic sita est\n
EDCS-09700088.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ursilla, servant of Caesar, lived 40 years. Ulpius Fortunatus made this for his well-deserving mother, placed here.
EDCS-09700088.summary: A funerary dedication by Ulpius Fortunatus to his mother Ursilla, a servant of Caesar, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700091.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Allia |(mulieris) liberta Hygia sibi et Fortunatae filiae et Hygino filio et Aulo Marcio Nymphodoto hoc monumentum sepulcrumque v\n
EDCS-09700091.translation: To the Divine Manes. Allia Hygia, freedwoman of a woman, [erected] this monument and sepulcher for herself, her daughter Fortunata, her son Hyginus, and Aulus Marcius Nymphodotus.
EDCS-09700091.summary: A funerary dedication for a family including the dedicator Allia Hygia, her children, and Aulus Marcius Nymphodotus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700092.text_edition: \n Lucius Artorius Luci libertus Chrysanthus Liviae |(mulieris) libertae Philaenini sic\n
EDCS-09700092.translation: Lucius Artorius Chrysanthus, freedman of Lucius, [erected this for] Philaenini, freedwoman of Livia. [Thus].
EDCS-09700092.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Artorius Chrysanthus, freedman of Lucius, for Philaenini, freedwoman of Livia, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700093.text_edition: \n Atania Luci liberta Anatole sibi et Quinto Raio Quinti liberto Cimbro viro suo\n
EDCS-09700093.translation: To Atania Anatole, freedwoman of Lucius, for herself and for her husband Quintus Raio Cimbros, freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-09700093.summary: A funerary dedication by Atania Anatole for herself and her husband Quintus Raio Cimbros, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700094.text_edition: \n Baloniae Heliadi Balonius Priscus frater bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09700094.translation: Balonius Priscus erected this for his well-deserving sister Balonia Heliadis.
EDCS-09700094.summary: A funerary dedication by Balonius Priscus to his sister Balonia Heliadis, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700095.text_edition: \n AIIASMI et Lucio Dasimio Daphno liberto suo\n
EDCS-09700095.translation: To Aiiasmi and to their freedman Lucius Dasimius Daphnus.
EDCS-09700095.summary: A funerary dedication to Aiiasmi and their freedman Lucius Dasimius Daphnus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700096.text_edition: \n s Publi libertus Isargyrus Servilia |(mulieris) liberta Chrysantis sic\n
EDCS-09700096.translation: [Erected by] s, freedman of Publius, [and] Servilia Chrysantis, freedwoman of a woman. [Thus].
EDCS-09700096.summary: A funerary dedication by two individuals, one a freedman of Publius and the other a freedwoman of a woman named Servilia Chrysantis, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700097.text_edition: \n Priscus pellio Prisco filio et vixit FIFSE\n
EDCS-09700097.translation: Priscus the skin-dresser [erected this] for Priscus his son, who lived [life]...
EDCS-09700097.summary: A funerary dedication by Priscus the skin-dresser for his son Priscus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700099.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Saturnino filio qui vixit annis II diebus XXII Saturninus et Silvana parentes bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-09700099.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Saturninus, a son who lived 2 years and 22 days. Saturninus and Silvana, [his] parents, made this for their well-deserving [son].
EDCS-09700099.summary: A funerary dedication by parents Saturninus and Silvana to their young son Saturninus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09700100.text_edition: \n Tampiae Decimi libertae Fa et Sexto Aburio Sexti filio filio suo et Tampiae |(mulieris) libertae \n
EDCS-09700100.translation: To Tampia, freedwoman of Decimus, and to her son Sextus Aburius Sextus, and to Tampia, freedwoman.
EDCS-09700100.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely commemorating a woman named Tampia, her son, and another freedwoman also named Tampia.

EDCS-09700101.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Bitalini sic sorori dulcissimae Agatho bene merenti fecit quae vixit annos XIIII menses X\n
EDCS-09700101.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Agatho made this for Bitalini, his most beloved sister, deserving of good things, who lived 14 years and 10 months.
EDCS-09700101.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Bitalini, set up by her brother Agatho.

EDCS-09700102.summary: A very brief inscription, likely a tombstone naming Tutoria Clara.

EDCS-09700103.text_edition: \n Quintus Baebius |(mulieris) libertus Eros sibi et suis fecit\n
EDCS-09700103.translation: The freedman Eros of Quintus Baebius made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-09700103.summary: A funerary inscription set up by a freedman named Eros for his household.

EDCS-09700105.text_edition: \n Have bona anima et tu Raia Felix Hermes Laetori Rufi libertus et Raia Paetina alumnae et libertae optimae et Laetorius Trophimus coniugi carissimae fecerunt et sibi et sueis sic\n
EDCS-09700105.translation: Greetings, good soul. And you, Raia Felix Hermes, and Raia Paetina, his adopted daughter and most excellent freedwoman, and Laetorius Trophimus made this for their dearest wife, and for themselves and their families.
EDCS-09700105.summary: A funerary inscription likely set up by Hermes and Raia Paetina for their wife, Raia Felix.
