EDCS-09700106.text_edition: \n Caius Avius Rufus IIIIvir quinquennalis vixit nnos XXX Titus Flavius Phraates fecit filio pientissimo et Avia Apate\n
EDCS-09700106.translation: Caius Avius Rufus, quinquennial magistrate, lived 30 years. Titus Flavius Phraates made this for his most dutiful son and for Avia Apate.
EDCS-09700106.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Avius Rufus, a local magistrate, set up by Titus Flavius Phraates for his son and Avia Apate.

EDCS-09700108.text_edition: \n Marcus Marius Marci filius Cornelia Bucco situs e(st)\n
EDCS-09700108.translation: Marcus Marius, son of Marcus, Cornelia Bucco rests here.
EDCS-09700108.summary: A funerary inscription indicating the burial place of Marcus Marius and Cornelia Bucco.

EDCS-09700110.text_edition: \n proconsul et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix nnicus tribunicia potestate XIII consul III pater patriae proconsul labentem vi[am ------\n
EDCS-09700110.translation: The proconsul and Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, by the tribunician power for the 13th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul, [repaired/built] the slipping road...
EDCS-09700110.summary: This inscription records the road repair or construction initiated by Emperor Marcus Aurelius during his reign, likely in the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-09700111.text_edition: \n elio Chrysantho Augustali viro pientissimo Claudia Fortunata uxor sibi posterisque suis fecit in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-09700111.translation: Claudia Fortunata made this for her most dutiful husband, Helion Chrysanthus, an Augustalis, and for herself and her descendants, 20 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-09700111.summary: A funerary monument established by Claudia Fortunata for her husband, Helion Chrysanthus, an Augustalis, specifying land dimensions.

EDCS-09700112.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Turraniae Quinti filiae Marcellae vixit annos XXV menses II Splatia Nice mater infelicissima et sibi et Turranio Exorato coniugi suo in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XV\n
EDCS-09700112.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Turrania Marcella, daughter of Quintus, who lived 25 years and 2 months. Her most unhappy mother Splatia Nice, and for herself and for her husband Turranius Exoratus. 15 feet in front, 15 feet in the field.
EDCS-09700112.summary: A funerary epitaph for Turrania Marcella, erected by her mother Splatia Nice and her husband Turranius Exoratus, detailing plot dimensions.

EDCS-09700113.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Modestus et Marcella sibi et Blando filio ministro Caesaris vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-09700113.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Modestus and Marcella made this for themselves and for Blandus, son of Caesar, their living attendant.
EDCS-09700113.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument set up by Modestus and Marcella for themselves and their son Blandus.

EDCS-09700502.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription recording the age at death of a person named Acume.

EDCS-09700895.text_edition: \n Olympas Tiberi Coeli Pii alumnus a Ponto nnum XIV\n
EDCS-09700895.translation: Olympas, alumnus of Tiberius Claudius Caesar, lived 14 years.
EDCS-09700895.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and age at death of Olympas, a freedman from Pontus.

EDCS-09700900.text_edition: \n Martei sic sacr<um=A> Publius Curtius Publi filius Salassus Publius Titius Luci filius IIIIviri de munere gladiatorio ex senatus consulto \n
EDCS-09700900.translation: Dedicated to Mars. Publius Curtius, son of Publius, of the Salassii tribe, and Publius Titius, son of Lucius, the four men in charge, provided gladiatorial games from the treasury, as decreed by the Senate.
EDCS-09700900.summary: This inscription records the provision of gladiatorial games by two magistrates, possibly in fulfillment of a senatorial decree.

EDCS-09700902.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Clufennio Titi filio Claudia Ingenuo Viruno evocato Augustorum Petronius Vettianus collega Iulius Pulcher veteranus Augusti heredes bene merenti\n
EDCS-09700902.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Petronius Vettianus, colleague and heir of Titus Clufennius, son of Titus, a Virunian evocatus of the Augusti, and Julius Pulcher, veteran of Augustus, [set this up for] a deserving recipient.
EDCS-09700902.summary: This funerary inscription is set up by colleagues and heirs for Titus Clufennius, a military officer, and for Julius Pulcher, a veteran.

EDCS-09700906.text_edition: \n Sextus Romanus Titi filius Pollia Iovi votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09700906.translation: Sextus Romanus, son of Titus, of the Pollia tribe, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-09700906.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Sextus Romanus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-09700910.translation: To Aulus Antonius Faustus, the Augustalis.
EDCS-09700910.summary: This is a dedication to Aulus Antonius Faustus, who held the title of Augustalis.

EDCS-09700911.text_edition: \n Quinto Baebio Festo Augustali Oviae Certae uxori in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XII\n
EDCS-09700911.translation: For Quintus Baebius Festus, the Augustalis, and Ovia Certa, his wife. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the rear.
EDCS-09700911.summary: This inscription records the property dimensions associated with Quintus Baebius Festus, an Augustalis, and his wife.

EDCS-09700917.text_edition: \n Lucio Neratio Luci filio Voltinia Prisco tribuno militum legionis XXII Primigeniae tribuno plebis VIIviro epulonum legato \n
EDCS-09700917.translation: To Lucius Neratius Priscus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, tribune of the soldiers of the 22nd Legion Primigenia, tribune of the plebs, member of the board of seven epulones, and legate.
EDCS-09700917.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription honoring Lucius Neratius Priscus for his various military and political offices.

EDCS-09700921.text_edition: \n Villia Prima sibi et Marco Attio Marci filio Claudia Marcello aedili\n
EDCS-09700921.translation: Villia Prima for herself and for Marcus Attius Marcellus, son of Marcus, the aedile.
EDCS-09700921.summary: This funerary inscription records Villia Prima setting up a monument for herself and Marcus Attius Marcellus, an aedile.

EDCS-09700922.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Cupito centurioni legionis VI victricis Aurelia Ursula coniux cum quo vixit bene merenti fecit si quis eum titulum emoverit davit sic fisco sestertia L milia nummum bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09700922.translation: To the divine shades. Aurelia Ursula, his wife, made this for her deserving husband, Aurelius Cupitus, centurion of the VI Victrix legion. If anyone removes this monument, he will forfeit 50,000 sesterces to the treasury. She made this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-09700922.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Cupitus, a centurion of the VI Victrix legion, erected by his wife Aurelia Ursula, with a penalty for its removal, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700923.text_edition: \n Quintus Curius Luci filius legionis I Lucius Curius pater Lucius Curius Luci filius legionis I\n
EDCS-09700923.translation: Quintus Curius, son of Lucius, of the I legion. Lucius Curius, his father. Lucius Curius, son of Lucius, of the I legion.
EDCS-09700923.summary: A tombstone fragment or inscription listing three individuals associated with the I legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700926.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavius Ianuarius exs sic numero Cimbrorum qui vixit annos LX Calubio filius patri dulcissimo fecit\n
EDCS-09700926.translation: To the divine shades. Calubius, son of Flavius Ianuarius, formerly of the Cimbri, who lived 60 years, made this for his most beloved father.
EDCS-09700926.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavius Ianuarius, a man of Cimbrian origin, erected by his son Calubius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700927.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Flavio Fortunato aedili Aemilia Sophe coniugi bene merenti fecit Dis Manibus Cnaeo Rustio Moderato Marcus Ulpius Moderatus patri bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09700927.translation: To the divine shades. Aemilia Sophe made this for her deserving wife, Titus Flavius Fortunatus, aedile. To the divine shades. Marcus Ulpius Moderatus made this for his deserving father, Cnaeus Rustius Moderatus.
EDCS-09700927.summary: A funerary epitaph recording two separate dedications: one for Titus Flavius Fortunatus, an aedile, by his wife Aemilia Sophe, and another for Cnaeus Rustius Moderatus by his son Marcus Ulpius Moderatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700929.text_edition: \n Cai Iuni Cai filii Attici IIIvri sic quinquennalis Valeria Rhodope coniugi incomparabili\n
EDCS-09700929.translation: To Caius Iunius Atticus, son of Caius, a quinquennial magistrate. Valeria Rhodope made this for her incomparable husband.
EDCS-09700929.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Iunius Atticus, a quinquennial magistrate, erected by his wife Valeria Rhodope, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700930.text_edition: \n Lucius Luccius Amethystus Augustalis sibi et Treboniae Cai libertae Primillae et Olynpo sic et Oeconomo libertis in fronte pedes XVI in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-09700930.translation: Lucius Luccius Amethystus, Augustalis, for himself and for Trebonia Primilla, freedwoman of Caius, and for Olynpus and Oeconomus, freedmen, 16 feet in front and 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-09700930.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Luccius Amethystus and his freedmen, specifying land dimensions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700931.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Terentio Macro veterano legionis II vixit annos LXXX Terentius Gallus et Terentius Macer patri bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-09700931.translation: To the divine shades. Terentius Gallus and Terentius Macer made this for their deserving father, Gaius Terentius Macerus, a veteran of the II legion, who lived 80 years.
EDCS-09700931.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Terentius Macerus, a veteran of the II legion, erected by his sons Terentius Gallus and Terentius Macer, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700932.text_edition: \n Aulus Allienus Auli filius Galeria Laetus praefectus fabrum Aulus Allienus Primus Augustalis iterum quinquennalis podium sua pecunia faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09700932.translation: Aulus Allienus Laetus, son of Aulus, prefect of engineers, and Aulus Allienus Primus, Augustalis and quinquennial magistrate, had the podium built at their own expense.
EDCS-09700932.summary: An inscription recording the construction of a podium by Aulus Allienus Laetus, prefect of engineers, and Aulus Allienus Primus, a quinquennial magistrate, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09700934.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X consuli III patri patriae colonia Iulia Concordia Beneventum optimo et liberalissimo principi\n
EDCS-09700934.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 10th time, consul for the 3rd time, Father of the Fatherland. The colony of Beneventum, Julia Concordia, [dedicates this to] the best and most generous princeps.
EDCS-09700934.summary: A dedication by the colony of Beneventum to Emperor Hadrian, dating to his 10th year of tribunician power and 3rd consulship, likely around 128-129 AD.

EDCS-09700935.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Antonino Caesari Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti filio imperatori destinato colonia Iulia Concordia Augusta Felix Beneventum\n
EDCS-09700935.translation: The colony of Beneventum, Julia Concordia Augusta Felix, [dedicates this to] Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Caesar, destined to be emperor, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus.
EDCS-09700935.summary: A dedication by the colony of Beneventum to Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (later Caracalla), the designated heir and son of Septimius Severus, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09700937.text_edition: \n hominis benignissimi et medici peritissimi qui vixit cum summa laude annos LXIII Marcus Cosinius Eutychianus et Soteris patri bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-09700937.translation: To the most kind man and most skilled doctor, who lived with the highest praise for 63 years. Marcus Cosinius Eutychianus and Soteris made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-09700937.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to a doctor named Eutychianus, with his age and his sons as dedicants.

EDCS-09701406.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano Augusto Germanico Dacico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VII imperatori IIII consuli V patri patriae Lucius Licinius Telesinus\n
EDCS-09701406.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 7th time, acclaimed imperator for the 4th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the fatherland. Lucius Licinius Telesinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-09701406.summary: A dedication to Emperor Trajan, listing his titles and honors, possibly part of an official inscription or a private dedication by Lucius Licinius Telesinus.

EDCS-09701407.text_edition: \n Dominis nostris Diocletiano et Maximiano Invictis Augustis et Constantio et Maximiano nobilissimis Caesaribus\n
EDCS-09701407.translation: To our lords Diocletian and Maximian, the unconquered Augusti, and to Constantius and Maximian, the most noble Caesars.
EDCS-09701407.summary: An inscription honoring the emperors Diocletian and Maximian, and the Caesars Constantius and Maximian, likely from the period of the Tetrarchy.

EDCS-09701408.text_edition: \n Gaiae Nummiae Ceioniae Umbriae Rufiae Albinae clarissimae puellae sacerdoti publicae\n
EDCS-09701408.translation: To Gaia Nummius Ceionia Umbria Rufia Albina, most distinguished maiden, public priestess.
EDCS-09701408.summary: An inscription honoring Gaia Nummius Ceionia Umbria Rufia Albina, a distinguished young woman and public priestess.

EDCS-09701411.text_edition: \n Quinto Pulfetio decurioni Beneventano Clodiae Tertiae Pulfetiae Anton[ius ------\n
EDCS-09701411.translation: To Quintus Pulfetius, decurion of Beneventum, for Clodia Tertia Pulfetia. Anton[ius ---]
EDCS-09701411.summary: Part of a funerary epitaph mentioning Quintus Pulfetius, a decurion, and Clodia Tertia, with an incomplete dedication.

EDCS-09701412.text_edition: \n Statio Cai filio Galeria Prisco aedili IIviro quinquennali Calvidia Loge uxsor sic\n
EDCS-09701412.translation: To Statio Priscus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, quinquennial duovir. His wife Calvidia Loge made this.
EDCS-09701412.summary: A funerary epitaph for Statio Priscus, an aedile and quinquennial duovir, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-09701413.text_edition: \n Caius Numisius Cai libertus Amphio Augustalis sibi et Caio Numisio Quinti filio Stellatina legionis VI patrono Numisiae Cai libertae Secundae concubinae Caio Numisio Cai liberto Primo Caio Numisio Cai liberto Aucto Numisiae Cai libertae Peregrinae\n
EDCS-09701413.translation: To Gaius Numisius Amphio, freedman of Gaius, Augustalis, for himself and for Gaius Numisius Stellatina, son of Quintus, patron of the VI Legion; and for Numisia Secunda, freedwoman of Gaius, concubine; and for Gaius Numisius Primus, freedman of Gaius; and for Gaius Numisius Auctus, freedman of Gaius; and for Numisia Peregrina, freedwoman of Gaius.
EDCS-09701413.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Numisius Amphio, an Augustalis, and his family, including freedmen and a concubine, indicating his status and relationships.

EDCS-09701414.text_edition: \n ------u]fi SAC veterano Statili Maximi legionis II Augustae EV\n
EDCS-09701414.translation: [...]fi, veteran of the II Augusta Legion, of Statilius Maximus EV
EDCS-09701414.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning a veteran of the II Augusta Legion, associated with Statilius Maximus.

EDCS-09701416.text_edition: \n Afiniae Carae vixit nnos XXIX menses V dies II Lucius Didius Karus et Cornelia Ianuaria parentes miserrimi sibi et suis fecerunt\n
EDCS-09701416.translation: Afinia Cara lived 29 years, 5 months, 2 days. Lucius Didius Karus and Cornelia Ianuaria, her most miserable parents, made this for themselves and their own.
EDCS-09701416.summary: A funerary epitaph for Afinia Cara, detailing her lifespan and dedicated by her grieving parents.

EDCS-09701417.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Baebiae Proculae Quintus Albius Rutilius Felicio matri pientissimae fecit\n
EDCS-09701417.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Quintus Albius Rutilius Felicio made this for his most dutiful mother, Baebia Procula.
EDCS-09701417.summary: A funerary dedication to Baebia Procula, described as a most dutiful mother, by her son.

EDCS-09701418.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Concordia Rufina quae vixit plus minus annis XLV Concordius Vitalis Concordiae\n
EDCS-09701418.translation: To the departed spirits. For Concordia Rufina, who lived about 45 years. Concordius Vitalis [made this] for Concordia.
EDCS-09701418.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Concordia Rufina, erected by Concordius Vitalis, likely her husband or relative.

EDCS-09701419.translation: For Lucius, the freedman Hilarus. [Erected by] Dardanus.
EDCS-09701419.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Lucius Hilarus, a freedman, by Dardanus.

EDCS-09701420.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Iuli Gemelli Caius Gemellus et Concordia Chrysogone parentes et sibi suisque vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-09701420.translation: To the departed spirits. For Caius Iulius Gemellus. Caius Gemellus and Concordia Chrysogone, parents, and for themselves and their family, made [this] while alive.
EDCS-09701420.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by parents, Caius Gemellus and Concordia Chrysogone, for their son Caius Iulius Gemellus and their family.

EDCS-09701421.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Mettiae Ledae Lucius Manilius Asiaticus coniugi inconparabili cum qua vixit annis XXXII mensibus VII sine ulla quaerella sic bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09701421.translation: To the departed spirits. For Mettia Leda. Lucius Manilius Asiaticus made [this] for his incomparable wife, with whom he lived 32 years and 7 months, without any complaint, for her well-deserving memory.
EDCS-09701421.summary: This tombstone was dedicated by Lucius Manilius Asiaticus to his wife Mettia Leda, honoring their long and happy marriage.

EDCS-09701422.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Octavius Liberatus et Octavia felicissima patri pecunia propria fecerunt\n
EDCS-09701422.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintus Octavius Liberatus and Octavia Felicissima made [this] for their father with their own money.
EDCS-09701422.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Octavius Liberatus and Octavia Felicissima dedicating a monument to their father.

EDCS-09701423.text_edition: \n Paccia |(mulieris) liberta Diodora sibei et Aulo Marcio Auli liberto Diphilo\n
EDCS-09701423.translation: Paccia Diodora, freedwoman of [a woman], for herself and for Aulus Marcius Diphilus, freedman of Aulus.
EDCS-09701423.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Paccia Diodora, a freedwoman, for herself and Aulus Marcius Diphilus.

EDCS-09701424.text_edition: \n Caius Rustius |(mulieris) libertus Iaso Rustia Cai liberta Nardis in fronte pedes XII \n
EDCS-09701424.translation: Caius Rustius Iaso, freedman of [a woman], Rustia Nardis, freedwoman of Caius, [boundary marker] in front 12 feet.
EDCS-09701424.summary: This inscription likely marks a property boundary belonging to Caius Rustius Iaso and Rustia Nardis, who were freedpersons.

EDCS-09701425.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Saeiae sic Sabinae quae vixit annos XXVII menses VI dies XXV Seius Crescens frater Caius Vegedius sic Ianuarius coniugi incomparabili cum qua vixit annos XVII menses VI sine ulla quaerella sic bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-09701425.translation: To the departed spirits. For Saeia Sabina, who lived 27 years, 6 months, 25 days. Seius Crescens, brother, and Caius Vegedius Ianuarius, [made this] for their incomparable wife, with whom they lived 17 years, 6 months, without any complaint, for her well-deserving memory.
EDCS-09701425.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Saeia Sabina by her brother Seius Crescens and her husband Caius Vegedius Ianuarius, commemorating their happy marriage.

EDCS-09701426.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Secundino filio dulcissimo qui vixit annos VII menses VII dies XXVII Secundinus pater fecit carissimo\n
EDCS-09701426.translation: To the departed spirits. For our sweetest son Secundinus, who lived 7 years, 7 months, 27 days. Secundinus, the father, made [this] for his dearest.
EDCS-09701426.summary: This funerary inscription honors Secundinus, a young son, erected by his father, Secundinus.

EDCS-09701427.translation: Lucius Teiburtius Lysimachus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-09701427.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucius Teiburtius Lysimachus, a freedman.

EDCS-09701428.text_edition: \n Heic est illa sita felix Pomponia Eleusis quae eximia virtute animi peperit sibei laudem et sibi praemeisit simili virtute et amore qum sic <g=O>emitu Rufi Rufi nomine ut est Helenus nei mirere hospes quis eam seic laudibus laudet ut meruit parque est patronus qui manumeisit \n
EDCS-09701428.translation: Here lies the fortunate Pomponia Eleusis, who by the exceptional virtue of her spirit earned praise for herself and was preceded by her own similar virtue and love, so that she greatly resembled Rufus, as Helenus did, lest you wonder, stranger, that anyone should praise her so highly, for she deserved it, and he is a worthy patron who freed her.
EDCS-09701428.summary: This epitaph praises the deceased Pomponia Eleusis for her exceptional virtue and mentions her patron, Rufus, and possibly a relative, Helenus.

EDCS-09800064.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto consuli III Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti filio publice decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09800064.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, consul for the third time, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09800064.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, likely commemorating a public building or honor, decreed by local officials.

EDCS-09800065.text_edition: \n Lucio Arrenio Luci filio Papiria Menandro aedili IIIIviro II quinquennali munerario civitatis Herdoniae omnibus honoribus et oneribus rei publicae functo patrono collegium fabrum tignariorum ob praecipuam adfectionem eius statuam ponendam merenti decrevit locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09800065.translation: To Lucius Arrenius Menander, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, former aedile, former duovir, former quinquennial official, festival organizer of the city of Herdonia, who fulfilled all the duties and burdens of public office, a patron; the guild of carpenters and timber workers, recognizing his outstanding consideration, decreed that a statue be erected to him, deserving as he is; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09800065.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue to Lucius Arrenius Menander, a prominent local magistrate and patron, by the guild of carpenters in Herdonia.

EDCS-09800066.text_edition: \n Arreniae Felicissimae sacerdoti [] [] Augustae Luci Arreni Menandri filiae collegium iuvenum ob merita eius locus datus decreto decurionum Dedicata Kalendis Iuniis\n
EDCS-09800066.translation: To Arrenia Felicissima, priestess of Augusta, daughter of Lucius Arrenius Menander; the guild of youths, because of her merits, granted the site by decree of the decurions. Dedicated on the Kalends of June.
EDCS-09800066.summary: This is a dedication of a site by the guild of youths to Arrenia Felicissima, priestess of Augusta and daughter of Lucius Arrenius Menander, recognizing her merits.

EDCS-09800067.text_edition: \n Bruttiae Luci filiae Nereidi Luci Arreni Menandri decreto et munif(icentia) collegii cannophorum ob merita eius locus datus decreto decurionum Dedicata Kalendis Martiis\n
EDCS-09800067.translation: To Nereis, daughter of Lucius, wife of Lucius Arrenius Menander, by the decree and generosity of the guild of reed-bearers, because of her merits, the site was granted by decree of the decurions. Dedicated on the Kalends of March.
EDCS-09800067.summary: This inscription records the granting of a site by the guild of reed-bearers to Nereis, wife of Lucius Arrenius Menander, in recognition of her merits.

EDCS-09800068.text_edition: \n Decimus Funius Decimi filius Gallus Caius Rubrius Cai filius Tintirius IIIIviri quinquennales balneum ab solo faciundum coiravere ex decreto decurionum eidem probavere\n
EDCS-09800068.translation: Decimus Funius Gallus, son of Decimus, and Caius Rubrius Tintirius, son of Caius, quinquennial duovirs, undertook the construction of a bathhouse from the ground up, according to the decree of the decurions, and approved it themselves.
EDCS-09800068.summary: This inscription states that the quinquennial duovirs, Decimus Funius Gallus and Caius Rubrius Tintirius, oversaw the construction of a new bathhouse in their city.

EDCS-09800069.text_edition: \n Marco Minatio Marci filio Galeria Successo quod annonam civibus commodioribus pretiis ultro obtulerit ob amorem eius statuam ponendam populus postulaverit sic consensu decurionum locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-09800069.translation: To Marcus Minatius Successus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, because he voluntarily offered the grain supply to the citizens at more favorable prices, because of his goodwill, the people requested that a statue be erected to him; thus, by the consent of the decurions, the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-09800069.summary: This inscription records the decree by the people and decurions to erect a statue to Marcus Minatius Successus for providing the grain supply to citizens at favorable prices.

EDCS-09800071.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Axsius sic Cnaei libertus Pylades Bassa Auli liberta Plaetura et Celso liberto\n
EDCS-09800071.translation: To Gnaeus Axsius Pylades Bassa, freedman of Gnaeus, and to Plaetura, freedwoman of Aulus, and to Celso, a freedman.
EDCS-09800071.summary: This is a dedication or record of property ownership involving three freed individuals: Gnaeus Axsius Pylades Bassa, Plaetura, and Celso.

EDCS-09800072.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucilio Euhodo Lucilia Primitiva coniuci sic fecit bene merenti cum quo vixit annis XXX\n
EDCS-09800072.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucilia Primitiva made this for her husband Lucilius Euhodus, who deserved well, with whom she lived for 30 years.
EDCS-09800072.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucilia Primitiva for her deserving husband, Lucilius Euhodus, commemorating their 30 years of marriage.

EDCS-09800073.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Metili Cai filii Fructi vixit annos XII menses VI dies XIIX filio rarissimo parentes infelicissimi Caius Metilius Fructus et Pomponia Ursula\n
EDCS-09800073.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Fructus, son of Caius Metilius, who lived 12 years, 6 months, and 18 days, a most unfortunate son, [from] most unfortunate parents, Caius Metilius Fructus and Pomponia Ursula.
EDCS-09800073.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Fructus, who died at the age of 12, set up by his grieving parents.

EDCS-09800075.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Anaxibi libertus Eros hic situs\n
EDCS-09800075.translation: Caius Julius Eros, freedman of Anaxibius, is buried here.
EDCS-09800075.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Eros, a freedman, identifying his former master.

EDCS-09800076.text_edition: \n Vetia Thallusa vixit annos XXV hic sita\n
EDCS-09800076.translation: Vetia Thallusa lived 25 years and is buried here.
EDCS-09800076.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vetia Thallusa, stating her age at death and burial.

EDCS-10701362.text_edition: \n Lucio Blassidio Populari et Clodiae Valentinae Blassidia Valentina filia fecit suis\n
EDCS-10701362.translation: Blassidia Valentina made this for her Lucius Blassidius Popularis and Clodia Valentina.
EDCS-10701362.summary: This is a funerary epitaph likely for parents, made by their daughter Blassidia Valentina.

EDCS-10701365.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus illa vixit annos I Sextus Dasumius Capriolus et Dasumia Nais ancillae bene merenti\n
EDCS-10701365.translation: To the departed spirits: [Illa] lived 1 year. Sextus Dasumius Capriolus and Dasumia Nais [set this up] for the well-deserving slave-girl.
EDCS-10701365.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to a slave-girl named Illa by her owners, Sextus Dasumius Capriolus and Dasumia Nais, and records her short life.

EDCS-10701366.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Luxiliae Doridi Dasumia Nais filia matri pientissimae fecit vixit nnos LII\n
EDCS-10701366.translation: To the departed spirits: Dasumia Nais made this for her most devoted mother, Luxilia Doridis, who lived 52 years.
EDCS-10701366.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by a daughter, Dasumia Nais, to her devoted mother, Luxilia Doridis, stating her age at death.

EDCS-10701679.text_edition: \n Caio Vibio Cai filio Postumo consuli VIIviro epulonum colonia Romulensis\n
EDCS-10701679.translation: To Gaius Vibius Postumus, son of Gaius, consul, member of the college of pontiffs, of the colonia Romulensis.
EDCS-10701679.summary: This is an inscription honoring Gaius Vibius Postumus, detailing his political and religious offices and his connection to the colonia Romulensis.

EDCS-10701680.text_edition: \n Caio Gabbio Messallae liberto Aequali adlecto in ordinem decurionum Larinatium biselliario Augustali bis Zosimus libertus patrono optime de se merito et sibi vivos posuit\n
EDCS-10701680.translation: Zosimus, freedman, erected this for Gaius Gabbius Messalla, his freedman, admitted to the order of decurions in Larinum, twice Augustalis of the bisellium, and for himself, for his patron who deserved exceedingly well of him.
EDCS-10701680.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by a freedman, Zosimus, for his patron, Gaius Gabbius Messalla, detailing the patron's public career and Zosimus's gratitude.

EDCS-10701681.text_edition: \n io Quinti filio oculo aedili moedia Myrtale mater infelix filio vixit annis XXXV\n
EDCS-10701681.translation: [---] to [---] Quinti, son of Quintus, aedile. Myrtale, an unhappy mother, [set this up] for her son who lived 35 years.
EDCS-10701681.summary: This funerary epitaph is from a grieving mother, Myrtale, for her son, who held the office of aedile and died at the age of 35.

EDCS-10701682.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antoni Marci liberti Ianuaria posuit\n
EDCS-10701682.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ianuaria [set this up].
EDCS-10701682.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the departed spirits, set up by Ianuaria for an unnamed person.

EDCS-10701683.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Didiae Publi filiae Maximae Publius Didius Philotimus et Pacidia Methe filiae pientissimae et sibi posuerunt vixit annos XIIX\n
EDCS-10701683.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Didius Philotimus and Pacidia Methe set this up for their most devoted daughter, Didia Maxima, daughter of Publius, and for themselves; she lived 18 years.
EDCS-10701683.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by parents, Publius Didius Philotimus and Pacidia Methe, to their devoted daughter Didia Maxima, stating her age at death.

EDCS-10701684.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Heraclae Gaveni servo Eucharisto filio Ingenuae filiae\n
EDCS-10701684.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Heracla, daughter of Gavenius, and for Eucharistos, son of Ingenua.
EDCS-10701684.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Heracla and Eucharistos, likely a freedman and his son.

EDCS-10701685.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lappiae filiae Pothusae Lappia Lupilia ma[ter ------\n
EDCS-10701685.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Pothusa, daughter of Lappia, and Lappia Lupilia, her mother [---].
EDCS-10701685.summary: A funerary dedication to Pothusa and her mother Lappia Lupilia, with the rest of the inscription missing.

EDCS-10701686.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Siddini et Peregrino dulcissimis filis Siddin et Marcia parentes merentibus fecerunt qui decesserunt die XIII Kalendas Augustas et die XVIII Kalendas Septembres anno uno\n
EDCS-10701686.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Siddin and Marcia, parents, made this for their most beloved sons Siddinus and Peregrinus, who died on the 13th day before the Kalends of August and the 18th day before the Kalends of September in the same year.
EDCS-10701686.summary: A funerary dedication by parents to their two sons who died in close succession, likely in the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-10701688.text_edition: \n ------ Aba]scanti C S Secundi tiae Sp\n
EDCS-10701688.translation: [---] Aba]scanti, freedman of C. S. Secundus, and his wife, Sp[---].
EDCS-10701688.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning a freedman and his wife.

EDCS-10701689.text_edition: \n Decimo Iulio Decimi liberto Diochari Augustali anni primi Iulia Decimi filia Tertulla filia Caio Luccio Luci filio Equiti nnorum V\n
EDCS-10701689.translation: For Decimus Julius Diocharus, freedman of Decimus, Augustalis of the first year. Iulia Tertulla, daughter of Decimus, and her daughter, for Gaius Lucius, son of Lucius, a knight, aged 5 years.
EDCS-10701689.summary: A funerary dedication for a 5-year-old boy, naming his father and the dedicator's family members.

EDCS-10701691.text_edition: \n Lucio Audio Luci filio Palatina Aufidiano patri optimo \n
EDCS-10701691.translation: For Lucius Audius, son of Lucius, of the Palatine tribe, to his most excellent father Aufidianus.
EDCS-10701691.summary: A funerary dedication from a son to his father, Lucius Audius Aufidianus.

EDCS-10701692.text_edition: \n Pacilia Rufionis liberta Fausta et Lucius Pacilius Rufionis libertus Epaphra filius vixit nnos XIX menses VIII hic siti\n
EDCS-10701692.translation: Fausta, freedwoman of Pacilia Rufionis, and Lucius Pacilius Epaphra, freedman of Pacilius Rufionis, lived 19 years and 8 months; they are buried here.
EDCS-10701692.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of a freedwoman and a freedman, likely a couple.

EDCS-10701693.text_edition: \n Cottidiani Cai Appulei Flacci nnorum XII hic situs est\n
EDCS-10701693.translation: Cottidianus, [son] of Caius Appuleius Flaccus, is buried here, aged 12 years.
EDCS-10701693.summary: A funerary inscription for a 12-year-old boy named Cottidianus.

EDCS-10701694.text_edition: \n Draco mulio Bassi Lupiensis hic situs est\n
EDCS-10701694.translation: Draco, a mule driver of Bassus Lupus, is buried here.
EDCS-10701694.summary: A funerary inscription for Draco, identified by his occupation as a mule driver for Bassus Lupus.

EDCS-10701696.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Bales sic vixit annos VIIII menses V et Iulia Zosima bixit sic annos II posuit Iulia Trepte sic filis merentibus Lucius Iulius Bales sic vixit annos LXV et coniugi merenti\n
EDCS-10701696.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Iulius Bales lived 7 years and 5 months, and Iulia Zosima lived 2 years. Iulia Trepte made this for her deserving sons, Lucius Iulius Bales, who lived 65 years, and for her deserving husband.
EDCS-10701696.summary: A funerary monument erected by Iulia Trepte for her two sons, one of whom also served as her husband, and for her deceased husband.

EDCS-10701697.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a centurion named Marcinus and possibly a place or person named Triptolemus.

EDCS-10701698.text_edition: \n Lucius Negilius Luci libertus Niger Lucius Negilius Luci libertus Mant Lucius Negilius Luci libertus Zethus\n
EDCS-10701698.translation: Lucius Negilius Niger, freedman of Lucius; Lucius Negilius Mant, freedman of Lucius; Lucius Negilius Zethus, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-10701698.summary: This inscription lists three freedmen of Lucius Negilius, likely part of a family tomb or ownership record.

EDCS-10701699.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Dama Caesaris nostri librarius vixit annos XXXI dies XXX Agathe mater filio bene merenti\n
EDCS-10701699.translation: To the Divine Shades. Dama, scribe of our Caesar, lived 31 years and 30 days. His mother Agathe, to her well-deserving son.
EDCS-10701699.summary: A funerary epitaph for Dama, a scribe of the emperor, who died at the age of 31 years and 30 days, dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-10701700.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maria Tyrannis vixit annos L Titus Duxius Callistus vixit annos LX hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-10701700.translation: To the Divine Shades. Maria Tyrannis lived 50 years. Titus Duxius Callistus lived 60 years. They are buried here.
EDCS-10701700.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maria Tyrannis and Titus Duxius Callistus, stating their ages at death and that they are buried together.

EDCS-10701704.text_edition: \n Lucius Seppius Luci filius Lucius Seppius Luci nepos Herculi\n
EDCS-10701704.translation: Lucius Seppius, son of Lucius; Lucius Seppius, grandson of Lucius, to Hercules.
EDCS-10701704.summary: A dedication to Hercules by Lucius Seppius and his son, also named Lucius Seppius.

EDCS-10800004.translation: For the safety and victory of our lords, the Emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Part(hi)cus Maximus, in his seventh year of tribunician power, consul twice, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, in his tribunician power. Officially.
EDCS-10800004.summary: A dedication for the safety and victory of Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, reflecting their titles and consular/tribunician powers, likely from the period of their joint rule.

EDCS-10800018.translation: Publius Alius Rufus is buried here. Faustus, his freedman, erected (this).
EDCS-10800018.summary: A funerary monument erected by Faustus, a freedman, for his former master Publius Alius Rufus.

EDCS-10800019.summary: A simple funerary epitaph stating that Anteros is buried at this location.

EDCS-10800020.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph identifying a person named Dionysius (with a partially restored gentile name) as buried here.

EDCS-10800021.translation: Faustus lived 2 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-10800021.summary: A funerary epitaph for a child named Faustus, who died at the age of two years.

EDCS-10800022.translation: Flavia Ny... lived for [3] years. Cass... M...
EDCS-10800022.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavia Ny, recording her name and uncertain age and familial connections.

EDCS-10800023.translation: Helvius Dionysius lived for 55 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-10800023.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Helvius Dionysius, recording his age and burial.

EDCS-10800024.translation: Lucius Maecius Amoenus, freedman of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-10800024.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Maecius Amoenus, a freedman, indicating his burial.

EDCS-10800025.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Naevius Priamus lived for 30 years. Dignitas made this for her dearest grandson. He is buried here.
EDCS-10800025.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Naevius Priamus at age 30, with a dedication from his grandmother Dignitas.

EDCS-10800026.translation: ... Nicopolis ... Lucius Vibius ... lived for [ ] years.
EDCS-10800026.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription mentioning Nicopolis and Lucius Vibius, with an incomplete recording of age.

EDCS-10800029.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Thalia.

EDCS-10800311.text_edition: \n Caius Licinius Iustus patroni et curatoris kalendarii Barinorum filius exornatus equo publico vixit nnos XVII menses V\n
EDCS-10800311.translation: Gaius Licinius Iustus, son of the patron and curator of the calendar of the Barini, honored with the public equestrian order, lived for 17 years and 5 months.
EDCS-10800311.summary: This inscription records the life and honors of Gaius Licinius Iustus, son of a patron and calendar curator, who died at age 17 years and 5 months.

EDCS-10800312.text_edition: \n Marcus Calvisius Cosmus hic situs est libertus patrono bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-10800312.translation: Marcus Calvisius Cosmus is buried here. His freedman made this for his well-deserving patron.
EDCS-10800312.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Calvisius Cosmus, with a dedication from his freedman.

EDCS-10800315.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Titi liberto Euphemo vixit annos LV filius patri pientissimo fecit\n
EDCS-10800315.translation: To Tito Flavio Euphemus, freedman of Titus. He lived for 55 years. His son made this for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-10800315.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Tito Flavio Euphemus, a freedman, at age 55, with a dedication from his son.

EDCS-10800316.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cnaeus Herrius Severus hortator vixit annos XVII Herrius Proculus et Gellia Proba filio bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-10800316.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cnaeus Herrius Severus, a trainer, lived for 17 years. Herrius Proculus and Gellia Proba made this for their deserving son.
EDCS-10800316.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cnaeus Herrius Severus, a trainer, at age 17, with a dedication from his parents.

EDCS-10800317.text_edition: \n Publius Petius Anteros fecit Marco Licinio Felici\n
EDCS-10800317.translation: Publius Petius Anteros made this for Marcus Licinius Felix.
EDCS-10800317.summary: A dedication made by Publius Petius Anteros to Marcus Licinius Felix.

EDCS-10900579.translation: In memory of Vitalis, his foster-son. Alexandria made this for one well-deserving.
EDCS-10900579.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Vitalis by Alexandria, likely his foster-mother.

EDCS-11500730.translation: For Marcus Bassaeus, son of Marcus, of the Palatine tribe, Axio, patron of the colony, curator of public affairs, twice chief magistrate, imperial procurator for the Ostian and Campanian roads, tribune of the soldiers of the XIII Legion Gemina, procurator of the region of Calabria, having held all the offices in Capua, patron of the colony of the Lupiensians, patron of the municipality of the Hudrentines. The entire order of the town council, for the public affairs well and faithfully managed, here first and alone gave gladiatorial contests of the victors of Campania with equal prizes and valuations. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-11500730.summary: A funerary monument honoring Marcus Bassaeus, a prominent local official and benefactor, by the municipal council of the Hudrentines, detailing his extensive career and a significant donation of gladiatorial games.

EDCS-11501332.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Albanus made this for his dearest son, Eusebetis.
EDCS-11501332.summary: A funerary dedication made by Albanus for his son Eusebetis.

EDCS-11600027.translation: Decciana Olympias for herself and for Decciana Cogita, Decciana Lupula, Vitali, Publius Caidius Trophimus, Lucius Triccius Epaphroditus.
EDCS-11600027.summary: A funerary inscription listing several individuals, possibly indicating a family or shared burial plot.

EDCS-11600029.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Neratia Paulina, his most excellent wife, who well deserved it. [He] made this.
EDCS-11600029.summary: A funerary dedication made by a husband for his esteemed wife, Neratia Paulina.

EDCS-11600030.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Neratia Firma, who lived 26 years. Neratius Faustus made this for his most dutiful sister.
EDCS-11600030.summary: A funerary dedication made by Neratius Faustus for his sister Neratia Firma, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-11600031.translation: Publius Annius [---], Accia [---], Annia [---], Annia Potestas. In front 12 feet, in the field [---] feet.
EDCS-11600031.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription recording plot dimensions and some names, likely for a burial plot.

EDCS-11600032.translation: For Quintus Granius Acceptus, freedman of Quintus. For Dynamis, freedwoman of Quintus. [His] son made [this].
EDCS-11600032.summary: A funerary dedication made by a son for his parents, Quintus Granius Acceptus and Dynamis, both freedpeople of a Quintus.

EDCS-12100068.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Julia Esperis, his wife, with whom he lived 25 years. Satrius Ianuarius made this for one well-deserving.
EDCS-12100068.summary: A funerary dedication by Satrius Ianuarius for his wife Julia Esperis, noting their 25 years of marriage.

EDCS-12100069.translation: Pullidia Primigenia, freedwoman of a man, made this for herself and for Gaius Genucius Novadus, freedman of a man, and for her son and fellow-soldier Gaius Pullidius Donatus, and for Novadus. In front 12 feet, into the field 15 feet.
EDCS-12100069.summary: This is a boundary inscription set by Pullidia Primigenia for her son and fellow-soldier Novadus, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12100070.translation: To the Divine Shades. Baebius Eubulus made this for Horatia Pelagia, his most dutiful mother, well-deserving.
EDCS-12100070.summary: A funerary dedication made by Baebius Eubulus for his mother Horatia Pelagia, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12100071.translation: To the Divine Shades. Primus and Chalemera made this for their most devoted son Primo, son of Labe(rius?) Primi, who lived for 8 years, 10 months, and one day. Farewell, Prime.
EDCS-12100071.summary: A funerary monument for Primo, son of Labe(rius?) Primi, erected by his parents Primus and Chalemera, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12100072.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Vibius Albanus arranged for this to be made for his father, Gaius Vibius Albanus.
EDCS-12100072.summary: A funerary dedication arranged by Gaius Vibius Albanus for his father of the same name, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12100075.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Annaeus Caricus made this for Lucius Pontius Amemptus, a dealer in woad, a well-deserving friend.
EDCS-12100075.summary: A funerary dedication by Quintus Annaeus Caricus for his friend Lucius Pontius Amemptus, a woad dealer, from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12200653.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titus Aurelius Avitus, a cavalryman who served as an *singularis* of Augustus under the turma of Aelius Saturninus, from Thrace, lived for 33 years and served for 16 years.
EDCS-12200653.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Aurelius Avitus, a *singularis* cavalryman, who served in the turma of Aelius Saturninus and died at age 33 after 16 years of service, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12200654.translation: To the Divine Shades. Dasius of the turma of Severus arranged for this for his brother Superus, a cavalryman and *singularis* of our Augustus from the turma of Aticamus.
EDCS-12200654.summary: A funerary dedication by Dasius for his brother Superus, a cavalryman and *singularis* in the Augustan guard, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12400515.translation: Publius Firvius Celer, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, made this for his brothers Numerius Firvius Maximus, son of Numerius, of the Galeria tribe, and Quintus Firvius Paulus, son of Numerius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-12400515.summary: A funerary monument erected by Publius Firvius Celer for his brothers Numerius Firvius Maximus and Quintus Firvius Paulus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12400529.translation: To the Divine Shades. Pontia Restuta made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-12400529.summary: A funerary dedication made by Pontia Restuta for herself during her lifetime, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12400539.translation: Marcus Mucius Celer, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, a brother, made this for Gnaeus Mucius Rufus, son of Gnaeus, of the Stellatina tribe, for Gnaeus Mucius Bassus, son of Gnaeus, of the Stellatina tribe, and for Perennia Maxima, his wife.
EDCS-12400539.summary: A funerary inscription erected by Marcus Mucius Celer for his brothers Gnaeus Mucius Rufus and Gnaeus Mucius Bassus, and for his wife Perennia Maxima, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12400543.translation: Sacred to Hercules Invictus. Aulus Paccius Crescens fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-12400543.summary: A dedication to Hercules Invictus by Aulus Paccius Crescens, likely a votive offering.

EDCS-12400545.translation: Numerius Bovius Hilarus, freedman of Numerius and Marcus, Augustalis magistrate, paved the road 58 feet long by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400545.summary: An inscription recording the paving of a road by the Augustalis magistrate Numerius Bovius Hilarus, funded by a decree of the local council.

EDCS-12400546.translation: Gaius Seppius Curva, son of Gaius, duumvir with judicial power, made (this) for himself and for his most excellent wife Spendia Prima and for his son Gaius Seppius Sabinus, who lived 8 years.
EDCS-12400546.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Seppius Curva, his wife Spendia Prima, and their son Gaius Seppius Sabinus, with the father holding a political office.

EDCS-12400557.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Vituria Spes, mother, (erected this) for Allius Trebius Clementianus, who lived 15 years and 6 months.
EDCS-12400557.summary: A tombstone set up by Vituria Spes for her son Allius Trebius Clementianus, who died in his mid-teens.

EDCS-12400564.translation: Gaius Lollius, son of Numerius, made (this) for himself and for Numerius Lollius, son of Numerius, his father, and for Ennia, daughter of Marcus, his mother.
EDCS-12400564.summary: A family tomb marker made by Gaius Lollius for himself and his parents, Numerius Lollius and Ennia.

EDCS-12400568.translation: Numerius Seppius Secundus, son of Aulus, [and] Critia Polla, daughter of Publius, [and] Seppia Posilla, daughter of Numerius. What it was fitting for a daughter to do for parents, the grieving parents did for their daughter.
EDCS-12400568.summary: A tombstone set up by parents, Numerius Seppius Secundus and Critia Polla, for their daughter Seppia Posilla, lamenting their loss.

EDCS-12400575.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Critonius Iustus made (this) for Gaius Betitius Honaratus, well-deserving.
EDCS-12400575.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Critonius Iustus for Gaius Betitius Honaratus, who is described as 'well-deserving'.

EDCS-12400576.translation: Eggia Gnesias, daughter of Gaius, her daughter-in-law, (erected this) for Claudia Maxima.
EDCS-12400576.summary: A tombstone set up by Eggia Gnesias for her mother-in-law, Claudia Maxima.

EDCS-12400578.translation: Heraiscus, slave of Eggia Nerulla, set up this altar to Jupiter Amarannus.
EDCS-12400578.summary: A dedication of an altar to Jupiter Amarannus by a slave named Heraiscus, who belonged to Eggia Nerulla.

EDCS-12400579.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Ciminius Maximus made (this) for his well-deserving wife Egnatia Spectata.
EDCS-12400579.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Ciminius Maximus for his wife Egnatia Spectata.

EDCS-12400581.translation: Cornelia Maxima, daughter of Decimus, for herself and her son Marcus Hirrius, son of Publius, [inscribed] from her will; Hilarus, a freedman, made [this monument] for 3,400 sesterces, by estimation.
EDCS-12400581.summary: This is a funerary monument for Cornelia Maxima and her son, paid for by her freedman.

EDCS-12400582.translation: Quintus Mummius Cuniculus, son of Quintus; Quintus Mummius Firmus, son of Quintus; Gaius Mummius Modestus, son of Quintus; Hirria Creysis, freedwoman of Decimus.
EDCS-12400582.summary: This inscription lists members of the Mummius family and a freedwoman, likely on a tombstone or family record.

EDCS-12400586.translation: Here lies in the sleep of peace Stefanus, who lived about four years.
EDCS-12400586.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Stefanus, indicating his approximate age at death.

EDCS-12400588.translation: For Lollia Prima, priestess, from Cereria.
EDCS-12400588.summary: This is a dedication to Lollia Prima, a priestess, by Cereria.

EDCS-12400589.translation: Marcus Vergilius Gallus, freedman of Caius, quinquennial.
EDCS-12400589.summary: This is a personal inscription identifying Marcus Vergilius Gallus by his name, status as a freedman, and his role as a quinquennial official.

EDCS-12400590.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pullidius Rufinus, father, made [this] for his well-deserving daughter Rufina.
EDCS-12400590.summary: This is a funerary dedication (dis manibus) from a father to his daughter.

EDCS-12400595.translation: To the god. Numerius Arrius Dexter, freedman of Marcus Nymphodotus, paid his vow willingly and deservedly. To the god. Numerius Arrius Dexter, freedman of Marcus Nymphodotus, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-12400595.summary: This inscription records Numerius Arrius Dexter fulfilling a vow to a deity.

EDCS-12400596.translation: To the Eternal God. Aeclania Primitiva paid her vow willingly.
EDCS-12400596.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aeclania Primitiva to an eternal deity.

EDCS-12400599.translation: Sacred to Hercules Aelianus, which Sammius Tertullinus dedicated from his higher office.
EDCS-12400599.summary: This is a religious dedication of a shrine or offering to Hercules Aelianus by Sammius Tertullinus.

EDCS-12400601.translation: Cantria Gemella to Juno. Paid her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-12400601.summary: This is a votive inscription by Cantria Gemella to the goddess Juno.

EDCS-12400602.translation: To Juno Augusta, by Marcus Mindius Marcellinus.
EDCS-12400602.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Juno Augusta by Marcus Mindius Marcellinus.

EDCS-12400603.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Hilara, freedwoman of Gaius Aulus Canonicus, has paid her vow willingly and deservedly for herself and her family.
EDCS-12400603.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Hilara, a freedwoman, and her family.

EDCS-12400604.translation: Eggia Sabina, priestess of the Great Mother, daughter of Bastulus.
EDCS-12400604.summary: This inscription identifies Eggia Sabina as a priestess of the Great Mother.

EDCS-12400606.translation: Gaius Pullidius Celer the elder has paid his vow to the divine spirit of Silvanus, as a token of gratitude.
EDCS-12400606.summary: This inscription is a votive offering to the Numina Silvani by Gaius Pullidius Celer the elder.

EDCS-12400607.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Simphorianus and Silvanus arranged for its construction.
EDCS-12400607.summary: This inscription dedicates an object to Silvanus, arranged by Simphorianus and Silvanus.

EDCS-12400609.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Julia Augusta.

EDCS-12400611.translation: [restored] to the sister of Caesar, [restored] son of Publius Flaccus, and Pertica(?) have had this made at their own expense.
EDCS-12400611.summary: This inscription records that Publius Flaccus and Pertica had a monument made at their own expense for the sister of Caesar.

EDCS-12400612.translation: To Nero Claudius Caesar, son of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-12400612.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Nero Claudius Caesar, son of Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus.

EDCS-12400617.translation: To the Divine Faustina the Pious, by decree of the decurions at public expense.
EDCS-12400617.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Diva Faustina Pia, ordered by the town council.

EDCS-12400619.translation: To the most powerful and most pious lord and master, Constantine the Great, Pious, Fortunate, Unconquered Augustus, Nonius Verus, a most distinguished man, corrector of Apulia and Calabria, devoted to his divine majesty and power.
EDCS-12400619.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Nonius Verus, corrector of Apulia and Calabria, to Emperor Constantine the Great.

EDCS-12400620.translation: To the divine nature and venerable lineage of our lord Flavius Julius Crispus, most blessed and most noble Emperor Caesar, Nonius Verus, a distinguished man, corrector of Apulia and Calabria, dedicated this.
EDCS-12400620.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Flavius Julius Crispus by Nonius Verus, who held the office of corrector of Apulia and Calabria.

EDCS-12400627.translation: To Eggius Ambibulus, son of Eggius, of the Cornelia tribe, to Pomponius Longinus Cassianus, to Lucius Maecius Postumus, consul, flamen Claudialis, salius collinus, praetor candidate, legate proconsul of the province of Macedonia, quaestor candidate, admitted among the patricians by the deified Trajan Parthicus, seviri equitum Romanorum, tribune of the legion XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, duovir quinquennalis, also patron of the colony by public decree of the curia.
EDCS-12400627.summary: This inscription lists the extensive career of an unnamed individual, likely Pomponius Longinus Cassianus or Lucius Maecius Postumus, detailing his public offices and honors, suggesting it is a dedication or a record of his achievements.

EDCS-12400629.translation: To Marcus Magius Maximus, son of Marcus, prefect of Egypt, from the people of Tarraco.
EDCS-12400629.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Magius Maximus, prefect of Egypt, by the people of Tarraco.

EDCS-12400631.translation: Gaius Togius Quintillus, a distinguished man, corrector of Apulia and Calabria, dedicated this.
EDCS-12400631.summary: This is a dedication by Gaius Togius Quintillus, who served as corrector of Apulia and Calabria.

EDCS-12400635.translation: Sempronius, an administrator of the privy purse, set this up for himself while alive.
EDCS-12400635.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument erected by Sempronius, an administrator of the imperial treasury.

EDCS-12400637.translation: To [---]o, son of Marcus, of the Cornelia tribe, Dextrus, aedile, duovir with judicial power, quaestor, tribune of the soldiers, prefect of the fabrum; to [---]nius, son of Quintus, of the Cornelia tribe, duovir, aedile.
EDCS-12400637.summary: This inscription records the honors and offices held by two individuals, [---]o Dextrus and [---]nius, likely as part of a public dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-12400643.translation: Marcus Patulacius Felix, freedman of Marcus, ordered this to be made by his will, under the supervision of Gaius Quintus, for Marcus Patulacius Maximus, son of Marcus, duovir, aedile, quaestor, and for Pontia, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-12400643.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Marcus Patulacius Felix, a freedman, commissioned monuments for Marcus Patulacius Maximus and Pontia through his will.

EDCS-12400646.translation: To Gaius Vibius Bastulus, son of Gaius, of the Cornelia tribe, duovir quinquennalis, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400646.summary: This is a public dedication by decree of the decurions to Gaius Vibius Bastulus, honoring his service as duovir quinquennalis.

EDCS-12400647.translation: The pontifex, duovir, [---]cius Marcellus, son of Marcus, pontifex, duovir, aedile; and [---]nio, son of Quintus, [---] have seen to the paving of the street through the Pecuarium forum, from the money which they owed for their honor, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400647.summary: This inscription records that two individuals, Marcellus and another unnamed official, used funds designated for public honors to pave a street by order of the decurions.

EDCS-12400651.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aenonius Aviola, having performed all the honors, lived 78 years; he made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-12400651.summary: This is a self-erected funerary monument for Publius Aenonius Aviola, who lived to 78 and had held public offices.

EDCS-12400652.translation: Gaius Arrius Flaccus, son of Numerius, (and) the Kamaenians built the porticoes around the forum at their own expense.
EDCS-12400652.summary: This inscription records the construction of porticoes around the forum by Gaius Arrius Flaccus and the Kamaenians, likely during the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400653.translation: From the will of Betitia M[---], a statue [---] of Marcus Mindius.
EDCS-12400653.summary: This fragment indicates a statue dedicated from the will of Betitia, likely a funerary or honorific monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400655.translation: To Lucius Calventius Lusius Nepos, son of Lucius, of the Cornelia tribe, a most distinguished man, citizen, patron, and most splendid curator of the colony of Aeclanenses, for (his) outstanding [service].
EDCS-12400655.summary: This is an honorific dedication to Lucius Calventius Lusius Nepos, a prominent official and patron of Aeclanenses, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400656.translation: To Publius Cantrius Italus, son of Publius, of the Cornelia tribe, duovir with judicial power, and to his son Publius Cantrius Italus, and to Baebia Lupula, daughter of Gaius, wife (of the elder Italus), from friends, for their merits, a place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400656.summary: This inscription records a place granted by decree of the decurions to Publius Cantrius Italus, a duovir, his son, and his wife, for their merits, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400657.translation: To Cantria Longina, daughter of Publius, priestess of the divine Julia Pia Augusta and of the Great Mother of the Gods, Cybele, and of Isis Regina, she dedicated this, for the honor of the priesthood, 50,000 sesterces to the state, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400657.summary: This inscription records a donation of 50,000 sesterces by Cantria Longina, a priestess of multiple deities, to the state for her priestly honor, by decree of the decurions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400658.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12400658.translation: Cnaeus Ennius Dexter made this to his mother and nurse, Cantria Paulla, daughter of Publius, priestess of Augusta in Aeclanum; she also dedicated a silver statue of Felicitas of Aeclanum.
EDCS-12400658.summary: This tombstone records Cnaeus Ennius Dexter's dedication to his mother and nurse, Cantria Paulla, a priestess, who also dedicated a statue of Felicitas, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400659.translation: To Cantria Paulla, daughter of Publius, priestess of the divine Augusta, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400659.summary: This is a public dedication, by decree of the decurions, to Cantria Paulla, daughter of Publius, a priestess of Augusta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400660.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-12400660.translation: Tiberius Claudius Maximus, son of Tiberius, grandson of Tiberius, quaestor, and duovir quinquennalis, when he was conducting the games at the age of 20 in the colony of Aeclanenses, he presented a public show, having obtained permission for its performance from the Emperor Antoninus Pius; in which honor he was buried. His mother, Geminia Sabina, daughter of Manius, for his honor, paved three miles of the road leading to Herdonias with the money provided within his year of office, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400660.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Tiberius Claudius Maximus, a young magistrate and performer of games, and his mother's paving of a road in his honor, with the monument likely dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-12400662.translation: The Luperci, by decree of the most splendid order, granted this place; Caius Eggius Nepos, father.
EDCS-12400662.summary: This inscription records the granting of a place by the decurions to the Luperci, with Caius Eggius Nepos mentioned as the father, likely a public or religious dedication.

EDCS-12400663.translation: To the gods of the underworld. To Caius Mammercius Proculus, a deserving priest and decurion, his wife made this, with whom he lived for 50 years, 5 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-12400663.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her husband, Caius Mammercius Proculus, a priest and decurion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400664.translation: To Gaius Neratius Proculus Betitius Pius Maximillianus, son of Gaius, grandson of Gaius, great-grandson of Gaius, great-great-grandson of Gaius, of the Cornelia tribe, quaestor, duovir for five years, patron of the colony, flamen of the deified Hadrian, curator of public works at Venusia, appointed by the deified Hadrian; curator of the calendar of the people of Nola, appointed by the Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius. Epaphroditus and Conventa, his freedmen, [granted this] place by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400664.summary: A funerary or honorific inscription for Gaius Neratius Proculus Maximillianus, detailing his career and benefactors.

EDCS-12400665.translation: To Gaius Neratius Proculus Maximillianus Betitius Pius, son of Gaius, grandson of Gaius, great-grandson of Gaius, great-great-grandson of Gaius, of the Cornelia tribe, patron of the colony. Epaphroditus and Conventa, his freedmen, [granted this] place by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400665.summary: An inscription marking a place granted by decree, likely funerary or honorific, for Gaius Neratius Proculus Maximillianus.

EDCS-12400667.translation: To Neratia Betitia Procilla, flaminica of Faustina Augusta, daughter of [Emperor Antoninus] Augustus Pius. [---], freedwoman.
EDCS-12400667.summary: An inscription honoring Neratia Betitia Procilla, a priestess, likely funerary, with ties to the imperial family.

EDCS-12400668.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Marcus Pomponius Bassus, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, great-grandson of Marcus, great-great-grandson of Marcus, of the Cornelia tribe, duovir for five years. Lest I waste away in idleness like cattle, I, Menander, turned a few witty plays and also diligently composed new ones myself; though they were committed to paper long ago, I, tormented by anxious cares and also by several bodily pains, found both excessively tedious, until I obtained the longed-for death, which, according to its custom, grants me all comforts. You who are about to enter this tomb, inscribe this eulogy, which may serve as a lesson to my descendants hereafter not to dread the rocks of life excessively, since there is a prepared harbor for voyagers that awaits us to eternal rest. But now, farewell, until life of equal value. Cantria Longina made [this] for her excellent, well-deserving husband.
EDCS-12400668.summary: A tomb inscription for Marcus Pomponius Bassus, a former duovir, containing a lengthy epitaph written by the deceased himself and dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-12400669.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Marcus Pomponius Bassus, Longinianus, Roman Equite, of the Laurentes Lavinates; Ambibolus the younger [dedicates this to his] uncle.
EDCS-12400669.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Pomponius Bassus Longinianus, dedicated by his nephew.

EDCS-12400670.translation: To Gaius Pontius Hyginus, dearest infant, decurion. Gaius Pontius Cogitatus, father.
EDCS-12400670.summary: A tombstone for an infant decurion, Gaius Pontius Hyginus, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-12400671.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Gaius Quintius Bassus, son of Gaius, of the Cornelia tribe, duovir, aedil, duovir with jurisdiction, quaestor, pontifex, our most excellent patron. Gaius Baebius Lupus and Quintia Dro[---].
EDCS-12400671.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Quintius Bassus, a former magistrate and priest, dedicated by his clients.

EDCS-12400672.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Sextus Secundinus, son of Sextus, of the Cornelia tribe, duovir, aedil, quaestor of public funds. Secundius Urbicus made [this] for his well-deserving patron.
EDCS-12400672.summary: A funerary inscription for Sextus Secundinus, a former magistrate, dedicated by his client.

EDCS-12400673.translation: Marcus Staius Cosmus by his will ordered the market building to be decorated, and Fisia S[---] completed the embellishment with the twentieth part [of his estate], and the same [Fisia] donated his son's school, when it was decorated.
EDCS-12400673.summary: A dedication recording a testamentary commission for market decoration and the completion and donation of a school, funded by an estate and a specific individual.

EDCS-12400674.translation: To Quintus Trebius Maximus, son of Marcus, son of Quintus, of the [---] decurion. [---] Aeclan[---], husband [of ---].
EDCS-12400674.summary: An incomplete inscription honoring Quintus Trebius Maximus, a decurion, likely funerary or honorific.

EDCS-12400675.translation: To Vibiae Liciniae Telesillae, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400675.summary: A posthumous dedication to Vibiae Liciniae Telesillae by order of the town council.

EDCS-12400697.translation: To Gaius Aletius Lupus, son of Publius, scribe and librarian; Eppia Rufa, daughter of Lucius; and Publius Aletius Lupus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-12400697.summary: An epitaph commemorating several individuals, including a scribe and librarian, likely from Beneventum.

EDCS-12400698.translation: Gaius Ennius Hilarus, freedman of Gaius, Augustalis, made this for himself and Appuleia Prima, freedwoman of Gaius, his concubine, and Ennia Erina, freedwoman of Gaius and Gaius, and his freedman Philletus Successus. This monument or tomb shall not follow an heir.
EDCS-12400698.summary: A funerary monument erected by Gaius Ennius Hilarus, an Augustalis from Beneventum, for himself and several family members and freedmen, with a clause preventing inheritance.

EDCS-12400699.translation: Gaius Eppius Iucundus, freedman of Gaius, Augustalis, for himself and Calpurnia Moderata, freedwoman of Gaius, and Maevia Fortunata, his wife.
EDCS-12400699.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Gaius Eppius Iucundus, an Augustalis, for himself, his wife, and a freedwoman.

EDCS-12400700.translation: To Lucius Postumius Primusgeni, freedman of Lucius, Augustalis, and Junia Rufilla.
EDCS-12400700.summary: A dedication to Lucius Postumius Primusgeni, an Augustalis, and Junia Rufilla.

EDCS-12400701.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Tillus Dioscorus, son of Publius, freedman, Augustalis, lived [X] years.
EDCS-12400701.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Tillus Dioscorus, an Augustalis, who lived for an unspecified number of years.

EDCS-12400702.summary: An incomplete inscription honoring a quinquennial Augustalis, likely Marcus [---].

EDCS-12400703.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Aurelius Aufentinus, a man of the highest virtue from Aeclanensium and Abellinatis, [---] years old. Pamp[hil]us, to his friend.
EDCS-12400703.summary: A funerary dedication by Pamp[hil]us to his friend Aurelius Aufentinus, a respected figure from the communities of Aeclanensium and Abellinatis.

EDCS-12400704.translation: Aeclanius Maximianus and Aeclania Marcella made this from their joint labor for themselves.
EDCS-12400704.summary: A funerary monument established by Aeclanius Maximianus and Aeclania Marcella for themselves, indicating a shared effort.

EDCS-12400705.translation: [---]scinus, son of Aeclanius, and S[---] and Martia[---], daughters? [---]
EDCS-12400705.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription mentioning individuals with the cognomen Aeclanius and possibly his daughters.

EDCS-12400706.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Herm[---] made this for his well-deserving wife, Aeccania Ama[---]ae.
EDCS-12400706.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband for his deceased wife, Aeccania Amanti, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400707.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aecla(nia) Gr[---] made this for her well-deserving son, Aeclan(ius) Grap[---].
EDCS-12400707.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother for her deceased son, Aeclan(ius) Grap[---], likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400708.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aeclanius Ianuarius made this for his well-deserving wife, Aeclan(ia) Spenti.
EDCS-12400708.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband for his deceased wife, Aeclan(ia) Spenti, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400709.translation: Aelius Secundus made this monument, Castaniola, for himself while still alive.
EDCS-12400709.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument commissioned by Aelius Secundus during his lifetime.

EDCS-12400710.translation: Cominia Felici[ss]ima made this for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Aem[ilius] [---]ae Pontius Quintianus.
EDCS-12400710.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife for her deceased husband, Marcus Aemilius Pontius Quintianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400711.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Cerialis and Marcella made this for their dearest son, Marcus Aeprius, son of Marcus, who lived 8 years, 10 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-12400711.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents for their young son, Marcus Aeprius, who died at the age of 8, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400712.translation: To Gaius Aetrius, son of Gaius, grandson of Gaius, great-grandson of Lucius, great-great-grandson of Gaius, [Cornelia] Maturo.
EDCS-12400712.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording a man's extensive genealogy, indicating a distinguished family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400713.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Vibius Cresce(n)s made this for his incomparable wife, Afinia Ienua(ria), who deserved well.
EDCS-12400713.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband for his deceased wife, Afinia Ienuaria, emphasizing her unique qualities, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400714.translation: [---] for Stephanus [---] for Agria, freedwoman of Quintus, Tertia [---] for Quintus Agrius, freedman of Quintus, Optatus, in front 12 feet [---]
EDCS-12400714.summary: This inscription appears to be a boundary marker or property description, mentioning several individuals and dimensions, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400715.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gnaeus Umbrius Primus made this for his wife, Allia Repentina, with whom he lived 14 years, and who deserved well.
EDCS-12400715.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband for his deceased wife, Allia Repentina, whom he had been married to for 14 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400718.translation: To the departed spirits. Ancharia Ianuaria Pla(?) made this for her deserving husband, Felicissimus.
EDCS-12400718.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deserving husband, Felicissimus.

EDCS-12400719.translation: Quintus Annius, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, and Quintus Annius Seleucus, freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-12400719.summary: A funerary epitaph listing two individuals, likely from the same family, with their names, patronymic, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-12400721.translation: To the departed spirits. Annia Successa made this for her deserving husband, Cornelius Felicissimus.
EDCS-12400721.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deserving husband, Cornelius Felicissimus.

EDCS-12400722.summary: A simple funerary epitaph dedicated to Annidia Hirpina.

EDCS-12400723.translation: To the departed spirits. Caius Antistius Cerialis made this for his incomparable son, Gaius Antistius Demetrius, who deserved well.
EDCS-12400723.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his deserving and incomparable son, Gaius Antistius Demetrius.

EDCS-12400724.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to record the names of at least two individuals, Arrius Philotaerus and Salvius.

EDCS-12400725.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Arrius Fortunatus made this for his deserving father, Marcus Arrius Priscianus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-12400725.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a son for his deserving father, Marcus Arrius Priscianus.

EDCS-12400726.translation: To Gaius Arrius Rufus, freedman of Numerius. 12 feet in front, 15 feet in the land.
EDCS-12400726.summary: An inscription marking a property boundary, indicating the dimensions of land belonging to Gaius Arrius Rufus, a freedman.

EDCS-12400727.translation: [...] Auctus [...] Milonius, son of Annius, Achoristus, for his brother.
EDCS-12400727.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Milonius Achoristus setting up a monument for his brother, Auctus.

EDCS-12400728.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Crescens made this for his deserving wife, Aurelia Marcia.
EDCS-12400728.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deserving wife, Aurelia Marcia.

EDCS-12400729.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Aurelia Claudia, dearest wife. Onesimus [erected this].
EDCS-12400729.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Onesimus to his beloved wife Aurelia Claudia.

EDCS-12400730.translation: Marcus Babrius, freedman of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, made this for himself and his family. It measures 12 feet in front and 15 feet in depth.
EDCS-12400730.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dimensions of a plot of land purchased by Marcus Babrius for himself and his family.

EDCS-12400731.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Babrius Lesbus and Alpia Helpidi. Vergilia Felicitas made this for her deserving husband and daughter.
EDCS-12400731.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Vergilia Felicitas for her husband Quintus Babrius Lesbus and daughter Alpia Helpidi.

EDCS-12400732.translation: Lived. For Gaius Babrius Pelops, freedman of Gaius, for himself and for Babria Chia, freedwoman of Gaius, and for his daughter Irpina (lived?) and for his son Irpinus and for Babria Restituta and Lived. Ingenua Babria. In front 20 feet, in depth 24 feet.
EDCS-12400732.summary: This is a funerary inscription for multiple family members, including Gaius Babrius Pelops and his relatives, with the dimensions of the plot provided.

EDCS-12400733.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Babrius Rufus lived 20 years. Babria Prima, his mother, made this for her deserving son.
EDCS-12400733.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Babria Prima for her son Gaius Babrius Rufus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-12400734.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Babria Bebinus, his wife. Theseus of the colony of Aeclanum set up [this] for [his] deserving wife.
EDCS-12400734.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Theseus to his wife Babria Bebinus in the colony of Aeclanum.

EDCS-12400735.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Gaius Betitius Pius, son of Gaius, of the Cornelia tribe. Buried here.
EDCS-12400735.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Betitius Pius, son of Gaius, indicating he is buried at this location.

EDCS-12400736.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Betitia Heliadis, his wife. Marcianus made this for his deserving slave-woman.
EDCS-12400736.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcianus for his slave-woman Betitia Heliadis, identified as his wife.
