EDCS-12400737.translation: For Betitia Iusta, daughter of Gaius. Epaphroditus and Herma [---]. Freedmen. The plot was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400737.summary: This inscription records a plot of land granted by decree of the decurions for Betitia Iusta, daughter of Gaius, and mentions her freedmen Epaphroditus and Herma.

EDCS-12400738.translation: To Betitia Maximilla, wife of Gaius, daughter of Gaius. Gaius Vibius Hilarius made this for himself and [his wife].
EDCS-12400738.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Gaius Vibius Hilarius for his wife Betitia Maximilla, daughter of Gaius, and himself.

EDCS-12400739.translation: To the departed spirits. Quintius made this for his well-deserving wife, Betitia Pontina, freedwoman of Numerius.
EDCS-12400739.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Betitia Pontina by her husband, Quintius.

EDCS-12400740.translation: To the departed spirits. Betitius Rufinus, the father, made this for his dearest daughter, Betitia Quintiliana, who lived 16 years and 10 months, sorrowfully, along with her husband, Claudius Iustinus, for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-12400740.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Betitia Quintiliana, set up by her grieving father and husband.

EDCS-12400741.translation: To the memory(?). Brittius Domitius made this for his dearest son, Brittio Domitius.
EDCS-12400741.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Brittio Domitius, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-12400742.translation: To the departed spirits. For Gaius Caelis Rufus, who lived 26 years and 7 months. Gaius Caelis made this for his most unfortunate son.
EDCS-12400742.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Caelis Rufus, set up by his father.

EDCS-12400744.translation: To the departed spirits. For his dearest son, Caesenius Vitalis, who lived 2 years, 10 months, and 9 days. Caesenius Vitalis placed this. He made it for his well-deserving son.
EDCS-12400744.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caesenius Vitalis, set up by his father of the same name.

EDCS-12400745.translation: To the departed spirits. Sylvester, the father, placed this for his dearest daughter, Cantria Primilla, who lived 4 years, 2 months, and 8 days. He made it for his well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-12400745.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cantria Primilla, set up by her father, Sylvester.

EDCS-12400747.translation: To the departed spirits. Admetus made this for his well-deserving companion, Casta.
EDCS-12400747.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Casta, dedicated by her companion, Admetus.

EDCS-12400749.summary: This is a funerary epitaph fragment that begins with a dedication to the departed spirits and the name Claudia.

EDCS-12400750.summary: This inscription fragment mentions Cominia Marcella, likely as the recipient of a monument.

EDCS-12400751.translation: To the departed spirits. Veratia Romana made this for her well-deserving husband, Cosius Simphorus.
EDCS-12400751.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Cosius Simphorus by his wife, Veratia Romana.

EDCS-12400752.translation: To the departed spirits. Saturninus Marulli made this for his most sweet wife Cossonia Icela and for himself.
EDCS-12400752.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Saturninus Marulli to his wife Cossonia Icela and himself, likely from a tombstone.

EDCS-12400754.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Dasimius Firmus, possibly the recipient of a tomb or other monument.

EDCS-12400755.translation: For Gaius Dossennius Martialis, for himself and his family. Frontage 20 feet, depth 15 feet.
EDCS-12400755.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot measuring 20 by 15 feet, dedicated by Gaius Dossennius Martialis for himself and his family.

EDCS-12400756.translation: For Eggius Encolpus, son of Quinctus.
EDCS-12400756.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Eggius Encolpus, son of Quinctus.

EDCS-12400757.translation: Eggius Feles lived 15 years and 4 months.
EDCS-12400757.summary: This inscription records the age of death for Eggius Feles, who lived 15 years and 4 months.

EDCS-12400758.translation: To the departed spirits of Eggia Bassula.
EDCS-12400758.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Eggia Bassula.

EDCS-12400759.translation: Eggia Vitalis, freedwoman of Lucius, made this from her own property for herself, her dearest husband Gaius Vibius Albinus, their daughter Vibia Albina, and all her family. In depth 17 feet.
EDCS-12400759.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Eggia Vitalis for herself, her husband, daughter, and descendants, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-12400760.translation: Marcus Egullius, son of [---] (of the Cornelia tribe), for himself and Ceia P. l. [---], made this while alive; the heir shall not follow.
EDCS-12400760.summary: This inscription records that Marcus Egullius, son of [---], and Ceia, freedwoman of Publius, made this monument while alive and stipulated that their heirs would not be obligated to maintain it.

EDCS-12400762.translation: Albanus made this for his dearest son Eusebetis.
EDCS-12400762.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Albanus for his beloved son Eusebetis.

EDCS-12400763.translation: Lucius Fabricius Apro lived 10 years [---]. Chreste, his wife, placed him here.
EDCS-12400763.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Lucius Fabricius Apro and states that his wife Chreste placed him here, likely on a tomb.

EDCS-12400764.translation: To Fannia Herculia, my sweetest wife, Betitius Rufus made this for her, with whom he lived in marriage for 26 years and 2 months, well-deserving.
EDCS-12400764.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome for a wife, set up by her husband after 26 years and 2 months of marriage.

EDCS-12400765.translation: To Poponius Feliciano, his son, Poponia Veneria made this.
EDCS-12400765.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome for a son, dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-12400766.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Bithus Crescens made this for Firma Albina, his wife, well-deserving.
EDCS-12400766.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome for a wife, dedicated by her husband.

EDCS-12400767.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavia Asinia made this for her well-deserving husband, Gaius Flavius Marcianus Abonianus.
EDCS-12400767.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome for a husband, dedicated by his wife.

EDCS-12400769.translation: Lucius Funius Antiochus, freedman of Lucius, is buried here. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-12400769.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome marking a burial plot with dimensions for Lucius Funius Antiochus.

EDCS-12400770.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. 24 feet in front, 21 feet in the field.
EDCS-12400770.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome indicating the dimensions of a burial plot for Decimus Clodius Gelotus.

EDCS-12400771.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Hermerotus, my comrade.
EDCS-12400771.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome dedicated to a deceased comrade.

EDCS-12400772.translation: Orfellia Galla, daughter of Publius, wife of Hostidius Justa, daughter of Gaius, made this for herself and her daughter, from her own property.
EDCS-12400772.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome, set up by a mother for herself and her daughter.

EDCS-12400774.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Maximus and Ianuaria made this monument for their son Ianuarius, who lived for 1 year, 6 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-12400774.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome for a young child, dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-12400776.translation: The most unhappy parents of Aulus Ignius Crispinus, Theia Iustina, and Aulus Ignius Crispinus, and his brother Gaius Trebatius, son of Gaius, and his mother Theia Iustina, placed this.
EDCS-12400776.summary: A funerary epitaph from Rome, unusually complex, dedicated by the parents and brother of the deceased.

EDCS-12400777.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Marcia made this for her brother Iulius Serenus, who deserved well.
EDCS-12400777.summary: A tombstone set up by Iulia Marcia for her brother Iulius Serenus.

EDCS-12400779.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Silvanus made this for Laudicenus, who deserved well.
EDCS-12400779.summary: A tombstone erected by Silvanus for Laudicenus.

EDCS-12400780.translation: Marcus Licinius Rufinus the younger, who lived for 2 years and 7 months. His mother made this for her sweetest son, who deserved well.
EDCS-12400780.summary: A tombstone for the son of Marcus Licinius Rufinus, erected by his mother.

EDCS-12400781.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lollia Albina, daughter, and Vibia Eutychia made this for their husband Quintus Lollius Hermes, who deserved well.
EDCS-12400781.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Lollius Hermes, erected by his daughter Lollia Albina and wife Vibia Eutychia.

EDCS-12400782.translation: To the memory of Lucceius, patron. Adventa(?), nurse(?), made this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-12400782.summary: A memorial stone for the patron Lucceius, possibly erected by his nurse Adventa.

EDCS-12400783.translation: Decius Marcellinus arranged for this to be made for Lucretia Lea, his sweetest wife.
EDCS-12400783.summary: A funerary monument arranged by Decius Marcellinus for his wife Lucretia Lea.

EDCS-12400785.translation: For Gaius Marcius Chilo, freedman of Gaius, 12 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-12400785.summary: A boundary marker for a property belonging to Gaius Marcius Chilo, a freedman.

EDCS-12400786.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For [---]MP[---], daughter of Marcus(?), exceedingly rare. Marcus Trebatius Quartus made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-12400786.summary: A tombstone erected by Marcus Trebatius Quartus for a woman, likely his wife or relative, whose name is mostly lost.

EDCS-12400788.translation: To the memory of Maria Prima. Gaius Avidius Aper for his wife(?), who deserved well, made this.
EDCS-12400788.summary: A memorial for Maria Prima, possibly erected by her husband Gaius Avidius Aper.

EDCS-12400790.translation: Lucius Munatius made this for Lucius Munatius Ianuarius, his freedman, who deserved excellently.
EDCS-12400790.summary: A funerary monument set up by Lucius Munatius for his freedman Lucius Munatius Ianuarius.

EDCS-12400791.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Naevius Felix, the father, made this sorrowfully for his son, Gaius Naevius Felix.
EDCS-12400791.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his son, both named Gaius Naevius Felix.

EDCS-12400792.translation: Macia Bibia made this for her well-deserving husband, Ofillius Lusianus.
EDCS-12400792.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, Ofillius Lusianus.

EDCS-12400793.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Oppia Prima made this for her well-deserving husband, Oppius Fortunatus.
EDCS-12400793.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, Oppius Fortunatus.

EDCS-12400794.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Eccia [---] for her well-deserving husband, Lucius Peduceus Caprio.
EDCS-12400794.summary: A funerary epitaph, likely set up by a wife for her husband, Lucius Peduceus Caprio.

EDCS-12400795.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Petronius Victor, the son, made this for his well-deserving Publius Petronius Taburnius.
EDCS-12400795.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a son for his father, Publius Petronius Taburnius.

EDCS-12400796.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Philistius Niceratus made this for his well-deserving wife, Philistia Aelatena.
EDCS-12400796.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his wife, Philistia Aelatena.

EDCS-12400797.translation: Gnaeus Pomponius Demetrius for himself and [---]
EDCS-12400797.summary: The beginning of a funerary inscription where Gnaeus Pomponius Demetrius is dedicating a monument to himself and likely others.

EDCS-12400798.translation: To the spirits of the departed. [---] for his [---] wife, Progilla.
EDCS-12400798.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph mentioning Progilla as a wife.

EDCS-12400799.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ermia Bassilla placed this for her well-deserving husband, Pomponius Protogenetus.
EDCS-12400799.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, Pomponius Protogenetus.

EDCS-12400801.translation: [---] Prisca made this for her fellow slave, Fortunatus.
EDCS-12400801.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Prisca for her fellow slave, Fortunatus.

EDCS-12400802.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Quintius Martialis made this for his well-deserving freedwoman Mul[3]inia Adrianin.
EDCS-12400802.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman for his freedwoman, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400803.translation: Gaius Raius Callistus, freedman of Publius, for himself and his wife Alaria Saturnina. Six feet in front, six feet in the land.
EDCS-12400803.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Raius Callistus and his wife, specifying the dimensions of their burial plot, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400804.summary: An incomplete inscription, possibly a funerary epitaph, with illegible portions, from an uncertain date.

EDCS-12400805.translation: Aulus Rhaesius Trophimus, patron, [dedicated this] to his freedwoman Rhaesia Priscilla.
EDCS-12400805.summary: A funerary dedication by a patron to his freedwoman, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400806.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Robilia Donata, mother, and Romulus, father, made this monument for their son Cominianus, who lived 3 years and 3 months; for a good man.
EDCS-12400806.summary: A funerary monument erected by parents for their young son, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400807.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Titinius Ianuarius made this for his very sweet wife Robilia Stattini, with whom he lived 5 years, 6 months, and 1 day; for her who deserved well.
EDCS-12400807.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, specifying the duration of their marriage, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400809.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Rufinius Silvester(?), who was also called Acu(r)ius, while alive...
EDCS-12400809.summary: An incomplete funerary dedication for Gaius Rufinius Silvester(?), likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400810.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Rufinia Successa.
EDCS-12400810.summary: A simple funerary dedication to Rufinia Successa, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400811.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Rufinia, mother deserving well, [she] placed [this]. AATO EI ANTIE.
EDCS-12400811.summary: A funerary dedication by a child for their mother, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400812.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Niconianus made this for his wife Secunda, who deserved well.
EDCS-12400812.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400813.translation: To Iucunda Sempronia. Gaius Sempronius, son of Lucius Pro[---], by the decision of Marcus Ofilius Tertius.
EDCS-12400813.summary: This inscription likely records a property transaction or legal settlement, mentioning individuals and potentially a settlement arbiter.

EDCS-12400814.translation: Numerius Spedius Dexsanicus, son of Vibius, of the Cornelia tribe, is buried here.
EDCS-12400814.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Numerius Spedius Dexsanicus, identifying his name, filiation, tribal affiliation, and indicating his burial place.

EDCS-12400816.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Tituleia Saturnina, mother, made this for her son Secundus, who lived 19 years, as deserving.
EDCS-12400816.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tituleio Secundus, erected by his mother Tituleia Saturnina, stating his age at death and her sentiment.

EDCS-12400817.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Eggius Proculus made this for his wife Trebatia Ianuaria, as deserving.
EDCS-12400817.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Trebatia Ianuaria, dedicated by her husband Lucius Eggius Proculus, expressing his affection.

EDCS-12400818.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Trebatia Ianuaria, mother. Marcus Trebatius Valens and himself.
EDCS-12400818.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Trebatia Ianuaria by Marcus Trebatius Valens, who also includes himself, possibly indicating a shared burial space.

EDCS-12400819.translation: For the memory of Trebius Leotius. Trebius Vitalis, father, made this for his dearest son, as deserving.
EDCS-12400819.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Trebius Leotius, erected by his father Trebius Vitalis, expressing paternal grief and affection.

EDCS-12400820.translation: Quintus Traebius Maximus, son of Quintus, for himself and his family.
EDCS-12400820.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Quintus Traebius Maximus's self-dedication and inclusion of his family for burial.

EDCS-12400822.translation: Veratia Romana made this for her daughter Felicitas Cosiae.
EDCS-12400822.summary: This inscription records Veratia Romana making a dedication, likely funerary, for her daughter Felicitas.

EDCS-12400823.translation: [---]dio, son of Quintus, of the Cornelia tribe, lived [---] years [---] months II. Paulus and Vergilia [made this] for their most dutiful son [and for themselves].
EDCS-12400823.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for a son, possibly named [---]dio, erected by his parents Paulus and Vergilia, who also included themselves in the dedication.

EDCS-12400824.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Vibia Felicitas made this for her husband Saturninus Basilius, with whom she lived 11 years, as deserving.
EDCS-12400824.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Saturninus Basilius, dedicated by his wife Vibia Felicitas, noting the duration of their marriage and her sentiments.

EDCS-12400825.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Vibius Felix, his exceedingly unfortunate son, who lived 19 years, 10 months, and 8 days. His father Trebatius Eroticus and his mother Quete erected this.
EDCS-12400825.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vibius Felix, set up by his parents.

EDCS-12400826.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Vibius Hermes erected this for his daughter Hermetionis.
EDCS-12400826.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hermetionis, set up by her father Gaius Vibius Hermes.

EDCS-12400827.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vibius Criscens made this for Vibius Secundus, well-deserving.
EDCS-12400827.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vibius Secundus, made by Vibius Criscens.

EDCS-12400828.translation: For Gaius Vibuleius Gallus, his brother, [erected according to his] will and by the arbitration of Gaius Betitius Eunus.
EDCS-12400828.summary: A funerary monument erected for Gaius Vibuleius Gallus as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-12400829.translation: Vitellia Q[---]mima and P[---]zmurna and Gratus [---] made [this].
EDCS-12400829.summary: A funerary monument whose dedicants and recipient are only partially preserved.

EDCS-12400830.translation: Gaius Umbrius Silvanus and Umbria Felicula, for themselves and their descendants.
EDCS-12400830.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Umbrius Silvanus and Umbria Felicula for themselves and their family.

EDCS-12400832.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Nonius [erected this] for the well-deserving Maenia.
EDCS-12400832.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maenia, set up by Nonius.

EDCS-12400835.summary: A funerary epitaph with a partially preserved recipient and dedicant.

EDCS-12400847.summary: A dedication to Gaius Ellius R[---] with only the beginning preserved.

EDCS-12400863.translation: Meri[---] /lianus for his wife Aniai[---] and for himself alive and for his [family].
EDCS-12400863.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Mer[---]lianus for his wife and for himself and his family.

EDCS-12400899.translation: Memorial for Iulius Fidens, acolyte. He lived 40 years, 6 months. Made by those who well deserved. (Possibly: his children/relatives)
EDCS-12400899.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument commemorating Iulius Fidens, an acolyte, who lived to be 40 years and 6 months old, likely erected by his beneficiaries.

EDCS-12400900.translation: Burial of Laurentius, who lived about 20 years.
EDCS-12400900.summary: This inscription records the burial of Laurentius, who died at approximately 20 years of age.

EDCS-12400902.translation: Burial of Virtutus, who lived two years and 30 days.
EDCS-12400902.summary: This inscription records the burial of Virtutus, who lived for two years and 30 days.

EDCS-12400906.translation: To the divine shades. Camuria Ursula made this for her son Lucius Camurius Erotianus, son of Lucius, of the Cornelia tribe, who lived 3 years, 8 months, 8 days, and for herself.
EDCS-12400906.summary: This funerary dedication from a mother to her young son, Lucius Camurius Erotianus, also includes herself, indicating a shared tomb, and dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12400907.translation: Sacred to the divine shades. Clodia Silvana placed this for her dearest, well-deserving husband, Marcus Aeclanius Felicissimus, with whom she lived 1 year, 6 months.
EDCS-12400907.summary: This funerary monument, dedicated to Marcus Aeclanius Felicissimus by his wife Clodia Silvana, records their short marriage of 1 year and 6 months.

EDCS-12400908.translation: Caius Vibbius Sergianus, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, had this made for his mother and sister, Maria Vibbia Tertia, daughter of Marcus, and Maria Vibbia Tertia, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-12400908.summary: This inscription records Caius Vibbius Sergianus commissioning a monument for his mother and sister, both named Maria Vibbia Tertia.

EDCS-12400909.translation: To the divine shades. Silvester Mandorinus for his good, well-deserving father, made this.
EDCS-12400909.summary: This funerary dedication is from Silvester Mandorinus to his father, honoring him as good and well-deserving.

EDCS-12400910.translation: To the divine shades. Celerinus (?) made this for his well-deserving adopted son Rufinus, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-12400910.summary: This funerary inscription honors Rufinus, an adopted son who lived 20 years, and was erected by Celerinus (?).

EDCS-12400914.translation: To the divine shades. Rubria Restituta made this for her well-deserving husband, Claudius Quintianus.
EDCS-12400914.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Rubria Restituta for her well-deserving husband, Claudius Quintianus.

EDCS-12400917.translation: Numerius Modius Primus, son of Numerius, of the Numerius tribe, for himself and his family, and for his 19-year-old brother Numerius Modius Paulus, son of Numerius, and for his mother Metia Prima, freedwoman of Sextus.
EDCS-12400917.summary: This inscription records Numerius Modius Primus dedicating a family tomb for himself, his brother Numerius Modius Paulus (aged 19), and his mother Metia Prima.

EDCS-12400919.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Bithynicus, son of Tiberius, of the Cornelia tribe, quaestor, duovir, aedile, duovir with judicial power, quinquennial duovir, prefect of craftsmen; here, with the permission of the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, he [built/restored] a road two miles long for those going into Apulia.
EDCS-12400919.summary: This inscription records the construction of a road by Tiberius Claudius Bithynicus, a local magistrate, with imperial permission, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12400920.translation: To the Divine Shades. Tiberius Claudius Maximus, duovir, aedile, quaestor, [responsible for] alimentary funds, lived 27 years and 5 months. Tiberius Claudius November and Claudia Hermio[n]e made [this] for their deserving son and for themselves.
EDCS-12400920.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Maximus, who held several magistracies and was involved with alimentary funds, set up by his parents.

EDCS-12400921.translation: For the memory of Marcus Mettius Sabinianus. Vibia Secunda had this made for her most sweet husband, and Marcus Mettius Felix for his most sweet brother.
EDCS-12400921.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a wife and a brother to the deceased Marcus Mettius Sabinianus.

EDCS-12400923.translation: To the Genius of the colony of Beneventum. Seppia Fidelis, freedwoman of Gaius, made [this] with her own money on a site granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400923.summary: This inscription records a public offering made by a freedwoman, Seppia Fidelis, to the Genius of Beneventum, with land granted by the town council.

EDCS-12400924.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Ennius Firmus, son of Gaius, with the permission of the decurions of the colony of Beneventum, as aedile of Beneventum, duovir with judicial power, quaestor, curator of the bath complex, appointed by the Emperor Caesar Hadrian Augustus.
EDCS-12400924.summary: This inscription records the career of Gaius Ennius Firmus, a local magistrate in Beneventum who also served as curator of the baths, appointed by Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-12400926.translation: Paccia Quintilla, daughter of Quintus, fulfilled her vow to Mefitis.
EDCS-12400926.summary: This is a votive inscription by Paccia Quintilla to the goddess Mefitis.

EDCS-12400927.translation: Erotice Virginia willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-12400927.summary: This is a votive inscription by Erotice Virginia, indicating a voluntary fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-12400929.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Marcus Aurelius Mucianus, a soldier of the Praetorian cohort Aquila, stipendiary of the sixteenth cohort, 40 years of age, of Thracian nationality. His brother Aurelius Silvanus, a vocatus, and Claudius, a freedman of a vocatus, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-12400929.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Mucianus, a Thracian soldier in the Praetorian Guard, erected by his brother and a freedman.

EDCS-12400934.translation: To the Divine Shades. Sabina Maria Iusta made [this] for her deserving mother Antigona, daughter of Annius Flavianus.
EDCS-12400934.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Sabina Maria Iusta for her mother, Antigona Flavianae.

EDCS-12400935.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Studius. Rutilia Maxima made [this] for her deserving husband, with whom she lived for 13 years and 26 days.
EDCS-12400935.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Rutilia Maxima for her husband, Marcus Aurelius Studius, whom she commemorates after 13 years and 26 days of marriage.

EDCS-12400936.translation: Babidia Psycharium, freedwoman of Gaius, lived 16 years. Lafria Philumina, freedwoman of Quintus, lived 26 years. Gaius Babidius Niger, freedman of Gaius, made this for his sister and wife.
EDCS-12400936.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two women, Babidia Psycharium and Lafria Philumina, with a dedication from Gaius Babidius Niger.

EDCS-12400937.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caldius Agricola made this for his son, Caldius Barbarus.
EDCS-12400937.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Caldius Agricola to his son, Caldius Barbarus.

EDCS-12400943.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sextus Livius Secundus made this for his most devoted and well-deserving father, Sextus Livius Modestus.
EDCS-12400943.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Sextus Livius Secundus to his father, Sextus Livius Modestus.

EDCS-12400946.translation: [She] lived 3 years. Maxima and Secundinus made this for their daughter.
EDCS-12400946.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a daughter, with a dedication from her parents, Maxima and Secundinus.

EDCS-12400950.translation: For Quartae Crescimiae Labrio, her co-slave with whom she lived for 13 years and 8 months, made this for her well-deserving companion.
EDCS-12400950.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quartae Crescimiae Labrio, dedicated by a fellow slave.

EDCS-12400955.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Puden(s) made this for his well-deserving wife, Tulliana Marcella.
EDCS-12400955.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Puden(s) to his wife, Tulliana Marcella.

EDCS-12400956.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Vipsanus Secundus made this for his holy wife, Veneria, and for himself, his sons, daughters, and grandchildren, and a place in every direction.
EDCS-12400956.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Lucius Vipsanus Secundus for his family, including himself, his wife Veneria, and descendants, possibly for a burial plot.

EDCS-12400960.translation: Under the consulship of Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus (for the 4th time) and Quintus Articuleius Paetus, the persons listed below, by command of the best and greatest prince, pledged properties so that the Ligures Baebiani may receive semi-annual interest from the purchase, and from his generosity, boys and girls may receive sustenance.
EDCS-12400960.summary: This extensive inscription details financial obligations and distributions for the Ligures Baebiani region during the consulship of Trajan and Quintus Articuleius Paetus, likely related to land ownership and welfare provisions.

EDCS-12400961.translation: Under the consulship of Titus Statilius Taurus and Manius Aemilius Lepidus, Tricunda, steward and manager of Bellona, willingly gives the lamp with its ornaments as a gift on the Ides of June in the Ligures Baebiani.
EDCS-12400961.summary: This inscription records a religious donation of a lamp and ornaments to the goddess Bellona by Tricunda, a steward and manager, during the consulship of Titus Statilius Taurus and Manius Aemilius Lepidus.

EDCS-12400962.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief pontiff, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the fatherland, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400962.summary: This is a public dedication to Emperor Gordian III, honoring his titles and dignities, passed by the local council (decurions).

EDCS-12400963.translation: To Furia Sabiniana Tranquillina, most sacred wife of our lord Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12400963.summary: This inscription honors Furia Sabiniana Tranquillina, wife of Emperor Gordian III, and was erected by public decree of the town council.

EDCS-12401002.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Lucius Trebonius Primus [built] the temple with a portico.
EDCS-12401002.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple and portico to Jupiter by Lucius Trebonius Primus.

EDCS-12401003.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Caius Umbrius Euphiletus gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-12401003.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter by Caius Umbrius Euphiletus.

EDCS-12401009.translation: Gaius Safronius Secundus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, aedile, councilor, curator of the Beneventum region of the Vetanus district, patron, built the triclinium at his own expense and gave a feast to the pagani on the occasion of the dedication, when Imperator Verus was consul for the third time.
EDCS-12401009.summary: This inscription records the construction of a triclinium and a feast by Gaius Safronius Secundus, a local magistrate and patron, during the consulship of Imperator Verus.

EDCS-12401021.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Caius Crispus Apro lived for two years and five months. Crispia Silene, his mother, [erected this] to her well-deserving son.
EDCS-12401021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Crispus Apro, who died at a very young age, set up by his mother Crispia Silene.

EDCS-12401033.translation: Hospitable stranger, stop and read what I am in the monument: Iuventia Hilara lie here. Lucius Albeius Hilarus erected the monument for his mother and for himself and his wife.
EDCS-12401033.summary: This is an epitaph for Iuventia Hilara, with the monument also intended for her husband Lucius Albeius Hilarus and for himself, indicating a family burial plot.

EDCS-12401034.translation: Titus Septimius Septimus, son of Publius, of the Stellatina tribe. Maxuma, daughter of Titus, wife of Postumia, daughter of Publius, Rufa. Hilara, freedwoman of Junia, wife.
EDCS-12401034.summary: This inscription seems to record the family members associated with a tomb or monument, listing two women who were wives, possibly of the same man or related individuals.

EDCS-12401044.translation: Sacred to Attis and Minerva Berecynthia. Concordia, freedwoman of the colony, January, cymbal player, in the second place for the criobolium performed for the Great Mother of the Gods, with Septimius Primitivus, augur and priest, handing over, and Servilia Varia and Terentia Elisviana, priestess of the XVvirate, leading, with Mammius Secundus preceding. These things by order of the Mother of the Gods, on the taurobolic altar, two [victims] with a heifer were burned, on the day before the Ides of April, when Modestus was consul for the second time and Probus was consul.
EDCS-12401044.summary: This inscription details a taurobolium ritual dedicated to Attis and Cybele, performed by Concordia and other officials, including priests and an augur, during the consulship of Modestus and Probus.

EDCS-12401045.translation: Sacred to Attis and Minerva Paracentia. Mummeia, daughter of Caius, Atticilla, priestess, for the taurobolium.
EDCS-12401045.summary: This inscription records a taurobolium rite dedicated to Attis and Minerva, performed by the priestess Mummeia Atticilla.

EDCS-12401046.translation: Sacred to Attis and Minerva Parachintia. Lucius Sontius Pineius Iustianus, Roman Equites, grandson, a man of principal standing, duovir and magistrate of the birthday of the colony, having performed all offices, and priest of the Great Mother of the Gods of Idaea, in his beginning, a taurobolium performed by himself, with Cosinia Celsina, his fellow priestess, handing over at the same time, with Flavius Liberalis, the chief public haruspex, preceding.
EDCS-12401046.summary: This inscription records a taurobolium initiated by Lucius Sontius Pineius Iustianus, a prominent local official and priest of Cybele, assisted by his co-priestess Cosinia Celsina and a haruspex.

EDCS-12401047.translation: Sacred to Attis and Minerva Paracentia. Terentia Flaviana, priestess in the second position and member of the XVvirali, dedicates this [monument] because of the taurabolium handed over by Servilia Varia, the first priestess.
EDCS-12401047.summary: This is a dedication to Attis and Minerva Paracentia by a priestess, likely related to a taurobolium ritual, from Benevento.

EDCS-12401048.translation: Sacred to Attis and Minerva Paracentia. Trebulana Iustina [dedicates this] with tympana because of the taurabolium handed over by Servilia Varia, the first priestess, on the 11th day before the Kalends of August.
EDCS-12401048.summary: This is a dedication possibly related to a taurobolium ritual, mentioning priestesses and a specific date, from Benevento.

EDCS-12401051.translation: To the Genius of the place and the divine spirit of Ceres. Concoridius, corn merchant of the colony.
EDCS-12401051.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius Loci and the Numina of Ceres by a local official, likely from Benevento.

EDCS-12401057.translation: To Silvanus. Marcus Cosinius Primitivus willingly paid his vow for the statue and base.
EDCS-12401057.summary: This is a votive offering to Silvanus by Marcus Cosinius Primitivus, including a statue and its base.

EDCS-12401062.translation: Publius Veidius Pollio, son of Publius, [dedicated this] Caesareum to Emperor Caesar Augustus and to the colony of Beneventum.
EDCS-12401062.summary: This is a dedication of a building or structure named 'Caesareum' to Emperor Augustus and the colony of Beneventum by Publius Veidius Pollio.

EDCS-12401065.translation: To Magnus and Invictus Gallienus Augustus, consul-designate for the 6th time, consul for the 7th time, by the pagus.
EDCS-12401065.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Gallienus by a rural administrative district (pagus), likely during his consulship.

EDCS-12401077.text_edition: \n Marco Caecilio Novatilliano curatori poetae et oratori inlustri viro clarissimo allecto inter consulares praesidi provinciae Moesiae quaestori Africae praefecto iuris dicundi Hispaniae citerioris et Calabriae splendidissimus ordo Beneventanorum privatim et publlicae sic patrocinio eius saepe defensi publice decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-12401077.translation: To Marcus Caecilius Novatillianus, curator of poets and orators, a most illustrious man, admitted among the consulars, governor of the province of Moesia, quaestor of Africa, prefect of justice in Hispania Citerior and Calabria, the most splendid order of Beneventans, both privately and publicly, because he often defended them with his patronage, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12401077.summary: This is an honorific inscription from the city of Beneventum to Marcus Caecilius Novatillianus, recognizing his distinguished career and patronage.

EDCS-12401078.text_edition: \n Marco Caecilio Novatilliano clarissimo viro oratori et poetae inlustri allecto inter consulares praesidi provinciae Moesiae superioris iuridico Hispaniae citerioris iuridico Apuliae et Calabriae praetori tribuno plebis quaestori provinciae Africae splendidissimus ordo Beneventanorum privatim et publice patrocinio eius saepe defensi publice decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-12401078.translation: To Marcus Caecilius Novatillianus, a most illustrious man, an orator and distinguished poet, admitted among the consulars, governor of the province of Upper Moesia, legal official of Hispania Citerior, legal official of Apulia and Calabria, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor of the province of Africa, the most splendid order of Beneventans, both privately and publicly, because he often defended them with his patronage, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12401078.summary: This is an honorific inscription from Beneventum to Marcus Caecilius Novatillianus, detailing his extensive administrative and legal career and his patronage.

EDCS-12401081.translation: The order of Beneventans, having put aside their fasces, decreed that this [statue or monument] be placed to Claudius Iulius Pacatus, a most illustrious man, consular governor of Campania, because of the fairness of his judgment and his patronage, now even as a private citizen.
EDCS-12401081.summary: This is an honorific inscription from Beneventum decreeing a monument to Claudius Iulius Pacatus for his fair judgment and patronage as a former consular governor.

EDCS-12401082.translation: To Adelfius Clodius Celsinus, a distinguished and most illustrious man, outstanding in his benevolence, authority, and justice, corrector of two regions, who surpassed the memorable examples of past judges with all virtues, the most splendid order of the city of Beneventum [dedicates this] to their most worthy patron.
EDCS-12401082.summary: This is an honorific inscription from Beneventum to Adelfius Clodius Celsinus, praising his virtues and recognizing him as a most worthy patron.

EDCS-12401089.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Rustia Sabina, who lived 7 years, 11 months, and 2 days. Gnaeus Marcius Rustius Rufinus, father, tribune of the VI cohort of vigilarii, and Salinatoria Augustina, mother, for their dearest daughter.
EDCS-12401089.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Rustia Sabina, set up by her parents.

EDCS-12401090.translation: For Marcus Marius Titius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Stellatina tribe, consul, legate of the I Minervia Pia Fidelis legion, curator of the colony of Augusta Agrippinensium, proconsul of the province of Sicily, curator of the Amerini, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor of the province of Macedonia, master of the squadrons of Roman knights, military tribune with laticlavus of the I Adiutricis Pia Fidelis legion, prefect of the four men judging disputes. Fides, with Hellas and Tertius, made [this] for [her] parents.
EDCS-12401090.summary: This is a funerary epitaph honoring a prominent Roman official, Marcus Marius Titius Rufus, detailing his extensive career, erected by his freedmen.

EDCS-12401094.translation: Sattius Crescens, a most distinguished man, curator of the public affairs of Benevento, from hidden places, dedicated [this] for the use and splendor of the baths.
EDCS-12401094.summary: This inscription records a donation made by Sattius Crescens, a curator of Benevento, for the city's baths.

EDCS-12401095.translation: The entire populace of Benevento decreed that this should be erected for Tanonius Marcellinus, a most distinguished man, of consular rank in Campania, their most worthy patron, on account of the outstanding benefits by which he long soothed the people's weariness.
EDCS-12401095.summary: This is a public dedication by the people of Benevento to their patron, Tanonius Marcellinus, for his services.

EDCS-12401108.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aelius Aeternalis, a soldier of the III praetorian cohort, century of Victor[ius?], received 9 units of [grain?] from Natalis, a stationarius of the province of Macedonia at Lychnidus, on the march [for the grain supply?]. Antonius Veturius, [his?] heir, made [this] for an excellent and deserving man.
EDCS-12401108.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelius Aeternalis, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, erected by his heir.

EDCS-12401109.translation: For Marcus Albius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Stellatina tribe, a standard-bearer in the XX legion. For Albia Hilara, freedwoman of Marcus, and for Curtia Chila, freedwoman of Publius. Epaphroditus and Celer, freedmen, saw to its construction.
EDCS-12401109.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a standard-bearer in the XX legion and two freedwomen, erected by their freedmen.

EDCS-12401112.translation: For Gaius Figilius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, in the VI legion. [Erected by?] Manlia, daughter of Publius. The heir saw to its construction.
EDCS-12401112.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Figilius Rufus, a soldier in the VI legion, erected by his heir.

EDCS-12401116.translation: Fufia Lychnis, freedwoman of [a woman], for herself and for Marcus Crassicius Castellus, son of Marcus, of the Stellatina tribe, a good man, in the XX legion; she ordered it to be made by her will.
EDCS-12401116.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fufia Lychnis and her husband Marcus Crassicius Castellus, who was in the XX legion, to be erected by Fufia's will.

EDCS-12401119.translation: For Lucius Labicius Celer, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, standard-bearer in the VI Ferrata legion.
EDCS-12401119.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Labicius Celer, a standard-bearer in the VI Ferrata legion.

EDCS-12401120.translation: For Lucius Laetilius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, military tribune of the XXII legion, awarded the hasta pura and the corona vallaris, aedile, quaestor, duovir with judicial power, prefect of the fabrica. Atteia Prisca, daughter of Quintus, his wife [erected this].
EDCS-12401120.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Laetilius Rufus, a military tribune, detailing his career and honors, erected by his wife.

EDCS-12401122.translation: From the will of Gaius Lisidius Ste(llatina), son of Marcus, of legion XXX, to his father Marcus Lisidius, son of Quintus, of the Stellatina tribe; to his mother Helvia, daughter of Lucius, Rufa; to his freedwoman Lisidia Prima, daughter of Gaius; to his freedwoman Lisidia Chresta, daughter of Gaius; to his freedman Gaius Lisidius Faustus, son of Gaius. This monument shall not follow its heir.
EDCS-12401122.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by inheritance, commemorating family members of Gaius Lisidius Stefullatina.

EDCS-12401123.translation: Gaius Luccius Sabinus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, from Beneventum, decurion, made this for himself while alive, and for his wife Ofillia Parata, and for his brother Lucius Verecundus, and for their descendants. He served in the urban cohort I, as an attendant to tribunes, as a 'secutor' optio, as an optio of the infirmary, as a 'carceris' singularis, as a 'beneficiarius' of a tribune, he was appointed by Annius Verus, prefect of the city, and 'tesserarius', optio, 'signifer', 'fisc(i) curator', optio of the records, 'cornicularius', 'beneficiarius' of a tribune, dismissed by the emperor Hadrian Augustus by Servianus III and Vibius Varus as consuls. He died on the 11th day before the Kalends of May, in the consulship of Erucius Clarus II. Frontage 20 feet, depth 20 feet.
EDCS-12401123.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating Gaius Luccius Sabinus, a soldier and official, detailing his career and death date, likely from the reign of Hadrian.

EDCS-12401124.translation: Marcus Nasellius Sabinus, son of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, prefect of cohort I of the Dalmatians, and Nasellius Vitalis, father, twice Augustalis, twice quinquennalis, for the common pagans of the pagus Lucullus, made a portico with an apparatorium and a crossroads from the ground up with their own money. And in perpetuity, on the 6th day before the Ides of June, on Sabinus's birthday, they decreed that 125 denarii be given annually to the pagans while feasting here, on the condition that they purify the pagus on the Nones of June and on the following days feast according to their custom. Likewise, on the 6th day before the Ides of June, on Sabinus's birthday, they shall feast here. If this is not done, then this place, as written above, along with the annual 125 denarii, shall pertain in perpetuity to the college of physicians and to their freedmen, so that they may feast here on the 6th day before the Ides of June, on Sabinus's birthday.
EDCS-12401124.summary: This inscription records the endowment by Marcus Nasellius Sabinus and his father of a public portico and crossroads, with provisions for annual feasts and a contingency for the college of physicians.

EDCS-12401125.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12401125.translation: To Gaius Oclatius Modestus, son of Gaius, of the Palatina tribe, augur, duumvir, quaestor, prefect of artisans in Rome, prefect of cohort II of the Pannonians, prefect of cohort III of the Ituraeans, military tribune of legion IV Scythica, curator of public affairs of Aecanum, and also honored for the care of the calendar of the public affairs of Canusium. From the divine Trajan Parthicus and from the emperor Hadrian Augustus, he presented this four-horse chariot with the image of the emperor Hadrian Augustus to the town council and his fellow citizens, without any request, from a thousand pounds of silver, with an additional 567 pounds and a sextans, and distributed one denarius per person to the people. He took care that it be dedicated publicly by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12401125.summary: This dedication commemorates Gaius Oclatius Modestus, a prominent local official, for his presentation of a four-horse chariot with the emperor's effigy, funded by silver and distributed to the populace.

EDCS-12401131.translation: Aulus Silanus, 'signifer' from Sicily, to his brother ID Silanus FT, 12 feet in front and 12 feet in depth, for piety.
EDCS-12401131.summary: This is a funerary marker for Aulus Silanus, a standard-bearer from Sicily, dedicated to his brother.

EDCS-12401138.translation: To Lucius Avidius, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, four-time magistrate, aedile; to his father Lucius Avidius, son of Lucius; to his mother Catia, daughter of Vibius.
EDCS-12401138.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Lucius Avidius and his parents by presumably a son with the same name.

EDCS-12401144.translation: To Numerius Afinius Hierax, son of Numerius, of the Palatina tribe, decurion of Beneventum, who lived 19 years and 31 days. Numerius Afinius Apulus and Afinia Acte, his most unhappy parents.
EDCS-12401144.summary: This epitaph records the premature death of Numerius Afinius Hierax, a young decurion from Beneventum, set up by his grieving parents.

EDCS-12401145.translation: To the shades of the departed. Trebonia Pelagia made this for her well-deserving husband Gaius Albanius Optatus, decurion of Beneventum, and for herself, and for Gaius Albanius... (incomplete).
EDCS-12401145.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Trebonia Pelagia for her husband Gaius Albanius Optatus, a decurion of Beneventum, and likely for herself and another family member.

EDCS-12401146.translation: To Verzobius Gaius Coelius Bassaeus Donatus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, 'praetor Cerialis', 'iure dicundo', quinquennalis, admitted to the order of decurions, Roman knight. Gaius Coelius Donatus and Bassaea Ianuaria, parents. The place was granted by decree of the decurions. Erected in the consulship of Praesens and Albinus, on the 16th day before the Kalends of December.
EDCS-12401146.summary: This epitaph honors Verzobius Gaius Coelius Bassaeus Donatus, a local official and Roman knight, set up by his parents, and includes the date of dedication.

EDCS-12401147.translation: To Navis Gaius Coelius Procilius Faustinus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, Bassaeus, 'praetor Cerialis', 'iure dicundo', quinquennalis, admitted to the order of decurions, Roman knight. Gaius Coelius Donatus and Bassaea Ianuaria, parents. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12401147.summary: This epitaph honors Navis Gaius Coelius Procilius Faustinus, a local official and Roman knight, set up by his parents, likely a sibling or relative of the individual in EDCS-12401146.

EDCS-12401148.translation: To Marcus Carfanius, son of Marcus, of the Stellatina tribe, decurion; from Carfania Tertia, freedwoman of Marcus, and Maelia Secunda, freedwoman of Quintus.
EDCS-12401148.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Marcus Carfanius, son of Marcus, a decurion, by two women, Carfania Tertia and Maelia Secunda.

EDCS-12401151.translation: Beneventum, prosperous. Marcus Flavius Marcellus Rutilius dedicated this for the honor of the duovirate. Place granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12401151.summary: This dedication in Beneventum by Marcus Flavius Marcellus Rutilius celebrates his honor of the duovirate and was approved by the local council.

EDCS-12401152.translation: To Marcus Gavius Sabinus, son of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, scribe of the aediles, who exercised judicial authority, prefect of the engineers; for himself and for Nasennia Iusta, daughter of Marcus, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-12401152.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Gavius Sabinus, who held various civic and administrative positions, and his wife Nasennia Iusta.

EDCS-12401154.translation: To Lucius Lollius Suavius, son of Lucius, aedile and prefect of the engineers. Lucius Lollius Orio, his father, a distinguished member of the Augustales and Claudialis orders, dedicated this for himself and his wife, Hirria Tertulla.
EDCS-12401154.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Lollius Orio to his deceased son, Lucius Lollius Suavius, who was an aedile and prefect of the engineers.

EDCS-12401157.translation: To Gaius Munatius Mocilius, a distinguished member of the Augustales; to Herennia Bassa, daughter of Tiberius, wife of Mocilius; to Marcus Gavius Bassus, son of Marcus, of the Stellatina tribe, aedile.
EDCS-12401157.summary: This is a complex funerary dedication honoring multiple individuals: Gaius Munatius Mocilius, his wife Herennia Bassa, and Marcus Gavius Bassus, an aedile.

EDCS-12401160.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Marcus Rutilius Aelianus, decurion of Beneventum, a teacher of grammar, who lived 75 years. Rutilia Primitiva, to her husband, with whom she lived 18 years.
EDCS-12401160.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating Marcus Rutilius Aelianus, a respected decurion and teacher, and his marriage to Rutilia Primitiva.

EDCS-12401161.translation: To Lucius Staius Scrateius Manilianus, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, quinquennial prefect, who exercised judicial authority, son of a Roman equestrian, chief physician of Beneventum. For the honor of the Ceriatitas, he was the first to distribute tokens, among which he gave gold, silver, bronze, clothing, lentils, and other things to the people. Lucius Staius Rutilius Manilius, his father.
EDCS-12401161.summary: This inscription details the benefactions of Lucius Staius Scrateius Manilianus, a prominent citizen and physician, who funded public distributions, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-12401162.translation: Marcus Tanonius Firmianus, aedile, who exercised judicial authority, made this base with a scale and bronze weights from his own funds.
EDCS-12401162.summary: This inscription records a public building project by Marcus Tanonius Firmianus, who as aedile and official with judicial power funded the construction of a base with weighing equipment.

EDCS-12401164.translation: Gaius Vibius Stephanus, aedile of Beneventum, made this for himself while alive. Friends, farewell.
EDCS-12401164.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Gaius Vibius Stephanus, an aedile of Beneventum, to himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-12401165.translation: To Gaius Umbrio Iustus, his most just great-grandson, decurion of Beneventum, who lived 30 years. Servilia Varia, mother.
EDCS-12401165.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Servilia Varia to her great-grandson, Gaius Umbrio Iustus, a decurion of Beneventum.

EDCS-12401168.translation: To Veturio, son of Quintus, of the Stellatina tribe, decurion, and Veturia Hospita; Celadus Augustalis, having been made quinquennalis by decree of the decurions free of charge, made this for his most loving parents.
EDCS-12401168.summary: A funerary dedication by Celadus Augustalis for his parents, Veturio and Veturia Hospita, who were local officials.

EDCS-12401187.translation: During the consulship of Emperors Licinius Valerianus Augustus for the fourth time and Licinius Gallienus Augustus for the third time, on the Ides of March. Since the youths who cultivate the god Hercules have undertaken a project, the elders have decided to co-opt a patron, believing that we will achieve greater benefits after our time; because we have perceived his great dignity in the past, and therefore we co-opt Rutilius Viator as patron, along with those listed below: the clarissimi viri Nonius Gratilianus and Egnatius Sattianus, and many splendid Roman equestrians and fellow citizens of that Viator, who have inspired us by their dignity and merits, to whom we offer (this) through Numisius Numisianus Attic(us?) and their ancestors.
EDCS-12401187.summary: A decree from a youth cult of Hercules to co-opt Rutilius Viator and others as patrons, praising their past merits and hoping for future benefits, likely dating to the reign of Valerian and Gallienus (253-260 AD).

EDCS-12401188.translation: To Vesedius Rufinus, vir perfectissimus, advocate of the treasury, supreme judge, promoted to the sacred office, most splendid patron of the city of Beneventum and of the Puteolans, patron of the studiorum for the fourth time; Martesius Verzobianus and Martesius of the Palladianorum and I[...]
EDCS-12401188.summary: An honorary inscription for Vesedius Rufinus, a high-ranking official and patron of several cities and collegia, likely dating to the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-12401189.translation: To Nebulus, to Gaius Vesedius Rufinus, a most perfect and outstanding man, son of Vesedius Justus, an illustrious and principal man of the Beneventanate city, patron, [---] the Palladian studium.
EDCS-12401189.summary: An honorary inscription for Gaius Vesedius Rufinus, son of Vesedius Justus, and patron of the Palladian studium, likely dating to the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-12401191.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12401191.translation: To Verzobius, to Gaius Umbrius Eudratus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, patron of the colony of Beneventum, a most generous and most honorable man, because he built the Canopus from the ground up at his own expense; the college of the Martenses infraforani set this up to their patron for his most outstanding generosity and honor.
EDCS-12401191.summary: A dedication by the collegium Martensium infraforanum to their patron Gaius Umbrius Eudratus, honoring him for building a Canopus at his own expense, likely dating to the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-12401197.translation: To Lucius Caecius Hilarus, freedman of Lucius, Augustalis; to Lucius Caecius Hermerotus, freedman of Lucii, Augustalis; to Lucius Caecius Felix, freedman of Lucii, Augustalis.
EDCS-12401197.summary: A funerary inscription listing three individuals, Lucius Caecius Hilarus, Lucius Caecius Hermerotus, and Lucius Caecius Felix, all Augustales, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-12401198.translation: To Gaius Calpurnius Pancarpus, Augustalis; to Calpurnia Faustus, freedwoman of Gaius; to Lucius Visellius Lygdamus, freedman of Lucius, son of Calpurnia Norma; and for themselves.
EDCS-12401198.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Calpurnius Pancarpus (Augustalis), Calpurnia Faustus, Lucius Visellius Lygdamus, and Calpurnia Norma, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-12401203.translation: Quintus Opimius Celadus, freedman of Quintus, Augustalis, for himself and for Quintus Opimius Rufus, son of Quintus, who lived 10 years, for his wife Papinia Anthi, for his father Lucius Rufrius Pamphilus, and for his mother Rufria Laidis.
EDCS-12401203.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Opimius Celadus (Augustalis) and his family, including his son Quintus Opimius Rufus who died at age 10, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-12401206.translation: Gaius (?) [...] Augustalis [...] spouse (?) [...] to Lucius Lollius Primigenius, freedman of Lucius, to Caesius Prisca, to Aulus Consius Aper.
EDCS-12401206.summary: A funerary inscription mentioning an Augustalis and several other individuals, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-12401207.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Acilia Thallia made this for her dearest and well-deserving husband, Lucius Valerius Tichus, Claudialis, Augustalis of Beneventum.
EDCS-12401207.summary: A funerary dedication by Acilia Thallia to her husband, Lucius Valerius Tichus, who was an Augustalis and Claudialis of Beneventum, likely dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-12401208.translation: Titus Vedianius Fructus, freedman of Titus, Augustalis, made this monument for himself and for Munatia Prisca, his deserving wife, willingly and for Asinia Tarentina, freedwoman of Gaius, and for Titus Vedianius Primigenius, freedman of Titus, and for Haminta, and for Saturninus, and for Dexter. In front, 14 feet; in the field, 14 feet.
EDCS-12401208.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Titus Vedianius Fructus for himself and his family, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE, indicating the dimensions of his plot.

EDCS-12401210.translation: Helenus, freedman of Quintus, Augustalis, made this for Quintus Verginus Eros, Augustalis, and for his brother Quintus Verginus Albanus.
EDCS-12401210.summary: A funerary monument erected by a freedman for his patron and his patron's brother, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-12401211.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aulus Vibbius Claudialis, Augustalis, curator for one day's festival, and his sons Aulis Vibbis Iustinus, Iustianus, and Ianuarius, erected this for their deserving father.
EDCS-12401211.summary: A funerary dedication to Aulus Vibbius Claudialis, an Augustalis and official for a festival, set up by his sons, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401216.translation: Gnaeus Rustius Festivus, freedman of Gnaeus, Mercurialis and merchant, made this monument for himself and for Rustia Ma[], freedwoman of Gnaeus, his fellow freedwoman; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-12401216.summary: A funerary monument established by Gnaeus Rustius Festivus for himself and his fellow freedwoman, Rustia Ma[], likely in the 1st-2nd century CE, with a clause preventing inheritance.

EDCS-12401225.translation: For Marcus Lucilius Dioclis, freedman of Marcus, the piper and craftsman of organs, and for his freedmen and freedwomen, by the authority of Marcus Ofillius Tertius.
EDCS-12401225.summary: A dedication to Marcus Lucilius Dioclis, a musician and organ maker, and his freedmen and freedwomen, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-12401227.translation: Publius Marcius Philodamus, freedman of Publius, the builder, for himself and his family. Iucunda, his darling, is buried here.
EDCS-12401227.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Marcius Philodamus, a builder, and his pet Iucunda, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-12401235.translation: Publius Aelius Venerianus placed this cinerary urn for himself and for his Felicitas and completed the tribunal with the permission of the pontiffs.
EDCS-12401235.summary: A dedication by Publius Aelius Venerianus for himself and Felicitas, also mentioning the completion of a tribunal, possibly in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-12401236.translation: For Aelia Procula, daughter of Titus, who lived 16 years. Aelius Inclutus and Avoleia Restituta, his mother's daughter, most miserable parents, [set this up] for their daughter.
EDCS-12401236.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelia Procula, who died at 16, erected by her parents, likely in the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-12401239.translation: To the deserving freedmen ... who were led ... in which house ... patron ... freedwoman ... because they saw ...
EDCS-12401239.summary: A fragmented inscription, likely a funerary dedication to freedmen by their patron or a freedwoman, from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401240.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Afinia Asiaticathe deserving.
EDCS-12401240.summary: A funerary dedication to Afinia Asiatica, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401244.translation: Alfia Fausta, freedwoman of a woman, [erected this] for herself and for Gaius Rubellinus Iucundus.
EDCS-12401244.summary: A funerary monument set up by a freedwoman for herself and another individual.

EDCS-12401253.translation: Publius Areius Rufus, son of Publius, of the Stellatina tribe. Publius Areius [---], of the Stellatina tribe [---].
EDCS-12401253.summary: Part of a funerary inscription, possibly recording a father and son, with names and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-12401267.translation: Gaius Badius Iucundus, freedman of Gaius, and his wife Arria Rufa, freedwoman of a woman, and for Publius Cornelius Diocles, freedman of Publius, and [---] for 12 years [---].
EDCS-12401267.summary: A funerary inscription dedicating a monument to several individuals including freedmen and possibly indicating years of life for one.

EDCS-12401272.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. His most unhappy mother placed [this] for Beneventanus, who lived VI years and XI days, and for Aquita.
EDCS-12401272.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother for her deceased child, Beneventanus, and another person named Aquita, specifying the child's age.

EDCS-12401285.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Cascellius Secundus made [this] for his father Marcus Cascellius Secundus, for his mother Cascellia Edone, for his brother Marcus Cascellius Crescentes, for his son Marcus Cascellius Secundus, who lived XIV years, for his son Marcus Cascellius Crescentes, who lived VI months and VI days, and for his son Marcus Cascellius Secundus, who lived I year and X months.
EDCS-12401285.summary: A funerary monument set up by Marcus Cascellius Secundus, commemorating his father, mother, brother, and three sons with their ages at death.

EDCS-12401287.translation: For Aulus Castricius Achilleus, his father, and for Culcia Isidora, his mother. Castricia Felicissima [erected this] for her dearest and incomparable parents.
EDCS-12401287.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a daughter to her parents, identifying the dedicant and the deceased.

EDCS-12401288.translation: For Castricia Isidora, dearest daughter, who lived XIV years and VIII months. Castricia Felicissima made [this] for [her] well-deserving [daughter].
EDCS-12401288.summary: A funerary dedication by Castricia Felicissima for her deceased daughter, specifying the daughter's age and the relationship.

EDCS-12401293.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Publius Rutilius Crescentianus made [this] for his deserving wife, Ceia Pelagia, who lived XVIII years, VIII months, and XXIII days.
EDCS-12401293.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ceia Pelagia, set up by her husband, stating her age at death.

EDCS-12401297.translation: For Claudia Capreola, who lived VII years, XI months, and VI days. Claudius Fortunatianus, father [erected this].
EDCS-12401297.summary: A funerary inscription for Claudia Capreola, set up by her father, recording her age at death.

EDCS-12401316.translation: Caius Herennius Rufus, son of Caius, of the Stellatina tribe, made [this] for his mother, Didia, daughter of Decimus, for himself and his family.
EDCS-12401316.summary: A funerary dedication by a son for his mother, also including himself and his family, with tribal affiliation noted.

EDCS-12401323.translation: To Erullia Primula, most unhappy mother.
EDCS-12401323.summary: This inscription is a tombstone epitaph dedicated to Erullia Primula by her mother, mourning her daughter.

EDCS-12401326.translation: Firmus made this for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-12401326.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Firmus for himself and his family during his lifetime.

EDCS-12401330.translation: Lucius Freganius Saturus, freedman of Lucius, and his freedwoman Fregania Helena, freedwoman of Lucius, and Fregania Diodora, freedwoman of Gaius, made this for themselves and their kin. In the front 12 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-12401330.summary: This is a funerary monument or tomb boundary inscription set up by freedmen for themselves and their kin, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE, and specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-12401342.translation: You who walk carefree in mind, traveler, and turn your gaze below, if you ask who I was, I am ashes and burnt embers. Before my bitter end, I was Helvia Prima. I enjoyed my husband Scrateius Cadmus, and we lived with a compatible and equal spirit. Now I am given to Dis, to remain for a long age, drawn down by fated fire and Stygian water.
EDCS-12401342.summary: This is a poetic epitaph on a tombstone for Helvia Prima, recounting her happy marriage and her descent into the underworld.

EDCS-12401364.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Licinia Harmona, daughter of Lucius, mother.
EDCS-12401364.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Licinia Harmona, identifying her by her father and her role as a mother.

EDCS-12401377.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Gaius Mamius Primigenius. Mariius Ianuaria made this for her well-deserving husband, with whom she lived for 36 years and 3 months.
EDCS-12401377.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Mamius Primigenius and is dedicated by his wife, Mariius Ianuaria, who specifies their marriage duration.

EDCS-12401380.translation: Titus Mario Chrestus, freedman of Titus, and Sextilia Chresta, freedwoman of Sextus, made this for their freedmen and freedwomen while alive.
EDCS-12401380.summary: This inscription records freedmen setting up a monument for their own freedmen and freedwomen during their lifetime.

EDCS-12401390.translation: ...nia, freedwoman of the woman, Mnemosyne, made this for her husband Marcus Salvius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-12401390.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Mnemosyne, a freedwoman, erected a monument for her husband, Marcus Salvius.

EDCS-12401392.translation: Caius Modius Felix and his son Caius Modius Felix, and his son Naturale Silvinus Cocceius, and his freedman Modius Renato, and his wife Sattia Silva, made this for the above-written while alive. Also for Caesias Veneria, relative.
EDCS-12401392.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Caius Modius Felix for himself, his sons, his freedman, his wife, and a relative, all while alive.

EDCS-12401400.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Numerius Fufius Modestus and Munnia Ianuaria made this for their most dutiful son, Marcus Munnius Ianuarius, and also for their son Numerius Fufius Modestus.
EDCS-12401400.summary: This funerary inscription is a dedication by parents to their deceased sons, Marcus Munnius Ianuarius and Numerius Fufius Modestus.

EDCS-12401401.translation: Lucius Naevius Herma, freedman of Lucius, for himself and for Cominia Lalage.
EDCS-12401401.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Naevius Herma for himself and Cominia Lalage, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-12401408.translation: Gaius Nonius Chilo, freedman of Gaius, for himself and for Marcus Postumius Dasius, freedman of Marcus, his brother, by the arbitration of Gaius Luccius Maximus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-12401408.summary: This inscription records a property transaction or division involving Gaius Nonius Chilo and Marcus Postumius Dasius, his brother, with Gaius Luccius Maximus acting as arbitrator.

EDCS-12401422.translation: Publius Oppius Pamphilus (?), freedman of Aulus.
EDCS-12401422.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Publius Oppius Pamphilus, a freedman.

EDCS-12401434.translation: [...]us, son of Marcus, Marcus Plau[tius(?) ...]. [...] Varia F [...]. [...] after his death.
EDCS-12401434.summary: This is a highly fragmented funerary inscription, likely for Marcus Plautius, mentioning a posthumous dedication.

EDCS-12401445.translation: Lucius Pupius Primigenius, a rope-maker, for himself and for Trebulana Modesta, and for Carpus, and for Lucius Pupius Hermetus, Expectatus.
EDCS-12401445.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Pupius Primigenius for himself and several other individuals, likely including family members or clients.

EDCS-12401447.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Quintillius Adiuutor lived for 5 years, 2 months, and 17 days. Bellicia Quintilia, his mother.
EDCS-12401447.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Quintillius Adiuutor, recording his age at death and dedicated by his mother.

EDCS-12401455.translation: [...] to Modesta, his wife, Gaius Sabidius Priscanus, with whom he lived for 30 years.
EDCS-12401455.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Modesta, wife of Gaius Sabidius Priscanus, recording their years of marriage.

EDCS-12401467.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Septicia Primilla. Septicius Primigenius, most unhappy father.
EDCS-12401467.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Septicia Primilla, dedicated by her grieving father, Septicius Primigenius.

EDCS-12401471.translation: [...] to Liconius Secundinus, for his incomparable and well-deserving wife, he made this.
EDCS-12401471.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Liconius Secundinus for his highly esteemed wife.

EDCS-12401478.translation: For Lucius Stenius An[n]ius Africanus, dearest infant, who lived 11 years, 8 months, and 11 days. The most unhappy parents, condemned by his loss to perpetual darkness and daily miserable wailing.
EDCS-12401478.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for the infant Lucius Stenius Annius Africanus, recording his age at death and expressing the profound grief of his parents.

EDCS-12401488.translation: To Tarquinia Modesta, who lived 15 years, 6 months, and 6 days. Modestus and Lasciva, her parents, and Lucentius, her husband, who lived 6 months and 7 days. For she was of exceptional beauty and wonderful grace, and had a spirit worthy of her body. Farewell, sweet one.
EDCS-12401488.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tarquinia Modesta, recording her age and praising her beauty and spirit.

EDCS-12401504.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Trebonia Ianuaria. Quintus Titacius Ephebus for his deserving wife, with whom he lived 40 years, and Trebonius Celer, his son, for his most dutiful mother, for himself, his freedmen and freedwomen, and his descendants, they made this.
EDCS-12401504.summary: A funerary monument erected by a husband and son for Trebonia Ianuaria, a devoted wife and mother, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12401510.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Turranius Aper, a most sweet man, who lived 40 years. Tadia Ursa for her most sweet husband, with whom she lived 17 years. [---] Tigrinus.
EDCS-12401510.summary: A funerary epitaph for Turranius Aper, commemorating his life and his relationship with his wife, Tadia Ursa.

EDCS-12401516.translation: Decimus Valerius Menecrates, freedman of Decimus, [for himself] and for Oppia Flora, freedwoman of Caius, and Aebutia Helena.
EDCS-12401516.summary: An epitaph recording the dedication of a tomb for several individuals, including freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-12401517.translation: Titus Valerius Pamphilus, freedman of Titus, for himself and his family, 13 feet in front, 13 feet in the field. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-12401517.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Valerius Pamphilus and his family, specifying the dimensions of the burial plot and its perpetual nature.

EDCS-12401518.translation: Valeria Polynice and Vettius Puteolanus, while alive, made this for themselves and their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-12401518.summary: A funerary monument established by two individuals, Valeria Polynice and Vettius Puteolanus, for themselves and their dependents.

EDCS-12401527.translation: Vesueida Tryphaenis, freedwoman of Quintus, made this for herself and for Quintus Vesuedius Galus, son of Quintus, her patron. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-12401527.summary: A funerary inscription by a freedwoman, Tryphaenis, for herself and her patron, Quintus Vesuedius Galus, stipulating that the plot is not inheritable.

EDCS-12401531.translation: Vibbius Hilarus, freedman of Aulus, made this [for himself] while alive, for Julia Lucinina, freedwoman of Caius. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-12401531.summary: A funerary dedication made by a living freedman, Vibbius Hilarus, for himself and a freedwoman, Julia Lucinina, ensuring the plot's perpetuity.

EDCS-12401537.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Vibia Prima. Vibia Secunda, her patroness, made this for her deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-12401537.summary: A funerary epitaph where a patroness, Vibia Secunda, honors her deserving freedwoman, Vibia Prima.

EDCS-12401538.translation: [---] Viciria Clienta took care to have this made.
EDCS-12401538.summary: An inscription recording that Viciria Clienta arranged for the construction or erection of a monument.

EDCS-12401543.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Maurus, son of Gaius Umbrius Primus, who lived 20 years and 7 months. Umbria Felicitas, mother, made this for her most beloved son, who deserved well.
EDCS-12401543.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maurus, set up by his mother, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12401588.translation: For Aurelia Severina, my dearest wife, a new convert, who lived with me 18 years. I, Aurelius Sabinianus, her husband, made this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-12401588.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Severina, dedicated by her husband, likely from the early Christian period given the term 'neofyt<y=E>tae'.

EDCS-12401591.translation: To the Holy Spirit. Lucretia Prima made this for Sextilius Leon, with whom she lived 33 years, 9 months, and 12 days, for her incomparable husband, who deserved well.
EDCS-12401591.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextilius Leon, dedicated by his wife, Lucretia Prima, likely from the Christian period.

EDCS-12401603.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Vereatio Luci filio Rufo Stellatina Benevento |(centurioni) principi legionis XXII Primigeniae Lucius Veratius Annianus patri bene merenti\n
EDCS-12401603.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Vereatius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, a principal centurion of the legion XXII Primigenia. Lucius Veratius Annianus made this for his deserving father.
EDCS-12401603.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Vereatius Rufus, a centurion, dedicated by his son, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12401606.translation: For Gaius Mettius Rufus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, Foronianus. Caelia Maxima, daughter of Publius, his mother, made this for her son, her husband, and her stepson, while alive. The fourth day before the Kalends of September.
EDCS-12401606.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her son, husband, and stepson, dated to the fourth day before the Kalends of September in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12401616.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Pullidius Phoebianus. Seppia Justinia made this for her incomparable wife, with whom she lived 42 years, who deserved well. Farewell, my beloved. Farewell, deceiver.
EDCS-12401616.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Pullidius Phoebianus, dedicated by his wife, Seppia Justinia, with a Greek farewell and a Latin valediction, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12401617.translation: who lived 25 years and [3] months. Quintus Saccidius Primus made this for his most beloved brother, Gaius Saccidius Primus, and for his parents, Quintus Saccidius Epitincanus and Saccidia Fortunata, who deserved well.
EDCS-12401617.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Saccidius Primus and his parents, dedicated by his brother, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12401622.translation: For the priestess of Juno Regina, Licinia Liciniana, daughter of Licinianus the tribune. Attia Attiane, her mother, [erected this].
EDCS-12401622.summary: An honorary or funerary inscription for a priestess of Juno Regina, dedicated by her mother, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12401626.text_edition: \n Salvio Curtio Luci filio Stellatina legionis XXX et Curtiae uxori Curtia Salvi filia Polla vixit nnos XII\n
EDCS-12401626.translation: For Salvius Curtius, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe, from the legion XXX, and for Curtia Polla, his wife, daughter of Salvius. She lived 12 years.
EDCS-12401626.summary: A funerary epitaph for Salvius Curtius and his wife Curtia Polla, who died at the age of 12, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12401630.translation: For Marcus Mamidius Modestus. Afinia Liberalis placed this for her incomparable husband, with whom she lived 10 years and 7 days, who deserved well.
EDCS-12401630.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Mamidius Modestus, dedicated by his wife, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-12401632.translation: Gaius Aufidius, son of Gaius, and Gaius Fufidius, son of Gaius Vindex, as members of the board of four officials with quinquennial authority, saw to the construction of a bridge by decree of the Senate, and they also approved it. It is established that it cost 12,000 sesterces.
EDCS-12401632.summary: This inscription records the construction and approval of a bridge by two high-ranking local magistrates, likely dating to the early Imperial period, with a stated cost.

EDCS-12401642.translation: Quintus Caucius, son of Aulus, of the Stellatina tribe, and Marcus Caucius, son of Quintus, had this made for their father.
