EDCS-18200132.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucretius Eutychus lived 40 years. Potentina placed this for her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-18200132.summary: A funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for Lucretius Eutychus, who lived 40 years, erected by his wife Potentina.

EDCS-18200133.translation: Tiberius Iulius Euander lived 35 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-18200133.summary: A tombstone recording the life of Tiberius Iulius Euander, who lived 35 years and is buried here.

EDCS-18200134.translation: Caius Iunius Felix lived 50 years. He is buried here. Caius Iunius made this for his father, worthy of [---].
EDCS-18200134.summary: A tombstone for Caius Iunius Felix, who lived 50 years, with a dedication from his son Caius Iunius to his father.

EDCS-18200135.translation: Phiale lived 16 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-18200135.summary: A tombstone for Phiale, who lived 16 years and is buried here.

EDCS-18200136.translation: Marcus Samiarius Valens lived 35 years. He is buried here. Calvia Veneria [made this] for her husband, who deserved well. [---]
EDCS-18200136.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Samiarius Valens, who lived 35 years, erected by his wife Calvia Veneria.

EDCS-18200137.translation: Caius Tigidius Barbarus Pineses lived 50 years.
EDCS-18200137.summary: This inscription records that Caius Tigidius Barbarus Pineses lived 50 years.

EDCS-18200140.translation: To the departed spirits. Ulpia Melaene placed this for her well-deserving father, Fortunatus, slave of our Caesar, a wolf-keeper, who lived [---] years. [---]
EDCS-18200140.summary: A funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld for Fortunatus, slave of Caesar and a wolf-keeper, erected by his daughter Ulpia Melaene.

EDCS-18200147.translation: To Tumius, son of Publius, of the Oufentina tribe, former duumvir and aedile; to Maxima, daughter of Aulus.
EDCS-18200147.summary: This funerary inscription honors two individuals, likely a husband and wife or family members, who held local magistracies.

EDCS-18200148.translation: Dedicated to the sacred Manes. Arrecina Proba lived 19 years. Her father made this from his own means for his well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-18200148.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arrecina Proba, recording her age at death and erected by her father.

EDCS-18200149.translation: To the Manes. Flavia Hedone lived 30 years. Marcus Arrecinus Anteros made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-18200149.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of Flavia Hedone and was made by her husband, Marcus Arrecinus Anteros.

EDCS-18200150.translation: Sacred to the Manes. Iulia Eutaxia had this made for her most devoted husband, Clatius Faustillus, who lived... years.
EDCS-18200150.summary: This funerary inscription, largely restored, indicates that Iulia Eutaxia commissioned a monument for her deceased husband.

EDCS-18200154.translation: She lives. Aulus Caecilius [name] nomenclator [official title] for Iuxsta; Sextia, farewell and be well to Cordia, his wife.
EDCS-18200154.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Sextia Cordia, wife of Aulus Caecilius, who was a nomenclator, with a farewell from his side.

EDCS-18200155.translation: Here lie the ashes. To Lucius Baebius Cosmus, freedman of Lucius. To Baebia Dorcas, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-18200155.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two freedmen, Lucius Baebius Cosmus and Baebia Dorcas.

EDCS-18200156.translation: Decimia Zosima, freedwoman of the woman. Publius Decimius Eros, freedman of the freedman.
EDCS-18200156.summary: This funerary inscription records the names of two freedpersons, one male and one female, possibly related.

EDCS-18200157.translation: [Names] to Propinquus, son of Aulus; [names] to Legitimus, son of Aulus; [names] to Emporus, son of Aulus; [names] to Modesta; [names] to Hyacinthus. Here lie.
EDCS-18200157.summary: This funerary inscription, with many restored names, lists several individuals who are buried here, possibly family members.

EDCS-18200158.translation: To Gaius Vettius Drimachus, freedman of the woman. To Hedylium, freedwoman of Aulus.
EDCS-18200158.summary: This funerary inscription records the names of two freedpersons, Hedylium and Gaius Vettius Drimachus.

EDCS-18200162.translation: Marcus Satrius, son of Marcus [name]. Gnaeus Pompeius [name]. As duumvirs for the second time, they saw to the repair of the pool, by decision of the decurions.
EDCS-18200162.summary: This inscription records the decision by two duumvirs, Marcus Satrius and Gnaeus Pompeius, to repair a pool, approved by the town council.

EDCS-18200163.translation: To Maxentius, who lived more or less 33 years; his brothers made this for their well-deserving brother.
EDCS-18200163.summary: This is a funerary monument for Maxentius, erected by his brothers.

EDCS-18200211.text_edition: \n Titus Tremelius Titi libertus Antiochus aedem Dianae et aram de lapide quadrato aedificandas et opere tectorio poliendas et signum Dianae faciundum statuendum dedicandum de sua pecunia curavit votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-18200211.translation: Titus Tremelius Antiochus, freedman of Titus, paid for the construction of a temple to Diana and an altar of squared stone, for the plasterwork to be finished, for a statue of Diana to be made, set up, and dedicated, all from his own funds, and willingly fulfilled his vow as was deserved.
EDCS-18200211.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple and altar to Diana, along with a statue, by Titus Tremelius Antiochus.

EDCS-18200221.translation: Octavia Plecusa made this for her fellow freedman Gaius Octavius Hermerotus, freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-18200221.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Octavia Plecusa to her fellow freedman, Gaius Octavius Hermerotus.

EDCS-18200229.translation: Quintus Statius and Quintus Sergius, quaestors.
EDCS-18200229.summary: This inscription records the names of Quintus Statius and Quintus Sergius, who held the office of quaestor.

EDCS-18200239.translation: To the departed spirits. Annius Nebius made this for his most sweet son, Annius Nebius, who lived 35 years, a well-deserving one.
EDCS-18200239.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Annius Nebius for his son, also named Annius Nebius.

EDCS-18200240.translation: For the well-deserving Bebius Rufinianus, whose memory was set up by Apridius, well-deserving.
EDCS-18200240.summary: This inscription records the erection of a memorial for Bebius Rufinianus by Apridius.

EDCS-18200241.translation: To the departed spirits. Celer made this memorial for Fortunata, his slave.
EDCS-18200241.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Celer for his slave Fortunata.

EDCS-18200242.translation: To the departed spirits. For Celerina, daughter of Celer.
EDCS-18200242.summary: This is a funerary monument for Celerina, daughter of Celer.

EDCS-18200243.translation: To the departed spirits. Trebatia Sabina made this for Cerrinia Veneria, her mother, a well-deserving one.
EDCS-18200243.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Trebatia Sabina for her mother, Cerrinia Veneria.

EDCS-18200245.translation: To the departed spirits. Cerrinia Veneria made this for her well-deserving husband, [Tre]batius Sabinus.
EDCS-18200245.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Cerrinia Veneria for her husband, Trebatius Sabinus.

EDCS-18200246.translation: To Titius Tatius, son of Gaius, to Silvester Trebatius.
EDCS-18200246.summary: This is a dedication to Titius Tatius and Silvester Trebatius, likely on a monument of some kind.

EDCS-18200247.translation: Liania Runela with her children, well deserving.
EDCS-18200247.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial set up by Liania Runela for her deserving children.

EDCS-18200248.translation: To the divine shades. Lusia Quieta placed this for her well-deserving husband, Agrius Rufilianus.
EDCS-18200248.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Lusia Quieta for her husband Agrius Rufilianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18200249.translation: To the divine shades. Tiberius Claudius Quintianus made this for his well-deserving father, Tiberius Claudius Quintianus.
EDCS-18200249.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a son for his father, both named Tiberius Claudius Quintianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18200251.translation: Marcus Antonius Rufus, son of Marcus, is buried here.
EDCS-18200251.summary: This is a simple tombstone inscription for Marcus Antonius Rufus, son of Marcus.

EDCS-18200252.translation: Camurtia Chresime lived 7 years and is buried here.
EDCS-18200252.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the short life of Camurtia Chresime, who lived for 7 years.

EDCS-18200253.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating that Faustius lived for 20 years, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-18200255.translation: Publius Laterius Niger, son of Publius, lived 5 years and 3 months and is buried here.
EDCS-18200255.summary: This tombstone records the life of Publius Laterius Niger, son of Publius, who died at the age of 5 years and 3 months.

EDCS-18200256.translation: Gaius Octavius Bathyllus, freedman of Gaius, lived 45 years and is buried here.
EDCS-18200256.summary: This tombstone inscription records the life of Gaius Octavius Bathyllus, a freedman, who lived for 45 years.

EDCS-18200257.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a name, Plato Mercurialis, on a monument.

EDCS-18200258.translation: To his deserving wife, Camuria Ianu[aria(?)]. Porcius Hyg[inus(?)] made this for [her]. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-18200258.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Camuria Ianu[aria(?)], set up by her husband Porcius Hyg[inus(?)], likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-18200259.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for a person named [?]us Primus, son of [?]o, and possibly related to Rufio.

EDCS-18200260.translation: Valeria Europa, freedwoman of Lucius, lived 80 years. She rests here.
EDCS-18200260.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valeria Europa, who lived 80 years, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-18200262.translation: Basseus Primus made this for his most sweet grandson, Basseus Zoticus, who lived 13 years, 10 months, and 12 days, to him well-deserving.
EDCS-18200262.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Basseus Zoticus, who died at the age of 13 years, 10 months, and 12 days, set up by his grandfather Basseus Primus.

EDCS-18200263.translation: Graeceia, daughter of Lucius, while alive.
EDCS-18200263.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Graeceia, daughter of Lucius, indicating she is remembered or honored while still alive.

EDCS-18200269.translation: Lucius Teidius, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe.
EDCS-18200269.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Lucius Teidius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-18200270.translation: To the memory of Vibbia Sophia, his most chaste wife, with whom he lived 20 years without complaint. Quintus Servillius Dionysius, and himself while alive, made this.
EDCS-18200270.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Quintus Servillius Dionysius for his wife Vibbia Sophia, with whom he lived 20 years, and also for himself.

EDCS-23800761.translation: To Vincidia Galatenis. Nympherusa rests here; she lived 18 years and Nymphio, her brother, lived 22 years. Vincidia Primigenia, freedwoman of a woman, mother, made this for her children; the heirs shall not follow.
EDCS-23800761.summary: This funerary inscription honors Nympherusa, who died at 18, and her brother Nymphio, who died at 22, set up by their mother Vincidia Primigenia.

EDCS-23800767.translation: To the departed spirits. Modestus made this for his most sweet brother, Maximo, out of duty.
EDCS-23800767.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Modestus for his brother Maximo.

EDCS-23800768.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Suellia Prisca made this for her deserving husband, Lucius Gavellius Felix, and for the guild of dendrophori.
EDCS-23800768.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Suellia Prisca for her husband Lucius Gavellius Felix and also for the guild of dendrophori (tree-bearers).

EDCS-23800772.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fortunatus, a freedman of our Caesar, lived 1 year and 23 days. Nice, his mother, made this.
EDCS-23800772.summary: A funerary dedication for Fortunatus, a slave of Caesar, by his mother.

EDCS-24700270.text_edition: \n Caio Falerio Cai filio Nigro IIIIviro aedlicia potestate IIIIviro iure dicundo auguri IIIIviro quinquennali decuriones et municipes ex senatus consulto pridie Kalendas Apriles scribendo adfuerunt Lucius Cornelius Titi filius Sura Aulus Mineius Auli filius Piper Quintus Minoreius Luci filius Longinus Lucius Aebutrius filius Lucius Laenius filius Flaccus Caius Fabius Cai filius Hadrianus Quintus Philonius filius Rufus Titus Hortionius Cai filius P LFlaccus imus Caius Philonius Quinti filius Lucius Servilius filius Cilo Marcus Fannius Marci filius Quintus Alfius Sexti filius quod decuriones et municipes cunctei postulaverunt de Caio Falerio Cai filio et Caio de ea re ita censuerunt quom iei de sua pecunia forum sternant canalesque o lapide faciendas polliciti sint idemque macellum e et et armamentarium apstenentiam quot a arias sua ostre M NN ponendam censuerunt\n
EDCS-24700270.translation: Caio Falernio Nigro, son of Caius, four-time official with the power of aedile, four-time official with judicial power, augur, and five-year official, the decurions and townsfolk, by decree of the senate, were present as scribes on the day before the Kalends of April: Lucius Cornelius Sura, son of Titus; Aulus Mineius Piper, son of Aulus; Quintus Minoreius Longinus, son of Lucius; Lucius Aebutrius, son of Lucius; Laenius Flaccus, son of Lucius; Gaius Fabius Hadrianus, son of Caius; Quintus Philonius Rufus, son of Quintus; Titus Hortionius Flaccus, son of Caius; Gaius Philonius, son of Quintus; Lucius Servilius Cilo, son of Lucius; Marcus Fannius, son of Marcus; Quintus Alfius, son of Sextus. As the decurions and townsfolk unanimously requested regarding Caio Falernio, son of Caius, they decreed concerning this matter as follows: that he should pave the forum with his own money and construct the channels in stone, and likewise construct the market and armory, and that he should place a cistern of stone, as they decreed.
EDCS-24700270.summary: A decree by the decurions and townsfolk of a municipality, recognizing the many offices held by Caio Falernio Nigro and detailing his promised public works funded by his own money, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-24700271.text_edition: \n Aulus Alsinaeus Cai filius Claudia pater atius Marci filius Claudia Rufus IIIIviri quinquennales murum et turris ex decurionum decreto faciunda locarunt filius Aulus Alsinaeus Auli filius IIIIviri ex decurionum decreto probarunt\n
EDCS-24700271.translation: Aulus Alsinaeus, son of Caius, and Claudia Rufus, son of Marcius, as four-time quinquennial officials, contracted for the construction of the wall and towers by decree of the decurions. Aulus Alsinaeus, son of Aulus, as a four-time official, approved them by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-24700271.summary: A record of a public works contract for building a wall and towers, awarded by decurions and approved by officials, likely in an Italian town.

EDCS-24700272.text_edition: \n Statius Cai filius Castrius Vibi filius IIviri aediles exs sic decurionum decreto faciundum coeraverunt\n
EDCS-24700272.translation: Statius Castrius, son of Caius, and Vibi, son of Caius, as duovirs and aediles, oversaw the construction by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-24700272.summary: A brief record of duovirs and aediles overseeing a construction project ordered by the decurions.

EDCS-26500800.translation: Marcus Avillius Apulo, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, lies here in Teano.
EDCS-26500800.summary: A funerary dedication for Marcus Avillius Apulo, a local resident of Teano.

EDCS-26500807.translation: Marcus Palius, son of Marcus, four-time official with judicial power, by decree of the senate, saw to the construction of a portico with balconies in the forum and a archway which leads into the forum, and he approved it.
EDCS-26500807.summary: A record of a magistrate undertaking and approving public works in the forum, including a portico and an archway, funded by the state or through his office.

EDCS-26500808.text_edition: \n Vestae Marcus Ursius Marci filius Tromentina donum dedit libens merito\n
EDCS-26500808.translation: Marcus Ursius, son of Marcus, of the Tromentina tribe, gave this gift to Vesta, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-26500808.summary: A votive offering to the goddess Vesta by Marcus Ursius.

EDCS-26500809.text_edition: \n Auli libertus Philemones sic immissa domui meae tigilla Lucius Cassius Luci libertus Primus Decimum Marcium Decimi libertum Philemonem precario rogavit utei sic eo pariete immissa essent\n
EDCS-26500809.translation: Aulus' freedman Philemon asked Lucius Cassius Primus, freedman of Lucius, by precarium, that timbers be placed in my house, in this wall.
EDCS-26500809.summary: A legal request concerning the installation of timbers in a shared wall between two freedmen, mediated by a third freedman.

EDCS-27500225.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Donatus, slave of our Caesar, lived 40 years.
EDCS-27500225.summary: A funerary dedication for Donatus, a slave of Caesar.

EDCS-27500227.translation: Gaius Pomponius Gallus, son of Caius, four-time official with judicial power, paved the alley with stone using the money he owed for his office and for the profits, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-27500227.summary: A record of a magistrate paving an alley with stone, using funds related to his public office and possibly as a form of expiating a fine or fulfilling an obligation.

EDCS-28801226.translation: Quintus Petreius Strenuos, son of Quintus, a butcher, aged 20, lies here. Quintus Petreius, freedman of Quintus, his father, erected this for himself, his son, and his family.
EDCS-28801226.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Petreius Strenuos, a butcher, set up by his father.

EDCS-28801227.translation: Aper, aged 20, overseer of Marcus Caerdius Secundionis, lies here. Firmus, his foster-brother, erected this.
EDCS-28801227.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aper, an overseer, erected by his foster-brother.

EDCS-28801229.translation: Agele, slave of Creperia Polyclita, lived 12 years.
EDCS-28801229.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Agele, a slave girl, who died at 12 years old.

EDCS-28801234.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Fulvius erected this for his well-deserving wife, Annea Rufina, who lived 17 years.
EDCS-28801234.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annea Rufina, a wife who died at 17, erected by her husband.

EDCS-28801235.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Voluptas erected this for her well-deserving comrade, Lucius Anicius Priscus, who lived [3] years and months.
EDCS-28801235.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Anicius Priscus, a comrade, erected by Voluptas.

EDCS-28900458.translation: Anniolena [---]ollae lived 80 years. Anniolenus [---] Antipater, for his patroness [---].
EDCS-28900458.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment mentioning Anniolena and Anniolenus, with Antipater acting as the dedicator for his patroness.

EDCS-28900459.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Firma erected this for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Anniolenus Homerus.
EDCS-28900459.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Anniolenus Homerus, erected by his wife.

EDCS-28900468.translation: Calpurnia [erected this] for Marcus Calpurnius Phoebus, who lived 18 years and 3 months.
EDCS-28900468.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Calpurnius Phoebus, who died at 18 years and 3 months, possibly erected by Calpurnia.

EDCS-28900472.translation: For Aulus Cascius Lybicus, son of Aulus, of the Camilia tribe, 20 feet in the field, 20 feet in the front.
EDCS-28900472.summary: This inscription records land boundaries for Aulus Cascius Lybicus.

EDCS-28900478.text_edition: \n Claudia Hilaritas hic sita est Primio coniux Primio filius Nardis filia vixit annos XL\n
EDCS-28900478.translation: Claudia Hilaritas is buried here. Primio, her husband, and Nardis, her daughter, erected this. She lived 40 years.
EDCS-28900478.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Hilaritas, who died at 40, erected by her husband and daughter.

EDCS-28900479.translation: Claudia Salva is buried here; she lived 30 years.
EDCS-28900479.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Salva, who lived for 30 years.

EDCS-28900481.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Gnaeus Cornelius Thalamus placed this for his most dutiful sister Cornelia Philemation and for himself.
EDCS-28900481.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Gnaeus Cornelius Thalamus to his sister Cornelia Philemation and himself, intended for the shades of the underworld.

EDCS-28900484.translation: For Salvius Curtius, son of Marcus, of the Camilia tribe. 12 feet in front, 14 feet in the depth.
EDCS-28900484.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a plot of land, likely for a tomb, belonging to Salvius Curtius.

EDCS-28900485.translation: Publius Curtius Severus, son of Publius, of the Horatia tribe, is buried here. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the depth.
EDCS-28900485.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Curtius Severus, indicating the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-28900488.translation: A burial place. For [---]ae, freedwoman of Lucius. For Ergasm[---]. Accius [---]acus is buried here. In front [feet...].
EDCS-28900488.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot and identifies the deceased, whose name is partially lost, and the person who placed the marker.

EDCS-28900490.translation: Fausta and Oreste[s] made this from their own savings for their deserving patroness, to bring to mind her death, so that whoever passes by as a traveler may read her name. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the depth.
EDCS-28900490.summary: This inscription records a memorial created by Fausta and Orestes for their patroness, indicating the dimensions of the memorial space.

EDCS-28900491.translation: To the shades of the underworld. Primulus placed this for his well-deserving former slave Faustilla.
EDCS-28900491.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Primulus to his well-deserving former slave, Faustilla.

EDCS-28900492.translation: [---] Faustus, freedman, is buried here. [---]e, his preserver, placed this.
EDCS-28900492.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Faustus, a freedman, and identifies the person who placed the marker as his 'preserver'.

EDCS-28900497.translation: A burial place. For Flavia Philemation, freedwoman of Lucius. Marcus Betutius, son of Caius, [laid this]. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the depth.
EDCS-28900497.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot belonging to Flavia Philemation, a freedwoman, and indicates its dimensions.

EDCS-28900498.translation: Lucius Sextus Flavius, son of Quintus, of the Pollia tribe. 12 feet in front, [12 feet in the depth].
EDCS-28900498.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a burial plot belonging to Lucius Sextus Flavius.

EDCS-28900499.translation: To Titus Flavius Secundus, who lived 40 years. Romania Chypare placed this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-28900499.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Secundus, erected by his wife Romania Chypare, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28900502.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription beginning with the dedication 'Dis Manibus' for a person named Gemina.

EDCS-28900508.translation: Lucius Iulius Candidus, who lived 20 years. Tiberius Iulius Arcas and Iulia Leuce [
EDCS-28900508.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Iulius Candidus, who lived 20 years, with Tiberius Iulius Arcas and Iulia Leuce mentioned as relatives, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28900510.translation: [...] mother Leona placed this for her dearest son, who lived 3 years and 2 months, well-deserving.
EDCS-28900510.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, erected by his mother Leona, and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28900511.translation: To the shades of Marci Lucci Macrini [...]. Castus and Ma[...] Lucci, his heirs, placed this.
EDCS-28900511.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for Marci Lucci Macrini, with his heirs Castus and Ma[...] Lucci identified as the dedicator(s), dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28900515.text_edition: \n Publio Magio Philoni Norbanae Pyrallidi Magia Publi filia Phaselis faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28900515.translation: Publia Magia Phaselis, daughter of Publius, had this made for Publius Magius Philon, of Norba, and for Pyrallis.
EDCS-28900515.summary: This is a dedication inscription for Publius Magius Philon of Norba and Pyrallis, arranged by Publia Magia Phaselis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28900517.translation: Marcus Marcius, son of Lucius, from Falernia, lies buried here.
EDCS-28900517.summary: This is a simple tomb inscription identifying Marcus Marcius, son of Lucius, from Falernia, as the deceased buried there.

EDCS-29100406.translation: [To the shades of] Martialis Marius, who lived 80 years. Paulina, Filista [for their father?], placed this for him, well-deserving.
EDCS-29100406.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for Martialis Marius, who lived 80 years, erected by his daughters Paulina and Filista, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100413.translation: To the shades of Novia Maximilla. Gaius Sextilius Firmus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29100413.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Novia Maximilla, erected by her husband Gaius Sextilius Firmus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100416.translation: To the shades of Ortoria. Gaius Iunius Choresus placed this for his wife, with whom he lived 12 years, well-deserving, and for himself.
EDCS-29100416.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ortoria, erected by her husband Gaius Iunius Choresus, who also reserved a place for himself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100417.translation: Ovia Glucera, freedwoman of Quintus, is laid here.
EDCS-29100417.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ovia Glucera, identifying her as the freedwoman of Quintus.

EDCS-29100420.translation: Of Marcus Percennius, son of Marcus, of the Horatia tribe: in front 12 feet, in depth 12 feet.
EDCS-29100420.summary: This inscription specifies land dimensions (12x12 feet) possibly for a burial plot, belonging to Marcus Percennius.

EDCS-29100427.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. For Pontia Felicula and Calcia Successa.
EDCS-29100427.summary: This is a dedication to the Deities of the Underworld for Pontia Felicula and Calcia Successa.

EDCS-29200345.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Spendopor, fellow slave, set this up for Euticia, fellow slave, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29200345.summary: This funerary dedication was set up by Spendopor, a slave, for his wife Euticia, also a slave.

EDCS-29200347.translation: Place. Of Tettia Optata, daughter of Marcus: in front 12 feet, in depth...
EDCS-29200347.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot with dimensions, belonging to Tettia Optata, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-29200348.translation: Tituleia Arbuscula, freedwoman of the woman: for herself and for Marcus Opsios Stratacus, freedman of Marcus, her husband, in front 12 feet, in depth 12 feet.
EDCS-29200348.summary: This funerary inscription marks a burial plot of 12x12 feet and is dedicated by Tituleia Arbuscula for herself and her husband Marcus Opsios Stratacus.

EDCS-29200352.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Sextus, of the Camilia tribe, from Dertona. The heirs by the will made this.
EDCS-29200352.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the heirs of Lucius Valerius of Dertona, as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-29200355.translation: To the Deities of the Underworld. Vitalis lived 75 years. Abascantus made this for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-29200355.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Vitalis (75 years) and was erected by his son Abascantus.

EDCS-29200371.summary: This fragmentary inscription records the name of a dedicator and the lifespan of an individual who lived 32 years.

EDCS-29200431.translation: Marcus Teuseves, who lived fifteen years, was received here in peace.
EDCS-29200431.summary: This epitaph records the reception of Marcus Teuseves, who died at the age of fifteen, into 'peace' (likely referring to the afterlife).

EDCS-29400003.text_edition: \n Aeliae Augusti libertae Philete Purpurio coniugi bene merenti et sibi posuit\n
EDCS-29400003.translation: Aelia Philete, freedwoman of Augustus, dedicated this to her well-deserving husband Purpurius and to herself.
EDCS-29400003.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a freedwoman to her husband and herself, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29900361.translation: Quintus Minucius Hyllus lived 50 years; he is placed here.
EDCS-29900361.summary: This is a simple tombstone inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, Quintus Minucius Hyllus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29900362.translation: Nicephoris, slave of Milesius, lived 45 years; she is placed here.
EDCS-29900362.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a female slave named Nicephoris, recording her age and owner, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29900363.translation: Lucius Avillius Leonida dedicated this to Avillia Prepusae, his freedwoman, who lived 27 years; she is placed here.
EDCS-29900363.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by a patron for his freedwoman, Avillia Prepusae, recording her age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301636.text_edition: \n Marcus Vecilius Marci filius Luci nepos Campus praefectus fabrum tribunus militum IIvir iure dicundo pontifex amphitheatrum loco privato suo et maceriam circum item sua pecunia in honorem Imperatoris Caesaris Augusti coloniaeque Luceriae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-30301636.translation: Marcus Vecilius, son of Marcus, grandson of Lucius, Campus, prefect of the military engineers, tribune of the soldiers, duumvir with judicial power, and pontifex, arranged for the construction of an amphitheater and surrounding wall on his private land, using his own funds, in honor of Emperor Caesar Augustus and the colony of Luceria.
EDCS-30301636.summary: This inscription records the generous public works project of Marcus Vecilius, a local magistrate and military officer, who funded the construction of an amphitheater and wall for the colony of Luceria, likely in the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-31300230.translation: Marcus Tillius Secundus, freedman of Marcus, a ring-maker, lived 35 years; he is placed here.
EDCS-31300230.summary: This tombstone inscription records the name, occupation (ring-maker), and age of a freedman named Marcus Tillius Secundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31300242.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Pontifex Maximus, invested with tribunician power for the 16th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Proconsul, Father of the Fatherland. Marcus Aurelius Nigrinus, evocatus of our Emperor, appointed to the ranks in the II Traiana legion, devoted to his divinity, consecrated this grove of Aquilonia on the 3rd day before the Ides of August, during the consulship of Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus and a fourth consul.
EDCS-31300242.summary: This inscription is a dedication of a grove to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus by Marcus Aurelius Nigrinus, a soldier and evocatus, likely from the period of Marcus Aurelius' reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-33500422.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Nymphodotus to Albinia Daphne, his dearest companion.
EDCS-33500422.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Nymphodotus to his beloved companion Albinia Daphne, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33500426.translation: To Pomponius, son of Caius, he is placed here. Greetings.
EDCS-33500426.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Pomponius, son of Caius, with a simple greeting, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33500428.translation: To the Divine Spirits. [To] Valerius Eu[...], [son of?] Valerius [...].
EDCS-33500428.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary dedication to a member of the Valerius family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-34000244.translation: Acerronia Posilla lived 75 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-34000244.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acerronia Posilla, who lived 75 years.

EDCS-34000245.translation: Marcus Antonius Sotericus, freedman of Iullus, Archela and Antus are buried here.
EDCS-34000245.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Antonius Sotericus and others, indicating they are buried here.

EDCS-34000248.translation: To the departed spirits. Viator lived 11 years and 8 months; his parents placed him here.
EDCS-34000248.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their son Viator, who died at age 11 years and 8 months.

EDCS-34000249.translation: [---] Flavius [---] perhaps lived sadly [---] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-34000249.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a person named Flavius, with significant portions missing.

EDCS-34000254.translation: To the departed spirits. Felicla, servant of Caesar, lived 24 years. Velox made this for a well-deserving fellow-servant. She is buried here.
EDCS-34000254.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felicla, a servant of Caesar, set up by her fellow-servant Velox.

EDCS-34000255.translation: [---]nius Hilarus lived 60 years; his daughter made this for her well-deserving father.
EDCS-34000255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Hilarus, set up by his daughter.

EDCS-34000256.translation: Marcus Massius Diapyros lived 40 years. Domitia Erotis and her sons made this for her well-deserving tent-mate.
EDCS-34000256.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Massius Diapyros, set up by his tent-mate Domitia Erotis and her sons.

EDCS-34000257.translation: [---]mus lived 17 years. His mother Apuleia Felicitas made this for her well-deserving son.
EDCS-34000257.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unnamed individual, set up by his mother Apuleia Felicitas.

EDCS-34200006.translation: Iulia Venusta lived 30 years. Her mother made this for her well-deserving daughter. She is buried here.
EDCS-34200006.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Venusta, set up by her mother.

EDCS-34200007.translation: To the departed spirits. Campatius Secundus lived 50 years; he is buried here. Furia Ma placed this for her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-34200007.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Campatius Secundus, set up by his wife Furia Ma.

EDCS-34200009.translation: Nearchius Actius lived 26 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-34200009.summary: This is a tombstone for Nearchius Actius, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-34200086.translation: Gaius Julius Pitheros, son of Gaius.
EDCS-34200086.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Pitheros, a freedman.

EDCS-34200089.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. The funeral of Cn. Pomponius Epaphroditus Cissianus, freedman of Augustus, has been provided for publicly.
EDCS-34200089.summary: This inscription records the public provision for the funeral of Cn. Pomponius Epaphroditus Cissianus, a freedman of Augustus.

EDCS-34200090.translation: Aulus Allienus Charito is buried here.
EDCS-34200090.summary: This is a tombstone for Aulus Allienus Charito.

EDCS-34200091.translation: Lucius Arruntius Hospes, freedman of Seleucus, is buried here.
EDCS-34200091.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Arruntius Hospes, a freedman.

EDCS-34200092.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Bennonius Phrernaes, freedman of Lucius, lived 35 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-34200092.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Bennonius Phrernaes, a freedman, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-34200093.summary: This is a tombstone for Calcdia Nice, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-34200094.translation: Lucius Cassius Fortunatus lived 26 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-34200094.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Cassius Fortunatus, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-34200095.translation: Cerdo Ani lib., lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-34200095.summary: This is a tombstone for Cerdo Ani lib., who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-34200096.translation: Claudia Polla lived [3] months; she is buried here.
EDCS-34200096.summary: This is a tombstone for Claudia Polla, who died after an unspecified number of years and 3 months.

EDCS-34200098.translation: Chloe, slave of Gnaeus Pompeius, lived 5 years.
EDCS-34200098.summary: This is a tombstone for Chloe, a slave of Gnaeus Pompeius, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-34200099.translation: Crescens for his Secundilla, companion in the barrack, lived 22 years, here laid.
EDCS-34200099.summary: This is a tombstone for Secundilla, who lived 22 years, likely set up by her companion Crescens.

EDCS-34200101.translation: Quintus Egnatius Epaphra lived 40 years, here laid.
EDCS-34200101.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Egnatius Epaphra, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-34200102.translation: To Epafra, companion in the barrack, well-deserving, perhaps a furnace-maker.
EDCS-34200102.summary: This is a dedication to Epafra, a companion in the barrack, possibly by profession a furnace-maker.

EDCS-34200103.text_edition: \n Eucles Epagathi vixit annos LX hic situs\n
EDCS-34200103.translation: Eucles, son of Epagathus, lived 60 years, here laid.
EDCS-34200103.summary: This is a tombstone for Eucles, son of Epagathus, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-34200104.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Fabius Hermes, who lived 4 years.

EDCS-34200107.translation: Haste Occia, slave, lived 29 years, here laid.
EDCS-34200107.summary: This is a tombstone for Haste Occia, a slave, who lived 29 years.

EDCS-34200108.translation: To the departed spirits; to Honesta, well-deserving.
EDCS-34200108.summary: This is a dedication to Honesta, possibly a tombstone for a deceased woman.

EDCS-34200109.translation: Publius Isuleius Ampliatus lived 60 years, here laid.
EDCS-34200109.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Isuleius Ampliatus, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-34200110.summary: This inscription names Isuleia, a freedwoman of Publius, and Callityche.

EDCS-34200111.translation: Gaius Julius Felicio lived 6 years, is buried here.
EDCS-34200111.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child named Gaius Julius Felicio, who died at the age of 6.

EDCS-34200113.translation: Licinia Synerusa lived 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-34200113.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinia Synerusa, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-34200114.translation: Marcia, freedwoman of [---]sothe, lived 26 years, is buried here.
EDCS-34200114.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcia, a freedwoman, who died at the age of 26.

EDCS-34200115.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maro, soldier of the Liburna [ship?], [belonging to the?] century of Superius.
EDCS-34200115.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Maro, identified as a soldier, possibly associated with a unit or a ship named Liburna and the century of Superius.

EDCS-34200117.translation: Melete, wife of Petronius Verus, lived [---] years, is buried here.
EDCS-34200117.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Melete, wife of Petronius Verus.

EDCS-34200118.translation: Nestina, aged 16 years, is buried here.
EDCS-34200118.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nestina, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-34200120.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Decimus Patronius Priscus lived 70 years, is buried here.
EDCS-34200120.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decimus Patronius Priscus, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-34200122.translation: Numerius Pomponius Sumphorus lived 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-34200122.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Numerius Pomponius Sumphorus, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-34200123.translation: Pomponia Calene lived 11 years, is buried here.
EDCS-34200123.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponia Calene, who died at the age of 11.

EDCS-34200124.translation: Pomponia Heuresis lived [---] years, is buried here.
EDCS-34200124.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pomponia Heuresis, with her age partially preserved.

EDCS-34200125.translation: Primogenia lived for 30 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-34200125.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Primogenia.

EDCS-34200126.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Sabinius Phlegon, likely a child.

EDCS-34200127.translation: Lucius Salvius Faustus is buried here.
EDCS-34200127.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Salvius Faustus.

EDCS-34200130.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Annius vixit annis XL Andius Marianus fi<l=I>ius eiusdem vixit annis XII \n
EDCS-34200130.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerius Annius lived for 40 years. Andius Marianus, his son, lived for 12 years.
EDCS-34200130.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, recording the lifespans of a father and son.

EDCS-34200131.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Venusta lived for 76 years and 7 months. Made (this monument) for (him/her) deserving well.
EDCS-34200131.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, recording the lifespan of Venusta and noting that the monument was erected by a loved one.

EDCS-34200132.translation: Zosimus, a home-born slave, lived for 27 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-34200132.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Zosimus, identified as a home-born slave (verna).

EDCS-34200139.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Allecinius [...]
EDCS-34200139.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, beginning with the name Marcus Allecinius.

EDCS-34200140.translation: Graecinia Sevia lived for 4 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-34200140.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Graecinia Sevia, likely a young child.

EDCS-34200141.translation: Aulus Hordionius Hesper lived for 65 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-34200141.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan and burial of Aulus Hordionius Hesper.

EDCS-34200143.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Julius Abascantus lived for 30 years; he is buried here. Sextia Saturnina erected (this monument) for (him/her) deserving well.
EDCS-34200143.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the spirits of the departed, recording the lifespan of Gaius Julius Abascantus and noting that Sextia Saturnina erected the monument.

EDCS-34200144.translation: Gaius Memnius [---] lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-34200144.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Memnius, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-34200146.translation: Quintus Plotuis Ianurius lived 25 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-34200146.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Plotuis Ianurius, recording his name and age at death.

EDCS-34200147.translation: Venonia [---] Prosdi[---] lived 4 years [---]; she is buried here [---]. Gratu[---]ater made this.
EDCS-34200147.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for a child named Venno[nia] Prosdi[---], recording her age and that the monument was made by Gratu[---]ater.

EDCS-34200151.translation: To the departed spirits. Ursulus lived 35 years. His deserving wife made this. // ]S[
EDCS-34200151.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ursulus, set up by his wife, recording his age at death.

EDCS-34200153.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Arrehenius Clemens lived [---] years. Cornelia Ocel[la ---
EDCS-34200153.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Marcus Arrehenius Clemens, mentioning Cornelia Ocel[la].

EDCS-34200156.translation: Wicked mother made this tomb for her daughter who died, more or less [---] years old. [---] [---]
EDCS-34200156.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a daughter, stating that her mother (described as 'wicked') commissioned the tomb and recording the daughter's age at death.

EDCS-34200158.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Maxima, for her daughter Freisidius Maxima, who lived 11 months, 14 days, 2 hours, placed this in recognition of her desert.
EDCS-34200158.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maxima, daughter of Freisidius Maximu, recording her precise age at death and erected by her father.

EDCS-34200170.translation: Lucius Clovatius Clarus gave this gift to the Good Goddess with his family.
EDCS-34200170.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Bona Dea by Lucius Clovatius Clarus and his family.

EDCS-35000004.text_edition: \n Caius Caninius Cai filius Rufus sibi et Caio Caninio Cai filio Rufo patri lanioni et Caio Caninio Mariano avo missicio et Cornificiae Glymmae matri et Caniniae Palladi aviae et libertis libertabusquae sic\n
EDCS-35000004.translation: Gaius Caninius Rufus, son of Gaius, for himself and for Gaius Caninius Rufus, son of Gaius, his father, the butcher, and for Gaius Caninius Marianus, his grandfather, a freedman, and for Caninia Palla, his grandmother, and for his freedmen and freedwomen who [---]
EDCS-35000004.summary: This is an epitaph for Gaius Caninius Rufus and multiple family members, including his father, grandfather, and grandmother.

EDCS-35000025.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to be a funerary epitaph for Cutia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-35200294.translation: Eperastus, slave of Alexsionus, lived 12 years. Because it was fitting for a son to do this for his mother, his mother made this for her son, after death intervened.
EDCS-35200294.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a boy named Eperastus, erected by his mother, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-35200317.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Erycia sic Cai liberta Oecumene vixit annum I menses XI quot sic decuit natam matri patrique parare hunc titulum miserae fecit uterque parens hic sita\n
EDCS-35200317.translation: To the Divine Shades. Erycia, freedwoman of Caius, Oecumene, lived 1 year and 11 months. Because it was fitting for a daughter to provide this for her mother and father, both parents made this monument for their miserable daughter, who rests here.
EDCS-35200317.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Oecumene, erected by her parents, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-37200049.translation: Acinia Sextia lived 55 years. She rests here.
EDCS-37200049.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Acinia Sextia, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-37200057.translation: Euphrosyne, of Phrygian origin, lived 50 years. She rests here. Her slave conservos Myro made this.
EDCS-37200057.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Euphrosyne, erected by her slave Myro, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-37200062.translation: Calligenes, slave of Cornelia, rests here. He lived [3] years. His brothers and mother placed this.
EDCS-37200062.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calligenes, a slave, erected by his brothers and mother, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-37200069.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Gaius Caecilius Irpinus, freedman of Occia, who lived 7 years and 6 months. Restitutus and Sophe made this for their son.
EDCS-37200069.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy, Gaius Caecilius Irpinus, erected by his parents, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-37200070.translation: [3]rius, freedman of Lucius [3]hilus, made this while alive.
EDCS-37200070.summary: This is a dedication, possibly for a tomb, made by a freedman to himself or a relative while still alive, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-37200071.translation: To Memmius Isargyrus. Memmia Thallusa [died aged] 23 years. Memmia Auge [granted] 10 feet in front and 10 feet of land.
EDCS-37200071.summary: This inscription records a land grant and possibly a funerary dedication for Memmius Isargyrus and Memmia Thallusa, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-37200072.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Abascantus, steward of our Emperor, whom Cleopatra, his wife, [served]. [To the Divine Shades]. For [3]ina, his dearest daughter, who lived [3] years. Hilarus and [3]tiane, her parents, made this.
EDCS-37200072.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription, possibly commemorating a household or family, for Abascantus and his daughter, erected by his wife and possibly other relatives, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-37200073.translation: For Callistus, son of Quintus Valerius Vegetus.
EDCS-37200073.summary: This is a dedication or funerary marker for Callistus, son of Quintus Valerius Vegetus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-38000180.translation: Here lies Davdatus, son of Azarias, and Sosana. In peace may they rest. // Here rests a righteous man, may his soul live forever in the Garden of Eden. I, Eliezar, son of [---], have placed this inscription in the good place for him.
EDCS-38000180.summary: This inscription, in Greek and Hebrew, records the burial of Davdatus and Sosana and was likely erected by a relative in the Roman East, possibly late antiquity.

EDCS-39100090.translation: Heraklas, son of Heraklides, from Alexandria. He lived 50 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-39100090.summary: A tombstone from Alexandria recording the lifespan and burial of Heraklas, son of Heraklides.

EDCS-41500056.translation: Gaius Minatius Bithus, an Augustalis, from the decree of the decurions, for repairing the road, 2000 sesterces.
EDCS-41500056.summary: An inscription detailing a public works project in which Gaius Minatius Bithus funded road repair with 2000 sesterces, as decreed by the local council.

EDCS-41700248.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Herennio Rufo Augustali Lucius Herennius Probus Apollinis sacerdos merenti fratri fecit\n
EDCS-41700248.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Herennius Probus, priest of Apollo, erected this for his deserving brother, Gaius Herennius Rufus, an Augustalis.
EDCS-41700248.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Herennius Probus to his brother, Gaius Herennius Rufus, who held the rank of Augustalis and served as a priest.

EDCS-43400313.translation: Primigenius, son of Verecundus. He lived 20 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-43400313.summary: A tombstone recording the brief life and burial of Primigenius, son of Verecundus.

EDCS-43400314.translation: Latinus, son of Iulius. He lived 26 years.
EDCS-43400314.summary: A tombstone recording the lifespan of Latinus, son of Iulius.

EDCS-43400315.translation: To the departed spirits. Senianus, son of Peducei Flaviani, lived 3 years. His father erected this for his sweetest son.
EDCS-43400315.summary: A funerary inscription by a father, Senianus, for his young son who lived only three years.

EDCS-43400317.translation: To the departed spirits. Pullia Zosime lived 17 years. Pinteros, her brother, erected this for his well-deserving sister.
EDCS-43400317.summary: A funerary inscription by Pinteros for his sister Pullia Zosime, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-43400325.translation: Lucius Agathopus. He lived 3 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-43400325.summary: A tombstone recording the very young age of Lucius Agathopus at the time of his death.

EDCS-43400326.translation: [?]mia Quarta lived 30 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-43400326.summary: A tombstone recording the death and burial of Quarta at the age of 30.

EDCS-43400328.translation: Pomponia Ianuaria, daughter of Gnaeus, lived for 2 years and 4 months; she is buried here.
EDCS-43400328.summary: A tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Pomponia Ianuaria.

EDCS-43400329.translation: Marcus Fundanius Vitalio lived for 40 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-43400329.summary: A tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Marcus Fundanius Vitalio.

EDCS-43400331.translation: To the departed spirits. Audia Euodia lived for 62 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-43400331.summary: A tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Audia Euodia.

EDCS-43400333.translation: Marcus Caecilius Vicarius lived for 60 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-43400333.summary: A tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Marcus Caecilius Vicarius.

EDCS-43400335.translation: Quintus Fulcinius Epaphroditus, son of Publius, lived for 45 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-43400335.summary: A tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Quintus Fulcinius Epaphroditus.

EDCS-43400336.translation: Lucius Aemilius Probatus lived for 50 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-43400336.summary: A tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Lucius Aemilius Probatus.

EDCS-43400340.translation: Pomponia Hermione, freedwoman of Gnaeus, lived for 35 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-43400340.summary: A tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Pomponia Hermione.

EDCS-43400341.translation: Lucius Laenius Euhodus lived for 7 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-43400341.summary: A tombstone records the lifespan and burial of Lucius Laenius Euhodus.

EDCS-44100273.translation: A public plot was granted to Gaius Flavius Figulus, quaestor, measuring 20 feet in frontage and 20 feet in depth.
EDCS-44100273.summary: A record of land allocation for a public purpose to the quaestor Gaius Flavius Figulus.

EDCS-44100278.translation: To the departed spirits. Septimia Chresime, freedwoman of Gaius, and Gaius Ummidius Hamillus made this for their daughter Asclepia, who lived 9 months and 14 days.
EDCS-44100278.summary: A tombstone set up by parents for their deceased daughter, recording her name, lifespan, and their relationship.

EDCS-44100279.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Neratia Pyrallis, her parents for their dearest daughter, who lived 3 years, 8 months, and 10 days. Pyrallis, farewell.
EDCS-44100279.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Neratia Pyrallis, set up by her parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-45500030.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Rubria Marcella set this up for herself while alive, and for her husband Quintus [---].
EDCS-45500030.summary: A funerary dedication by Rubria Marcella for herself and her husband, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46400273.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Nigrinus, the farm manager, lived 50 years, is buried here. Megiste [---].
EDCS-46400273.summary: This inscription records the death and burial of Nigrinus, a farm manager, with a partial dedication from Megiste, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46400274.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Argyrus the slave lived 41 years. Secunda made this for her well-deserving fellow slave, who is buried here.
EDCS-46400274.summary: A funerary epitaph for Argyrus, a slave, erected by his fellow slave Secunda, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46400275.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Germanus, slave of Audii.
EDCS-46400275.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for Germanus, a slave, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46400882.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Clodius Marianus, son of Lucius, of the Maecia tribe, patron of the municipality, lived 61 years and 11 months, is buried here. Lucius Clodius Pollio Iustus, son of Lucius, for his most excellent father.
EDCS-46400882.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Clodius Marianus, a prominent local official, set up by his son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46601024.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Calliste, slave of Rei Crispinus. Claudia Epitetu, her mother, for her well-deserving daughter, who lived 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-46601024.summary: A funerary inscription for Calliste, a slave, erected by her mother Claudia Epitetu, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46601025.translation: To Hilarus, of Caesar, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-46601025.summary: A funerary inscription for Hilarus, possibly an imperial freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46601027.translation: Icarus, of the Quintus Lucius Lutatius Catuli family, is buried here.
EDCS-46601027.summary: An epitaph recording the burial of Icarus, associated with the prominent Lutatii Catuli family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46601029.translation: [---] Romanius, son of Marcus, of the Camilia tribe, the priest, saw to the construction of the baths at his own expense.
EDCS-46601029.summary: An inscription recording that Romanius Sacerdos, a priest, funded the construction of public baths, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-48700044.text_edition: \n Amme hic sita Susus magister gregarius \n
EDCS-48700044.translation: Amme Susus, a common soldier, lies here.
EDCS-48700044.summary: This is a tombstone for Amme Susus, identified as a common soldier.

EDCS-51200109.translation: Quintus Volumnius Asiaticus, aedile, priest, quaestor, and duovir, lived 80 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-51200109.summary: This epitaph records the life and offices held by Quintus Volumnius Asiaticus, who lived to be 80.

EDCS-51200110.translation: To Lucius Gellius Primigenius, freedman of Lucius, an Augustalis. The town council, at the request of the people, granted him a statue for his many benefactions. For this honor, he paid 10,000 sesterces to increase the sum of the annual grain dole, and at the request of the same people, a statue made of bronze was erected...
EDCS-51200110.summary: This inscription records public honors, including a bronze statue, granted to Lucius Gellius Primigenius for his benefactions and his financial contribution to the city's grain dole.

EDCS-51200112.translation: Quintus Coelius Sacerdos. 13 feet in front, 13 feet in the field.
EDCS-51200112.summary: This inscription marks a burial plot measuring 13 by 13 feet, belonging to Quintus Coelius Sacerdos.

EDCS-51200116.translation: ...with tribunician power twice, consul twice, father of the fatherland, most merciful proconsul, restorer of public liberty and Roman dignity. Gaius Egnatius Certus Sattianus, a most distinguished man, restored [him] to his ancestral household gods by the judgment of the divine power.
EDCS-51200116.summary: This is a dedication to an unnamed emperor or important figure, praising his virtues and restoration of liberty, erected by Gaius Egnatius Certus Sattianus.

EDCS-52600144.translation: Gaius Canellius Ianuarius, freedman of Gaius, an Augustalis Claudialis, for himself, and for Pomponia Felicula, freedwoman of Lucius, his companion, and for his freedman Gaius Canellius Alcimus.
EDCS-52600144.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Canellius Ianuarius for himself, his female companion, and his freedman.

EDCS-52600169.translation: Terraea Honorata, freedwoman of Numerius, lived 13 years; [she is buried] here.
EDCS-52600169.summary: This epitaph records the death of Terraea Honorata, who died at the age of 13.

EDCS-52600170.translation: Terraea Lycoris, freedwoman of Numerius, lived 32 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-52600170.summary: This epitaph records the death of Terraea Lycoris, who died at the age of 32.

EDCS-52600171.summary: This is a tombstone for Vibia Helpis.

EDCS-52600172.translation: Sergia Leda lived 50 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-52600172.summary: This epitaph records the death of Sergia Leda, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-52600173.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Octavius Ptolomeus.

EDCS-52600174.translation: Quintus Aruntius Maximus lived for 1 year and lies here.
EDCS-52600174.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Aruntius Maximus, who died as an infant.

EDCS-52600175.translation: Aufidia Lais lived for 60 years and lies here.
EDCS-52600175.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aufidia Lais, recording her lifespan.

EDCS-52600177.translation: Hele(n)us, freedman of a woman, lived for 7 years and lies here.
EDCS-52600177.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Helenus, a freedman, noting his age at death.

EDCS-52600178.translation: Claudia [3]cia lived for [3] years and lies here.
EDCS-52600178.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Claudia [3]cia.

EDCS-52600179.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Clio.

EDCS-52600309.translation: Caius Julius Herma lived for 50 years and lies here.
EDCS-52600309.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caius Julius Herma, stating his name and age at death.

EDCS-53000374.translation: To the departed gods. His mother Campata Severa, who lived 21 years, set this up for her dutiful son Iulius Helius, who lived 20 years, [as deserving of goodwill].
EDCS-53000374.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Campata Severa for her son Iulius Helius, recording their ages and emphasizing filial piety.

EDCS-55702371.translation: Marius Martialis set this up for his son Caius Marius Marullus, who lived 14 years, well deserving, and for Iunia Sica.
EDCS-55702371.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Marius Martialis for his son Caius Marius Marullus and possibly for Iunia Sica, recording the son's age.

EDCS-55702375.translation: To the departed gods. Aephytikanos and Fortuna, parents, made this for their sweetest son, who lived 2 years and 2 months.
EDCS-55702375.summary: This funerary inscription was made by parents Aephytikanos and Fortuna for their young son.

EDCS-55702377.translation: Of Lucius Eggius Amimetus, freedman of Blastus.
EDCS-55702377.summary: This is a funerary inscription naming Lucius Eggius Amimetus, a freedman of Blastus.

EDCS-56000345.translation: Publius Servilius Diophantus, freedman of Publius, and Publius Servilius Alexander, freedman of Publius, made (this) while alive.
EDCS-56000345.summary: This funerary inscription records that Publius Servilius Diophantus and Publius Servilius Alexander, freedmen of Publius, erected this monument during their lifetime.

EDCS-57800021.translation: To the divine shades. [---] Rufinus and Pletoria, to his well-deserving wife, made (this); she lived with him for 11 years.
EDCS-57800021.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a wife, Pletoria, by her husband, Rufinus, and records their marriage duration.

EDCS-57800035.translation: Marcus Mevius Clemens, son of Publius, of the Cornelia tribe, as aedile, quaestor, and one of the four officials with judicial power, (dedicated this) for himself and his family.
EDCS-57800035.summary: This is a funerary and dedicatory inscription for Marcus Mevius Clemens, detailing his magistracies and dedicating the monument to himself and his family.

EDCS-57800043.translation: To the divine shades. Ennius Gemno made (this) for his son Ennius Aquelius, who was well-deserving, together with his family.
EDCS-57800043.summary: This funerary inscription records Ennius Gemno dedicating a monument to his deserving son, Ennius Aquelius, and his family.

EDCS-57800048.translation: For Numerius Ennius Pollio, son of Numerius, of the Cornelia tribe, according to his will.
EDCS-57800048.summary: This inscription records a dedication for Numerius Ennius Pollio, likely from a tomb, as specified in his will.

EDCS-59400021.translation: To the divine shades. Tiberius Claudiusvellespontianus, a marble worker, lived 60 years. Iulia Ino lived 70 years. Their daughter placed (this) for her most dutiful parents, buried here.
EDCS-59400021.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Hellenpontianus and Iulia Ino, detailing their ages and erected by their daughter.

EDCS-60700014.translation: Titus Vedianius Celer, freedman of Titus, and Auguralis, for himself and for Sardia Helpidius Modesta, daughter of Helpidius.
EDCS-60700014.summary: This funerary inscription records Titus Vedianius Celer, a freedman and Auguralis, dedicating the monument for himself and his daughter, Sardia Helpidius Modesta.

EDCS-60700015.translation: Aulus Ignius Segalus, freedman of Aulus, for Aulus Ignius Epicadus, freedman of Aulus, and for Liconia Faustus, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-60700015.summary: This inscription likely records the dimensions of a plot of land for Aulus Ignius Segalus, dedicated to freedmen Aulus Ignius Epicadus and Liconia Faustus.

EDCS-60700017.translation: Se[---] Rufilla, for herself and her family, for her beloved pet Specula, for Pastor, and for Crescentius Lucius Acastus.
EDCS-60700017.summary: This funerary inscription names Se[---] Rufilla dedicating a plot of land for herself, her family, her pet Specula, and others, specifying its dimensions.

EDCS-60700018.translation: To Socratenus, a boy who lived 5 years, 5 months, and 1 day. Florentinus and Maria made this for their most deserving pupil.
EDCS-60700018.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Florentinus and Maria for their deserving pupil Socratenus, who died in childhood.

EDCS-60700020.translation: To the departed spirits. Domitius Callistus made this for his well-deserving daughter Domitia.
EDCS-60700020.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Domitius Callistus for his well-deserving daughter Domitia.

EDCS-60700021.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Vibius Apollinaris and Sossia Fortunata, most wretched parents, made this for their most unfortunate son Gaius Vibius Apollinaris, aged 12 years and 3 months.
EDCS-60700021.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Gaius Vibius Apollinaris and Sossia Fortunata for their son, who died at the age of 12 years and 3 months.

EDCS-60700022.summary: The beginning of a funerary epitaph, likely for Avidius Divitius.

EDCS-60700023.translation: ... of Fabi Successus, who lived 17 years and 11 months. Aedia Ianuaria made this for her deserving son.
EDCS-60700023.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Aedia Ianuaria for her deserving son, Fabi Successus, who died at the age of 17 years and 11 months.

EDCS-60700024.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Fullonius Maximus for himself and his freedmen and freedwomen, and their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-60700024.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Fullonius Maximus for himself and his extended household of freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-60700025.translation: To the departed spirits. Bassaeus Hermes made this for his most sweet daughter Liconia Marcellina, who lived 11 years and six months, for her well-deserving.
EDCS-60700025.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Bassaeus Hermes for his daughter Liconia Marcellina, who died at the age of 11 years and six months.

EDCS-60700035.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Prima for Didi Felix, her husband, with whom she lived [---] years...
EDCS-60700035.summary: A funerary epitaph begun by Iulia Prima for her husband Didi Felix, with the duration of their marriage lost.

EDCS-61700689.translation: To the departed spirits. For Claudia Fadilla, daughter of Tiberius, a woman of most illustrious memory.
EDCS-61700689.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Claudia Fadilla, daughter of Tiberius, described as a woman of illustrious memory.

EDCS-61700710.translation: To the departed spirits. For Cominius Hilarus, a most sweet son, who lived 25 years...
EDCS-61700710.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cominius Hilarus, who died at the age of 25, described as a most sweet son.

EDCS-62101312.translation: Gaius Trebius Maximus and Quintus Coponius Naso, as aediles of the college of four, paved the side street in honor, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-62101312.summary: This inscription records the paving of a side street by two officials, aediles, in fulfillment of a senatorial decree.

EDCS-62101313.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the divine Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 7th time, consul for the 3rd time, remade the Appian Way, 15 miles and 2700 feet long, which had been lost due to age, at a cost of 1,157,000 sesterces, adding to the 569,100 sesterces which the landowners contributed.
EDCS-62101313.summary: This inscription documents Emperor Hadrian's extensive repair and reconstruction of the Appian Way, detailing the length, cost, and contributions made.

EDCS-62501259.translation: Mysticus, slave of Quinta Terrea, lived 35 years; here he lies.
EDCS-62501259.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the name, owner, and age of a deceased slave named Mysticus.

EDCS-62900252.translation: Severus lived [approximately] 30 years; here he lies.
EDCS-62900252.summary: A fragmented tombstone inscription for a person named Severus, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-63600292.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Capriolus lived 30 years.
EDCS-63600292.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld spirits, stating the name and age of the deceased, Capriolus.

EDCS-63600325.translation: Gaius Fannius Romanus, freedman of Gaius, for himself and his family, 13 feet in front and 15 feet in the field.
EDCS-63600325.summary: A funerary inscription marking a burial plot for Gaius Fannius Romanus and his family, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-63600329.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Hygia made this for her well-deserving husband, Caius Betitius Callianax.
EDCS-63600329.summary: A funerary epitaph where Hygia dedicates a tomb to her deserving husband, Caius Betitius Callianax.

EDCS-63600330.translation: Blassia Salvia, freedwoman of Numerius, 14 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-63600330.summary: A funerary inscription indicating the dimensions of a burial plot belonging to Blassia Salvia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-63600331.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cantria Maxima made this for her most beloved, well-deserving brother, Publius Cantrius Maximus.
EDCS-63600331.summary: A funerary epitaph where Cantria Maxima dedicates a tomb to her brother, Publius Cantrius Maximus.

EDCS-63600332.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Publius Cantrius Trophimus and Cantria Lupa made this for their most devoted son, Cantrius Trophimus, who lived 15 years, 3 months, and 11 days.
EDCS-63600332.summary: A funerary inscription by parents, Publius Cantrius Trophimus and Cantria Lupa, for their son Cantrius Trophimus, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-63600333.translation: Asellius Ursus, who lived for six years, made this monument. Asellia Iucunda set it up for her son.
EDCS-63600333.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name of the deceased, his age, and the dedicator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-63900074.inscription_type: senatus consulta, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-63900074.text_edition: \n Senatus consultum in Palatio in porticu quae est ad Apollinis scribundo adfuerunt Caius Ateius Luci filius Aniensis Capito Sextus Pompeius Sexti filius Octavius Cai filius Stellatina Fronto Marcus Asinius Curti filius Arnensis Mamilianus Caius Gavius Cai filius Poblilia Macer quaestor Aulus Didius filius Gallus quaestor quod Marcus Silanus Lucius Norbanus Balbus consules verba fecerunt commentarium ipsos composuisse sic uti negotium iis datum de rebus ad libidinem feminarum pertinentibus aut ad eos qui contra dignitatem ordinis sui in scaenam ludumve prodirent seve? auctorarent uti sancitur senatus consultis quae de ea re facta essent superioribus annis adhibita fraude qua maiestatem senatus minuerent quid de ea re fieri placeret de ea re ita censuere placere ne quis senatoris filium filiam nepotem neptem pronepotem proneptem neve quem cuius patri aut avo vel paterno vel materno aut fratri neve quam cuius viro aut patri aut avo paterno vel materno aut fratri ius fuisset unquam spectandi in equestribus locis in scaenam produceret auctoramentove rogaret ut in? harena? depugnaret aut ut pinnas gladiatorum raperet aut rudem tolleret aliove quod eius rei simile ministraret neve si quis se praeberet conduceret neve quis eorum se locaret idque ea de causa diligentius caveri dum ne dolo malo perseverent qui eludendae auctoritatis eius ordinis gratia quibus sedendi in equestribus locis ius erat aut publicam ignominiam ut acciperent aut ut famoso iudicio condemnarentur dederant operam et postea quam ei desciverant sua sponte ex equestribus locis auctoraverant se aut in scaenam prodierant neve quis eorum de quibus supra scriptum est si id contra dignitatem ordinis sui faceret libitinam haberet praeterquam si quis iam prodesset sic in scaenam operasve suas ad harenam locasset sive natus natave esset ex histrione aut gladiatore aut lanista aut lenone quodque senatus consulto quod Manio Lepido Tito Statilio Tauro consulibus referentibus factum esset scriptum comprehensum esset ne cui ingenuae quae minor quam annorum XX neve cui ingenuo qui minor quam annorum XXV esset auctorare se operasve suas ad? harenam? scaenamve s locare permitteretur nisi qui eorum a divo Augusto aut ab Tiberio Caesare Augusto in coniectus esset <qui eorum> is qui ita coniecisset auctorare se operasve suas locare si eum divus Augustus aut Tiberius Caesar Augustus ad larem redducendum esset statuissent id servari placere praeterquam \n
EDCS-63900074.translation: A Senate decree, written in the portico adjoining the Temple of Apollo on the Palatine. Present were the quaestors Caius Ateius Capito and Aulus Didius Gallus. The consuls Marcus Silanus and Lucius Norbanus Balbus presented the following motion: concerning the matter referred to them regarding women's licentiousness, or concerning those who, against the dignity of their rank, appeared on the stage or in the arena, or solicited for it, as is established by the Senate decrees of previous years that were circumvented by deceit, which diminished the majesty of the Senate, what did they decree should be done about it? They decreed as follows: It is decreed that no senator's son, daughter, grandson, granddaughter, great-grandson, or great-granddaughter, nor anyone whose father or paternal or maternal grandfather or brother, nor any woman whose husband or father or paternal or maternal grandfather or brother had the right to view from the equestrian seats, shall bring them onto the stage or solicit them to fight in the arena, or take up the trainer's sword. Nor shall anyone offer himself, or hire himself out, nor shall any of those mentioned above provide services for such activities. Furthermore, it is decreed that measures be taken more diligently to prevent those who have the right to sit in the equestrian seats from circumventing this decree, either by accepting public disgrace or by being condemned by a notorious judgment. And subsequently, when they had abandoned their position, they voluntarily offered themselves from the equestrian seats or appeared on the stage. And none of those mentioned above shall have the right to a funeral (libitinam) if they act against the dignity of their rank, except if someone has already performed on the stage or hired out their services for the arena, whether born of an actor, gladiator, trainer, or pimp. And concerning the Senate decree, which was made when Manius Lepidus and Titus Statilius Taurus were consuls, and was recorded and inscribed, it is decreed that no freeborn woman under 20 years of age, nor any freeborn man under 25 years of age, shall be permitted to offer themselves or their services for the arena or stage, unless they have been enlisted by the deified Augustus or Tiberius Caesar Augustus, and those who were enlisted by him, if the deified Augustus or Tiberius Caesar Augustus had decided that they should be returned to the household gods, that this should be observed, except...
EDCS-63900074.summary: A senatorial decree from approximately 30 AD, detailing regulations against senators' family members and young freeborn individuals participating in theatrical or gladiatorial performances, likely issued to uphold the dignity of the senatorial class.

EDCS-64000043.translation: Valeria Bathyllis, freedwoman of Lucius, lived for 50 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-64000043.summary: A funerary epitaph for a freedwoman, recording her name, length of life, and burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000044.translation: Here lies Eros. He set [this up] for his deserving wife.
EDCS-64000044.summary: A funerary epitaph with a dedication to a deceased wife, identifying the deceased and the surviving spouse, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000045.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vebeius Hilario lived for 21 years. He is buried here. Aesius (his father) and Aemilia Hilara (his mother).
EDCS-64000045.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young man, recording his name, age, burial place, and his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000046.translation: Caltia Praetoria lived for 37 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-64000046.summary: A funerary epitaph for a woman, recording her name, age, and burial place, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000047.translation: Here lies [---], slave of Cornelius, who lived for 26 years. To his most dutiful brother. Dionysius (father) lived for [---] years. Aphrodisius lived for [---] years. Dionysius lived for [---] years.
EDCS-64000047.summary: A fragmented funerary epitaph recording the death of a slave and the ages of several individuals, likely family members, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000048.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Pacilia Helpis lived for 52 years. She is buried here. To [my] deserving mother.
EDCS-64000048.summary: A funerary epitaph for a mother, recording her name, age, burial place, and the dedication from her child, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64000049.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Iulia Thalia, daughter of Spurius, lived for 4 years and 20 days. She is buried here. Flavia Athenais lived for 35 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-64000049.summary: A funerary epitaph for two individuals, one a young child and the other an adult woman, recording their names, ages, and burial places, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64300049.translation: Amia, freedwoman of Dasimia, lies here. Greetings and farewell.
EDCS-64300049.summary: A funerary epitaph for a freedwoman, recording her name and a farewell greeting, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64300050.translation: Gaius Ventidius, son of Gaius, from Brundisium, lived 2 years and 16 days. Here lies Gaius Ventidius Bales, a veteran *scaripharius* of the 3rd cohort, 1st centuria of Lucius Valerius. Here lies.
EDCS-64300050.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Gaius Ventidius Bales, a veteran soldier, who died at a young age.

EDCS-64300051.summary: This is a tombstone recording the short life of a young girl named Iulia Ianuaria.

EDCS-64400078.translation: Liburnia Primigenia lived 45 years. Bisellius Restitutus placed this for his well-deserving wife, who is buried here.
EDCS-64400078.summary: This tombstone records the death of Liburnia Primigenia at age 45 and was erected by her husband, Bisellius Restitutus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-64400524.translation: To the departed spirits. Claudius Herma lived 18 years. Here he lies.
EDCS-64400524.summary: This tombstone records the death of Claudius Herma at age 18.

EDCS-64400525.translation: To the departed spirits. Sin[---] lived 30 years.
EDCS-64400525.summary: This tombstone records the death of an individual named Sin[---] at age 30.
