EDCS-02900098.translation: To Castricius, son of Marcius, and to Castra, daughter of Quintus, wife of Novius. [---]
EDCS-02900098.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates a monument to Castricius and his wife Castra.

EDCS-02900099.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Antonia Ianuaria, his wife, and Fructus and Maxima and Maximus and [---] made this for their deserving father.
EDCS-02900099.summary: This funerary inscription records children, Antonia Ianuaria, Fructus, Maxima, and Maximus, dedicating a monument to their deserving father.

EDCS-02900100.translation: Herennia Secunda made this for her deserving husband, Staio Felix.
EDCS-02900100.summary: This funerary inscription records Herennia Secunda dedicating a monument to her deserving husband, Staio Felix.

EDCS-02900101.translation: Iucundus, freedman of Publius, an Augustalis, [erected this] for himself, for his son Publius Galeria Rufinus, aged 29, designated magistrate, and for [---]
EDCS-02900101.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Iucundus for himself and his son, who was a designated magistrate.

EDCS-02900102.translation: To our Lords Emperors Flavius Valentinianus, Valens, and Gratianus, ever Augusti, born for the good of the Republic, [we erect this] 5000 paces
EDCS-02900102.summary: This is a milestone or boundary marker dedicated to the emperors Valentinian, Valens, and Gratian.

EDCS-02900105.translation: Marcus Avillius Maxsimus Caesianus [erected this] for his brothers Avillius Acoristus and Caesia Cai, freedwoman, his mother
EDCS-02900105.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates brothers and their mother, set up by Marcus Avillius Maxsimus Caesianus.

EDCS-02900119.translation: Caesonia Potita, freedwoman of Titus, made this for her husband Dioscurides, freedman of Caius, deserving, while she was alive. 12 feet in front, [---] feet in the ground
EDCS-02900119.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by a wife to her deserving husband, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-02900123.translation: Marcus Aemilius Lepidus, son of Marcus, consul for the 11th time
EDCS-02900123.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to an official career, mentioning Marcus Aemilius Lepidus who served as consul multiple times.

EDCS-02900133.translation: The college of Vulcan had the work of constructing [this] carried out
EDCS-02900133.summary: This inscription records the construction work undertaken by the college or association of Vulcan.

EDCS-02900134.translation: Lucius Ard lived 8 years and 3 months
EDCS-02900134.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Ard.

EDCS-03000112.translation: Probus (father) and Secunda (mother) placed [this] for Ianuaria
EDCS-03000112.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their daughter Ianuaria.

EDCS-03000116.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Minatia, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-03300071.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a property marker or a dedication related to the Cordii family.

EDCS-03300073.translation: Lupula, daughter of Salvia Occia, placed this for her dearest mother.
EDCS-03300073.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a daughter for her mother, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-03300075.translation: To Gaius Julius Proculus, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe, who served as consul twice, quindecimvir for sacred rites, fetial of the province of Lugudunensis, praetor, tribune of the plebs, and moneyer, the people of Larinum granted this site, free of charge, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-03300075.summary: This is a dedication honoring Gaius Julius Proculus for his extensive career in public office, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-03300079.translation: Numerius, centurion of the legion, for himself.
EDCS-03300079.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Numerius, a centurion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03300081.summary: This is a tombstone or dedication fragment mentioning Tiberius Claudius Fortunatus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700055.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to a freedwoman or daughter named Charito, associated with Marcus Fabius, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700059.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aeneas, a slave belonging to Betilienus Marci, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700060.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to Philipus, associated with Betilienus, possibly dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700061.summary: This is a funerary epitaph or dedication for Lucius Albius Dama, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700067.translation: For the safety and victory of our lords, the Emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, in his seventh year of tribunician power, consul for the second time, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, in his year of tribunician power.
EDCS-03700067.summary: This is a dedication honoring Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, likely dating to around 200 CE based on the consular and tribunician power references.

EDCS-03700074.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating that Anteros is buried here, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700075.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Onius Dionysius.

EDCS-03700076.translation: Faustus lived for 2 years and is laid here.
EDCS-03700076.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Faustus, who died at the age of two.

EDCS-03700078.translation: Helvius Dionysius lived for 55 years and is laid here.
EDCS-03700078.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Helvius Dionysius, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-03700080.translation: Dignitas made this for her dearest grandson Caius Naevius Priamus, who lived for 30 years, in honor of the departed spirits; he is laid here.
EDCS-03700080.summary: This tombstone, dedicated by Dignitas to her grandson Caius Naevius Priamus who died at 30, is an offering to the departed spirits.

EDCS-03700084.summary: This tombstone records the lifespan of Thalia, who lived for 28 years.

EDCS-03700090.summary: This inscription, likely a tombstone, commemorates Alexander the faithful.

EDCS-03700102.translation: To the departed spirits. Septimia Maxuma, daughter of Titus, for her most dutiful mother, who lived 56 years, 5 months, and 20 days, well-deserving, with the respect due to her. Traveler, pause, pious one, under the spreading shade of the oak. Here the children of the gods placed me. You do not know what evils of fate befell me, what wounds of destiny. Sweetly, through love of her husband, always remember Septimia, the chaste mother, whose offspring perpetuates her beauty.
EDCS-03700102.summary: This tombstone is dedicated to the memory of Septimia Maxuma's pious mother by her children, lamenting her fate and praising her beauty and chastity.

EDCS-04900039.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Lucius Marcius Saturninus.

EDCS-04900041.translation: To Gaius Herennius Lupercus, patron of all the citizens of Larinum, for your merits and noble origin, we offer this tablet of patronage, which we beg you to accept with a willing heart; for we desire to return to your patronage and beg you to receive us under your protection, our excellent lord. Accept it happily. Done by the consuls Flavius Leontius and Bonosus, on the Kalends of April.
EDCS-04900041.summary: The citizens of Larinum offer a tablet of patronage to their esteemed patron Gaius Herennius Lupercus, acknowledging his merits and seeking his protection.

EDCS-05000128.translation: Hail, Princeps! Theodorus (freedman) for himself and for his patron Marcus Creperius Princeps and for Creperia Regilla.
EDCS-05000128.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication made by the freedman Theodorus for himself, his patron Marcus Creperius Princeps, and Creperia Regilla.

EDCS-05200080.summary: This inscription records the name of an individual, Marcus Octavius Eudemus, likely a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-07000307.summary: This inscription records the age at death of an individual named Agathas, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-07000310.summary: This inscription records the age at death of an individual named Tutoria Daphnis, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-07000311.summary: This inscription records the age at death of an individual named Onesiphorus, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-07000314.summary: This inscription records the age at death of an individual named Irenia Tigris, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-07000320.summary: This inscription records the age at death of an individual named Servus, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-07000325.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, Imperator for the 3rd time, Consul for the 2nd time, Proconsul, Father of the Fatherland, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-07000325.summary: This inscription is a formal dedication to Emperor Septimius Severus, honoring his titles and authority, likely erected by a local government body.

EDCS-07000327.translation: To the departed spirits, for Gania Felicitas. Publius Publicius Oristanus made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-07000327.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband, Publius Publicius Oristanus, to his deceased wife, Gania Felicitas, honoring her memory.

EDCS-07000328.translation: To the departed spirits, for Lucius Trebellius Domitius Verus.
EDCS-07000328.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to Lucius Trebellius Domitius Verus, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-07400199.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelia Pomponiana, who lived for one year and four days. Cornelius Paulinus, her father, placed this for his well-deserving daughter.
EDCS-07400199.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a father, Cornelius Paulinus, to his young daughter, Cornelia Pomponiana, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-07400223.translation: To our lord Flavius Claudius Julianus, pious, happy, unconquered, and triumphant, always Augustus, born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-07400223.summary: This inscription is a dedication or honorific title for the emperor Flavius Claudius Julianus, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-07600164.summary: This is a very short inscription identifying a person named Vibius Crispus who held the office of curator, likely related to local administration or a religious college.

EDCS-08200706.translation: Lucius Iulius Julianus Priscus, veteran of the 12th Legion 'Fulminata', lived 75 years, is buried here.
EDCS-08200706.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and burial of Lucius Iulius Julianus Priscus, a veteran of a specific Roman legion, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08200721.translation: To Publicia, daughter of Marcus, Firmilla, to whom a place was granted by decree of the decurions. Marcus Publicius Quartinus, by whose dedication places were granted by her to individual decurions for 800 sesterces, and to individual townspeople and residents for 300 sesterces.
EDCS-08200721.summary: This inscription records a land grant for burial plots within a municipality, detailing the costs associated with the grants and naming the individuals involved, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200728.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcia Zmaragdis lived 42 years, is buried here. Sextius Celsinus to his deserving wife.
EDCS-08200728.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcia Zmaragdis, erected by her husband Sextius Celsinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200729.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription recording the name and lifespan of Titus Publicius Primogenes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200731.translation: To the Genius of the decurions and the people. Titus Pollionius, son of Titus, [dedicated this] from a gold pound, while he held the unpaid office of augur, along with his freedmen and public slaves.
EDCS-08200731.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Titus Pollionius to the Genius of the local council and populace, likely associated with his tenure as an augur, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200732.translation: To the divine spirit of Atilia Dionysia.
EDCS-08200732.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial to Atilia Dionysia, likely referring to her 'genius' or divine spirit, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200802.translation: Crispia Secunda, daughter of Marcus, lived 30 years. Lucius Calventius Proculus, son of Lucius, lived [age missing]. Lucius Calventius Modestus, son of Lucius, lived 55 years.
EDCS-08200802.summary: This inscription is a funerary text recording the names and ages of Crispia Secunda and Lucius Calventius Modestus, and the name of Lucius Calventius Proculus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200814.summary: This is a very short funerary inscription identifying a person named Felix who is buried here, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08200886.summary: A funerary inscription from Severa to her devoted son, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200932.translation: Theodosius the bishop carried out the construction.
EDCS-08200932.summary: An inscription recording that Bishop Theodosius commissioned a construction project, likely from the late Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-08200935.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Allia made this for her deserving husband, Argentarius, for 300 years (a symbolic expression of longevity or a debt).
EDCS-08200935.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Argentarius by his wife Allia, possibly indicating a long-lasting marriage or a symbolic vow, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200958.translation: A fortunate man. After the consulship of our lords Arcadius III and Honorius II Augusti, on the sixth day before the Kalends of April. With Valerius Fortunatus and Aurelius Silvanus, quinquennales, reporting, a decision was made concerning the co-option of a patron, Flavius Successus, an honored and distinguished man, because he protects and favors each individual and all with such familiarity and his industry. It was therefore decreed that a bronze tablet, inscribed, be offered by the living principales and dedicated among the household gods.
EDCS-08200958.summary: An inscription from the late 4th or early 5th century AD recording the co-option of Flavius Successus as a patron of a community, likely in honor of his beneficial actions.

EDCS-08200959.translation: Data, freedwoman of Sextilia, a woman, and Data, freedwoman of Publius, their beloved daughter, 14 years old, had this made at her own expense.
EDCS-08200959.summary: A funerary epitaph for Data, a young girl, set up by her parents, who were freedpeople, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200963.translation: Barbarus lived 1 year and 6 months. He lies here.
EDCS-08200963.summary: A simple tombstone recording the name and age of a child, Barbarus, who died in infancy, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200964.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iustus, his patron, placed this for Clodius Eutyches, a good freedman of Titus, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-08200964.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a patron for his deceased freedman, Clodius Eutyches, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200966.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Iedius Epaphroditus lived 12 years. He lies here. Titus Flavius Da[us?], his brother, placed this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-08200966.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy, Iedius Epaphroditus, set up by his brother, Titus Flavius Da[us?], from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200967.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Flavia Faustina lived 4 years. Her mother placed this for her daughter.
EDCS-08200967.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl, Flavia Faustina, set up by her mother, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200968.translation: Grittia Trophima lived 25 years. She lies here.
EDCS-08200968.summary: A tombstone recording the name and age of Grittia Trophima, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08200970.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Silvanus, likely a child.

EDCS-08200985.translation: To the departed spirits. Aemilia Charia lived for 9 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08200985.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Aemilia Charia, who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-08200986.translation: To the departed spirits. Barinus made this for his well-deserving mother, Antonia Euthycia, who lived for 90 years.
EDCS-08200986.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Barinus for his mother, Antonia Euthycia, who died at the age of 90.

EDCS-08200987.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Appalenus Aeliofon lived for 80 years. Lacaena [made this] for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-08200987.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely set up by Lacaena for her patron Marcus Appalenus Aeliofon, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-08200989.translation: To the departed spirits. Apphiadis lived for 45 years. Marcus Appalenus Phoebus made this for his well-deserving mother. Ades Palenus and Phoebus made this for their son.
EDCS-08200989.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Apphiadis at age 45, and is dedicated by her son, Marcus Appalenus Phoebus, and also by Ades Palenus and Phoebus for their son.

EDCS-08200991.translation: Caecilia Phoebe lived for 40 years. She is buried here. Marcus Caecilius Felicio made this for his most sweet wife, well-deserving, whom cruel fate separated from us so quickly.
EDCS-08200991.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Caecilius Felicio to his wife, Caecilia Phoebe, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-08200993.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelia lived for 25 years. She is buried here.
EDCS-08200993.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Cornelia at the age of 25.

EDCS-08200996.translation: Lucius Lucanius made this for his well-deserving son, Sextio, who lived for 15 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-08200996.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Lucius Lucanius to his son, Sextio, who died at the age of 15.

EDCS-08200997.translation: To the departed spirits. For Lupulae, her well-deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-08200997.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a fellow slave to Lupulae.

EDCS-08201000.translation: Gaius Tarutius Philomusus, freedman of Gaius, for himself and Tarutia Medona, freedwoman of Gaius, and Acratus.
EDCS-08201000.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a self-dedication and dedications to his freedpersons by Gaius Tarutius Philomusus.

EDCS-08201002.translation: Utilis lived 22 years. His father placed him here.
EDCS-08201002.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Utilis and commending him to his father's care.

EDCS-08201005.translation: The body of Dumnana, daughter of Muscatus, and her mother Dumnana rests in this tomb. You who read this, pray for them. Whoever damages this tomb shall pay 200 gold pieces to the public treasury.
EDCS-08201005.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Dumnana and her mother, includes a prayer for them, and sets a penalty for damaging the tomb.

EDCS-08201017.translation: To our most noble Caesar, our lord Flavius Julianus, Annius Antiochus, a most accomplished man, corrector, devoted to his piety.
EDCS-08201017.summary: This is an honorary inscription dedicating an effigy or statue to Emperor Flavius Julianus (Julian the Apostate), likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-08201024.translation: Marcus Voconius Firmus willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-08201024.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Marcus Voconius Firmus fulfilling a vow to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-08201027.translation: Pharnaces. Lucius Veratius Restitutus and Anircasil.
EDCS-08201027.summary: This inscription seems to record names, possibly indicating a joint dedication or ownership, but the context is very brief.

EDCS-08201032.translation: For the illustrious and revered memory of Flavius Theodosius, father of our Invincible and everlasting Emperor Theodosius, the people of Apulia and Calabria erected a gilded equestrian statue, according to their vow and devotion, under the supervision and completion of Flavius Sexion, a most perfect man, corrector of Apulia and Calabria.
EDCS-08201032.summary: This inscription records the erection of a gilded equestrian statue in honor of Flavius Theodosius, father of Emperor Theodosius, by the people of Apulia and Calabria, likely in the late 4th or early 5th century CE.

EDCS-08201045.translation: To Titus Avidiaccus, freedman of Titus, Augustalis.
EDCS-08201045.summary: This is an honorary inscription for Titus Avidiaccus, a freedman and an Augustalis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201058.translation: Avillia Prima, freedwoman of Publius. To Lucius Lucilius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe. Avillia Tertia, freedwoman of Publius. Avillia Potestas, freedwoman. Avillia Lasciva, freedwoman.
EDCS-08201058.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing several individuals, likely family members or patrons and freedwomen, with their names and relationships.

EDCS-08201059.summary: This is a simple dedication or funerary inscription mentioning Gaius Baberius and Baberia.

EDCS-08201065.translation: To FICRII, to Blassidia, to Dia.
EDCS-08201065.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or funerary marker for FICRII, Blassidia, and Dia.

EDCS-08201069.translation: Chresimus, freedman of Gaius Cocius (and) of the wife, (and) Dius, freedman of Nonia.
EDCS-08201069.summary: This inscription records the dedication by Chresimus and Dius, freedmen, likely to themselves or associated patrons.

EDCS-08201072.translation: Publius Dasimius Zeno, freedman of Publius; Publius Dasimius Antaeus, freedman of Publius; Publius Dasimius Hilarus, freedman of Publius (plural).
EDCS-08201072.summary: This inscription lists three individuals, all freedmen of a Publius Dasimius, likely related and commemorating their association.

EDCS-08201075.translation: Pieris, freedwoman of Egnatia, for herself and for Niphadi, freedwoman of Manius Aquillius Ballista.
EDCS-08201075.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Pieris for herself and for Niphadi, a freedwoman of Manius Aquillius Ballista.

EDCS-08201085.translation: For Lucius Lepidius Suavis, freedman of (the wife); for Venusta, daughter of Lucius, of Lepidia; for Nysa, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-08201085.summary: This inscription seems to be a funerary dedication for several individuals, including freedmen and a daughter of Lepidia.

EDCS-08201087.translation: Licinia Sosima, freedwoman of Marcus; Marcus Licinius Antigonus, freedman of Marcus, brother.
EDCS-08201087.summary: This epitaph commemorates Licinia Sosima and Marcus Licinius Antigonus, siblings and freedmen of a Marcus Licinius.

EDCS-08201089.translation: Medella, daughter of Dasmus, was buried on the third day before the Kalends of January, when Gaius Piso and Manius Acilius were consuls.
EDCS-08201089.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial date of Medella, daughter of Dasmus, and the consuls for that year.

EDCS-08201090.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavius Fortunatianus made this for his well-deserving wife, Mettia Marullina, who lived 19 years, 9 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-08201090.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Mettia Marullina and was erected by her husband, Flavius Fortunatianus.

EDCS-08201095.translation: Gaius Poblicius Eros, freedman of the people; Sabellia Salvia, freedwoman of the wife; Gaius Poblicius Felix.
EDCS-08201095.summary: This inscription lists three individuals, likely freedmen and associated family members, possibly commemorating their status or a shared monument.

EDCS-08201101.translation: Tutoria Ammia, freedwoman of the wife; Lucius Tutorius Protogenes, freedman of the wife, (son of) Lucius Ennius, son of Lucius.
EDCS-08201101.summary: This inscription lists members of the Tutoria family, including freedmen, likely related to a Lucius Ennius.

EDCS-08201106.translation: ...culae lived 20 years, Vellaeae Lalage, freedwoman of Sextus.
EDCS-08201106.summary: This fragmented inscription records the lifespan of Vellaeae Lalage, a freedwoman of Sextus, and possibly another individual named ...culae.

EDCS-08201110.translation: The deposition of the holy memory of Acacius, who lived for more or less 35 years and died in peace, during the consulship of Eutharicus Cillicanus.
EDCS-08201110.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and age of Acacius in the year of the consul Eutharicus Cillicanus.

EDCS-08201111.translation: While our lord Jesus Christ reigns, here rests in peace the holy memory of Paulus, who lived 45 years, 3 months, and 15 days, and was buried in peace on the 7th day before the Ides of September, in the eighth indiction, during the consulship of Mavoultus.
EDCS-08201111.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and burial details of Paulus, including his age and burial date, within a Christian context.

EDCS-08201112.translation: In the name of the Lord, here rests in peace the good memory of Oliba, and she was buried on the 18th day before the Kalends of July, after the consulship of Basilius, a man of the highest distinction, in the sixth indiction. Here rests in peace the holy memory of Eusebius, primicerius, who lived for more or less 40 years, and was buried in peace on the 5th day before the Kalends of June, eight times after the consulship of Basilius, a man of the highest distinction.
EDCS-08201112.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Oliba and Eusebius, including their ages, burial dates, and references to the consulship of Basilius and various indictions.

EDCS-08201113.translation: He rests in peace, the holy memory of Iustus, who lived 45 years, died in peace on the 6th day before the Kalends of August, during the consulship of Rusticius, a man of the highest distinction.
EDCS-08201113.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death and age of Iustus, who died during the consulship of Rusticius.

EDCS-08201116.translation: To Claudius Julianus Augustus Aeternus, restorer of the Roman world and eternal prince, from the order of Acerra.
EDCS-08201116.summary: A dedication by the municipal council of Acerra to the emperor Claudius Julianus, likely an honorary inscription.

EDCS-08201121.translation: Wars waged from the Marsic War: Publius Petinius and Publius Publilius, aediles; Lucius Sempronius and Lucius Scribonius, from the Kalends of July. Paulus Aemilius and Caius Memmius, from the Kalends of November. Marcus Herennius, Illyric war from the Kalends of July to the Kalends of July. Quintus Larcius and Caius Rumeius, duoviri; Marcus Metilius and Lucius Annaeus, aediles. This year quaestors were created from the Kalends of July to the Kalends of July: Caius Sulpicius and Caius Salvius Bubulcus, quaestors. Imperator Caesar (consulship) II, Lucius Volcacius, from the Kalends of January. Lucius Autronius, from the Kalends of May. Lucius Flavius and Caius Fonteius, from the Kalends of July. Marcus Acilius, from the Kalends of September. Lucius Vinucius, from the Kalends of October. Quintus Laronius, from the Kalends of July to the Kalends of July. Caius Aemilius and Quintus Pontienus, duoviri; Caius Valerius and Caius Turpilius, aediles. Lucius Livius Ligus and Lucius Cornelius, quaestors. Cnaeus Domitius and Caius Sosius, from the Kalends of July. Lucius Cornelius, from the Kalends of November. Marcus Valerius, from the Kalends of July to the Kalends of September. Praefects: Titus Licinius and Lucius Cornelius, from the Kalends of September to the Kalends of February. Caius Plotius and Caius Annaeus, duoviri, from the Kalends of July to the Kalends of February. Caius Annius and Sextus Vettius, aediles, from the Kalends of July to the Kalends of July. Lucius Scutarius and Marcus Calpurnius, quaestors. Imperator Caesar (consulship) III, Marcus Valerius, from the Kalends of May. Marcus Titius, from the Kalends of October. Cnaeus Pompeius, Actian War from the Kalends of February to the Kalends of July. Sextus Titius and Lucius Geminius, duoviri; Publius Sextilius and Quintus Luccius, aediles, from the Kalends of July to the Kalends of January. Lucius Scutarius and Titus Sepunius, duoviri. Titus Antonius and Marcus Valerius Messalla, aediles. Lucius Annius and Caius Valerius, quaestors. Imperator Caesar (consulship) IIII, Marcus Licinius, from the Kalends of July. Caius Antistius, Alexandrian War from the Ides of September. Marcus Tullius, from the Kalends of November. Lucius Saenius, from the Kalends of January to the Kalends of January. Lucius Cornelius and Quintus Vettius, duoviri; Caius Cassius and Caius Geminius Niger, quaestors. Imperator Caesar V, Sextus Appuleius and Lucius Oppius and Lucius Livius, duoviri quinquennales. Marcus Narius and Caius Mestrius, aediles. Quintus Plaestinus and Sextus Fadius, quaestors. Imperator Caesar VI, Marcus Agrippa, also with censorship power, performed the lustrum from the Kalends of January to the Kalends of July. Lucius Gavius and Caius Geminius Niger, duoviri; Quintus Caetronius and Caius Clodius, aediles.
EDCS-08201121.summary: This lengthy inscription is a list of magistrates (consuls, duoviri, aediles, quaestors, prefects) and significant military events (Marsic War, Illyric War, Actian War, Alexandrian War) for multiple years, likely a chronicle or calendar of public offices and wars.

EDCS-08201122.translation: To the August Lares, Gaius Avittius Epaphroditus, Augustalis magistrate.
EDCS-08201122.summary: A dedication to the Augusti Lares by Gaius Avittius Epaphroditus, who held the office of Augustalis.

EDCS-08201125.translation: To the Manes, Niceni Sentia Rufina, her son Leonicus made this for his mother.
EDCS-08201125.summary: A funerary dedication to the deified spirits (Manes) by Leonicus for his mother, Sentia Rufina.

EDCS-08201127.translation: The freedwoman of Lucius, Firmilla, daughter of the freedman Ludinus, fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08201127.summary: A dedication recording a vow fulfilled by Firmilla, a freedwoman, likely for a deity.

EDCS-08201128.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Parthicus, in his fourth consulship, father of the fatherland, proconsul, son of the deified Severus, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201128.summary: An official dedication by the decurions to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, listing his titles and consulships.

EDCS-08201129.translation: Aulus Aelius Restitutianus, a man of highest rank, corrector of Apulia and Calabria, dedicated this in honor of the distinguished city of the Venusines.
EDCS-08201129.summary: An imperial official, Aulus Aelius Restitutianus, dedicated a monument to the city of Venusia, likely in the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08201137.translation: Quintus Ovius, son of Ovius, tribune of the plebs, paved the road.
EDCS-08201137.summary: A tribune of the plebs, Quintus Ovius, paved a road, likely in the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-08201138.translation: Quintus Ravelios, Publius Cominios, son of Publius, and Lucius Malios, son of Caius, quaestors, consulted the senate and they decreed that it should be either sacred or public.
EDCS-08201138.summary: Two quaestors, Quintus Ravelios and Publius Cominios, and Lucius Malios consulted the senate on the status of a piece of land, likely in the Republican period.

EDCS-08201148.translation: The heirs of Minatia, daughter of Caius, left for the colonists of the colony of Venusia, from her inheritance, 250,000 sesterces, in full without deduction of the inheritance tax, on the condition that my heirs [fulfilled this legacy].
EDCS-08201148.summary: A will concerning a bequest of 250,000 sesterces from Minatia to the colonists of Venusia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201150.translation: Aulus Ivellius, also as aedile, decorated Isis in honor.
EDCS-08201150.summary: Aulus Ivellius, serving as aedile, decorated a statue or temple of Isis, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201155.translation: To Kaesonius Battius, quaestor, duovir, and aedile, publicly.
EDCS-08201155.summary: A public dedication to Kaesonius Battius, who held multiple magistracies, likely in the Roman colony of Venusia during the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201159.translation: To the gods of the underworld. The colleagues of the water carriers and his associates erected this for Sextus Peduceus Facetus.
EDCS-08201159.summary: A funerary monument erected by a collegium (guild) for Sextus Peduceus Facetus, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201164.translation: The gladiatorial troupe of Caius Salvius Capito, son of Aulus. Here lies Equites Mandatus Rab, 3 crowns, 2 Thracians; Secundus Pompei, 2 crowns, 2 Thracians; Caius Masonius, 7 crowns, 4 Thracians; Phileros Domiti, 12 crowns, 11 Thracians; Optatus Salvi, a recruit; Caius Alfidius, a recruit; Murmillones Quintus Cleppius, a recruit; Iulius, a recruit, a retiarius, 10 crowns.
EDCS-08201164.summary: A list of gladiators and their victories, likely from a gladiatorial school or tomb, possibly from the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-08201165.translation: Oceanus, an archer, 6 crowns, 4 victories; Dorus Pisces, a veles, 2 victories; Mycter ofilius, a hoplomachus, 1 victory; Phaeder Avili, a Thracian, 12 crowns, 8 victories; Hilario Arrius, 7 crowns, 5 victories; Aquila Pisces, 12 crowns, 6 victories; Quartio Munil, 1 victory; Caius Perpernius, a recruit; Murmillones Amicus Munil, 1 victory; Quintus Fabius, 5 crowns, 3 victories; Eleuther Munil, 1 victory; Caius Memmius, 3 crowns, 2 victories; Anteros Munil, 2 victories; Atlans Don, 4 victories, 1 crown; essedarius Inclutus Arrius, 5 crowns, 2 victories; Samnite Strabo Don, 3 crowns, 2 victories; retiarius Caius Clodius, 2 victories; scissor Marcus Caecilius, a recruit; gallus Quintus Granius, a recruit.
EDCS-08201165.summary: A list of gladiators, their specializations, and victories, likely from a gladiatorial school or tomb, possibly from the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-08201167.translation: Chrysantus, the chief actor, to Hygieia.
EDCS-08201167.summary: A dedication to the goddess Hygieia by Chrysantus, a performer, likely in the Roman period.

EDCS-08201170.translation: To his freedman Philargyrus, the perfumer, and his household, he manumitted them and the money
EDCS-08201170.summary: This fragmentary inscription seems to record the manumission of a freedman and his household, along with funds, likely related to a will or estate.

EDCS-08201172.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Bratius Prasantis, IIIVSDI, actor, to his wife SOP rus.
EDCS-08201172.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Bratius Prasantis, identified as an actor, and his wife.

EDCS-08201174.translation: Marcus Aninius, son of Spurius. In width 12 feet, in depth 12 feet. A common plot belonging to Titus Masus, freedman of Titus.
EDCS-08201174.summary: This inscription defines the dimensions of a burial plot (12x12 feet) belonging to Marcus Aninius and Titus Masus, likely for funerary purposes.

EDCS-08201175.translation: Sextus Aninius, son of Sextus. In width 12 feet, in depth 12 feet.
EDCS-08201175.summary: This inscription specifies the dimensions of a burial plot (12x12 feet) belonging to Sextus Aninius.

EDCS-08201176.translation: Annia, daughter of Cinna. Lucius Cornelius Cinna, son of Publius. Publius Cornelius Cinna, son of Lucius. Lucius Cornelius Cinna, son of Lucius. Horatia.
EDCS-08201176.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, possibly family members, with their patronymic or gentilicium and cognomen, potentially related to inheritance or ownership.

EDCS-08201177.translation: The property of Marcus Antonius Briso, son of Marcus.
EDCS-08201177.summary: This inscription marks the property belonging to Marcus Antonius Briso.

EDCS-08201178.translation: Gaius Antistius Ergasimus, freedman of Menophilus, erected this for himself and for his son Comitus, aged six, who lies here, while still alive.
EDCS-08201178.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Gaius Antistius Ergasimus for himself and his young son, Comitus.

EDCS-08201179.summary: This is a short inscription identifying Antistia, daughter of Gaius, likely a tombstone or boundary marker.

EDCS-08201180.translation: Marcus Antonius Eutactus erected this for Antonia Nymphe, freedwoman of Marcus, and for himself and Antonia Telete.
EDCS-08201180.summary: This funerary inscription records that Marcus Antonius Eutactus set up the monument for his freedwoman Antonia Nymphe and for himself and Antonia Telete.

EDCS-08201181.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription mentioning the gentilicium Appius, possibly part of a name or a dedication.

EDCS-08201182.translation: To the Divine Manes. Appuleia Panthera erected this for her well-deserving husband Iunius Primio and for herself.
EDCS-08201182.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-08201184.translation: To Atilia The, her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08201184.summary: A funerary dedication to a wife.

EDCS-08201185.translation: Castor, freedman of Caius Salvius Capito, made this for his dearest wife Babullia Anthi, and for himself and his children.
EDCS-08201185.summary: A funerary monument erected by a husband for his wife, himself, and his children.

EDCS-08201186.summary: An incomplete funerary dedication for a person named -lio, son of Lucius.

EDCS-08201187.summary: An incomplete inscription, likely a funerary or public dedication.

EDCS-08201188.translation: Marcus Bruttius Primus, freedman of Marcus, erected this for himself and for Probata, freedwoman of Alfita, measuring 12 feet in front and 12 feet in depth.
EDCS-08201188.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman for himself and another freedwoman, specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-08201189.translation: A plot was granted by decree of the decurions to the freedwoman Spodria, freedwoman of Lucius Vibellius Lucius, and to Vivin, son of Quintus Cidoni, son of Quintus, and to the woman Caesia.
EDCS-08201189.summary: A grant of burial land by local officials.

EDCS-08201190.translation: Fortunatus erected this for his well-deserving wife Callonia Neapoli ana.
EDCS-08201190.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband for his wife.

EDCS-08201192.translation: Clonidius Fortunatus and Calvius Ponus erected this for their sister Calvia Successa and for [---] LXICONI.
EDCS-08201192.summary: A funerary dedication by siblings for their sister and another individual.

EDCS-08201193.translation: To Marcus Caninius Modestus, and to Baebia Accepta, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-08201193.summary: An inscription likely recording funerary dedications to two individuals, one of whom is identified as a freedwoman.

EDCS-08201194.translation: Cascia Capriola set this up for her beloved Silvanus, her foster-child and home-born.
EDCS-08201194.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Cascia Capriola for her foster-child and home-born Silvanus.

EDCS-08201195.translation: His companions set this up for Lucius Cassius Panychus.
EDCS-08201195.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the companions of Lucius Cassius Panychus.

EDCS-08201196.translation: To the departed spirits of Publius Catinius, son of Publius.
EDCS-08201196.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Publius Catinius, son of Publius.

EDCS-08201197.translation: To the departed spirits. Cornelia Victoria set this up for her most dutiful daughter, Claudia Cyrilla, daughter of Tiberius.
EDCS-08201197.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Cornelia Victoria for her daughter, Claudia Cyrilla.

EDCS-08201198.translation: Claudius Firminus set this up for his father, Tiberius Claudius Maximus, son of Tiberius.
EDCS-08201198.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Claudius Firminus for his father, Tiberius Claudius Maximus.

EDCS-08201199.translation: To Lucius Clodius, son of Lucius, (and) Horatia.
EDCS-08201199.summary: This inscription records Lucius Clodius, son of Lucius, and Horatia, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-08201200.translation: Copiosa, freedwoman of Cominia, (dedicated this) to Drimacus.
EDCS-08201200.summary: This inscription records Copiosa, freedwoman of Cominia, and Drimacus, possibly a funerary dedication or a record of manumission.

EDCS-08201201.translation: Consia Maximilla, daughter of Quintus, lies here; she lived 7 years. With her parents alive, she occupied 14 feet in front and 13 feet in the field.
EDCS-08201201.summary: This is a tombstone for Consia Maximilla, who died at age 7, with dimensions of the burial plot.

EDCS-08201202.translation: Constantine, while you were prudent, you ordered this tomb to be made for yourself.
EDCS-08201202.summary: This inscription records Constantine's self-funded tomb construction while he was still alive.

EDCS-08201203.translation: Creperia Hiera, daughter (of Caius Arrius Iustus), set this up for her parents, Marcus Creperius Iustus, son of Marcus, and Salvia Veneria.
EDCS-08201203.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Creperia Hiera for her parents, Marcus Creperius Iustus and Salvia Veneria.

EDCS-08201204.translation: To Quintus Drussius, son of Drussia Tar, his mother, for her well-deserving son; he lived 4 months.
EDCS-08201204.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a mother to her son, noting his age at death.

EDCS-08201206.translation: To Quintus Publicius Hermetus, his well-deserving tent-mate, she erected this.
EDCS-08201206.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by an unknown woman to her tent-mate (contubernalis).

EDCS-08201207.translation: To Duceae A, she lived 24 years. Caius Geminius Nymphius erected this to his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-08201207.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband to his wife, recording her age at death.

EDCS-08201209.translation: To Eugamus, slave of Pompeia Festa. Pompeia Pelagia, mother, because the son owed his mother.
EDCS-08201209.summary: A funerary epitaph with a complex dedication suggesting a son's debt or obligation to his mother, set up by the mother.

EDCS-08201210.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, to Eurycidus. Silvester erected this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-08201210.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Silvester to Eurycidus, a deceased person.

EDCS-08201211.translation: To Naida, freedwoman of Marcus Fadius.
EDCS-08201211.summary: An inscription dedicating a monument or space to Naida, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08201212.translation: To Faenia Felicula, daughter of Lucius, according to the will.
EDCS-08201212.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that Faenia Felicula was honored according to the terms of a will.

EDCS-08201213.translation: Caius Falerius, son of Spurius, 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-08201213.summary: An inscription likely recording land dimensions or boundaries for Caius Falerius.

EDCS-08201214.translation: Falerius Murrus, son of Caius, 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-08201214.summary: An inscription likely recording land dimensions or boundaries for Falerius Murrus.

EDCS-08201216.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, to Flaminia Lucilla, who lived 3 years. Flaminius Felix and Flavia Cabnis erected this for their sweetest daughter and for themselves.
EDCS-08201216.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by parents for their young daughter, also including a dedication for themselves.

EDCS-08201217.translation: Lucius Livius Trophimus erected this for his well-deserving friends Flaminia Lucia, freedwoman of Lucius, and Marcus Creperius Merope, freedman of Marcus, and also for Aphrodisius, his slave.
EDCS-08201217.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Livius Trophimus for his friends and a slave, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201218.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. His freedwoman erected this for Flavia Hygia of Turis, his patroness.
EDCS-08201218.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a freedwoman to her patroness, Flavia Hygia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201219.translation: Aprilis' brother made this for his well-deserving Fortunata and Arethusa.
EDCS-08201219.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by a brother for two women, Fortunata and Arethusa, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201220.translation: He erected this for his son Caius, his wife Frontonis, and his daughter Tae.
EDCS-08201220.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a man for his son, wife, and daughter, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201221.translation: Clitus for himself and Restituta Fullonia, daughter of Lucius, and Maxim for themselves and...
EDCS-08201221.summary: This is a funerary inscription begun by Clitus for himself and Restituta, and continued by Maxim, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201222.translation: Quintus Heius, son of Quintus, [set this up on] twelve feet in front and twelve feet in the rear.
EDCS-08201222.summary: This inscription appears to record land dimensions or boundaries, likely related to property ownership or burial space, for Quintus Heius, son of Quintus, in the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201223.translation: Nica erected this for her husband Heraclidas Aelicena, from her own means.
EDCS-08201223.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a wife, Nica, for her husband Heraclidas, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201224.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely identified a person, Quintus Here, son of Quintus, possibly for a funerary monument or property marker, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201227.translation: Dio, freedman of Caius Horatius, and Mitulleia...
EDCS-08201227.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely identifying individuals, possibly Dio, a freedman of Caius Horatius, and Mitulleia, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201228.translation: Caius Iunius for his father, by will.
EDCS-08201228.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Caius Iunius set up the monument for his father as stipulated in his will, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201232.summary: This fragmentary inscription, identified by its EDCS-ID, mentions Lysius and a colony, suggesting a dedication or funerary context.

EDCS-08201238.translation: To Maria, daughter of Lucius, and to Pol Marcus Pisentius, and to Maria, daughter of Publius, [he/she] admitted...
EDCS-08201238.summary: This inscription names several individuals, including Maria, daughter of Lucius, Pol Marcus Pisentius, and Maria, daughter of Publius, likely part of a family dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-08201242.translation: Nedymus set this up for his colleagues and friend.
EDCS-08201242.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Nedymus to his colleagues and a friend, likely on a public or collegial monument.

EDCS-08201243.summary: This short inscription records a dedication made by Asch Nice, likely on a funerary or personal monument.

EDCS-08201252.translation: He took care to have this made for Poblicius Secu[---], Titinius Secu[---], Poblicia Sec[---].
EDCS-08201252.summary: This inscription indicates that someone arranged for the construction of a monument for Poblicius Secu[---], Titinius Secu[---], and Poblicia Sec[---], likely a family tomb.

EDCS-08201253.translation: By the authority of Polyclitus, freedman.
EDCS-08201253.summary: This inscription specifies that something was done under the authority of Polyclitus, a freedman, possibly relating to the execution of a monument or legal matter.

EDCS-08201254.translation: To Pompeia Benigna. Pompeia Magna, her sister.
EDCS-08201254.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Pompeia Magna to her sister, Pompeia Benigna, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-08201273.translation: [---] and Silvina, parents, for their deserving son.
EDCS-08201273.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication by parents, Silvina and another individual, to their 'deserving son', clearly a funerary monument.

EDCS-08201274.translation: Lucius Staedius Amianthus, and Maria Horm[e]. Lucius Staedius, son of Lucius, of the Horatia tribe, a son, set this up.
EDCS-08201274.summary: This inscription records the placement of a monument by Lucius Staedius, son of Lucius, for himself and Maria Horme, indicating a family tomb or dedication.

EDCS-08201276.translation: Successa, Ceciena, Edone, slaves, set this up.
EDCS-08201276.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by three individuals identified as slaves (Successa, Ceciena, Edone), likely for a shared purpose or monument.

EDCS-08201277.translation: Gaius Sulpicius, son of Lucius, of the Horatia tribe.
EDCS-08201277.summary: This inscription is a designation of personal identity, likely on a tombstone, including name, father's name, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-08201278.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording the name of Quintus Tannonius, likely part of a larger dedication or epitaph.

EDCS-08201281.summary: This inscription records a dedication by a client, Geminatus, to his patron Thelesina.

EDCS-08201283.translation: Phaedrias and Thetis placed [this] for their brother Receptus.
EDCS-08201283.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by Phaedrias and Thetis for their deceased brother, Receptus.

EDCS-08201284.translation: [---] Vectisius, Tiro of the Horatia tribe.
EDCS-08201284.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely an epitaph, identifying an individual named Vectisius.

EDCS-08201285.translation: Bivellia Cloe placed [this] for Titia, freedwoman of Gaius, aged nine.
EDCS-08201285.summary: This epitaph records a monument set up by Bivellia Cloe for a young girl, Titia, who died at age nine.

EDCS-08201286.translation: Titiolaeia placed [this] for her most beloved daughter Titiolaeia Isias, well deserving.
EDCS-08201286.summary: This epitaph is a dedication by Titiolaeia for her daughter Isias, expressing affection and recognition of merit.

EDCS-08201287.translation: Gaius Tullius, freedman of Gaius, [---]; Vettia, freedwoman of Gaius, Salvi[---]; Tullia, daughter of Gaius, Poli[---] made [this] with their money.
EDCS-08201287.summary: This inscription records a monument financed by three individuals, Gaius Tullius, Vettia, and Tullia, possibly as a joint project or group dedication.

EDCS-08201289.translation: Titus Tullianus Philostratus, freedman of Titus, for himself and for Tulliana Pamphila, freedwoman of Titus, and for his patron Titus Tullianus Antiochus.
EDCS-08201289.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a dedication made by Titus Tullianus Philostratus for himself, his wife Pamphila, and his patron Antiochus.

EDCS-08201292.translation: Valeria Prima, freedwoman of Calenus, placed [this].
EDCS-08201292.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Valeria Prima, a freedwoman of Calenus.

EDCS-08201305.translation: Itsogellia Fauslina made this for her daughter.
EDCS-08201305.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a father for his daughter.

EDCS-08201306.summary: This is a funerary monument recording the parentage of Avittia Comla.

EDCS-08201307.translation: Ovius Claudius, son of Lo. For his father.
EDCS-08201307.summary: This funerary inscription records the name of Ovius Claudius and his relation to his father.

EDCS-08201316.translation: Gaius Vatius erected this for his well-deserving freedwoman Successa, daughter of Micia.
EDCS-08201316.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Vatius for his freedwoman Successa.

EDCS-08201324.translation: Illia Osin [erected this] for her son Osin Primo.
EDCS-08201324.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Illia Osin for her son Primo.

EDCS-08201328.summary: This is a funerary monument recording the name and parentage of Atius Setius.

EDCS-08201329.translation: [---], freedman of the woman, [erected this] for himself [---] and for Bibius, freedman of the woman.
EDCS-08201329.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a freedman for himself and another freedman.

EDCS-08201330.translation: Lucius Dictorius, freedman of Lucia, [erected this] for [---]ius Li.
EDCS-08201330.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Dictorius, a freedman, for Li.

EDCS-08201336.translation: Quintus Rufus, son of Elle Ekaq.
EDCS-08201336.summary: This funerary inscription records the name and parentage of Quintus Rufus.

EDCS-08201346.summary: This is a funerary monument indicating the burial place of Faustinus the father.

EDCS-08201347.translation: Here rests Faustina, daughter of Faustinus the father, of age 14 years and 5 months, who was the only one of her parents; her parents say that two apostles and two bishops (or: scribes) mourned her, and she caused great sorrow to her parents and tears to the city. May her soul rest in peace. Mashka was a great-granddaughter of Faustinus the father and granddaughter of Bitius and Asellus, who were elders of the city.
EDCS-08201347.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young girl named Faustina, lamenting her passing and mentioning her esteemed ancestors.

EDCS-08201348.translation: To Oppia Prochne Auctiana Secunda, from Creperia Tiatice, for Isis.
EDCS-08201348.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Isis by Creperia Tiatice for Oppia Prochne Auctiana Secunda.

EDCS-08201355.translation: To the departed spirits of Felicitas, who lived 9 years, 18 days, and to the departed spirits of Eyplus, who lived 8 years, 7 months, 20 days. Epaphroditus and Secunda, parents.
EDCS-08201355.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of Felicitas and Eyplus, children of Epaphroditus and Secunda.

EDCS-08201357.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription commemorating Metilia Torquata.

EDCS-08201360.translation: To the propagator of the Roman empire, our lord Flavius Valentinianus Augustus, ever victorious, the devoted council, by decree of the decurions, has ordered this to be set up.
EDCS-08201360.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the town council to Emperor Flavius Valentinianus Augustus, honoring his role in propagating the Roman Empire.

EDCS-08201361.translation: To Cornelia Marullina, daughter of Lucius, most distinguished lady.
EDCS-08201361.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Cornelia Marullina, described as a most distinguished lady.

EDCS-08201362.translation: Lucius Lucilius Pansa Priscilianus erected this for Ni Orfita, daughter of the consul Servius Cornelius Scipio Orfitus, and sister of the consul Orfitus, to the most holy [woman].
EDCS-08201362.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Lucius Lucilius Pansa Priscilianus for Ni Orfita, daughter and sister of consuls.

EDCS-08201366.translation: Gaius Luccius, son of Gaius, of the Horatian tribe, duovir with power to administer justice in Venusia. Marcus Luccius, son of Gaius, of the Horatian tribe, designated duovir with power to administer justice in Venusia.
EDCS-08201366.summary: This inscription records two brothers, Gaius and Marcus Luccius, who held the office of duovir iure dicundo in Venusia.

EDCS-08201367.translation: To Publius Mammercius Maximus, son of Publius, aedile, duovir, quinquennial duovir, quaestor, for his deserving wife Satria Secunda, and for [their] children Sabinus and Silvana, and for himself, he made [this].
EDCS-08201367.summary: This inscription records a man named Publius Mammercius Maximus dedicating a monument to his wife, children, and himself, listing his public offices.

EDCS-08201368.translation: For Lucius Vinnius Frontonas, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, aedile, iure dicundo, for [his] merits, by public decree of the decurions, Aurelia Marcella, his grandmother, and Marcus Annius Marcellus, his brother, with remission of public expense, [erected this].
EDCS-08201368.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by the grandmother and brother of Lucius Vinnius Frontonas, honoring his public offices and merits, with public funds remitted.

EDCS-08201370.translation: To Ata Modesta, freedwoman of Lucius, daughter of Lucius, lived 23 years. Her husband Lucius Aurelius Bithynus, freedman of Lucius, and Secundus, freedman of Aulus Sicinius, and Secunda, freedwoman of Ata, made this. Ata, freedwoman, made this.
EDCS-08201370.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ata Modesta, set up by her husband and others.

EDCS-08201371.translation: To Betitia Thisbe Albana, freedwoman, 17 years old, made this for herself and her family. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-08201371.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Betitia Thisbe Albana, set up by herself for her family.

EDCS-08201372.translation: Gaius Cosconius Eros, freedman, according to his will.
EDCS-08201372.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument established by Gaius Cosconius Eros, a freedman, as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-08201373.translation: To Gaius Marius Proculus, father, Gaius Marius Crescens made this for his well-deserving father. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-08201373.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Marius Proculus, set up by his son Gaius Marius Crescens.

EDCS-08201375.translation: To the departed spirits. Secundus, who served...
EDCS-08201375.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, likely for a person named Secundus.

EDCS-08201376.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Sempronia Severa made this for her most dutiful brother, Lucius Sempronius Constans, son of Lucius Sempronius Severus and Sempronia Ursa.
EDCS-08201376.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Sempronius Constans, dedicated by his sister Sempronia Severa.

EDCS-08201377.translation: Tussidia Fortunata and Publius Cerrinius Felix, most wretched parents, owe this to Sextus Tussidius Felix, who lived 20 years; here their wretched parents must be buried.
EDCS-08201377.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Sextus Tussidius Felix at the age of 20, set up by his parents.

EDCS-08201378.translation: To the departed spirits. Publius Burbatius Ianuarius made this for his well-deserving wife, Vibienia Faussta.
EDCS-08201378.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibienia Faussta, set up by her husband Publius Burbatius Ianuarius.

EDCS-08201381.translation: LAO OI AIG Chrestinus arranged for this to be made at his own expense for his mother Anteron, a fellow freedman.
EDCS-08201381.summary: This is an inscription recording the arrangement for a monument to be built by Chrestinus for his mother, Anteron.

EDCS-08201383.translation: Sextilia Accepta arranged for this altar to Bona Dea to be made at her own expense and for [her] health.
EDCS-08201383.summary: This is an inscription recording Sextilia Accepta dedicating an altar to Bona Dea at her own expense for her health.

EDCS-08201390.summary: This is a dedication to Romano, who held the priestly office of Augustalis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08201392.translation: Lucius Artorius Nicephor, freedman of Lucius, is buried here.
EDCS-08201392.summary: This inscription records the burial of Lucius Artorius Nicephor, a freedman, indicating a funerary monument.

EDCS-08201394.translation: Gaius Vergilius Maximus and Anicia Apra for their most dutiful son Gaius Vergilius Maximus, who lived 18 years; the people bore witness to him.
EDCS-08201394.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Vergilius Maximus, erected by his parents, noting his young age and public recognition.

EDCS-08201402.translation: The distinguished council of the city of Theanensis, together with their fellow citizens, erected this for Flavius Uranio, a most accomplished man, governor of the province, defender of laws, and moderator of justice, a worthy patron.
EDCS-08201402.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the city council and citizens of Theanensis to their patron Flavius Uranio, honoring his role as provincial governor and administrator of justice, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-08201404.translation: To Pomponia Drusilla, daughter of Gaius. Marcus Numisius Quintianus Laurens Lavinas, son of Marcus, grandson of Marcus, of the Cornelia tribe, Flamen Floralis and patron of the municipality, [dedicated this] to his most exceptional wife. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201404.summary: This funerary dedication by Marcus Numisius Quintianus for his wife Pomponia Drusilla, a Flamen Floralis and municipal patron, indicates a monument established by official decree, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-08201413.translation: To Lucius Pontius Argentarius, freedman of Lucius, and to Sorgia Horme, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-08201413.summary: This inscription records a dedication or funerary marker for two individuals, Lucius Pontius Argentarius and Sorgia Horme, both freedpersons, probably from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08201415.translation: Lucius Sestius Salvius, freedman of Lucius, [erected this] for Rufila, his fellow freedwoman.
EDCS-08201415.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Lucius Sestius Salvius dedicated a monument to Rufila, another freedperson, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08201418.translation: To the departed spirits. Spectatus, who lived 18 years, 4 months, and 8 days, [this monument was erected by] his unfortunate father for his son.
EDCS-08201418.summary: This is a tombstone for Spectatus, who died at the age of 18, erected by his grieving father, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08201423.translation: Gaius Salvius Eutychus paid his vow to the household gods of the Casani family for the safe return of our Rectina.
EDCS-08201423.summary: This inscription records Gaius Salvius Eutychus fulfilling a vow to the Lares Casani for the safe return of Rectina, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08201424.translation: Quintus Pullius, son of Vibius, master of the district, had this made by the decision of the chosen men and approved it himself.
EDCS-08201424.summary: This inscription records Quintus Pullius, as master of a local district, overseeing and approving a public work or project commissioned by a committee, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08201425.translation: To Lucius Caesar, son of Augustus.
EDCS-08201425.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Caesar, adopted son of Augustus, likely from the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-08201426.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, high priest, holding tribunician power for the 3rd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08201426.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Emperor Trajan, detailing his titles and powers, likely erected by local magistrates around 98-100 CE.

EDCS-08201428.translation: To Gaius Vibius Postumus, son of Gaius, praetor, proconsul, by the municipes and inhabitants.
EDCS-08201428.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to Gaius Vibius Postumus, a former governor, by the local citizens and residents.

EDCS-08201431.translation: Marcus Valerius Hispanus, son of Gaius, from Leonica, a knight, decurion of the ala of Patruius, is buried here.
EDCS-08201431.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Valerius Hispanus, a Roman knight and cavalry officer, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08201434.translation: To Gaius Raio Capito, son of Marcus, prefect of the engineers, aedile for the 4th time, magistrate for the 2nd time, magistrate for five-year terms for the 2nd time, by the municipes and inhabitants.
EDCS-08201434.summary: This dedication honors Gaius Raio Capito, a local magistrate who held multiple offices, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08201438.translation: To the departed spirits. Sextus Cerrinius Chresimus, freedman of a woman, a physician, an Augustalis, erected this himself while alive.
EDCS-08201438.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Sextus Cerrinius Chresimus, a freedman and physician who held the rank of Augustalis, for himself.

EDCS-08201439.translation: To Gaius Mammio Felix, freedman of Pelasgus, an Augustalis honored again with the bisellium.
EDCS-08201439.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Mammio Felix, a freedman and Augustalis, who received special honors including the bisellium.

EDCS-08201440.translation: To the departed spirits. Gnaeus Petronius Restitutus, freedman of a woman, an Augustalis, for himself and for his wife, Cluentia, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-08201440.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gnaeus Petronius Restitutus, an Augustalis, and his wife Cluentia.

EDCS-08201441.translation: Decimus Graecinius Praxagoras, freedman of Decimus, a physician, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-08201441.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Decimus Graecinius Praxagoras, a freedman and physician, for himself.

EDCS-08201442.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Agathopus made this for his parents Agathopo and Severa, deserving children.
EDCS-08201442.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Agathopus dedicates a monument to his parents, Agathopo and Severa.

EDCS-08201443.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Amomus set this up for his brother Tyrannus.
EDCS-08201443.summary: A funerary inscription recording a monument set up by Amomus for his brother Tyrannus.

EDCS-08201444.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Anicius Gaio, son of Fructosus.
EDCS-08201444.summary: A funerary dedication to Marcus Anicius Gaio, son of Fructosus.

EDCS-08201445.translation: For Asubria, to Quintus Mincius and Fulvia Erato, with Quintus Asubrius and Quintus Mincius, and to her husband Titus Vettius. Unfortunate one.
EDCS-08201445.summary: A funerary dedication by Asubria, possibly to her parents and husband, indicating a difficult life.

EDCS-08201446.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Veratia Prisca, grandmother, and Pomponius Amandus, grandfather, and Voltius Priscus, grandfather, made this for their most devoted grandson Quintus Cassius Osoberus, and for their unfortunate son.
EDCS-08201446.summary: A funerary monument erected by grandparents for their grandson Quintus Cassius Osoberus and an unnamed son.

EDCS-08201447.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Quintus Calavius Euodus, who lived 6 years, 11 months, and 3 days. Quintus Calavius [dedicated this].
EDCS-08201447.summary: A funerary dedication to Quintus Calavius Euodus, a child who died at age 6, by Quintus Calavius.

EDCS-08201448.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Colius Teres, who lived 12 years, 5 months. Marcus Colius Teres, his father, and Gavia Libertas, his mother, made this for their son.
EDCS-08201448.summary: A funerary monument for Marcus Colius Teres, a boy who died at age 12, erected by his parents.

EDCS-08201449.translation: For Didia Barbi, daughter of Decuma Oppianica and Billiena, and her mother. For her husband.
EDCS-08201449.summary: A funerary dedication for Didia Barbi, possibly by her mother and husband.

EDCS-08201450.translation: For Marcus Drusus Philodamus, freedman of Marcus, for himself and his household, while living.
EDCS-08201450.summary: A self-funded funerary monument for Marcus Drusus Philodamus and his family, likely from the 1st century CE.

EDCS-08201451.translation: Burial place of Caius Gabbius Zosimus. In front 59 feet, in the field 35 feet.
EDCS-08201451.summary: A property marker for the burial plot of Caius Gabbius Zosimus, specifying its dimensions.

EDCS-08201452.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Gavia Apricula. Clentius Priscianus made this for his exceptionally rare wife, with whom he lived 30 years without complaint, who deserved well.
EDCS-08201452.summary: A funerary monument erected by Clentius Priscianus for his beloved wife Gavia Apricula, commemorating their 30 years of marriage.

EDCS-08201453.translation: To Quintus Itius, freedman of Quintus, for his patron Quintus, son of Quintus, of the Clustumina tribe, and for himself and for Itia Sympherusa and for Quintus Itius Celadus and for Gnaeus Maius, freedman of Gnaeus; 12 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-08201453.summary: This is a boundary inscription detailing land allocation and ownership, likely for a funerary plot, referencing a patron-freedman relationship and family members.

EDCS-08201454.summary: This is a very brief dedication or memorial inscription, possibly for Caius Lindius Pudens.

EDCS-08201455.translation: Fructosa, his mother, placed this for her son Macarius, who lived 18 years and 3 months, a well-deserving son.
EDCS-08201455.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her deceased son, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-08201456.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Moecius Onesimus made this for his most beloved freedwoman, Charite, a well-deserving woman, who lived 23 years.
EDCS-08201456.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Lucius Moecius Onesimus for his freedwoman Charite, stating her age at death.

EDCS-08201457.translation: To the freedmen of Gnaeus Monnius Faustus and Philargurus; 14 feet in front, 18 feet in the field and further.
EDCS-08201457.summary: This is a boundary inscription, likely for land or a funerary plot, detailing dimensions and possibly belonging to freedmen of Gnaeus Monnius Faustus and Philargurus.

EDCS-08201458.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Natalinus Primigenius.
EDCS-08201458.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication to Natalinus Primigenius.

EDCS-08201459.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Oriens willingly placed this for Rhodius, his nephew, for Calvilla, his sister, and for his brothers, well-deserving souls. Hail and farewell.
EDCS-08201459.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Oriens for various family members, including a nephew, sister, and brothers, expressing affection and good wishes.

EDCS-08201460.translation: Caius Otacidius Pisidinus, while alive, for himself and for Ortoria Lochias, his companion, for his nephew Marcus Pontius Marcellus, and for his father Caius Otacidius Florus; 20 feet in the field, 12 feet in front.
EDCS-08201460.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Caius Otacidius Pisidinus for himself and several family members, likely marking a tomb or burial plot with specified dimensions.

EDCS-08201461.translation: To Lucius Plotius Anthus and Flavia, freedwoman of Quintus, and to Amoena and Flavia Didyma; 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-08201461.summary: This inscription likely marks a burial plot or property, possibly funerary, belonging to Lucius Plotius Anthus and several women, including a freedwoman.

EDCS-08201462.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Procula, who lived 1 month and 20 days, who... [---] P.
EDCS-08201462.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Procula, detailing her short lifespan and possibly indicating a votive offering or location.

EDCS-08201463.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Flavius Proculus placed this for his dearest wife Raia Fortunata, with whom he lived for 28 years, and for himself.
EDCS-08201463.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Flavius Proculus to his wife Raia Fortunata, also including himself.

EDCS-08201464.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Rufus, who lived for 30 years. His father Rufus and mother Saturnina placed this.
EDCS-08201464.summary: A funerary monument set up by parents Rufus and Saturnina for their son Rufus.

EDCS-08201465.translation: For Gaius Sacrius Frontonius, brothers. 12 feet in front and 10 feet in the ground.
EDCS-08201465.summary: A funerary monument with dimensions specified, intended for Gaius Sacrius Frontonius and his brothers.

EDCS-08201466.translation: He made this for himself, and for Gaius Annidius Machaera the patron, and for his own people. 10 feet in the ground and 12 feet in front.
EDCS-08201466.summary: A funerary monument built by an individual for himself, his patron Gaius Annidius Machaera, and his family, with dimensions specified.

EDCS-08201467.translation: He made this for himself, and for Annidius and Machaera the patron, and for his own people. 10 feet in the ground and 12 feet in front.
EDCS-08201467.summary: A funerary monument built by an individual for himself, his patron Annidius Machaera, and his family, with dimensions specified.

EDCS-08201468.translation: He placed this monument for himself and his own people and Cordo. 12 feet in front and 10 feet in the ground. It shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-08201468.summary: A funerary monument for himself, his family, and Cordo, with dimensions specified and a clause preventing inheritance.

EDCS-08201469.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Quintus Seius Primus.

EDCS-08201471.translation: Varia, freedwoman of mulieris, and Primigenia, freedwoman of Varia mulieris, and Agilis, alive, for herself and for Marcus Numisius Capito, her husband. 12 feet in front and 10 feet in the ground. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-08201471.summary: A funerary monument built by two freedwomen for themselves, their husband Marcus Numisius Capito, with specified dimensions and a clause preventing inheritance.

EDCS-08201472.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Venusina, daughter, who deserved well of her parents. She lived 2 years, 9 months. Hail and farewell.
EDCS-08201472.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by parents to their daughter Venusina, recording her age at death.

EDCS-08201473.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Placed by Baebia Vinatris for her most dutiful mother Danae.
EDCS-08201473.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Baebia Vinatris to her mother Danae.

EDCS-08201475.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Julia Beronice Similis set this up for her deserving wife.
EDCS-08201475.summary: A funerary dedication by Julia Beronice Similis for her wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08201486.translation: Terentia Ampelius, by decree of the town councillors.
EDCS-08201486.summary: An honorific inscription recording a decree of the town councillors for Terentia Ampelius, possibly related to a public office or honor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201508.summary: A funerary dedication for Lucius Lucerus Messi, identified as a husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201510.translation: For Sextus Fiscenius Successus, the Augustalis.
EDCS-08201510.summary: A dedication to Sextus Fiscenius Successus, who held the prestigious position of Augustalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201511.translation: Gaius Marius, son of Gaius, freedman, Augustalis, for himself and for Gaius Marius Atticus, and Maria Lib[...]
EDCS-08201511.summary: A funerary and self-dedicatory inscription for Gaius Marius, an Augustalis, and members of his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201515.translation: To Lucius Ocratio Corinthus Apollinaris, Ocratia Silvana [sets this up] for her deserving husband.
EDCS-08201515.summary: A funerary dedication by Ocratia Silvana for her husband, Lucius Ocratio Corinthus Apollinaris, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201517.translation: To Marcus Staïus Lygdamus Apollinaris, freedman of Marcus and [his wife], also an Augustalis, 15,000 sesterces. Staïa [3]CAR.
EDCS-08201517.summary: A funerary inscription that records a payment of 15,000 sesterces for Marcus Staïus Lygdamus Apollinaris, who was an Augustalis, and also mentions Staïa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201521.translation: Antipater, the steward, made this for his wife Lesbia, aged 23 years, and for his daughter, aged 9 months, from his own funds.
EDCS-08201521.summary: A funerary dedication by Antipater, a steward, for his wife Lesbia and infant daughter, indicating a personal expenditure, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201523.translation: Gaius Hezzius, son of Gaius, the haruspex. Hezzia, his wife.
EDCS-08201523.summary: A dedication or memorial for Gaius Hezzius, a haruspex (diviner), and his wife Hezzia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201529.translation: [...] to Maccio, son of Aulus, of the Pupinia tribe, his patron, and for himself. Mac[cia ...]
EDCS-08201529.summary: A funerary or dedicatory inscription mentioning a patron, Maccio, and the dedicator, possibly Maccia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08201530.translation: Adbenta made this for her deserving wife.
EDCS-08201530.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a wife, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-08201531.translation: To Albania Capriola and Vellaeus Nigrus.
EDCS-08201531.summary: This is a dedication, possibly for a public work or a private benefit, to two individuals, Albania Capriola and Vellaeus Nigrus.

EDCS-08201533.summary: This inscription likely records the names of two individuals, Angilio Asota and Vinia, possibly as part of a dedication or property marker.

EDCS-08201535.translation: Apelles, son of Memno, pious and dutiful.
EDCS-08201535.summary: This is a funerary epitaph honoring Apelles, son of Memno, describing him as pious and dutiful, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201544.translation: Hellius Bassus, son of Marcus, and Claudius [---].
EDCS-08201544.summary: This inscription lists two individuals, Hellius Bassus and Claudius, likely as part of a dedication or funerary monument.

EDCS-08201549.translation: Living: Marcus Cronius, son of Spurius, of the Talania tribe, and his wife Talania Tertulla, daughter of Quintus. Albana, aged 12, dutiful to his own people. His mother Iucunda made this for her son. Talania Helpis, freedwoman of the woman, [is buried here].
EDCS-08201549.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph for Marcus Cronius, his wife Talania Tertulla, their son Albana who died at 12, and the freedwoman Talania Helpis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08201556.translation: [Made] for Iulatia [---]tini, wife of [---], by decree of [---], for Marcus Fabius, freedman of Marcus, [and] Cocceius [---].
EDCS-08201556.summary: This inscription records a construction or benefaction made under specific instructions, involving Iulatia, her husband, Marcus Fabius, and Cocceius.

EDCS-08201564.translation: Lucius Licinius, son of Lucius, Centu.
EDCS-08201564.summary: This inscription records the name and parentage of Lucius Licinius Centu, likely a funerary epitaph or a record of civic status.

EDCS-08201567.translation: Gaius Marius, son of Lucius, for himself and for his wife Albicia Meo[---].
EDCS-08201567.summary: This funerary inscription indicates that Gaius Marius, son of Lucius, made arrangements for himself and his wife Albicia Meo[---].

EDCS-08201575.translation: Publius Pet[---], son of Publius Petro A[---].
EDCS-08201575.summary: This inscription seems to be a partial record of a person named Publius Pet[---], son of Publius Petro A[---], possibly a funerary epitaph or a dedication.

EDCS-08201580.summary: This inscription appears to be a very fragmentary funerary text mentioning a person named Primus Avenutus.

EDCS-08201590.translation: [Publius] Stenius Salvius, freedman of Publius, is buried here.
