EDCS-12401085.summary: This inscription honors Marcus Flavius Cornelius Marcellinus for his service and affection towards the city of Beneventum, likely set up in the forum by the local populace.

EDCS-12401086.translation: With our lords and Augusti safe, Flavius Lupus, a most distinguished man and consular governor of Campania, acting from his own resources, [erected this for] Insontius Secundinus and O.R.T.
EDCS-12401086.summary: This inscription records the completion of work by Flavius Lupus, consular governor of Campania, for Insontius Secundinus and O.R.T., likely in the 3rd or 4th century CE.

EDCS-12401087.translation: To Fonteia Celerina, a most distinguished woman and most holy wife.
EDCS-12401087.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Fonteia Celerina, the wife of an unknown individual, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12401091.translation: Numisius Licinianus, a most distinguished man and curator of the public affairs of Beneventum, took care of [this monument].
EDCS-12401091.summary: This inscription records Numisius Licinianus, a curator of Beneventum, overseeing the erection of a monument, likely in the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401097.translation: To Amantius, beloved by all and loving all, equally to the most noble and just Valerius Publicola, a most distinguished man and consular governor of Campania, from his ancestors, the most splendid order of Beneventum and the most honorable people decreed that it should be erected.
EDCS-12401097.summary: This is a senatorial and popular decree honoring Valerius Publicola, a consular governor of Campania and patron of Beneventum, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401099.translation: Canius M., tribune of the plebs, [son of] Aulus, freedman of Aulus P...
EDCS-12401099.summary: This incomplete inscription identifies Canius M. as a tribune of the plebs and son of a freedman, likely from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-12401107.translation: Tertia, wife of Lucius Acilius, son of Aulus, of the Stellatina tribe, [serving in] the VIth Legion Marcia, made [this].
EDCS-12401107.summary: This inscription records Tertia, the wife of Lucius Acilius of the VIth Legion, as the maker of a monument, likely a tombstone, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12401110.translation: [...], son of Titus Avidienus, of the Stellatina tribe, [serving in] the VIth Legion [...], centurion, decurion.
EDCS-12401110.summary: This fragmented inscription honors Avidienus, a centurion and decurion in the VIth Legion, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12401111.translation: Gaius Clodius, son of Publius, of the Stellatina tribe, [serving in] the XXXth Legion, and Valeria Diodora, freedwoman of Sextus, his heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-12401111.summary: This inscription records that the heir of Gaius Clodius (of the XXXth Legion) and Valeria Diodora (freedwoman of Sextus) arranged for a monument to be made, likely a tombstone, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12401113.translation: The burial of Marcianus, on the sixth day before the Kalends of October.
EDCS-12401113.summary: This inscription records the burial date of a man named Marcianus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12401114.translation: The burial of Pomponia Legitima, wife of Pomponius, the patron, who lived 50 years, dearest wife, [on the] second day before the Kalends of September.
EDCS-12401114.summary: This inscription records the burial date and age of Pomponia Legitima, the wife of a patron named Pomponius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-12401117.translation: To Lucius Fullonius Severus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, a centurion (primo pilo) of the tenth Gemina legion.
EDCS-12401117.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Fullonius Severus, a high-ranking officer in the Roman army.

EDCS-12401118.translation: To Lucius Herennius Fuscus, a veteran and building surveyor, and to Iunia Rufina, his incomparable wife, and Lucius Herennius Fuscus, to his father of rare affection.
EDCS-12401118.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Herennius Fuscus, a veteran and surveyor, and his wife and father, set up by his son.

EDCS-12401121.translation: [---] to Libon, of the Stellatina tribe, who served as a military tribune and as a duovir for judging cases.
EDCS-12401121.summary: This inscription records the offices held by an individual named Libon, likely a local magistrate and military officer.

EDCS-12401126.translation: Pompeius, son of Titus, of the Stellatina tribe, [served] in the thirtieth legion.
EDCS-12401126.summary: This inscription records the service of Pompeius, a soldier in the thirtieth legion.

EDCS-12401127.translation: Numerius Pomponius Scaeva, son of Numerius, a soldier of the second praetorian cohort of Calena, [dedicated this] to his freedwoman and to himself.
EDCS-12401127.summary: This is a dedication made by Numerius Pomponius Scaeva, a member of the praetorian guard, for himself and his freedwoman.

EDCS-12401128.translation: [---] of the Stellatina tribe, [served] in the sixth legion, to his wife and to the freedwoman of the woman, Quartilla.
EDCS-12401128.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication made by a soldier of the sixth legion to his wife and her freedwoman.

EDCS-12401129.translation: To Titus Ruf[---], [served] in the fifth Futilis legion.
EDCS-12401129.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial to Titus Ruf[---], who served in the fifth Futilis legion.

EDCS-12401133.translation: Flavius Valerianus and Aurelia Iusta made [this] to Valerian, a veteran of the seventh praetorian cohort, their well-deserving father.
EDCS-12401133.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Flavius Valerianus and Aurelia Iusta for their father, a veteran of the praetorian guard.

EDCS-12401134.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Gaius Umbrius Adiutor, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, a soldier of the second praetorian cohort. Gaius Umbrius Mansuetus and Umbria Eucarpia [set this up] for their most devoted brother, and for Gaius Umbrius Mansuetus and Umbria Eucarpia, their parents.
EDCS-12401134.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Gaius Umbrius Adiutor, a soldier of the praetorian guard, set up by his siblings who also commemorate their parents.

EDCS-12401135.translation: [---] to Titus, son of [---], of the Stellatina tribe, [served] in the thirtieth legion; his heir arranged for this to be made by will.
EDCS-12401135.summary: This inscription indicates that a monument was erected by the heir of an individual named Titus, who served in the thirtieth legion, according to his will.

EDCS-12401137.translation: From the third mile marker, Diomedes, 12 feet in front and in the field feet
EDCS-12401137.summary: This inscription appears to be a boundary marker or measurement, possibly related to land ownership or a road, with 'Diomedes' potentially being a name or a place.

EDCS-12401139.translation: The consul Aulus Cervios, son of Aulus, dedicated this.
EDCS-12401139.summary: This is a dedication by Aulus Cervios, son of Aulus, who held the office of consul, likely dating to the Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-12401140.translation: Publius Cincius, IIvir, as aedile, [dedicated this] to Publius Cincius, son of Seppia, and N. Cincius.
EDCS-12401140.summary: This inscription records an aedile, Publius Cincius, dedicating something in honor of Publius Cincius and N. Cincius, possibly family members or patrons, within a town as a IIvir.

EDCS-12401142.translation: Lucius, son of Caius Suessianus; Lucius, son of Caius Amos; Lucius, son of Marcus Nonius; Lucius, son of Lucius Munatius; Lucius, son of Caius Vaterius; Lucius, son of Caius Freganius; Cnaeus, son of Cnaeus Suellius, quaestors.
EDCS-12401142.summary: This inscription lists several individuals, identified by their fathers and tribal affiliations, who served as quaestors, likely in a provincial administration.

EDCS-12401143.translation: To Gaius Adiectius, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, called Macedon, a praetor, Cerialis, for dispensing justice, Gaius Adiectius Narcissus and Ceia Lucilla gave this place as a gift to their grandson by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-12401143.summary: This is a funerary or honorific dedication by Gaius Adiectius Narcissus and Ceia Lucilla to their grandson, a former praetor, with the grant of the plot being approved by the local council (decurions).

EDCS-12401150.translation: Gaius Ennius, son of Marcus, and Gaius Bergonius, son of Quintus, as aediles, paved the road and made the drains.
EDCS-12401150.summary: This inscription records the public works undertaken by two aediles, Gaius Ennius and Gaius Bergonius, who paved a road and constructed drainage systems.

EDCS-12401153.summary: This is a very abbreviated inscription identifying Aulus Ign as a IIvir (a duumvir, a chief magistrate in Roman municipalities).

EDCS-12401155.translation: Beneventi, IIvir, lived 30 years. Magia Apr, most unfortunate mother.
EDCS-12401155.summary: This funerary inscription records the life and death of a man who was a IIvir in Beneventum, and mourns him as his mother, Magia Apr.

EDCS-12401156.translation: Lucius Marius, D[edicavit?]; Publius Fufius, D[edicavit?].
EDCS-12401156.summary: This inscription seems to record dedications made by two individuals, Lucius Marius and Publius Fufius, though the verb is abbreviated or missing.

EDCS-12401158.summary: This inscription identifies Munatia, daughter of Marcus, as a priestess, indicating her religious role within her community.

EDCS-12401159.translation: Quintus Paucius, son of Quintus, of the Pupinia tribe, decurio.
EDCS-12401159.summary: This inscription records Quintus Paucius, a decurio, likely indicating his tribal affiliation and role in local government.

EDCS-12401163.translation: To Aulus Vibbius Procus, son of Aulus, of the Palatina tribe, for his distinguished service as aedile, prefect of craftsmen, duovir, quaestor, and pontiff; from his freedmen Ursio, Daphine, Hermes, and Sabina, to their well-deserving patron.
EDCS-12401163.summary: This is a dedication by freedmen to their patron, Aulus Vibbius Procus, listing his various civic and religious offices, suggesting a date in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401169.translation: Gaius Concordius Syriacus, a Roman knight, accountant for the public affairs of Beneventum, organizer of the two-day games, a Latin poet crowned during the games of his homeland, made this for himself while alive; he lived 58 years, 6 months, 12 days, 3 hours. You first established actors of the stage in Beneventum with your studies.
EDCS-12401169.summary: This epitaph commemorates Gaius Concordius Syriacus, a multifaceted individual who was a Roman knight, public accountant, event organizer, and poet, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401170.translation: To Domitia Cyparidis, most chaste woman. Optatus, assistant in the provincial registry office, in charge of the wool accounts, set this up for her, with whom he lived for 18 years, 5 months, 22 days.
EDCS-12401170.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Optatus to his deceased partner, Domitia Cyparidis, detailing his administrative role and their years together, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401172.translation: He also completed the basilica in which the records of the games exhibited by him were placed. He presented games of palm branches for their dedication in the name of his Euploia. He made the portico, with all its elegant adornment, in which the presentation of the first five-year quinquennial games is contained through his princely munificence, according to the will of his Euploia.
EDCS-12401172.summary: This inscription details the completion of a basilica, the funding of games, and the construction of a portico by an unnamed individual for his Euploia, highlighting his generosity and civic contributions during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401190.translation: To Gaius Vibius Crispinus, patron of the Collegium Martense Verzobianum, by his grandfather and ancestors, and patron of the collegium and the city.
EDCS-12401190.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Vibius Crispinus as a patron of the Collegium Martense Verzobianum and the city, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401192.translation: To Gaius Umbrius Vibius Numisius Drusus, the most religious patron, always most generous towards his own people, by the Collegium Martense Verzobianum.
EDCS-12401192.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Collegium Martense Verzobianum to their patron, Gaius Umbrius Vibius Numisius Drusus, emphasizing his religious devotion and generosity, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401193.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. The members of the Martenses, filled with love for the studies of Augustianus, placed this for the innocent Aquinius Iavius, a deserving man, who lived 36 years.
EDCS-12401193.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by the Martenses to honor their fellow member, Aquinius Iavius, who died at the age of 36, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401194.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Successus, who lived 50 years, made this for the well-deserving colleague.
EDCS-12401194.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a colleague to Successus, who lived 50 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401195.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Numerius Afinius Tacitus, Claudialis Augustalis, AFIN.
EDCS-12401195.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Numerius Afinius Tacitus, likely an Augustalis from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401196.translation: Anteros, freedman of Titus, Augustalis; Philippus, freedman of Quintus; [son of] Moctina, freedwoman of Quintus.
EDCS-12401196.summary: This inscription lists freedmen, one of whom, Anteros, held the title Augustalis, likely belonging to a collegium or association.

EDCS-12401199.translation: Prima, freedwoman of Lisidius, for herself and for Sophomene, wife of Gaius Lepidius Rufionius, Augustalis.
EDCS-12401199.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a freedwoman for herself and her husband's associate, who was an Augustalis.

EDCS-12401200.translation: Lucius Lollius Suavis for himself and for his wife Hirria Aegiale, for Pomponia Cale, his mother, Lucius Lollius Suavis Augustalis, again, Lucius Lollius Aegialus Augustalis, again, and Hirria Cai.
EDCS-12401200.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Lucius Lollius Suavis for himself and various family members, including two Augustales, indicating a potential familial connection to the association.

EDCS-12401201.translation: Lucius Lollius Suavis, freedman, Suavis, freedman, Augustalis, again, in the order of A equ.
EDCS-12401201.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to list individuals, including two who held the title Augustalis, possibly related to a collegium or administrative group.

EDCS-12401202.translation: For Publius Marcius Albanus, Augustalis, Etrilia Clemens, daughter of Spurius, for her husband and for herself and for Publius Marcius Pylades Secundus and Modestus.
EDCS-12401202.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Etrilia Clemens, daughter of Spurius, for her husband Publius Marcius Albanus, an Augustalis, and other family members.

EDCS-12401204.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Servenus Alexander, Augustalis Claudialis of Beneventum, lived 97 years, 2 months, 12 days. For Iunia Capreola, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-12401204.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Servenus Alexander, an Augustalis from Beneventum, who lived to be 97 and is commemorated by his wife.

EDCS-12401205.translation: Gaius Sulpicius Lucifer, freedman of Gaius, Augustalis, for himself and for Sulpicia Thallusa, freedwoman of Gaius, his most devoted wife, this monument will not follow an heir.
EDCS-12401205.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Sulpicius Lucifer, an Augustalis, for himself and his wife, with the stipulation that it is not to be inherited.

EDCS-12401209.translation: Publius Veidius Philocles, freedman of Publius, Augustalis of Beneventum, gave gladiators. He took care to have this monument made for the sake of memory for himself and his family, for his brother Publius Veidius Philodamus, freedman of Publius, and for Varia Prima, his wife, and for his brother Publius Veidius Philotimus, freedman of Publius, and for his mother Veidia, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-12401209.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a funerary monument, including gladiatorial games, by Publius Veidius Philocles, an Augustalis of Beneventum, for himself and his family.

EDCS-12401213.translation: For Lucius Helvius Hilarus, freedman of Lucius, nummularius, Mercurialis; for Messia Diodora, freedwoman of Quintus; for Lucius Helvius Paetus, freedman of Lucius, nummularius.
EDCS-12401213.summary: This inscription records a dedication for nummularii (money-changers), including a Mercurialis, likely members of a collegium.

EDCS-12401217.translation: [...]us Amaranthus, Augustalis, refector, pectenarius, for himself and for [Lysimachus], his father, and for [son of ...], freedman of Quintus, HEV[...], and for [son of ...], freedman of Marcus, Agilus, Constans Secundus.
EDCS-12401217.summary: This fragmented inscription lists individuals, including an Augustalis who held the titles refector and pectenarius, likely within a collegium or association.

EDCS-12401218.translation: To Tito Asinio Antiocho, freedman of Titus, the clothier, for himself and his freedmen Eron and Stephano.
EDCS-12401218.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tito Asinio Antiocho, a clothier, and his freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401219.translation: Quintus Caesellius Surus, freedman of Quintus, a merchant, and Domatia Fausta, freedwoman of a woman named Domatia, and Caius Domatius Pylax, freedman of a woman named Domatia, a merchant, and Quintus Caesellius Pamphilus, freedman of a man named Quintus, patron, and Domatia Talia, freedwoman of a woman named Domatia, patroness, while alive made this for themselves and their kin.
EDCS-12401219.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Caesellius Surus and Domatia Talia, along with other family members, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401220.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Casineia Artemisia made this for her husband Marcus Casineius Paetus, a physician, to whom she was married for 25 years, a man of great merit.
EDCS-12401220.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Casineius Paetus, a physician, dedicated by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401221.translation: To Aulus Clodius Demetrius, freedman of Aulus, a physician; for himself and for Clodia Alexandra, freedwoman of Aulus, and for Clodia Glycerai, freedwoman of Aulus. This monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-12401221.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Clodius Demetrius, a physician and freedman, along with two women, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401222.translation: Of Marcus Egnatius Pamphilus, freedman of Marcus, the charcoal-burner.
EDCS-12401222.summary: This is a funerary boundary marker for Marcus Egnatius Pamphilus, a charcoal-burner, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401223.translation: To the Divine Spirits and to the memory of Epigonus, the most faithful actor.
EDCS-12401223.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Epigonus, an actor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401224.translation: Oia Almoi, Hermione Scaura, whose nephew is Syrophoenix, made this while alive.
EDCS-12401224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Oia Almoi and Hermione Scaura, commissioned by their nephew Syrophoenix, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401226.translation: Of the sons of Marcius Sermonicus, the vase-maker.
EDCS-12401226.summary: This is a funerary boundary marker for the sons of Marcius Sermonicus, a vase-maker, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401229.translation: Titus Scanianius Ero, the treasurer, for himself and for Titus Scanianius Sam, the patron.
EDCS-12401229.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Scanianius Ero, a treasurer, and his patron, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401230.translation: Of Tiberius Felix, most enthusiastic of all craftsmen, who lived 17 years, 11 months, and 8 days, [by his] very unhappy parents.
EDCS-12401230.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tiberius Felix, a young boy with diverse interests, dedicated by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401231.translation: Chre and Abidia Phr, living, for themselves. Abidia, 52 years old.
EDCS-12401231.summary: This funerary epitaph records the age of Abidia.

EDCS-12401232.translation: Gaius Acellius Primigenius and Ocellia Feli.
EDCS-12401232.summary: This is a funerary epitaph mentioning Gaius Acellius Primigenius and Ocellia Feli.

EDCS-12401233.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Oppia Secundina, to her incomparable husband Marcus Aelius Cresimus, with whom she lived for 15 years without any complaint, to him who deserved well.
EDCS-12401233.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her well-deserving husband, noting their 15 years of marriage.

EDCS-12401234.translation: For the memory of Titus Aelius Successus. Aelia Modestina and Pardus made [this] for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-12401234.summary: This funerary epitaph records a memorial created by Aelia Modestina and Pardus for Titus Aelius Successus.

EDCS-12401237.translation: For Aelia Rhodine, their foster-daughter, who deserved well. Aelius Panthius and Pismatius made [this].
EDCS-12401237.summary: This funerary epitaph records a memorial for Aelia Rhodine, made by Aelius Panthius and Pismatius.

EDCS-12401238.translation: To the Divine Spirits, sacred to Aemilius.
EDCS-12401238.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Aemilius.

EDCS-12401241.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Agathopus, his son. Modestus, father.
EDCS-12401241.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Modestus to his son, Agathopus.

EDCS-12401242.translation: To Gaius, son of Caius. Albia Cai made [this] for herself and for Vibia.
EDCS-12401242.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Albia Cai for herself and for Vibia, and also mentions Gaius, son of Caius.

EDCS-12401243.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Alexandria lived 4 years, 11 months, 4 days. To the Divine Spirits of Primi. To the Divine Spirits of Caesarini.
EDCS-12401243.summary: This funerary epitaph records the ages of Alexandria, Primi, and Caesarini.

EDCS-12401245.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Naevia Sabina made [this] for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Allius Apriculus.
EDCS-12401245.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Naevia Sabina to her well-deserving husband, Marcus Allius Apriculus.

EDCS-12401246.translation: To the spirits of Allia Felicitas, who lived 6 years, 3 months, and 20 days. Allia Iustina, a most unhappy mother, made this.
EDCS-12401246.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Allia Felicitas, dedicated by her grieving mother.

EDCS-12401247.translation: Iunia Sabina made this for her incomparable and deserving husband, Amarfius Clementus.
EDCS-12401247.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife to her deserving husband.

EDCS-12401248.translation: Gaius Naevius, heir, made this for his deserving Amarfia Secundina, an innocent woman, who lived 25 years, 6 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-12401248.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amarfia Secundina, dedicated by her heir.

EDCS-12401249.translation: Lucius Antonius, son of Lucius, of the Ant[onia tribe?], and Gaius Valerius, freedman of Caius, with whom he lived without complaint. Made this for the deserving one.
EDCS-12401249.summary: This funerary epitaph appears to be a dedication from a freedman to a partner with whom he had a harmonious relationship.

EDCS-12401250.translation: Antonia Euche, freedwoman of Caius, made this for herself and for Marcus Ovius, freedman of the woman, Communis, freedman of the woman, and Suavius, freedman of Caius, and Restitutus, freedman of Marcus, Aventinus, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-12401250.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Antonia Euche for herself and several freedmen, possibly her family or associates.

EDCS-12401251.translation: To his deserving wife, from her husband Aper.
EDCS-12401251.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, likely a dedication from a husband to his wife.

EDCS-12401252.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the dead. To Aptus, the colleagues made this for the deserving one, who lived 100 years, 10 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-12401252.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by colleagues to Aptus, who lived to a remarkable age.

EDCS-12401254.translation: Argentaria Maximilla, daughter of Lucius, for her excellent daughter.
EDCS-12401254.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a mother to her daughter.

EDCS-12401255.translation: To the spirits of the dead. Alexander made this for his incomparable wife Argentilla, who lived 30 years, 8 months, and 22 days, and for himself, most loving. For the deserving one.
EDCS-12401255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Alexander to his deceased wife Argentilla and for himself.

EDCS-12401256.translation: To Gaius Asillius Onesimus, an incomparable spouse, with whom she lived 30 years. Avillia Qu[...]
EDCS-12401256.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph, a dedication from Avillia to her spouse Gaius Asillius Onesimus.

EDCS-12401257.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Tatineius Liberalis, dearest son, who lived 27 years. Catineia Libera and Iustina, his most wretched parents (dedicated this).
EDCS-12401257.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young man named Tatineius Liberalis, dedicated by his grieving parents.

EDCS-12401258.translation: 107 units of length belonging to Marcus Audeius Philodamus, freedman of Marcus, 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the land.
EDCS-12401258.summary: This inscription appears to record land measurement or boundaries, possibly related to property belonging to Marcus Audeius Philodamus.

EDCS-12401259.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. (She) lived 41 years, 8 months, and 5 days. Iustianus Martius, freedman of the emperors, (dedicated this).
EDCS-12401259.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Audeia Sabina, who lived to an advanced age, with a dedication by Iustianus Martius.

EDCS-12401260.translation: To Aufillia, freedwoman of a woman, for herself and Aufillius.
EDCS-12401260.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aufillia, a freedwoman, for herself and Aufillius.

EDCS-12401261.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Marcus Aurelius Antinoüs, who lived 22 years, 7 months. Ulpia Agrippina, his mother, and Aurelia Beronice, his most devoted sister, made this for their dearest son and brother.
EDCS-12401261.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young man named Marcus Aurelius Antinoüs, dedicated by his mother and sister.

EDCS-12401262.summary: This is a brief dedication, likely a tombstone or memorial, for Marcus Aurelius Faustinus.

EDCS-12401263.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Marcus Aurelius Herculanus, who lived 7 years, 5 months, 17 days. Marcus Aurelius Alexander, for his dearest son, whose death was bitter.
EDCS-12401263.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young boy named Marcus Aurelius Herculanus, dedicated by his father, Marcus Aurelius Alexander.

EDCS-12401264.translation: Turselius Maximus made this for Aurelia Hammonia, his most beloved wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-12401264.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Turselius Maximus to his beloved wife, Aurelia Hammonia.

EDCS-12401265.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Caius Tetius Iustinus made this for Aurelia Postina, his deserving wife.
EDCS-12401265.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Caius Tetius Iustinus to his wife, Aurelia Postina.

EDCS-12401268.summary: This is a concise memorial inscription, likely a tomb marker, dedicated to Bassus.

EDCS-12401269.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying a man named Bassaeus Astur.

EDCS-12401271.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Bassaea the Studious, my dearest daughter, who lived 12 years, 3 months, and 4 days. Her father, Titlius the Studious, made this.
EDCS-12401271.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a father to his young daughter, Bassaea the Studious.

EDCS-12401274.translation: Burreia Iunilla made this for her husband Restitutus Burreius, who lived 21 years, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-12401274.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deceased husband, Restitutus Burreius.

EDCS-12401276.translation: Lucius Coelius, son of Lucius, for himself and his family: 12 feet in front, 10 feet in the rear.
EDCS-12401276.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording land dimensions for a family plot belonging to Lucius Coelius.

EDCS-12401277.translation: [...]esius LEG. For Lucius Caesius, son of Lucius. Fructus, freedman of Lucius, and Titus Ventidius, his freedman, and [...]aesius, his father Faustus.
EDCS-12401277.summary: This funerary inscription names Lucius Caesius and others, including freedmen and potentially a father.

EDCS-12401278.summary: This fragment of an inscription mentions the father of Ruo and a person named Faustus.

EDCS-12401279.translation: Caesia, freedwoman of a woman, for herself, and for Lucius Caesius, freedman of a woman, Modestus, and for Lucius Aitios Felix, and for Caesius, freedman of a woman, Auctus, and for Communis.
EDCS-12401279.summary: This funerary inscription is set up by Caesia, a freedwoman, for herself and several other individuals, including freedmen.

EDCS-12401280.translation: Caesia, freedwoman of Licinius, for Caesia, freedwoman of a woman, for Aulus Plautius, freedman of Aulus, Amphio, and Viticula.
EDCS-12401280.summary: This funerary inscription records dedications by Caesia, a freedwoman, for herself and several other people, including freedmen.

EDCS-12401281.translation: Lucius Coelius, for himself and for Varia.
EDCS-12401281.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Coelius for himself and for Varia, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-12401282.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Calhemera lived 26 years. Her master made this for her because of her services and integrity, and because she loved him with full affection.
EDCS-12401282.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a slave named Calhemera, dedicated by her master in recognition of her loyalty and service.

EDCS-12401283.translation: To Caius Calpenus Eutychus, freedman of Caius, for himself and his family, and for Specula, freedwoman of Serveia, 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-12401283.summary: This is a boundary inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, defining the extent of a plot of land belonging to Caius Calpenus Eutychus.

EDCS-12401284.translation: Marcus Calventius Philomusus, freedman of Marcus, made this according to the will of Marcus Calventius Auctus, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-12401284.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the construction of a monument by Marcus Calventius Philomusus for his relative, Marcus Calventius Auctus, as stipulated in a will.

EDCS-12401286.translation: To the Divine Shades. Casineia Rufina made this for her husband Casineius Ianuarius, most holy and deserving.
EDCS-12401286.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Casineia Rufina to her husband, Casineius Ianuarius.

EDCS-12401289.translation: The decurions decreed that Caio Catio, son of Caius, and Paulla, daughter of Caius, daughter of Balbus Opetreius, should be carried to the funeral rites at public expense from the forum and the tribunal, and buried here; and for Fuficia Viola, freedwoman of Aulus, Caius Catius, son of Caius, [made this] for his parents and wife.
EDCS-12401289.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a public burial decreed for Caio Catio and Paulla, and also includes a dedication by Caius Catius for his parents and wife.

EDCS-12401291.translation: To Caucia Diodora, by Lucius Procilius.
EDCS-12401291.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, from Lucius Procilius to Caucia Diodora.

EDCS-12401292.summary: This is a tombstone or dedication for Lucius Cauponius, son of Caius.

EDCS-12401294.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ceionia Sefare made this for her mother Ceionia Agrippina, most beloved daughter, deserving.
EDCS-12401294.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Ceionia Sefare to her mother, Ceionia Agrippina.

EDCS-12401295.translation: To Quintus Cenenius Iucundus, who lived one year and [one] month, by Cerrinia.
EDCS-12401295.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Cenenius Iucundus, recording his age at death and the dedicator, Cerrinia.

EDCS-12401296.summary: This is likely an epitaph or dedication for Publius Cipius Alpam.

EDCS-12401298.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cleppiana Secundina made this for Cleppiana Augustina, who lived 55 years.
EDCS-12401298.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Cleppiana Secundina for Cleppiana Augustina, recording her age at death.

EDCS-12401299.translation: To Gaius Clodius, son of Numerius, of the Pupinia tribe. Numerius Clodius Capito, son of Numerius, of the Pupinia tribe, dedicated this.
EDCS-12401299.summary: A funerary dedication by Numerius Clodius Capito for his relative Gaius Clodius.

EDCS-12401300.translation: To the Divine Shades. Clodia Iustina, Gaius Clodius Sabinus, Gaius Clodius Festus, and Gaius Clodius Festivus made this for their deserving father, Gaius Clodius Festus.
EDCS-12401300.summary: A funerary dedication by children for their father, Gaius Clodius Festus.

EDCS-12401301.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titilia made this for her most excellent husband, who lived [XX] years.
EDCS-12401301.summary: A funerary dedication by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-12401302.translation: To Codiflanus Felix, freedman of Gaius Appius and the freedwoman of Appia Caius.
EDCS-12401302.summary: An epitaph for Codiflanus Felix, a freedman.

EDCS-12401303.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lollius son of Fortunatus made this for his deserving father, Concordius.
EDCS-12401303.summary: A funerary dedication by a son for his father, Concordius.

EDCS-12401304.translation: To the Divine Shades. The mother made this for her dearest son, Quintus Concordius Primus, who lived 5 years, 7 months, and 20 days.
EDCS-12401304.summary: A funerary dedication by a mother for her young son, Quintus Concordius Primus.

EDCS-12401305.translation: Concordius Suavis for himself and for Urria Optata, and Quintus.
EDCS-12401305.summary: A dedication or epitaph possibly for multiple individuals, including Concordius Suavis and Urria Optata.

EDCS-12401307.translation: To Lucius Cornelius Juvenis, who lived 24 years and 6 months. Parents Lucius Cornelius Pudens and [---] Sabina.
EDCS-12401307.summary: A funerary dedication by parents for their son, Lucius Cornelius Juvenis.

EDCS-12401308.translation: To Eupolus, freedman of Lucius Cornelius, [who lived] 7 years.
EDCS-12401308.summary: A funerary dedication for a young freedman named Eupolus.

EDCS-12401309.summary: An epitaph for Marcus Cosinius Crispinus.

EDCS-12401310.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Cosinius Primitivus and Cosinia Fortunata made this for their most excellent father.
EDCS-12401310.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Marcus Cosinius Primitivus and Cosinia Fortunata to their father.

EDCS-12401311.translation: Cosinia Maximina made this for her incomparable son, and for her husband Quintus Maelius Moderatus, and for her freedman Marcus Cosinius Oecogenus, and for herself.
EDCS-12401311.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Cosinia Maximina for her son, husband, freedman, and herself.

EDCS-12401312.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Crassus Fortunatus made this for his dearest son Marcus Crassus Saturninus, who lived 13 years, 10 months, and 3 days.
EDCS-12401312.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Marcus Crassus Fortunatus for his son Marcus Crassus Saturninus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-12401313.translation: For Publius Curtius Philocalus, freedman of Publius.
EDCS-12401313.summary: This brief inscription records a dedication to Publius Curtius Philocalus, identified as a freedman.

EDCS-12401314.translation: Naevia Optata [made this] for Aulus Curtius Primianus, who lived 23 years and 6 months.
EDCS-12401314.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by Naevia Optata for Aulus Curtius Primianus, likely her son.

EDCS-12401315.translation: For Gaius Curtius Secundus, from Gaius Curtius and Curia Curtia.
EDCS-12401315.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Gaius Curtius and Curia Curtia for Gaius Curtius Secundus.

EDCS-12401317.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Titius Urbicus, the heir, [made this] for his deserving mother Didia Procula, wife of Gaius Titius Euporianus.
EDCS-12401317.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication by the heir Gaius Titius Urbicus for his mother Didia Procula.

EDCS-12401318.translation: For the diligent Clodia Mami, freedwoman of Mani, and for Lucius Clodius Albanus, son of Lucius, first fetial, and for Cyparissus Felix Secundus. This monument shall not pass to an heir.
EDCS-12401318.summary: This funerary epitaph records dedications for Clodia Restituta, Lucius Clodius Albanus, and Cyparissus Felicis Secundus, with a clause stating the monument is not inheritable.

EDCS-12401320.translation: For Lucius Egnatius Varus, son of Publius, 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-12401320.summary: This inscription records land dimensions associated with Lucius Egnatius Varus, son of Publius.

EDCS-12401321.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Gaius Ennius Agilis and Fundania Quinte made this for their sweetest son Gaius Ennius Agilis, who lived 8 years and 6 months, and for their wife, Octavia Silvinia, with whom he lived 19 years and 9 months, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-12401321.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication made by Gaius Ennius Agilis and Fundania Quinte for their son Gaius Ennius Agilis, his wife Octavia Silvinia, and themselves.

EDCS-12401322.translation: This monument will not pass to an heir. Marcus Ennius Macedo, freedman of Marcus Ennius, for himself and Pollia Syntyche, daughter of Ennius Iucundilla. You, traveler, who hasten towards the Alban hills, pause your swift feet for a little while, and do not let it be a heavy grief for us to mourn. When life had already filled my years twice over six times, and promised the joys of marriage, death, hastening, took away the years of my growth. Both parents grieved and perpetually mourn. Now I lie buried in this eternal resting place, and I myself lament the end of my fate. Maxsim.
EDCS-12401322.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Ennius Macedo and Pollia Syntyche, detailing their relationship and expressing grief over the death of a child, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401324.translation: Vilia Fausta for herself and her husband Valerius Lucrio.
EDCS-12401324.summary: A funerary dedication by Vilia Fausta for herself and her husband, Valerius Lucrio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401325.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Valeria Secundina made this for her incomparable husband, Titus Faelis Crescens, with whom she lived for 3 years and 6 months, a deserving man.
EDCS-12401325.summary: A funerary dedication by Valeria Secundina for her husband, Titus Faelis Crescens, highlighting their short but loving marriage, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401327.translation: Flavius Umbrius Fortunius made this for Flavius Apro, who lived 12 years, 7 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-12401327.summary: A funerary epitaph by a father, Flavius Umbrius Fortunius, for his son, Flavius Apro, who died at a young age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401328.translation: Quintus Flurius for Quintus Flurius Lucius, Luccia Maria.
EDCS-12401328.summary: A dedication or funerary inscription by Quintus Flurius, potentially for family members Quintus Flurius Lucius and Luccia Maria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401329.summary: A simple funerary dedication or identification of Freganius Nicephor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401331.translation: Rusticulus. Tullonia Lea made this for her husband, Fufius Iustinus, a good man, with whom she lived for sixty years.
EDCS-12401331.summary: A funerary epitaph by Tullonia Lea for her beloved husband, Fufius Iustinus, commemorating their long marriage of sixty years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401332.translation: Fufius Restutus made this for Marcus Fufius Restutus, who lived 10 years and 8 days.
EDCS-12401332.summary: A funerary epitaph by a father, Fufius Restutus, for his son, Marcus Fufius Restutus, who died at a young age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401333.translation: Fufia Gratilla, freedwoman of Publius, for herself and for Publius Fufius Onesimus, Publius Fufius Fuscus, Enniae Regillae, Fufia Secundae, Fufia Atiaticheni, Publius Fufius Fructus. 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-12401333.summary: A funerary dedication by Fufia Gratilla for herself and multiple other individuals, specifying plot dimensions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401334.translation: Fuficia Cleopatra, freedwoman of a woman, made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-12401334.summary: A funerary dedication made by Fuficia Cleopatra for herself during her lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401336.translation: Lucius Fulvius Avitus: 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-12401336.summary: This is a funerary boundary inscription recording land dimensions, likely for a tomb or plot belonging to Lucius Fulvius Avitus.

EDCS-12401337.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Furius Ierax made this for himself and for his deserving wife, Sestidia Provincia, while he was alive.
EDCS-12401337.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Marcus Furius Ierax for himself and his wife, Sestidia Provincia, indicating it was made during his lifetime.

EDCS-12401338.translation: To Galla, son of Caius, and to Marcio, son of Marcus. Auge, freedwoman of a woman, made this for herself and her husband.
EDCS-12401338.summary: This inscription records a tomb or monument erected by Auge, a freedwoman, for herself and her husband, commemorating Galla and Marcio.

EDCS-12401339.translation: Vibeia Salvia, freedwoman of Caius, and Gnaeus Haius Tertius, son of Cnaeus, are buried here: 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-12401339.summary: This is a funerary boundary inscription for Vibeia Salvia and Gnaeus Haius Tertius, specifying the dimensions of their burial plot.

EDCS-12401340.translation: [...rrius...]tus made this for himself and for his descendants, the Helpidi.
EDCS-12401340.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the construction of a monument by [---]rrius [-]tus for himself and the descendants of the Helpidi family.

EDCS-12401341.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Helvius Primillus lived for 5 days. Caius Helvius Primillus made this for the deserving (infant).
EDCS-12401341.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for the infant Caius Helvius Primillus, who lived only 5 days, set up by his father, also Caius Helvius Primillus.

EDCS-12401343.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed and to the memory of Caius Herennius Modestus. Aulus Vibbius Egnatianus for his dearest comrade.
EDCS-12401343.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deceased Caius Herennius Modestus by his very close companion Aulus Vibbius Egnatianus.

EDCS-12401344.translation: Caius Herennius Pyramus, freedman of Caius, made this for himself, for his wife Herennia Thisbe, and for Herennia Faventina, Laurus, and Quinte, his family.
EDCS-12401344.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Caius Herennius Pyramus for himself, his wife, and other family members, including freed persons.

EDCS-12401345.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Caecius Tintinabelus made this for his deserving wife, Herennia Urania. To the Spirits of the Departed. Tintinabelus made this for Urania, deserving.
EDCS-12401345.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by Marcus Caecius Tintinabelus for his wife Herennia Urania, with a repeated dedication.

EDCS-12401346.translation: Terconia Hilara is buried here: 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-12401346.summary: This is a funerary boundary inscription indicating the dimensions of the burial plot for Terconia Hilara.

EDCS-12401347.translation: To Hilarus, freedman of Caius, and Repentinus, freedman of Titus, in accordance with the will of Helena, freedwoman of Titus, [as] his beloved pet.
EDCS-12401347.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to pets by their owner, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401348.translation: He lived 14 years, 9 months, and 2 days. Aulus Ignius Felix and Domitia Quintilla made this for their beloved ward.
EDCS-12401348.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a young person, set up by their guardians, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401349.translation: Ignius, son of Aulus, of the Pupinia tribe.
EDCS-12401349.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying an individual by name and tribal affiliation, likely dating from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-12401350.translation: To the Divine Shades. He lived 15 years, 3 months, and 15 days. Soterichus and Leda, parents, made this.
EDCS-12401350.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young person, erected by their parents, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401351.translation: To Tiberius Julius Vestorianus, son of Tiberius, of the Stellatina tribe.
EDCS-12401351.summary: This is a funerary epitaph identifying an individual by name and tribal affiliation, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401352.translation: To the Divine Shades, sacred. Lucius Lucretius made this for his most dutiful father, Julius Victor, a good man, who lived 50 years and 6 months.
EDCS-12401352.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a father, erected by his son, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401353.translation: To Julia Basseaea Irenis. Her mother Valeria Felicissima made this for her well-deserving mother.
EDCS-12401353.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a mother, erected by her daughter, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401354.translation: To Julia Felicula. Trebellius Urnius made this for his incomparable wife, with whom he lived 20 years, 2 months, and 5 days, for her deserts.
EDCS-12401354.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a wife, erected by her husband, recording their years of marriage, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401355.translation: To the Memory of Julia Prima, a most faithful and incomparable woman. Numerius Blaesius Iulius Dexter, and Tiberius Julius Concordius Lupulus, and Marcianus, her sons, made this for their sweetest mother.
EDCS-12401355.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a mother, erected by her three sons, emphasizing her virtues, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401356.translation: To the Divine Shades. Julia Strationice made this for her daughter Julia Prima, who lived 3 years, 7 months, and 7 days.
EDCS-12401356.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, erected by her mother, likely dating from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-12401357.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Julia Veneria, who lived 45 years. Voconius Felicissimus for his wife, who lived 32 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-12401357.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Voconius Felicissimus to his wife, Julia Veneria, who lived 45 years and for whom he also provided a tomb for himself, who lived 32 years.

EDCS-12401358.translation: To Iunia Rutilia Iunianena, who lived 42 years and 1 month. Rufinus for his most beloved tent-mate, well-deserving, made (this).
EDCS-12401358.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iunia Rutilia Iunianena, who lived 42 years and 1 month, dedicated by Rufinus, her tent-mate.

EDCS-12401359.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Lucius Labienus Anatellontis. Lucius Labienus Anatello, his son, made (this) for his father.
EDCS-12401359.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Lucius Labienus Anatello to his father, Lucius Labienus Anatellontis.

EDCS-12401360.translation: To Gaius Larius, son of Quintus, of the Stellatina tribe.
EDCS-12401360.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Larius, son of Quintus, indicating his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-12401361.translation: To Lucius Latinus Stabilius, son of Lucius. To Lucius Latinus, son of Lucius, his father. To Maria, daughter of Caius, his mother. To Gaius Latinus Secundus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-12401361.summary: This funerary epitaph lists three family members: Lucius Latinus Stabilius, his father Lucius Latinus, and his mother Maria, as well as his brother Gaius Latinus Secundus.

EDCS-12401363.translation: To Licinia Covia Daphne. Lucius Licinius Erotion, her father, for his most sweet daughter, who lived 23 years, 11 months, and 17 days, well-deserving, made this monument enclosed by a wall.
EDCS-12401363.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Licinia Covia Daphne, dedicated by her father Lucius Licinius Erotion, detailing her age at death and the enclosure of her tomb.

EDCS-12401365.translation: Who lived 11 years and 7 months. Licinia Ianuaria for her well-deserving husband made (this).
EDCS-12401365.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life span of an unnamed individual and was made by Licinia Ianuaria for her husband.

EDCS-12401366.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Gaius Licinius Felix, who lived 12 years and 6 days. His parents made (this) for their son.
EDCS-12401366.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by the parents to their son, Gaius Licinius Felix, who died at the age of 12 years and 6 days.

EDCS-12401367.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Aulus Livius Mucro.
EDCS-12401367.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to Aulus Livius Mucro.

EDCS-12401368.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Lollius Chryseros.
EDCS-12401368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Lollius Chryseros.

EDCS-12401369.translation: Lucius Lollius Hilarus, freedman of Lucius, and Lollia Eleutheris, freedwoman of Lucius, [set this up] in front 12 feet, in the field 10 feet.
EDCS-12401369.summary: This is a boundary marker specifying land dimensions, set up by Lucius Lollius Hilarus and Lollia Eleutheris.

EDCS-12401371.translation: To the Divine Shades. Luccius Redemptus and Aepidia Secundina made [this] for their son, Gaius Luccius Rufinus, who lived 6 years, 8 months, 10 days.
EDCS-12401371.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Gaius Luccius Rufinus, erected by his parents Luccius Redemptus and Aepidia Secundina.

EDCS-12401372.translation: To Gaius Lucretius Statilius, who lived 17 years and 2 months. Lucretius Cavus, his father, whom you quickly deceived, [established this]...innocent...
EDCS-12401372.summary: This is a tombstone fragment honoring Gaius Lucretius Statilius, erected by his father Lucretius Cavus.

EDCS-12401373.translation: Titus Diphilus, freedman of Titus Maenius, [set this up] in front 12 feet, in the field 12 feet.
EDCS-12401373.summary: This is a boundary marker specifying land dimensions, set up by Titus Diphilus, freedman of Titus Maenius.

EDCS-12401374.translation: To the Divine Shades of Marcus Maenius Philocalus.
EDCS-12401374.summary: This is a tombstone honoring Marcus Maenius Philocalus.

EDCS-12401375.translation: [To] Polla, daughter of Lucius, daughter of Quintio Philumena, and Mallia Inventa, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-12401375.summary: This is a tombstone fragment honoring Polla, daughter of Lucius and Quintio Philumena, and Mallia Inventa.

EDCS-12401376.translation: [From the gods SEM] D SIC I N. Mamercia...
EDCS-12401376.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Mamercia and possibly a dedication or religious formula.

EDCS-12401378.translation: To the Divine Shades. Maria Rufina made [this] for her most dutiful parents, Lucius Marius Ampiatus and Octavia Afinia.
EDCS-12401378.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Lucius Marius Ampiatus and Octavia Afinia, erected by their daughter Maria Rufina.

EDCS-12401379.translation: Marcus Marius Capreolus made [this] for himself while alive, and for his wife Satria Voluptas, and for Marius Capreolus and Vettia Ha in a...
EDCS-12401379.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Marcus Marius Capreolus established a burial plot for himself, his wife, and others.

EDCS-12401381.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marius Iustus, a man more than most kind, [lies] here.
EDCS-12401381.summary: This is a tombstone honoring Marius Iustus, described as a very kind man.

EDCS-12401382.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aletia, a freedwoman, identifying her patron as Maria.

EDCS-12401383.translation: And for Maria's freedman Felix, and for Primigenia, freedwoman of Sex. Aelia Chrysan, 14 feet in front, 14 feet in the field.
EDCS-12401383.summary: This funerary inscription records land boundaries and dedications to Maria's freedman Felix and Primigenia, freedwoman of Sex. Aelia Chrysan.

EDCS-12401384.translation: Lucius Pescennius Felix made this for Maria, a freedwoman, and for Pescennia, freedwoman of Lucius, and for himself.
EDCS-12401384.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maria, a freedwoman, and Pescennia, freedwoman of Lucius, made by Lucius Pescennius Felix for them and himself.

EDCS-12401385.translation: I, Maro, beloved pupil of Gaius Calpurnius Lausus, my master, always your companion wherever you went, minister of the cup, learned in the palaestra, a boy of equestrian rank, buried here, lived eighteen years.
EDCS-12401385.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maro, a young boy and slave of Gaius Calpurnius Lausus, detailing his life and death at eighteen years old.

EDCS-12401386.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph identifying Messius as the freedman of Decimus.

EDCS-12401387.translation: To the divine spirits. Valeria Prima made this for her husband, Marco Metilio Emineo, who lived with her for 15 years, 3 months, and 20 days, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-12401387.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated by Valeria Prima to her well-deserving husband, Marco Metilio Emineo, detailing the length of their marriage.

EDCS-12401388.translation: To Metilia, daughter of Lucius Puscilus. To Metilia Potima, dearest mother.
EDCS-12401388.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates two women named Metilia, one the daughter of Puscilus, and the other identified as a mother.

EDCS-12401389.translation: To the divine spirits, sacred. Mettia Successa made this for Lucius Mettius Fortunatus, her master, patron, and husband, with whom she lived 20 years, a well-deserving man. To the divine spirits. Mettius Titus, son of [---], who lived 70 years, 3 months, and 12 days, and Vibia R., his wife, [---] FIIC IMTI, a well-deserving man.
EDCS-12401389.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription dedicated by Mettia Successa to her husband Lucius Mettius Fortunatus, and also records the death of Mettius Primitivus (also called Sotatus) and his wife Vibia R.

EDCS-12401391.translation: nia Modesta made [this] for her patron.
EDCS-12401391.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by nia Modesta for her patron.

EDCS-12401393.translation: Lucius Molvius Flaccus, freedman of Lucius. For Lucius Molvius Calenus, patron, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-12401393.summary: This funerary inscription appears to record two freedmen of the same patron, Lucius, possibly clarifying their relationship or status within the household.

EDCS-12401395.translation: To Mummeia, daughter of Mummeius, who lived 24 years, 7 months, 25 days. Caius Titius Ianuarius [dedicated this].
EDCS-12401395.summary: This is a tombstone for Mummeia, who died young, erected by Caius Titius Ianuarius.

EDCS-12401396.translation: The heir caused this monument to be built for Gaius Munatius Chrestus, freedman of Caius; for Neotini, freedwoman of Laelia, freedman of Lucius; for Gaius Munatius Salvius, freedman of Caius; for Munatia Hilarus, freedwoman of Caius; for Gaius Munatius Primus, freedman of Caius.
EDCS-12401396.summary: This funerary monument was erected by an heir for several freedmen and freedwomen associated with the Munatii family.

EDCS-12401397.translation: To the Divine Shades of Marcus Munatius Felicissimus, who lived 15 years, 6 months, 10 days. Munatius Aemilianus and Epuria Capreola, parents [dedicated this].
EDCS-12401397.summary: This tombstone is for Marcus Munatius Felicissimus, who died as a youth, dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-12401398.translation: To the Divine Shades of Munatia Prisca, a woman of rare chastity.
EDCS-12401398.summary: This inscription honors Munatia Prisca, emphasizing her virtue.

EDCS-12401399.translation: To the Divine Shades of Veiedia Fortunata, his wife, with whom he lived 22 years, 3 months; who lived more than 7 days. Marcus Munius Proculeianus, kinsman, made [this] for her well-deserving. To the Divine Shades of Marcus Munius Vitalis, who lived 39 years, 3 months, 22 days. Marcus Munius Proculeianus, [his] most innocent brother, made [this] for him well-deserving.
EDCS-12401399.summary: This is a double tombstone erected by Marcus Munius Proculeianus for his wife, Veiedia Fortunata, and his brother, Marcus Munius Vitalis.

EDCS-12401402.translation: Naevia Viticula, freedwoman of Marcius, for herself and for Gaius Pontius, freedman of Caius; and Marcus [dedicated this].
EDCS-12401402.summary: This is a tombstone for Naevia Viticula and potentially others, indicating self-dedication and inclusion of others.

EDCS-12401404.translation: To Natria Sabina, who lived 8 years, Marcus Brittius Ter Proba [dedicated this].
EDCS-12401404.summary: This tombstone is for Natria Sabina, who died as a child, erected by Marcus Brittius Ter Proba.

EDCS-12401405.translation: To Nemitia Martiana, with whom he lived 24 years. Privatus [dedicated this] to his incomparable wife.
EDCS-12401405.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Privatus to his wife Nemitia Martiana.

EDCS-12401406.translation: To the Divine Shades of Neratia Iucunda, his dearest wife, and for himself. Caius Betitius Secundus [dedicated this].
EDCS-12401406.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Caius Betitius Secundus to his beloved wife Neratia Iucunda and for himself.

EDCS-12401407.translation: To the Divine Shades of Neria Aetia, his well-deserving wife. Caius Mutius Sabinianus made [this].
EDCS-12401407.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Caius Mutius Sabinianus to his wife Neria Aetia.

EDCS-12401409.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Nummia Quadratilla, sweetest granddaughter of Lucia.
EDCS-12401409.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Nummia Quadratilla, a young woman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401410.translation: Statius Nicephor for himself and for his freedwoman Sergia Arbuscula, and for his freedman Lucius Sergius Eros, and for his freedman Lucius Sergius.
EDCS-12401410.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Statius Nicephor for himself and members of his household, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401411.translation: To Obideius, son of Quintus, of the Stellatina tribe. To Obideius, son of Quintus, of the Stellatina tribe. [---] aessia Rufa to [---]eius, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-12401411.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary dedication, possibly with incomplete or damaged text, mentioning members of the Stellatina tribe, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401412.summary: This is a dedication to Ocellia, identified as a freedwoman and possibly named Optata, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401413.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Oclatia Ampliata, who lived 25 years, 10 months, 6 days. Caius Vitalis Ampliatus, the most unhappy parents.
EDCS-12401413.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Oclatia Ampliata, recording her age at death and a dedication by her parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401414.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication, possibly funerary or honorific, from Titus Octavius to himself or another Titus Octavius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401415.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Quintus Octavius Felicioni. Octavius Felicio, his son, and Cerrinia Asiatice, with whom he lived for 30 years and 2 months without complaint, made this for their well-deserving husband.
EDCS-12401415.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son and wife for Quintus Octavius Felicioni, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401416.summary: This is an incomplete funerary inscription or dedication for Octavius Iucundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401417.translation: To Octavia Ampelius, freedwoman of Lucius, for herself and for her son Lucius Octavius Albanus, freedman of Lucius, and for her daughter Octavia Albana.
EDCS-12401417.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Octavia Ampelius for herself and her children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401418.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Octavia Crescentina, who lived an ancient life, most serious in loyalty and diligence. Caius Valerius Ianuarius, her husband, with his sons, made this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-12401418.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband and sons for Octavia Crescentina, praising her virtues, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401419.translation: Gaius Ofellius, freedwoman of Ofellia; Gaius Ofellius, freedman; Gaius Ofellius, freedman; Gaius Ofellius, freedman; Ofellia.
EDCS-12401419.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, likely related to a family or a funerary context, with multiple freedmen and freedwomen bearing the name Gaius Ofellius and Ofellia.

EDCS-12401420.translation: To the departed spirits. Caecia Montana made this for her well-deserving husband, Gaius Ofilius Modestus, also known as Asparagus, with whom she lived for 17 years.
EDCS-12401420.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a wife for her husband, providing his name, an alternative name, and the duration of their marriage, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401423.translation: To the departed spirits. For Oppia Optata, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-12401423.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a freedwoman named Oppia Optata, mentioning her patron Lucius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401424.translation: Auctus, freedman of a woman, for himself and his patroness, in front 12 feet.
EDCS-12401424.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedman named Auctus for himself and his patroness, specifying the dimensions of his plot, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401425.translation: Paccia Vitalis, freedwoman of Gaius, for herself and for Marcus Valerius Tutor and Pietas.
EDCS-12401425.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedwoman named Paccia Vitalis for herself and for Marcus Valerius Tutor and Pietas, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401426.translation: To Gaius Papirius. Statia, freedwoman of Marcus, [made this] for her husband, because I hoped [we] would be together after my death, unfortunate one, I established this for the ashes and LA.
EDCS-12401426.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife, Statia, for her husband, Gaius Papirius, expressing her grief and anticipation of reunion in death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401427.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication to Gaius Papirius, with the meaning of 'NEVEN' unclear due to the missing text.

EDCS-12401428.translation: Numerius Onesimus, freedman of Numerius Pedius, for himself and his family, 12 feet in front and 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-12401428.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedman named Numerius Onesimus for himself and his family, specifying the dimensions of his burial plot, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401429.translation: Gaius Chryseros, freedman of Gaius Petillius, for himself and for Curtia Egloge, freedwoman of a woman, and for Curtia Restituta, freedwoman of a woman, and for the freedman of a woman Zmaragdus, and for Crustidia Tertia, freedwoman of Titus, and for Gaius Petillius.
EDCS-12401429.summary: This is a complex funerary dedication by Gaius Chryseros for himself and multiple family members and freedpersons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401430.translation: To Gaius Petronius Primus, who lived 39 years. Cerrinia Iulia [made this].
EDCS-12401430.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Cerrinia Iulia for her husband Gaius Petronius Primus, stating his name and age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12401431.translation: Petronia Philsia [made this] for her husband Numerius Stenius Saturninus.
EDCS-12401431.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to a husband, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12401432.translation: Gaius Petuellius, son of Quintus, of the Falerna tribe, while alive, arranged for this monument to be made for himself and for his wife Epidia, daughter of Publius, of the Neria tribe, and approved it.
EDCS-12401432.summary: This is a funerary monument inscription from the 1st-2nd century CE, indicating a man commissioned his and his wife's tomb while alive.

EDCS-12401433.translation: Fructilla, freedwoman of a woman, [made this] for Philema and for herself and her [family], with 11 feet in front and 11 feet in the ground.
EDCS-12401433.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a burial plot or tomb, with dimensions specified, likely dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-12401435.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aulus Plautius Restutus made this for his deserving wife Turpilia Clementi.
EDCS-12401435.summary: This funerary dedication, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE, records a husband's memorial to his deserving wife.

EDCS-12401436.translation: To the departed spirits. Pietas, of the Martensii, who lived 27 years, 10 months, 15 days, for Hispania, an incomparable wife, Pontius Priscus, her husband, made this for the deserving.
EDCS-12401436.summary: This funerary inscription from the 2nd-3rd century CE records the death of Hispania and the memorial erected by her husband, Pontius Priscus.

EDCS-12401437.translation: To the departed spirits of Pomponia Prima, who lived...
EDCS-12401437.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription from the 2nd-3rd century CE, honoring Pomponia Prima.

EDCS-12401438.translation: To the departed spirits of Gaius Pompilius Vitalis, who lived 40 years and 9 days. Epicharis, his wife from the Flaminia tribe, with whom he lived 20 years, set this up for the deserving.
EDCS-12401438.summary: This funerary inscription from the 2nd century CE records the death of Gaius Pompilius Vitalis and was erected by his wife, Epicharis, who also lists their duration of marriage.

EDCS-12401439.translation: Gaius Pontius P[---] and Publius Pontius [---]
EDCS-12401439.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE, mentioning two individuals named Pontius.

EDCS-12401440.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE, identifying Pontia Arura as a concubine.

EDCS-12401442.translation: Ania and Pass [made this] for Primio, her living husband.
EDCS-12401442.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE, records a dedication by Ania and Pass to her husband Primio.

EDCS-12401443.translation: Edius Priscus made this for my heirs, my sons listed below who shall bear my name, along with their grandsons. The garden, shop, and upstairs rooms. And if any harm comes to my sons concerning animals...
EDCS-12401443.summary: This is a fragment of a will or testament from an individual named Edius Priscus, detailing provisions for his heirs regarding property and potential future issues.

EDCS-12401444.translation: Caius Pullidius Asclepiodotus made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-12401444.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Caius Pullidius Asclepiodotus for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-12401446.translation: Quintus Quint Saturninus [made this] for his father, Quintius.
EDCS-12401446.summary: A dedication from Quintus Quint Saturninus to his father, Quintius, likely on a tomb or memorial.

EDCS-12401448.translation: AL [made this] for himself and for his daughter Rufina, daughter of Quintus, from Synhete.
EDCS-12401448.summary: An inscription recording that AL dedicated a monument to himself and his daughter Rufina, whose father was Quintus.

EDCS-12401449.translation: Lucius Rufrius arranged for [this] for himself and for his brother Rufrius.
EDCS-12401449.summary: This inscription indicates that Lucius Rufrius commissioned a monument for himself and his brother, Rufrius.

EDCS-12401450.translation: Cnaeus Rustius [made this] for Rustia, daughter of Titus, and for Cnaeus, son of Cnaeus Rustius. Cnaeus Cnaeus Rustius [made] this monument.
EDCS-12401450.summary: A monument inscription likely commissioned by Cnaeus Rustius, dedicating it to Rustia and Cnaeus, son of Cnaeus Rustius.

EDCS-12401451.translation: Of Cnaeus Rustius, son of Cnaeus, freedman of Theudosos: 10 feet in front, 10 feet in the land.
EDCS-12401451.summary: This inscription specifies the dimensions of a plot of land belonging to the freedman Theudosos, who was associated with Cnaeus Rustius.

EDCS-12401452.translation: The freedmen Phileros, Ochus, and Artemo made [this] for Marcus' freedwoman Rustia, in accordance with the will; [they] took care of what was to be made, [since the freedman] did not wish [to do it].
EDCS-12401452.summary: This inscription records that the freedmen Phileros, Ochus, and Artemo executed a commission for the freedwoman Rustia, as stipulated in a will, due to another freedman's refusal.

EDCS-12401453.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Herennia Onesime made [this] for her son, Marcus Rutilius Severianus, who lived 10 years, 4 months, and 19 days, her dearest and most unfortunate son, who deserved well.
EDCS-12401453.summary: A tombstone inscription from Herennia Onesime dedicated to her young son, Marcus Rutilius Severianus, providing details of his age at death.

EDCS-12401454.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Naevia Quintilla made [this] for her husband, Caius Sabernus Maximus, who lived 55 years and 2 months, for her well-deserving husband with whom she lived 16 years and 2 months.
EDCS-12401454.summary: A funerary inscription by Naevia Quintilla dedicated to her husband, Caius Sabernus Maximus, detailing his lifespan and the duration of their marriage.

EDCS-12401456.translation: To Samia Clementina, my dearest, Zosimus, of our Emperor.
EDCS-12401456.summary: A funerary dedication by Zosimus to his dearest Samia Clementina.

EDCS-12401457.translation: Marcus Sassius, son of Marcus; Quintus Sassius [son of Marcus].
EDCS-12401457.summary: This inscription lists the names of two individuals, likely related, possibly from a tombstone or property marker.

EDCS-12401458.translation: To Satria, freedwoman of Quintus, named Iucunda; 20 feet in front, [10] feet in the field.
EDCS-12401458.summary: A boundary marker or funerary inscription for Satria Iucunda, specifying land dimensions.

EDCS-12401459.translation: To the departed spirits; Betitius Callistus made this for his well-deserving Satria Vera, from Isena Noricuia.
EDCS-12401459.summary: A funerary dedication by Betitius Callistus to his well-deserving Satria Vera.

EDCS-12401460.translation: To Sattia Ianuaria, most chaste woman, who lived 37 years, 7 months, 8 days; Sextus Marius Hilarus made this for his incomparable wife, with whom he lived 23 years, 9 months, 11 days, for her well-deserving.
EDCS-12401460.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sattia Ianuaria, detailing her lifespan and the duration of her marriage.

EDCS-12401462.translation: To Spurinus, son of Spurinus, from the Stellatina tribe; his daughter Secunda.
EDCS-12401462.summary: This inscription seems to be a funerary dedication or memorial for Spurinus, set up by his daughter Secunda.

EDCS-12401463.translation: [---] to Uto Secundus, her dearest son, made this.
EDCS-12401463.summary: A fragmented funerary dedication for a son named Uto Secundus.

EDCS-12401464.translation: Caius Sentius Hector, freedman of Caius; Herennia Quarta, freedwoman of Caius; 12 feet in front.
EDCS-12401464.summary: An inscription likely marking property boundaries or a tomb, mentioning two freed individuals and a measurement.

EDCS-12401465.translation: Marcus Seppius Macero, son of Marcus, had this made for Vibbia, daughter of Lucius, his mother and heir.
EDCS-12401465.summary: A dedication or commission of a monument by Marcus Seppius Macero to his mother and heir, Vibbia.

EDCS-12401466.translation: To the departed spirits; Ligeria Semne made this for her well-deserving hospes, Marcus Septicius Felicissimus.
EDCS-12401466.summary: A funerary dedication by Ligeria Semne to her well-deserving hospes, Marcus Septicius Felicissimus.

EDCS-12401468.translation: To the Deified Spirits. Lucius Servilius, son of F., made this for himself while alive, and for Gaius Occlus Faustus, son of I.L., and for Vibius, son of Vibius; for Vibia Tritae, daughter of Agi, and for Vibius, son of Vibius; for Vibius and Vibia January, for Vibius Dio and Vibia Trophime, his own freedwoman, and for Felicitas, and for their descendants.
EDCS-12401468.summary: This funerary monument, dedicated to the Deified Spirits, was set up by Lucius Servilius for himself and his family, including freedpeople and descendants.

EDCS-12401469.translation: To the Deified Spirits. Sestidius Primus made this for his deserving wife, Sestidia Felicissima.
EDCS-12401469.summary: This is a funerary inscription to the Deified Spirits, with Sestidius Primus dedicating it to his well-deserving wife.

EDCS-12401470.translation: Sextius, aged 6 years, is buried here.
EDCS-12401470.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of a young boy named Sextius.

EDCS-12401472.translation: To Silenus, freedman of Seius, and to the freedmen of Silenus.
EDCS-12401472.summary: This inscription records the dedication to Silenus, a freedman, and other freedmen associated with him.

EDCS-12401473.translation: To the Deified Spirits. Gaius Umbrius Primus made this for his incomparable wife, Spedia Apula, who lived 32 years, 6 months, and 6 days, for his well-deserving wife with whom he lived 7 years, 7 months, and 7 days.
EDCS-12401473.summary: This funerary inscription to the Deified Spirits documents the life span of Spedia Apula and her marriage to Gaius Umbrius Primus.

EDCS-12401474.translation: For Spedia or Cerrinia Celerina, his well-deserving wife, with whom he lived 37 years, 8 months, without any complaint. Gaius Liconius Celer made this.
EDCS-12401474.summary: This funerary monument was dedicated by Gaius Liconius Celer to his wife, Spedia/Cerrinia Celerina, noting the duration of their virtuous marriage.

EDCS-12401475.translation: Staberius [made this for] Turturia Nices [in a plot of land] 5 feet in [depth] and [---] feet in front.
EDCS-12401475.summary: This is a boundary marker or plot inscription related to Staberius and Turturia Nices.

EDCS-12401476.translation: To Lucius Staia Herodotos, who lived 5 years, 8 months, and 15 days. Lucius Staia son of Lucius Rutilius, to his dearest brother.
EDCS-12401476.summary: This is a tombstone for a child named Lucius Staia Herodotos, erected by his brother.

EDCS-12401477.translation: Statia, daughter of Publius Posilla, by her will, for herself and for Gaius Fufius.
EDCS-12401477.summary: This inscription indicates a legacy or provision made in a will by Statia Posilla for herself and Gaius Fufius.

EDCS-12401479.translation: Strabo, freedman and heir, made this.
EDCS-12401479.summary: This inscription records that Strabo, a freedman who was also an heir, erected the monument.

EDCS-12401480.translation: Successus, son of the second V(?)
EDCS-12401480.summary: This inscription likely records a name and patronymic, possibly for a funerary monument.

EDCS-12401482.translation: Suellius, son of (?) and (?) who lived 59 days, the most unfortunate parents achieved (?)
EDCS-12401482.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child who died at 59 days old, set up by their parents.

EDCS-12401483.translation: Sextus Suellius Dama for himself and for Suellia Salvia and all their family, in front between the monument of Titus Rustius and Lucius Gellius, 12 feet in the land.
EDCS-12401483.summary: This is a funerary inscription specifying burial plot dimensions and location, set up by Sextus Suellius Dama for himself and his family.

EDCS-12401484.translation: For Suellia Sabina, freedwoman of a woman.
EDCS-12401484.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Suellia Sabina, a freedwoman.

EDCS-12401485.translation: Suessus made this for his incomparable and deserving father, Successus, a faithful man.
EDCS-12401485.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Suessus for his father, Successus.

EDCS-12401486.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph stating the burial of Lucius Tanonius Bassus.

EDCS-12401487.translation: Flavius Successus and Terentia Hermione, most sorrowful parents, made this for their sweetest daughter Tannonia Paterniana, who lived 12 years and 4 months.
EDCS-12401487.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl, Tannonia Paterniana, set up by her grieving parents.

EDCS-12401489.translation: Lucius Tautonius Ergasimus, freedman of Lucius Tautonius, for himself and for his wife Quintia Grata, freedwoman of a woman, who lived 17 years, and for Lucius Tautonius Proculus, son of Lucius Tautonius, of the tribe Stellatina, (with) 520 (?) [---] (?) [---] (?)
EDCS-12401489.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Tautonius Ergasimus, his wife, and his son, specifying their familial relationships and ages.

EDCS-12401490.translation: For Publius T. Calavius, his wife.
EDCS-12401490.summary: This is likely a funerary dedication for the wife of Publius T. Calavius.

EDCS-12401491.translation: Lucius Cornelius Firmus and Teiedia Fortunata, alive, made this for themselves (as a tomb) for the Divine Shades; they bought the place for 120 denarii.
EDCS-12401491.summary: This is a funerary commission inscription where Lucius Cornelius Firmus and Teiedia Fortunata purchased a burial plot for themselves.

EDCS-12401492.translation: To Terentia Valentina, my incomparable wife, Aulus Buttius Tertius [dedicated this to] his most deserving soul, with whom he lived for 25 years without a single complaint.
EDCS-12401492.summary: A funerary dedication by a husband to his deceased wife, dating potentially to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE based on the names and sentiment.
