EDCS-18200274.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vibbia Procula made this for her brother Aulus Vibbius Felix, who lived 20 years and 1 month, a well-deserving brother.
EDCS-18200274.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aulus Vibbius Felix, erected by his sister Vibbia Procula, indicating his age at death.

EDCS-19700420.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name, possibly a funerary or dedication marker.

EDCS-20100108.summary: A dedication, likely funerary, to a woman named Catilia Severa.

EDCS-20100110.translation: To Lucius Audio, son of Lucius, a quaestor; and to Lucius Graecius, son of Lucius, a quinquennial official.
EDCS-20100110.summary: A dedication honoring two officials, likely from the town council (IIIIvir and quinquennalis), possibly on a public building or monument.

EDCS-20401442.translation: The church of Beata rejoices in its bishop Andrew, with his clergy equally exulting. Timotheus with his own, who, aided by divine benefit, fulfilling his vow from the gifts of the holy people of God of the present court, completed the adornment with safer work.
EDCS-20401442.summary: This inscription celebrates a bishop named Andrew and his clergy, detailing a vow fulfilled through divine aid and the completion of some ornate work for the church.

EDCS-20401447.summary: This is a simple inscription of a person's name, possibly a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-20401452.translation: Publius Fufidius Bassus, son of Caius, of the Galeria tribe, appointed decurion of Compsa and Erdonia, and a IIIIvir of Erdonia, made this for himself and for his dearest freedwoman wife, Lollia Attica.
EDCS-20401452.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Fufidius Bassus, who held significant magistracies in Compsa and Erdonia, and for his wife Lollia Attica.

EDCS-20401453.translation: To Innocens, a woman, a tympanist, wife of the Best Mother of the Gods, Marcus, for his deserving wife, made this.
EDCS-20401453.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Innocens, wife of Marcus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23300139.translation: Aplati, freedman of Lucius Do Hy.
EDCS-23300139.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a freedman named Aplati, probably dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800757.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for a freedwoman named Brundisina, who belonged to Fabia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800758.translation: Here lies Stationa, the fuller, Euthycus.
EDCS-23800758.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Euthycus, a fuller and probably the owner of the establishment named Stationa, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800760.translation: Q(uintus) Marcius Rhodo[--] and Ocius Barnaeus [---] to the Augustales, the road and the sidewalks [---]
EDCS-23800760.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription concerning the construction or repair of a road and sidewalks, commissioned by Q. Marcius Rhodo and Ocius Barnaeus for the Augustales, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800762.translation: To Ingenuus, his son, aged 13 years [---]
EDCS-23800762.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for Ingenuus, the son of the dedicator, who died at the age of 13, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800765.translation: This place is the burial site of Amphelium, [son/slave] of Cornelia, freedwoman of Quintus, and of Nebullus.
EDCS-23800765.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial place of Amphelium, associated with Cornelia and Nebullus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800770.translation: To Mopsidus, Felix, companion of Caesar [---]
EDCS-23800770.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Mopsidus, a companion of Caesar named Felix, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800771.translation: To Lexis, his fellow-soldier of Felix, freedman of Tiberius Caesar [---]
EDCS-23800771.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Lexis, the fellow-soldier of Felix, a freedman of Tiberius Caesar, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800776.translation: Flavius Restitutus made this for his well-deserving wife, Cocidia Saturnina.
EDCS-23800776.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by Flavius Restitutus for his wife Cocidia Saturnina, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23800783.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely naming a person, possibly Publius Veidius Pollio.

EDCS-24700063.translation: Aulus Titinius, son of Aulus, dedicated a shrine to Diana as a vow. Aulus Titinius, son of Aulus, fulfilled a vow to Artemis.
EDCS-24700063.summary: This bilingual inscription records Aulus Titinius, son of Aulus, dedicating a shrine or fulfilling a vow to the goddess Diana/Artemis.

EDCS-24700064.translation: Titus Terentius, son of Titus, lived 84 years. Titus Terentius, son of Titus, Claudia's, was dictator of Fidenae four times. Publius Terentius, son of Titus, lived 21 years. Titus Terentius, son of Titus, lived 27 years.
EDCS-24700064.summary: This epitaph records the lifespans of several members of the Terentia family, including a four-time dictator of Fidenae.

EDCS-24700264.translation: Lucius Gellius, son of Lucius, praetor, by decree of the senate, 52.
EDCS-24700264.summary: This inscription likely records Lucius Gellius, son of Lucius, who held the office of praetor, with the number 52 possibly indicating a sum of money or a year.

EDCS-25601378.translation: My homeland is Myrrha; I am of Lycian stock. As a merchant, I came for my brother Zosimus, whom I dedicated to mortality for remembrance. Not so was Nireus raised in beautiful Syme, nor the children of Leda in the whirls of the Eurotas. Amethystos, brother of Zosimus, dedicated this.
EDCS-25601378.summary: This funerary epigram is by Amethystos, brother of Zosimus, commemorating his brother who was a merchant from Lycia.

EDCS-25601495.translation: Here lie in peace Letus and Benedictula Flora and Luminosus, who lived as long as God willed, deposited on the 13th day before the Kalends of December, in the 8th indiction, after the consulate of the most distinguished Basil, in the 18th year.
EDCS-25601495.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Letus, Benedictula Flora, and Luminosus, with a date given by indiction and consul, likely from the 6th century AD.

EDCS-25601496.translation: Here rests in the sleep of peace my sweet son Stefanus, who lived 17 years.
EDCS-25601496.summary: This epitaph records the death of Stefanus, son of the author, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-26200660.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription simply naming two individuals, possibly Trebius and Loisios.

EDCS-26500795.translation: To Minerva Victrix. Publius Titinius, son of Aulus, duovir, placed the statue.
EDCS-26500795.summary: This dedication records Publius Titinius, son of Aulus, in his role as duovir, placing a statue to the goddess Minerva Victrix.

EDCS-26500797.translation: I made this monument for myself, for my son, and for Vettia Agathe, my wife, and for my freedman Prarius. I give it to them thus: if any born of them are buried in this monument or sepulcher, they will not pass to the heir.
EDCS-26500797.summary: This funerary inscription records a man establishing a family tomb for himself, his wife, son, and freedman, with specific conditions for inheritance of burial rights.

EDCS-26500798.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph identifying the deceased, Ovis, and his father, Ovius.

EDCS-26500801.translation: Dasmus the dancer dedicated this to Hercules.
EDCS-26500801.summary: This is a dedication of an object or monument to the god Hercules by a freedman named Dasmus, who was a dancer.

EDCS-26500805.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name on a tombstone or property marker, identifying Numerius Statius.

EDCS-26500806.translation: Gaius Quinctius Valgus, son of Gaius, and Lucius Sepunius, son of Lucius, quinquennales, saw to the construction of the wall, gates, forum, portico, and curia, and the cistern, by the decision of the decurions, and they also approved them.
EDCS-26500806.summary: This inscription records the completion and approval of public works, including a wall, gates, forum, portico, curia, and cistern, by two quinquennales, Caius Quinctius Valgus and Lucius Sepunius, acting on the decision of the local town council (decurions).

EDCS-26700045.translation: Numerius Maccius, son of Lucius, quaestor.
EDCS-26700045.summary: This inscription records the name and office of Numerius Maccius, son of Lucius, who held the position of quaestor.

EDCS-26700047.translation: Publius Claudius, grandson of Publius, quaestor.
EDCS-26700047.summary: This inscription records the name and office of Publius Claudius, grandson of Publius, who held the position of quaestor.

EDCS-28500320.translation: Orontius, most distinguished man, corrector, made this.
EDCS-28500320.summary: This is a dedication by Orontius, identified as a 'vir clarissimus' (most distinguished man) and 'corrector', likely a high-ranking official, for a building or monument he commissioned.

EDCS-28801228.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of Afrius, son of Caius, followed by an abbreviation, possibly indicating a military rank or status.

EDCS-28801230.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription that appears to name Caius Albius multiple times, with other illegible or missing text.

EDCS-28801231.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that names Publius Albius, with the beginning of the inscription missing.

EDCS-28801233.translation: Ursula, mother of Ulpius Hister's slave Amoenae, and Amoenus, his brother, erected this for Pyladius, their fellow slave, who deserved well.
EDCS-28801233.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Ursula and Amoenus for their fellow slave Pyladius.

EDCS-28900461.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription possibly dedicating a monument to a patron named Gaius Asinius.

EDCS-28900462.translation: Thalamus erected this for her friend Firmilla, daughter of Augen[---], slave of [---], who deserved well.
EDCS-28900462.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Thalamus for her friend Firmilla.

EDCS-28900463.translation: Avittia Tertulla, freedwoman of Gaius, erected this for Norius Felix.
EDCS-28900463.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Avittia Tertulla for Norius Felix.

EDCS-28900464.translation: Aulus [---], members of the association [---] Stephan [---].
EDCS-28900464.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning members of an association and possibly a person named Stephan.

EDCS-28900465.translation: Brutius Cal[---] for his wife with whom he lived [---].
EDCS-28900465.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Brutius Cal[---] and his wife.

EDCS-28900469.translation: [---] Camillius [---], son of [---], of the tribe Horatia [---] Vitrias[ia ---].
EDCS-28900469.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a person from the Camillii family, possibly of the Horatia tribe.

EDCS-28900470.translation: [---] Gaius Capi[---] erected this for his wife [---].
EDCS-28900470.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Gaius Capi[---] setting up a monument for his wife.

EDCS-28900471.translation: Pomponia Heraclia erected this for her mother Carpinia Dorcas and her grandmother Carpinia Firmilla, while they were alive.
EDCS-28900471.summary: This is a monument set up by Pomponia Heraclia for her mother and grandmother during their lifetime.

EDCS-28900475.translation: [---]a Cerialis erected this for [---]atri, who deserved well.
EDCS-28900475.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by [---]a Cerialis for [---]atri.

EDCS-28900477.translation: Gaius Volteius made this for his most devoted friend Gaius Clatius Nymidianus.
EDCS-28900477.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Gaius Volteius for his friend Gaius Clatius Nymidianus.

EDCS-28900482.translation: Crispinus placed this for his well-deserving fellow slave Albana.
EDCS-28900482.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Crispinus for his fellow slave Albana.

EDCS-28900483.translation: For Crittius Heleno, and for Ampliatae [daughter?] of [---]briae Probatae, aged 17.
EDCS-28900483.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Crittius Heleno and Ampliata, who was 17 years old.

EDCS-28900486.translation: Publius Antonius Acho, freedman of Egnatia, arranged for this to be made for Lucius Epidius Primus.
EDCS-28900486.summary: This funerary monument was arranged by Publius Antonius Acho, freedman of Egnatia, for Lucius Epidius Primus.

EDCS-28900493.summary: This is a funerary dedication, possibly by or for a slave named Felicio Servus.

EDCS-28900495.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Firminus, son of Publius.

EDCS-28900496.translation: Firmus placed this for his well-deserving fellow slave.
EDCS-28900496.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Firmus for his well-deserving fellow slave.

EDCS-28900501.translation: Lucius Fulcinius Helenus made this for himself.
EDCS-28900501.summary: This is a funerary monument made by Lucius Fulcinius Helenus for himself.

EDCS-28900504.translation: Quintus Herennius, son of Quintus, [belonging to] the Horatia tribe.
EDCS-28900504.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying Quintus Herennius, son of Quintus, and his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-28900506.translation: His heirs arranged for this to be made for their freedman Hilarus, according to his will.
EDCS-28900506.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by the heirs of Hilarus, as stipulated in his will.

EDCS-28900507.translation: Probus and Secunda, father and mother, set this up for Ianuaria.
EDCS-28900507.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by parents for their daughter Ianuaria.

EDCS-28900509.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the names of Iunius Si... and Gaius Iunius.

EDCS-28900512.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucilia for her son.

EDCS-29100407.translation: To the physician, Antiochus the father. While alive: 'A pleasant light; as one alive, enjoy good fortune.'
EDCS-29100407.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Antiochus the father for a physician, including a Greek epitaph.

EDCS-29100410.translation: To Neria, daughter of Publius. Bassae, for Publius Nerius, son of Publius.
EDCS-29100410.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary monument mentioning Neria and Publius Nerius.

EDCS-29100414.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record parts of names, possibly Lucius Apro...

EDCS-29100418.translation: Lucius Marcius Theo... arranged for this to be made for Quintus Ovius ... and Ovia, freedwoman of Quintus. Cimb[er]...
EDCS-29100418.summary: This is a funerary monument arranged by Lucius Marcius Theo... for Quintus Ovius and Ovia, a freedwoman.

EDCS-29100423.translation: ... Pissenius ...; Publius Muttienus Pisticus for [his father?] and for his daughter V...
EDCS-29100423.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary monument set up by Publius Muttienus Pisticus for family members.

EDCS-29100424.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that includes the statement 'He lives' and names Plaestinus and Horatia.

EDCS-29100425.translation: Marcus Pom[p...] son of Marcus, of the Horatia tribe. ...atr...
EDCS-29100425.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that records parts of a name and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-29100426.translation: To Pompeia Faceta. From Mestes her brother and Ionicus her son.
EDCS-29100426.summary: This is a funerary dedication from a brother and son to Pompeia Faceta.

EDCS-29100428.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely begins a funerary dedication to Primigenia and mentions a Primigenius.

EDCS-29100429.translation: To Probus, dearest son, who lived...
EDCS-29100429.summary: This is the beginning of a funerary dedication for a son named Probus.

EDCS-29200333.translation: To the father Hermes and mother Euticia, to their son Maximus, who lived 14 years, for his well-deserved merit, they placed [this monument].
EDCS-29200333.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents Hermes and Euticia for their son Maximus, who died at age 14.

EDCS-29200335.translation: [...]a Pyrallis, for her well-deserved merit, and for herself, placed [this monument].
EDCS-29200335.summary: This is a funerary dedication placed by Pyrallis for her deceased spouse and for herself.

EDCS-29200337.translation: To Rudiae Severa. From Rudia Amoena, for her dearest daughter and for herself, placed [this monument].
EDCS-29200337.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Rudia Amoena for her daughter Rudiae Severa and herself.

EDCS-29200338.translation: To Salvius, of the two Cornelii [...]ani and Proculi. Drussia Restituta made [this] for her well-deserved uncle.
EDCS-29200338.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Drussia Restituta for her uncle Salvius, who was connected to two men named Cornelius.

EDCS-29200340.translation: [...] of Seppia Rufina. A slave for [his?] brother and for himself placed [this monument?].
EDCS-29200340.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription likely by a slave for his brother and himself.

EDCS-29200341.translation: The burial place of the freedman Philadelphus, freedman of Publius Servius Stator. [This plot is for?] the freedwoman Crysarion, freedwoman of Publius Servius. [His?] heir, Publius Servius Philargurus.
EDCS-29200341.summary: This inscription delineates a burial plot belonging to freedmen and their heirs, associated with Publius Servius Stator and his freedmen.

EDCS-29200344.translation: [...] of Caesar. Sophe and Dissus placed [this?].
EDCS-29200344.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly indicating ownership or dedication by Sophe and Dissus, possibly related to someone named Caesar.

EDCS-29200346.translation: To Tertullus (?) and Cresta, Tertulla, mother, made [this] for her children and sister [in her] unlucky [life], [at the age of] 20 years.
EDCS-29200346.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Tertulla for her children and sister, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE based on typical epigraphic conventions.

EDCS-29200349.translation: Lucius Trebius, freedman of a woman, and Diodorus, freedman of a woman, [erected this] for Faustus, fellow freedman of a woman.
EDCS-29200349.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Trebius and Diodorus, recognizing their relationship as freedmen, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200351.translation: Ulpius Ianuarius set [this] up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-29200351.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Ulpius Ianuarius for his wife, typical of private funerary monuments from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200353.translation: [This belongs to] Iucunda, [and to] Apro, son of Gaius Vibius Septimus, [and to] of Vibiae Ampliata and Vibiae Peloris.
EDCS-29200353.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragmented funerary monument or boundary marker, indicating ownership or dedication to several individuals, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200354.translation: Regilla, mother, [made this for] Vibuleia Quinta, freedwoman of Marcus.
EDCS-29200354.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Regilla for her freedwoman, Vibuleia Quinta, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200356.translation: Gaius Volteius Maximus, son of Gaius, set [this] up for his father Gaius Volteius Attalus and his mother Oseia Rufa, daughter of Marcus.
EDCS-29200356.summary: This is a family funerary monument erected by Gaius Volteius Maximus for his parents, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200372.summary: This is a fragmented inscription indicating that someone named [-]nius set up a monument, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29400001.translation: For Gaius Albanus Secundus (?), freedman of Gaius.
EDCS-29400001.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, for Gaius Albanus Secundus (?), a freedman, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29900360.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or refers to an official act during the reign of Emperor Augustus, dating to the late 1st century BCE or early 1st century CE.

EDCS-31300237.translation: Marcus Rutilius Lupus set [this] up for his excellent friend Gaius Julius Cypaerus, honored with a bisellium and a Claudian Augustalis.
EDCS-31300237.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Marcus Rutilius Lupus for his esteemed friend Gaius Julius Cypaerus, a distinguished freedman of senatorial rank, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-32900023.summary: This inscription records the name of Caius Iunius Draconis, likely a funerary or ownership marker.

EDCS-33500421.translation: To the holy god, on the 14th day before the Kalends of June. From Saint John to Christ, his servants. Stefanus the bishop.
EDCS-33500421.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a holy god by Stefanus, a bishop, likely made around the 14th day before the Kalends of June.

EDCS-33500425.translation: To Fortuna, for himself and for [---]nius Inventus, patron.
EDCS-33500425.summary: This fragmented inscription records a dedication to the goddess Fortuna, intended for the dedicator and his patron, [---]nius Inventus.

EDCS-33500427.translation: Publius Satrius, son of Publius, freedman, and Apollonida, made this for himself and his family while alive. Satria Erotis, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-33500427.summary: This funerary inscription records Publius Satrius and his wife Satria Erotis, who made the monument for themselves and their family.

EDCS-33500430.translation: Acte, to good people. In the consulship of Flavius Constantius and Valerius Maximus, on the 9th day before the Kalends of June, in Luceria in the curia. Aurelius Iulianus and Iunius Longinus, the two quinquennial magistrates, with the consent of the whole order, made these pronouncements: 'It is best to always seek the patronage of distinguished and excellent men. Therefore, it is decreed that patronage be offered to so distinguished a man, of consular rank, because of his love for the order and the citizens. It is resolved by the two magistrates that a table of patronage, engraved on bronze, be presented, so that he may favor us and our fatherland in all things, as a patron.'
EDCS-33500430.summary: This inscription from Luceria records a decree by the local magistrates and town council to bestow honorary patronage upon a distinguished man, likely in recognition of his support for the city, during the consulship of Flavius Constantius and Valerius Maximus.

EDCS-34200011.translation: Of Iucundus, son of Nearchus, slave, aged 17.
EDCS-34200011.summary: This inscription records the name and age of a deceased slave named Iucundus, son of Nearchus.

EDCS-34200100.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Camisca lived 45 years. She is buried here. Her daughter placed this for her deserving mother with goodwill.
EDCS-34200100.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Camisca at age 45 and was placed by her loving daughter.

EDCS-34200105.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Fabia, wife of M. Lufus, lived 20 years.
EDCS-34200105.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Fabia, wife of M. Lufus, at the age of 20.

EDCS-34200106.translation: And Fadia, mother, is buried here.
EDCS-34200106.summary: This fragmentary inscription indicates that a woman named Fadia, identified as a mother, is buried at this location.

EDCS-34200112.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Laenius Cerialis lived 25 years. He is buried here.
EDCS-34200112.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Laenius Cerialis at the age of 25.

EDCS-34200116.summary: This inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, records the name Marsicia, identified as a mother.

EDCS-34200119.translation: Of Decimus Patronius Philargyrus, son of Decimus Patronius.
EDCS-34200119.summary: This inscription records ownership or dedication by Decimus Patronius Philargyrus, son of Decimus Patronius.

EDCS-34200135.translation: [...] to Publius Epidius, son of Publius, M[...].
EDCS-34200135.summary: This inscription is a dedication, honoring Publius Epidius, son of Publius, with the initial part of the recipient's name or cognomen missing.

EDCS-34200145.summary: This is a personal greeting or epitaph for Caius Mutius Fauster.

EDCS-34200154.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Cornelius... lived 50 years. His wife made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-34200154.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Cornelius, recording his lifespan and the maker of the monument, his wife.

EDCS-34200160.translation: Quintus Tutisulenus Digitulus, son of Caius, from the Emilia tribe, according to his will.
EDCS-34200160.summary: This inscription records Quintus Tutisulenus Digitulus, son of Caius (tribe Emilia), as being commemorated or fulfilling a bequest as per his testament.

EDCS-34200163.translation: ... and daughter restored the name, and Numerius Afinius Saturninus, the husband [...]
EDCS-34200163.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary monument where a daughter and her husband, Numerius Afinius Saturninus, restored or established the names of the deceased.

EDCS-34200164.translation: To Antonius Eron, freedman of Caius, and Antonia Nobilina, freedwoman of Caius, and Antonia Secunda, freedwoman of Caius. F [perhaps indicating 'fecit' - made].
EDCS-34200164.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating the monument to three individuals: Antonius Eron, Antonia Nobilina, and Antonia Secunda, all freedpersons of a certain Caius.

EDCS-34200165.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Ito Severus, husband, Fortunata Artemiodora made this for her well-deserving husband, with whom she lived 47 years without any complaint.
EDCS-34200165.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Fortunata Artemiodora for her husband Marcus Aurelius Ito Severus, emphasizing their long and harmonious marriage.

EDCS-34200166.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Cerrinius Sostratus [made this] for his wife, Cerrinia Chysidis.
EDCS-34200166.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Publius Cerrinius Sostratus commemorates his wife, Cerrinia Chrysidis.

EDCS-34200167.translation: To Memoria, my most virtuous wife, with whom I lived for 20 years without complaint. Quintus Servilius Primus [erected this].
EDCS-34200167.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Quintus Servilius Primus to his wife Memoria, who died after 20 years of marriage.

EDCS-34200168.translation: To Veiedius Redemptus, a boy of rare simplicity and integrity, to whom such a great dignity had come through kinship, but fate took him away. He lived 17 years and 15 days. Cornelius Firmus and Veiedia Fortunata [erected this] to their sweetest son.
EDCS-34200168.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Cornelius Firmus and Veiedia Fortunata to their son Veiedius Redemptus, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-34300171.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Fulvius Zosimus.

EDCS-34300175.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Alaucus.

EDCS-34300204.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lusi Mediq, possibly from Calama.

EDCS-34300217.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Eude, son of Marcus Octavius.

EDCS-34300245.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Cornelius Samo.

EDCS-34300246.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Cornelius Firmus.

EDCS-34300255.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Titus, identified as a potter.

EDCS-34300256.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aulus Vibius Arteius.

EDCS-35000006.summary: This inscription, likely from a boundary marker, records a measurement related to Nero.

EDCS-35000011.translation: Sextus Torius Epaphra, aged 40 years, is buried here.
EDCS-35000011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Torius Epaphra, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-35000016.translation: Gaius Gen, Lucius Sec, lived 50 years, here.
EDCS-35000016.summary: This fragmented epitaph records the name and lifespan of at least two individuals.

EDCS-35000037.translation: To Camuria Panu, his deserving wife. To the Spirits of the Dead.
EDCS-35000037.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by an unknown person to their deserving wife, Camuria Panu.

EDCS-35000043.translation: his deserving wife is buried here.
EDCS-35000043.summary: This fragmented epitaph records that an unknown woman, described as 'his deserving wife,' was buried here.

EDCS-35200316.translation: Ofillia Quintilla, hail! And you who read, hail! If the untimely laws of fate had not been, a mother ought to have been read before on this title.
EDCS-35200316.summary: This poignant epitaph laments that Ofillia Quintilla's mother should have been honored on the monument before her daughter, suggesting the mother died first or the daughter's death was unexpectedly early.

EDCS-35200318.translation: Silvanus Veticator, son of Claudius Dicaeus, freedman, lived 30 years, is buried here. I implore you, stone, may you rest lightly upon his bones, if you do not wish to be a burden already on his tender age.
EDCS-35200318.summary: This epitaph is for Silvanus Veticator, a freedman, who died at 30, and includes a plea to the stone to rest lightly on his remains.

EDCS-36400254.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying Tarula as a freedwoman of Sulla.

EDCS-37200050.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Callia Orata, likely a funerary dedication.

EDCS-37200053.summary: This fragmented inscription indicates that a wife made the monument for her son.

EDCS-37200055.translation: To Gnaeus Suellius, Suellia Festa to her well-deserving father; Suellia Primigenia to her wife.
EDCS-37200055.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by daughters for their father and by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-37200056.translation: The slave of Flavius lived [X] years; he is buried here.
EDCS-37200056.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a slave named Flavius, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-37200065.translation: To Lucius Albius Rufus; Licinia, daughter of Lucius, [---], his wife, made this.
EDCS-37200065.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Albius Rufus, made by his wife.

EDCS-37200066.translation: Lucius Trasuleius Figulus, son of Bl, of the Menenia tribe, made this tomb while alive. Naevia, daughter of Cnaeus, his wife, is buried here.
EDCS-37200066.summary: This is an epitaph for Lucius Trasuleius Figulus, who built his own tomb, and his wife Naevia.

EDCS-37200074.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Foebus and Eutychia, most excellent parents, for their dearest daughter Foebadia, who lived 5 years, 6 months, and 13 days, they made this for her as she deserved well.
EDCS-37200074.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents to their young daughter, specifying her age at death.

EDCS-37200075.translation: To our lord Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius, 'Divine', Happy, Unconquered Augustus, Consul, by public decree of the decurions.
EDCS-37200075.summary: This inscription records a public dedication to Emperor Constantine the Great, likely a statue or monument.

EDCS-37200077.translation: Here rests in the sleep of peace Lucerna, who lived 26 years; deposited.
EDCS-37200077.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucerna, recording her age at death and burial.

EDCS-37200078.translation: Here rests in the sleep of peace Crescentia, an honorable woman, who lived approximately 40 years; her burial was on the day of the Kalends of May.
EDCS-37200078.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Crescentia, recording her approximate age at death and date of burial.

EDCS-38000165.summary: This is a simple Greek funerary epitaph for Nomereius.

EDCS-38000168.summary: This is a simple Greek funerary epitaph for Hesperatos.

EDCS-38000173.translation: Here lie the children, Sarra, daughter of Bitus, and Faustinus, son of Vincomalus. May peace be upon their resting place.
EDCS-38000173.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for two children, Sarra and Faustinus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-38000175.translation: Here lies Marcellus, father of fathers and patron of the city.
EDCS-38000175.summary: This inscription honors Marcellus, described as a "father of fathers" and patron of the city, likely a prominent citizen from the Roman period.

EDCS-38000176.translation: Here lies Auxaneus, father and patron of the city.
EDCS-38000176.summary: This inscription honors Auxaneus, described as a "father" and patron of the city, likely a prominent citizen from the Roman period.

EDCS-38000177.translation: Here lies Fausteina, mother, wife of Auxanius, father and patron of the city.
EDCS-38000177.summary: This inscription honors Fausteina, wife of Auxanius, who is also described as a "father" and patron of the city, likely a prominent Roman family.

EDCS-38000183.translation: Here rests Sabatai who lived 16 years.
EDCS-38000183.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a boy named Sabatai who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-39100100.translation: To Helios Mithras, for the salvation of Brittius Praesentis. Sagaris, steward.
EDCS-39100100.summary: This is a religious dedication to the gods Helios Mithras by Sagaris, a steward, for the welfare of Brittius Praesentis.

EDCS-40700006.translation: Publius Plotus Quintus and Herennius, as duovirs, as praetors, as curators of the aqueducts.
EDCS-40700006.summary: This inscription records the names and official capacities of Publius Plotus Quintus and Herennius, who served as duovirs, praetors, and curators of the aqueducts.

EDCS-41700247.translation: Titus Allius Felix, as a member of the board of four, made this little shrine in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-41700247.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Allius Felix, a member of a local college of four officials, dedicating a small shrine.

EDCS-43400323.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary epitaph for a woman named Valeria, freedwoman of Sextus.

EDCS-44100275.translation: Victoria the faithful departed on the 8th day before the Kalends of May.
EDCS-44100275.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death date of Victoria, described as faithful.

EDCS-44100276.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely continues the names of individuals, possibly in a funerary context.

EDCS-44100277.translation: Pilipus, craftsman of Cepalon, from Alexandria.
EDCS-44100277.summary: This inscription identifies Pilipus, a craftsman from Alexandria, mentioning his origin and profession.

EDCS-45600041.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Pinarius Felix made this for Rabilia Restuta, to whom he lived for 38 years, 7 months, and 5 days, a deserving woman.
EDCS-45600041.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Lucius Pinarius Felix to his deceased partner, Rabilia Restuta, specifying their years together.

EDCS-45600042.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly part of a larger text, mentioning a person named Gaius.

EDCS-46300029.translation: To the Divine Shades. Because of his most sacred judgment and his distinguished merits as count of the factories of the whole city of Beneventum, the Leontiani, as patrons of the city, decided that a statue should be erected to Pullio Argolicus, named Rum or Lim, and to the count of the factories of the whole city of Beneventum, their patron, during the reign of our lords Constantius Augustus for the 6th time and Constantius Caesar for the 2nd time.
EDCS-46300029.summary: This inscription records the erection of a statue to Pullio Argolicus and a count of factories, honoring their service to the city of Beneventum under Emperors Constantius and Constantius Caesar.

EDCS-46400803.translation: To Pullo, augur, military tribune, a man of the Mali, son of Gaius Firmilius Rufus, of the Pupinia tribe; and to Lucius Terentius Rufinus, son of Lucius, of the Stellatina tribe.
EDCS-46400803.summary: This inscription records two individuals, Pullo and Lucius Terentius Rufinus, likely as recipients of honors or dedications, mentioning their tribal affiliations and military roles.

EDCS-46601026.translation: Marcus Massius Diapyrus lived for 40 years. Domitia Erotis and her sons made this for her deserving companion.
EDCS-46601026.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Domitia Erotis and her sons to Marcus Massius Diapyrus, their deserving companion, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-48700071.summary: This is a simple dedication made by Gaius Volusius Gallus.

EDCS-48700074.translation: Under the consulship of Emperor Domitian Caesar Augustus Germanicus and Lucius Minucius Rufus, Crispinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-48700074.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Crispinus during the consulship of Domitian and Lucius Minucius Rufus, likely in the late 1st century CE.

EDCS-48700076.translation: Under the consulship of Minucius Rufus, the quinquennial festival of the Varianum.
EDCS-48700076.summary: This inscription refers to the quinquennial festival of the Varianum during the consulship of Minucius Rufus, dating to around 100 CE.

EDCS-48700077.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Quintus Adius commissioned this monument.

EDCS-48700689.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Priscianus [dedicated this] for his slave Eutuchetes.
EDCS-48700689.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Shades made by Iulius Priscianus for his slave Eutuchetes.

EDCS-48700690.translation: Protogene, Hierophilus, freedman of Florus, is buried here.
EDCS-48700690.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Protogene, freedman of Florus, is buried here.

EDCS-48700748.translation: This monument will not pass to an heir or owner; Lucius Conicius Martialis [dedicated this] for Flavia Musa, his mother.
EDCS-48700748.summary: This inscription records a monument that is not to be inherited, dedicated by Lucius Conicius Martialis for his mother Flavia Musa.

EDCS-48700750.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a dedication or ownership statement, referring to Aulus Manneius Cro.

EDCS-48700760.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a dedication or ownership statement, referring to Lucius Rasius Pisanus.

EDCS-48700763.translation: Of Rectus, son of Caius Volusenus.
EDCS-48700763.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a dedication or ownership statement, referring to Rectus, son of Caius Volusenus.

EDCS-48700776.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a dedication or ownership statement, referring to Aulus Vibius Scrofula.

EDCS-51200113.translation: From the testament of Veturia Telete, freedwoman of Sextus, and Veturia Tertia, freedwoman of the woman, by the arbitration of Tertia.
EDCS-51200113.summary: This inscription records the execution of a will, likely concerning property distribution.

EDCS-51800164.translation: Quintus Fabius Tertius, freedman of Quintus, and Fabricia Mamertina, freedwoman of Quintus, and Nice Peregrin, are buried here.
EDCS-51800164.summary: This is a tombstone inscription identifying the individuals buried and their relationships.

EDCS-51800165.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Maximus, freedman, made this for his well-deserving wife, Pacuvia Felicitas, who lived with me for 25 years and 6 months.
EDCS-51800165.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedman for his wife, specifying their years of marriage.

EDCS-52600142.translation: [?] to Oppia, daughter of Marcus. [?] Quintus Octavius Rufus, son of Quintus. [?] Herennia, daughter of Marcus, from her mother Herennia [?].
EDCS-52600142.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a funerary dedication for Oppia and possibly Herennia, mentioning a father and mother.

EDCS-52600143.translation: Marcus Murrius [?] to Greceia, daughter of Aulus [?], for Marcus Calvius [?].
EDCS-52600143.summary: A fragmented inscription that may record a dedication or a legal transaction involving several individuals.

EDCS-52600176.summary: This is a short inscription identifying a freedwoman and her former master.

EDCS-53500016.translation: Decumanus, kardo XI, decuratus, Marcus Fulvius Flaccus, son of Marcus, and Caius Sempronius, son of Tiberius, were the triumviri for judging and assigning lands.
EDCS-53500016.summary: This inscription identifies members of the III viri agris iudicandis adsignandis commission, likely responsible for land distribution.

EDCS-53500018.translation: Caius Baebius Lupulus and his son Caius Baebius Lupulus gladly fulfilled a vow to the god Silvanus, as was deserved.
EDCS-53500018.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to the god Silvanus by two individuals with the same name.

EDCS-53500019.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Magullia Romana, dearest companion, with whom I lived for 13 years. The collegium Mercuriales dedicated this work.
EDCS-53500019.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Magullia Romana by her companion, noting the duration of their relationship and mentioning the collegium Mercuriales.

EDCS-53800055.summary: This is a simple inscription bearing a single name, likely identifying an individual or a tomb.

EDCS-53800081.summary: This inscription likely records the name of a freedman, Eutychianus, possibly as a dedicant or the subject of the inscription, with a partial name and title.

EDCS-55702372.translation: Sabinus, a relative by marriage, erected this willingly for a well-deserving person.
EDCS-55702372.summary: This inscription is a dedication erected by Sabinus for a well-deserving individual, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-55702373.translation: For Quintus Stadius Bassus, as a IIvir (two-man official) of Saepinum, and for his wife Tantae Secundae, Statius Lepidus, his freedman, made this according to the will, with an additional 446 sesterces from his own funds.
EDCS-55702373.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by a freedman for his patrons, Quintus Stadius Bassus and his wife, with specific financial details including a bequest and additional personal expenditure.

EDCS-55702376.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly a dedication, mentioning parts of names and an unknown quantity or object, likely from an early imperial period.

EDCS-55702396.summary: This inscription likely names Publius Veidius Pollio, possibly indicating ownership or dedication of a monument.

EDCS-55702399.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Valeria Ursula placed this for her husband Valerius Secundus, who deserved well, and for herself.
EDCS-55702399.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Valeria Ursula for her deserving husband, Valerius Secundus, and also for herself.

EDCS-56000340.translation: Numerius Tutorius, son of Blattus, of the tribe Oufentina. Tutoria, daughter of Numerius.
EDCS-56000340.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary epitaph or a record of family members, naming Numerius Tutorius and Tutoria, with tribal affiliation provided.

EDCS-56400009.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Calavia Loccias made this for her dearest son, Calavius Buttianus Callitanus, who lived 28 years and 6 months, a most unfortunate but well-deserving person.
EDCS-56400009.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Calavia Loccias to her beloved son, Calavius Buttianus Callitanus, detailing his age and expressing grief and affection.

EDCS-56500223.translation: The Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the divine Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 13th time, Imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 5th time, father of the country, built the road and bridges from Beneventum to Brundisium with his own money.
EDCS-56500223.summary: This is a monumental inscription detailing Emperor Trajan's construction of a major road and bridges, funded personally, with a full list of his titles and honors, dating to his 13th tribunician power, 6th acclamation as Imperator, and 5th consulship.

EDCS-57700005.translation: To a good memory. Here lies in peace the lady Epiphania. She was buried on the 10th day before the Kalends of February, on the fourth day of the week, after the consulship of Basilius, having lived more or less 32 years.
EDCS-57700005.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Epiphania, including her burial date, day of the week, the name of a consul, and her approximate age at death.

EDCS-57700007.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Octavius Iustianus, son, [erected this] for Cerrinia Voluptas.
EDCS-57700007.summary: A funerary dedication by a son to his deceased mother, Cerrinia Voluptas.

EDCS-57700008.translation: For Publius Lucius Marion, freedman of Publius, and Lycinini, freedwoman of Papia, daughter of Marcus. Tania Philocharis, his wife, and her heirs according to the will, [erected this].
EDCS-57700008.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife and heirs for her deceased husband, Publius Lucius Marion, and his co-freedwoman Lycinini.

EDCS-57800022.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Marcus Coaius Faustinus, father, made this for his most beloved daughter, C. B. Coruna, who deserved well.
EDCS-57800022.summary: A funerary epitaph by a father for his deceased daughter.

EDCS-57800023.translation: Augustalis, freedman of Publius, [erected this] for himself and for Publius Galeria Rufinus, his son, who lived 25 years, and for [---], a man who held the office of iure dicundo. [---]
EDCS-57800023.summary: An epitaph for Augustalis, a freedman, and his son Publius Galeria Rufinus, also mentioning an official, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-57800024.translation: Publius Coianius Modestus, for his father Publius Coianius Modestus, who held the office of iure dicundo and the quinquennial office, who deserved well, made this. [Erected] to the spirits of the underworld for Publius Coianius Modestus, son of Publius, Galeria Modestus, who held the office of iure dicundo and the quinquennial office.
EDCS-57800024.summary: A funerary monument erected by a son, Publius Coianius Modestus, for his father of the same name, who was a magistrate.

EDCS-57800025.translation: Vibidia Sexta, freedwoman of Fortunatus, dedicated [this] to Venus. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-57800025.summary: A dedication to Venus by a freedwoman, Vibidia Sexta, with the land granted by local officials.

EDCS-57800026.translation: Plautia Methe made [this] for her husband Felicioni, slave of Plautius, and for her husband [---], slave of Plautius Methe.
EDCS-57800026.summary: A funerary dedication by Plautia Methe for her husband Felicioni and possibly another husband.

EDCS-57800027.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. The son [erected this] for his foster-child Tharilidius Futacius, who deserved well, for himself and his family.
EDCS-57800027.summary: A funerary inscription by a son for his beloved foster-child, Tharilidius Futacius.

EDCS-57800029.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Sabenia Proba, who lived 20 years and 10 months, made this for her husband Ceius Rufilianus, who lived 43 years, who deserved well.
EDCS-57800029.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deceased husband.

EDCS-57800030.translation: To the spirits of the underworld of the people of Aeclanum. [Erected] for Caius Caesar, grandson of the deified [Caesar].
EDCS-57800030.summary: A dedication to the spirits of the underworld for Caius Caesar, possibly a local official or member of a prominent family, connected to the deified Julius Caesar.

EDCS-57800031.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Gnaeus Flavius Secundianus made this at his own expense and gave it as a gift to himself and his family, with the gods watching.
EDCS-57800031.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Gnaeus Flavius Secundianus, likely an ex-voto offering at his own expense.

EDCS-57800032.translation: To Jupiter, Pius, the college of four officials, at their own expense, with the consuls Plancus and Silanus.
EDCS-57800032.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by a college of four officials, dated by the consuls Plancus and Silanus.

EDCS-57800034.translation: Hagina, your mother wishes that no child of yours will be born like you, a pious man; it is not possible for a like man to be born, it is not possible for a like man to be born.
EDCS-57800034.summary: This is a personal inscription, possibly a tombstone or a message related to a child, expressing a strong sentiment about the character of the deceased or a living person.

EDCS-57800041.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription identifying the deceased as Lucius Titius Caupo.

EDCS-57800044.translation: Son of Marcus, from the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-57800044.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription identifying an individual by his father's name and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-57800045.translation: To the Divine Shades, Dio Trebatius, freedman of Cnaeus Publius.
EDCS-57800045.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the Manes (spirits of the dead) for Dio Trebatius, a freedman.

EDCS-57800046.translation: To the Divine Shades, Popilia Bassa made this for her cherished nurse, Popilia Felicla, who deserved well.
EDCS-57800046.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Popilia Bassa for her beloved nurse, Popilia Felicla.

EDCS-57800047.translation: Herennia Quinti filia, mother, for herself and for her husband Caius Pomponius Cai filius, from the Galeria tribe, and her son Caius Pomponius Cai filius, from the Galeria tribe, for Lucius Pomponius Cai filius, from the Galeria tribe, who held the office of duovir iure dicundo.
EDCS-57800047.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a mother for her deceased husband, son, and possibly another relative (Lucius Pomponius), indicating family relationships and one's civic role.

EDCS-57800049.translation: Gaius Fannius, freedman of Gaius, a Roman citizen.
EDCS-57800049.summary: This inscription identifies Gaius Fannius as a freedman of a Gaius and a Roman citizen.

EDCS-57800050.translation: Son of Numerius, Herennius, a college of four officials.
EDCS-57800050.summary: This inscription identifies Herennius by his father's name and his membership in a college of four officials.

EDCS-57800054.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Vettiae Marcellinae, his deserving wife, made this for Gaius Vettius Indymion.
EDCS-57800054.summary: A funerary dedication by Vettiae Marcellinae for her husband, Gaius Vettius Indymion.

EDCS-57800056.translation: Avillia Vera bought this monument for her husband Publius, her son Quintus Furvius, and Liciscus, who lived well.
EDCS-57800056.summary: A funerary monument purchased by Avillia Vera for her family members.

EDCS-59900095.translation: Here rests in peace Benenatus, an honorable man, who lived approximately.
EDCS-59900095.summary: A tombstone inscription for Benenatus, noting his honorable life.

EDCS-59900096.translation: Here rests in peace Georgius, a little one.
EDCS-59900096.summary: A tombstone inscription for a child named Georgius.

EDCS-59900111.summary: A short inscription wishing life to Amaliginusi.

EDCS-59900119.translation: Berecunde, servant, a repository, may you be in peace.
EDCS-59900119.summary: An inscription possibly related to a burial place or memorial for a servant named Berecunde.

EDCS-59900120.translation: Here lies Zosimus, born blind, who danced perhaps seventy-two years. May his spirit be with Jupiter.
EDCS-59900120.summary: An epitaph for Zosimus, noting his blindness and dancing, with a wish for his afterlife.

EDCS-59900126.translation: Sinatri, may you live in God.
EDCS-59900126.summary: A Christian inscription wishing life in God to Sinatri.

EDCS-59900133.translation: The sorrowing sons Euthichyus and Balens placed the bones of Eugenia Kestia here.
EDCS-59900133.summary: A funerary inscription recording the placement of remains by sons for their mother, Eugenia Kestia.

EDCS-60600067.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus, freedman of Fabius, made this while alive. He lived 10 years.
EDCS-60600067.summary: A funerary dedication by Marcus, a freedman, for himself, noting his lifespan.

EDCS-60600068.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Lucius Clodius Festus lived for 31 years.
EDCS-60600068.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of the deceased, Lucius Clodius Festus.

EDCS-60700016.translation: Insteia Iucunda, freedwoman of Insteia, and Insteia Eleusina, freedwoman of Insteia, [gave this] to their patron Lucius Pedius Theophilus, freedman of Lucius, and to Lucius Chresimus, freedman of Lucius, and to their patroness Insteia Hilara, freedwoman of Caius. Primogenes and Felix gave [this].
EDCS-60700016.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by freedmen and their families to their patrons, indicating a complex web of patronage relationships.

EDCS-60700019.translation: Viator made [this] for his excellent parents, Viatoris and Privata, and for himself and his descendants while alive.
EDCS-60700019.summary: A funerary monument set up by Viator for his parents and himself, indicating his intentions for longevity and posterity.

EDCS-60700026.translation: Synhetus, freedman of [---], who first [---] the day [---]
EDCS-60700026.summary: A fragmentary inscription that appears to be a funerary epitaph, possibly related to a freedman and the marking of a day.

EDCS-60700028.translation: To our lord Iovianus Augustus, Pious, Happy, Unconquered, good for the state, born for the republic.
EDCS-60700028.summary: A laudatory inscription for Emperor Jovian, praising his virtues and suitability for rule.

EDCS-60700030.translation: [---]ssius, son of Lucius Niger; [---]ssius, son of Lucius Rufus, made [this].
EDCS-60700030.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording the names of two individuals, possibly brothers, who commissioned a work.

EDCS-60700034.translation: Aftona, who lived 10 years and 5 days, buried on the 10th day before the Kalends of August.
EDCS-60700034.summary: A funerary epitaph stating the name, age, and burial date of Aftona.

EDCS-61900012.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Petronia Modesta, his wife. Marcus Misennius Primus.
EDCS-61900012.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Marcus Misennius Primus to his deceased wife, Petronia Modesta.

EDCS-62800028.translation: Decumanus (vertical axis) and Kardo (horizontal axis). Marcus Fulvius Flaccus, son of Marcus, and Caius Sempronius Gracchus, son of Tiberius, are the IIIviri for judging and assigning lands.
EDCS-62800028.summary: An official inscription naming two magistrates, Marcus Fulvius Flaccus and Caius Sempronius Gracchus, who served as the IIIviri agris iudicandis adsignandis, likely related to land distribution.

EDCS-62900251.translation: Macrino, Dasi miae [son? daughter?], lived 8 months.
EDCS-62900251.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age (in months) of a very young individual named Macrino.

EDCS-63600289.translation: Abbot Paschasius, cultivator of justice and lover of the highest piety, lies buried in this tomb; his door was open to guests, never closed to any; no one needing food or shelter was refused; he was a comforter of widows and a provider for all orphans, and a useful remedy for everyone in all things; closing a ninety-year life without fault, he is returned to the earth in body and his spirit to the ether, laid to rest on the 11th day before the Kalends of October in the thirteenth year after the consulship of Basilius.
EDCS-63600289.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Abbot Paschasius, detailing his virtuous life and burial date.

EDCS-63600290.translation: Succesus(?), the collegium, dedicated this by his own will and money. To the Most Holy Divinity.
EDCS-63600290.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Succesus to a divine entity on behalf of a collegium.

EDCS-63600324.translation: Marcus Quinctius Protus, freedman of Marcus, for himself and his family, [erected this] for Gaius Quinctius Papa, freedman of Gaius, and Marcus Quinctius Gemellus, freedman of Marcus.
EDCS-63600324.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Quinctius Protus for his family members, Marcus Quinctius Gemellus and Gaius Quinctius Papa.

EDCS-63600327.translation: For Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, in his seventh tribunician power and designated second consul; and for Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, in his twelfth tribunician power, eleventh acclamation as emperor, third consul, father of the country, proconsul, his son, for the decennial vows.
EDCS-63600327.summary: This inscription records a dedication made for the decennial vows of Emperor Marcus Aurelius and Emperor Septimius Severus, likely during the reign of the latter's son Caracalla.

EDCS-63600334.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph naming Lucius Eggius Amimetus Blastus.

EDCS-63600375.translation: Quintus Marcius Thoantus, freedman of Quintus, and Marcia Glucera, freedwoman of Quintus, and [---] Zosimae, Quintus Marcius [---] made [this].
EDCS-63600375.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quintus Marcius for his family members, including Marcia Glucera and Zosimae.

EDCS-64000041.translation: Gaius Oppius Honoratus, father, and Flavia Romana, mother, unhappy parents.
EDCS-64000041.summary: This inscription is a parental dedication by Gaius Oppius Honoratus and Flavia Romana, indicating a loss.

EDCS-64300053.translation: Sacred to Sol. Quintus Terentius Anteros, freedman of Quintus, an Augustalis.
EDCS-64300053.summary: This is a dedication to the god Sol by Quintus Terentius Anteros, an Augustalis and freedman.

EDCS-64300090.translation: Lucius Campanius Flaccus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-64300090.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph or an inscription naming Lucius Campanius Flaccus.

EDCS-64500493.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that likely names Cnaeus Pomponius Nes[---].

EDCS-64500497.translation: To Stoichais, wife of Atticus, publicly honored.
EDCS-64500497.summary: This is a public dedication to Stoichais, wife of Atticus.

EDCS-64600280.translation: Sextus Hellenius Rufinus, approved in his time in the profession of bookselling, honored with public salaries, and Arria Hesperis, his wife, made this for themselves and their descendants while alive.
EDCS-64600280.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Sextus Hellenius Rufinus and his wife Arria Hesperis, commemorating his career as a bookseller honored with public salaries, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-64800023.translation: Quintus Camurius Primus (?), freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-64800023.summary: This inscription records the name of Quintus Camurius Primus, a freedman, possibly from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-64900346.translation: To the departed spirits. Geminia lived [X] years. Caius Geminius Pisoninus placed this here for his mother.
EDCS-64900346.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Geminia and was dedicated by her son Caius Geminius Pisoninus.

EDCS-64900609.summary: This is a simple funerary marker for Tunnia Lucinis.

EDCS-64900889.summary: This inscription begins the name and filiation of Aulus Fabius, son of Aulus.

EDCS-64900893.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to record names and possibly a dedication to someone named Secundus.

EDCS-64900894.summary: This fragmentary inscription names an individual potentially called Hilarus and indicates a parentage.

EDCS-64900895.translation: To Herennius, son of Caius, [...] for Herennia, daughter of Caius, his sister.
EDCS-64900895.summary: This inscription, though damaged, seems to commemorate Herennius, son of Caius, and possibly his sister Herennia.

EDCS-65300032.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Babinius Eronis, likely identifying ownership or a dedication.

EDCS-65300036.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely dedicating an honorific monument to the emperor Constantine, perhaps in the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-65300081.translation: Delliorum's firstborn, Erotis, made [this] for her son.
EDCS-65300081.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Erotis dedicates a monument to her firstborn son, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65300082.translation: Gaius Vettius Menophilus, freedman of Gaius, made [this] for himself and for his patron Gaius Vettius Nicephorus.
EDCS-65300082.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where the freedman Gaius Vettius Menophilus dedicates a monument to himself and his patron, Gaius Vettius Nicephorus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65300083.translation: Gaius Petronius Verecundus Sabinus and his daughter [Sat(tia?)] Sabina made [this] for their most devoted father, Gaius Petronicus Sattius Sabinus, who served as aedile, duumvir with judicial power, quinquennalis, and designated quaestor, presenting the gladiatorial games of Beneventum for four days, with prices not set.
EDCS-65300083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Petronicus Sattius Sabinus, a local magistrate, erected by his children to commemorate his public service and the gladiatorial games he sponsored, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65300089.translation: Gaius Umbricius Felix, a scribe, lives for his most chaste wife of Umbria Romana.
EDCS-65300089.summary: This inscription, possibly funerary or commemorating a marriage, honors Umbria Romana, the wife of the scribe Gaius Umbricius Felix, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65900023.translation: Sabinus paid his vow to Juno.
EDCS-65900023.summary: This is a dedication recording that Sabinus fulfilled a vow made to the goddess Juno, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65900026.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary or dedicatory, mentioning individuals named Iuven and Senilis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65900040.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Plautia Modestilla, who lived 26 years, 4 months, and 25 days. CEP MO [erected this] for his wife, a well-deserving, loving daughter, and sister.
EDCS-65900040.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Plautia Modestilla, detailing her age and relationship to her grieving spouse, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65900065.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Marcus Acilius Am-, likely part of a name on a monument, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-65900097.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership mark, referring to Gnaeus Macedo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-66800022.translation: Sixteen miles for our Lords Diocletian and Maximian Augusti and for the most noble Caesars Constantius and Maximian, [to mark the distance] for our Lords Flavius Valentinianus, Valentius, and Gratianus, ever Augusti, born for the good of the state.
EDCS-66800022.summary: This is a milestone inscription from the late Roman period, marking a distance of sixteen miles for the emperors Diocletian, Maximian, Constantius, and Maximian, and also for Valentinian, Valens, and Gratian.

EDCS-67400195.translation: boundary, from here towards the front of the Roscia road, attributed to the public arbiter for Modesta, daughter of Caius [---] Rosciae, and other rights which Roscius Secundus recovered from the heirs of Caius [---] Modesta's [---] Heli, and donated.
EDCS-67400195.summary: This is a boundary stone inscription, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, detailing land ownership and rights related to a woman named Modesta.

EDCS-69000010.translation: Gaius Natrius, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, lies here. Gaius Natrius, son of Lucius, made this while alive for himself and his son Gaius Natrius, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe.
EDCS-69000010.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from ancient Rome, recording the burial of Gaius Natrius and mentioning his father and son, also named Gaius Natrius.

EDCS-69200214.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Hilara for her husband, Publius Varius Priscus, with whom she lived for 19 years, and for their children Torquatis Primus, Priscus, and Prisca, and for their most devoted father.
EDCS-69200214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the Roman period, dedicated by Flavia Hilara to her husband Publius Varius Priscus, mentioning their children and his father.

EDCS-70901072.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely from the Roman period, with insufficient text to determine its purpose.

EDCS-71000305.translation: To a Good Memory. Here rests in the sleep of peace Limpidia, an honorable woman, who lived for more or less [years]. Her burial was on the twelfth day before the Kalends of December, during the consulship of the illustrious men Flavius Asterius and Praesidius.
EDCS-71000305.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the late Roman or early Byzantine period, recording the burial of Limpidia and mentioning the consuls Flavius Asterius and Praesidius.

EDCS-71200698.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription from the Roman period, possibly related to a will or testament.

EDCS-72300176.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Publius Varius Secundus. Publius Varius Secundus, his son, and Titia Sabina [erected this].
EDCS-72300176.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the Roman period, dedicated by a son and his mother to their deceased father and husband, Publius Varius Secundus.

EDCS-72500082.translation: Here rests in the sleep of peace Gutta, who lived for more or less 20 years and [lived] with her husband for six months and six days. Her burial was on the tenth day before the Kalends of December, during the consulship of the most illustrious man Flavius Iohannes.
EDCS-72500082.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the late Roman or early Byzantine period, recording the burial of Gutta and mentioning her husband and the consul Flavius Iohannes.

EDCS-72500234.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription from the Roman period, likely an epitaph for a woman named Varia Felissima.

EDCS-72700075.translation: To Lucius Caelsius, son of Lucius, called Celer, from Cassia Grata.
EDCS-72700075.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Cassia Grata to Lucius Caelsius Celer, likely from the Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-72700076.translation: And Umfritus, his son, who lived 13 years and 3 months, deposited.
EDCS-72700076.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Umfritus at the age of 13 years and 3 months, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-72700077.translation: Eutychus with his father Majori, in peace with the just.
EDCS-72700077.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Eutychus and his father Majori, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-72700117.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antoninus Augustus, Parthicus, Father of the Fatherland, the road to the bridge of Aufidus and Venusia, interrupted by floods and robbers, restored by the people of Aeclanum at their expense.
EDCS-72700117.summary: This is a public works inscription from Aeclanum, dedicated to Emperor Marcus Antoninus Augustus, concerning the restoration of a road, likely from the Antonine period (mid-2nd century AD).

EDCS-72700119.translation: To Diana of Nemi, from Felicia Maxima Luciniana for the safety and return of her husband Lucius Lucinius, she vowed and paid her offering.
EDCS-72700119.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana Nemorensis by Felicia Maxima Luciniana for her husband's safe return, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-72700120.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, Father of the Fatherland, subjugator of the state.
EDCS-72700120.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated to Emperor Antoninus Pius, likely from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-72700121.translation: Here lies. Publius Claudius Valentinus and his brother Marcus Claudius.
EDCS-72700121.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Claudius Valentinus and his brother Marcus Claudius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-72700122.translation: To Quintus Paedius, son of Quintus, praetor, military tribune, patron of the colony. The state of Aeclanum at its expense.
EDCS-72700122.summary: This is a dedication by the state of Aeclanum to Quintus Paedius, honoring his public service as praetor and military tribune, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-72700123.translation: Lucius Secundus, son of Lucius, of the Cor (nelian tribe), Crescens, decurion and prefect of the corn supply of the provinces of Apulia and Lucania, erected a statue of Mercury in honor of the quinquennality, in whose dedication the decurions and the people each gave a denarius; he gave it at his own expense.
EDCS-72700123.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Secundus Crescens, a local official, of a statue of Mercury to commemorate his quinquennal office and generous donations, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-72700136.translation: [...]fius of Cundinus, juryman, commander of the fleet of Concordia, ... Mar incomp f
EDCS-72700136.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a naval commander and a juryman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-72800037.summary: This inscription is likely the beginning of a dedication or funerary text, possibly for members of the gens Allia and Horatia, with the name Pa potentially being the start of another name or cognomen.

EDCS-72800038.summary: This inscription appears to be a simple funerary or dedicatory text, possibly recording the name of Marcus Ofa Speratus.

EDCS-74400129.translation: Maximus, slave of Marcus Marius Rufinus, consul, gladly paid his vow with his people.
EDCS-74400129.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Maximus, a slave of the consul Marcus Marius Rufinus, likely on a public monument during his consulship.

EDCS-74700038.translation: Lucius Titius Copo, Lucius Titius Copo
EDCS-74700038.summary: This inscription, possibly a funerary text, records the name Lucius Titius Copo twice, perhaps for emphasis or to indicate two individuals with the same name.

EDCS-75200372.summary: This inscription is too fragmented to determine its purpose, but it likely records names such as Cinna, Genucia, and Max[...], possibly in a funerary context.

EDCS-75200373.translation: Lache and Prima, parents, lived for 11 months and 15 days.
EDCS-75200373.summary: This funerary inscription records the short lifespan of a child, whose parents were Lache and Prima.

EDCS-75200383.summary: This inscription is likely funerary, recording the name Sextus Deccianus, a freedman of Sextus, with the name truncated.

EDCS-75200385.translation: C. Abisius Fus[cus]'s ...dus, the farm manager, dedicated [this].
EDCS-75200385.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by a farm manager ('vilicus') identified as ...dus, possibly related to C. Abisius Fuscus.

EDCS-75400211.summary: This inscription records Vitalis, who are identified as slaves of Augustus, possibly on a monument related to the imperial household.

EDCS-75400212.translation: Lucius Licinius [i.e., Lucius Licinius Pus] and Licinia Melitina.
EDCS-75400212.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary or dedicatory text mentioning Lucius Licinius Pus and Licinia Melitina.

EDCS-75500096.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Trebonia Hygia and Marcius Euplus made this for their beloved son, Caius Marcius Felix, who lived twenty-two years and six months, as a mark of deserving.
EDCS-75500096.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Marcius Felix, set up by his parents.

EDCS-76000090.summary: A very short inscription, likely a personal note or ownership mark, possibly naming 'Neophytus' and indicating possession or a relationship.

EDCS-76000249.translation: To the Most High, Responsive God. Publius Anicius Nicephorus commemorated his benefactors, Anicius the patron and the father, and [commemorated] Euplos, your good journey to Eutychia, may all your journeys be good.
EDCS-76000249.summary: A dedication to a supreme deity, possibly syncretic, by Publius Anicius Nicephorus, mentioning benefactors and wishing good fortune.

EDCS-76100085.translation: The workshop of Caius Cornelius Ursus.
EDCS-76100085.summary: This inscription records the name of the owner or proprietor of a workshop, Caius Cornelius Ursus.

EDCS-76100091.summary: This inscription names an individual, Saturn, and identifies his father as Histon, likely a personal name or owner's mark.

EDCS-76100104.translation: To Athena. Leon and his wife Glauca.
EDCS-76100104.summary: A dedication to the goddess Athena by Leon and his wife Glauca.

EDCS-76200045.translation: Florianus rests here, deposited, aged 50. His deposit was on the 15th day before the Kalends of September.
EDCS-76200045.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and burial date of Florianus, who was 50 years old.

EDCS-76200221.summary: A dedication to the goddess Aphrodite, initiated by Iulius A... (name incomplete).

EDCS-76300035.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ero[--]osa lived 65 years, she rests here. Her daughter for her deserving mother.
EDCS-76300035.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ero[--]osa, who lived 65 years, set up by her daughter.

EDCS-76300053.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Seppia Secunda, his wife, and Caius Egnatius Saturninus, his son, made this for Caius Egnatius Saturninus, as a mark of deserving.
EDCS-76300053.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Egnatius Saturninus, set up by his wife and son.

EDCS-76300074.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Clusovius Cevienus set this up for Cicilia, well deserving.
EDCS-76300074.summary: A funerary dedication by Publius Clusovius Cevienus for Cicilia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76300174.translation: To the spirits of the departed. U(lpius) Euschenus lived 20 years, 5 months, 11 days. He set this up, well deserving.
EDCS-76300174.summary: A funerary dedication for Ulpius Euschenus, recording his age at death, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76600138.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gnaeus Aurelius Marcellinus, husband, made this for his most cherished wife, Sempronia Lucia, his freedwoman, who deserved well.
EDCS-76600138.summary: A funerary dedication by Gnaeus Aurelius Marcellinus for his wife Sempronia Lucia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-76800146.translation: Vappa on the Pettian estate, near the lake, in the first row, from the first cask twenty-five amphorae, from the second cask. Vappa Pettian.
EDCS-76800146.summary: An inscription related to wine production or storage on the Pettian estate, possibly a record of yield or distribution, date uncertain but likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200227.summary: A simple name inscription, possibly identifying an owner or a deceased individual, date uncertain but likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200228.summary: A simple name inscription, possibly identifying an owner or a deceased individual, date uncertain but likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200229.summary: A simple name inscription, possibly identifying an owner or a deceased individual, date uncertain but likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200230.summary: A simple name inscription, possibly identifying an owner or a deceased individual, date uncertain but likely Roman Imperial period. Contains a probable typo 'DrIaco'.

EDCS-77200235.summary: A name inscription, possibly indicating ownership or parentage, date uncertain but likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200243.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Claudius Nero, son of Gaius, grandson of Marcus, consul, for the most successful deed accomplished in Picenum against the Carthaginian leader Hasdrubal, from the station of the people and soldiers at Lupiae. A. H. P.
EDCS-77200243.summary: A dedication commemorating a military victory in Picenum by Gaius Claudius Nero during his consulship, likely dating to the Second Punic War (218-201 BCE).

EDCS-77200244.translation: To Publius Cesellius Numerianus, son of Gaius, twice appointed IIvir, four times appointed IVvir of the Republic, military tribune, who lived 42 years and 2 months. His father and his mother Tiburtia, his incomparable mother, placed this monument.
EDCS-77200244.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Cesellius Numerianus, detailing his career and age, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200260.translation: To Lucius Eupolion, son of Lucius, grandson of Lucius, a most distinguished man. Having discharged all public and military duties in his homeland, and while he was diligently working for the public welfare, he died fighting bravely against the ambushes of seditious men. The entire colony of Lupiae, by public decree, erected this bronze statue in the forum.
EDCS-77200260.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Lucius Eupolion by the colony of Lupiae, commemorating his public service and heroic death in defense of the state, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200261.translation: To Publius Paccursio, son of Publius, grandson of Quintus, a most brave man, whose military valor, when it had become evident, in equipping and leading citizens to battle, and finally, while he was exhorting them with words and his own example to charge into the most crowded enemy formations, the Lucanian and Tarentine wedges, was wounded and, alas, returned to his men. The entire order and people of Lupiae, with profuse tears, erected this bronze statue in the forum to perpetuate his name and fame.
EDCS-77200261.summary: A funerary monument erected by the people of Lupiae to Publius Paccursio, honoring his bravery and sacrifice in battle, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200263.translation: To Quintus Fabio Balbo, former IVvir, patron of the Tusculan municipality, military tribune of the II Agrippinian Legion, curator of the Via Salentina. In return for the outstanding benefits conferred upon all, the inhabitants of Lupiae, Hydruntum, and Neritum, [dedicate this] to their most excellent patron, in everlasting memory. He dedicated it.
EDCS-77200263.summary: An honorary inscription dedicated by the inhabitants of Lupiae, Hydruntum, and Neritum to their patron Quintus Fabio Balbo, acknowledging his benefits and listing his career posts, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200264.translation: Quintus Ennius, son of Accius Ennius, of Puteoli, made this well in the atrium of his house, for public convenience and advantage. He donated it.
EDCS-77200264.summary: An epigraphic notice recording the construction of a well by Quintus Ennius for public use, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200265.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Flavius Constantinus Augustus, unconquered, son of the divine Constantius Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Imperator four times, Consul three times, Father of the Fatherland, because by his valor he defeated the tyrants of the empire and compelled them to withdraw from Gaul and all of Italy, the order and people of Lupiae dedicated [this] to his divine majesty and to himself.
EDCS-77200265.summary: An honorary inscription dedicated by the colony of Lupiae to Emperor Constantine the Great, celebrating his victory over tyrants and his power, likely from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-77200266.translation: Dedicated. To Marcus Luplius, son of Marcus, who held all the highest offices in the Lupian Republic, for his well-managed public service. The order and the common people, in the public forum, erected [this monument]. He lived 69 years, 9 months, and 20 days. Died on the Kalends of May.
