EDCS-77200266.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Luplius, honoring his public service and listing his age at death and date of demise, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200267.translation: To the divine spirits. To Publius Pacursio, son of Ansiatus, formerly IIIvir, military tribune of the fatherland, guardian of the Lupian Republic. The order and people of Lupiae erected this public monument in bronze for their excellent citizen, who deserved well of the Republic, and ordered gladiatorial games to be held. He lived 45 years, 2 months. Died on the Ides of Quintilis.
EDCS-77200267.summary: A funerary monument with an endowment for games, dedicated to Publius Pacursio by the people of Lupiae, honoring his service and specifying the details of his death and burial, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200268.translation: To the divine spirits. To Titus Follio Carinus, son of Quintus, patron of the Truentine municipality, curator of the Via Appia, prefect of the Fabri, twice IVvir. He died in office. In honor of his virtue, this place was publicly granted by him. He dedicated it himself.
EDCS-77200268.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Follio Carinus, listing his public offices and noting that he died in service, with the burial plot granted by himself, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200274.translation: To the divine spirits. To Lucius Heterius, son of Quintus, from the family of Cosennia, centurion of the Galbian Legion, who served 16 years and 10 months. His comrade-in-arms, Iulius Roscius, placed this monument with good will.
EDCS-77200274.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Heterius, a centurion, erected by a fellow soldier, detailing his service length, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-77200276.translation: To the divine Octavius, son of the divine Caesar, Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Imperator ten times, Consul, Tribune of the Plebs twenty-seven times, the municipal order of Benevento, Pater Patriae.
EDCS-77200276.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Octavius, likely establishing his imperial cult or authority in Benevento, dating to his tribunician power in his twenty-seventh year.

EDCS-77200277.translation: To the departed spirits. To Q. Valerius, son of Palus, a Paraedius, aedile four times, quinquennial magistrate of the colony of Narona, patron of the municipality of Narona, curator of the Via Traiana. Herinnia to her most sweet wife, with good will and duty she placed this.
EDCS-77200277.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Q. Valerius, a prominent local official and magistrate in Narona, set up by his wife, Herinnia.

EDCS-77700090.translation: Lucius Giganius Atimetus, by decree of the Ceriales, undertook to have a wall built from the ground up at his own expense.
EDCS-77700090.summary: This inscription records a private building project, the construction of a wall, funded by Lucius Giganius Atimetus at the behest of the Ceriales.

EDCS-77700138.translation: To our Lords, Gratian Augustus, in his second consulship. The Ides of April.
EDCS-77700138.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Gratian, likely marking a public event or building, dated to April 13th in his second consulship.

EDCS-78800001.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription commemorating the life of Tiberius Claudius Dex.

EDCS-78800046.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating the name and age at death of Alexander.

EDCS-78800134.translation: To our Lords, Gratian Augustus, in his second consulship. The Ides of April.
EDCS-78800134.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Gratian, likely marking a public event or building, dated to April 13th in his second consulship.

EDCS-79000043.translation: To the departed spirits. Lucius Mil[--]
EDCS-79000043.summary: This is a funerary inscription beginning with a dedication to the departed spirits for Lucius Mil[--].

EDCS-79000045.translation: Caius Iulius, son of Caius, mi[--]
EDCS-79000045.summary: This inscription appears to be the beginning of a funerary epitaph or dedication, identifying Caius Iulius, son of Caius.

EDCS-79000049.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Ilia Rufilla is buried at this location.

EDCS-80300148.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Publius Atrius of Teanum, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-80300150.translation: Gnaeus Raevidius, son of Lucius, of the tribe Velina.
EDCS-80300150.summary: This inscription records the name and tribal affiliation of Gnaeus Raevidius, son of Lucius.

EDCS-81200086.translation: To our lord Iovianus Augustus, pious, fortunate, unconquered, born for the good of the state.
EDCS-81200086.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Iovianus Augustus, hailing his virtues and role in the state, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-81400227.translation: To the divine spirits of Vecillus, son of Euacaeus.
EDCS-81400227.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Vecillus, son of Euacaeus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81900006.translation: Crescens, father, and Soteris, mother, made this for their sweetest daughter Crescentilla, who lived for 11 years, 6 months, and 2 days. Epaphroditus the grandfather placed it, and also Ulpius Probus.
EDCS-81900006.summary: This is a funerary monument for Crescentilla, detailing her lifespan and erected by her parents and grandfather, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82000025.translation: Gaius Aquilius, son of Lucius, procurator of the twentieth tax for the freedmen, for himself.
EDCS-82000025.summary: This is a dedication by Gaius Aquilius, son of Lucius, who served as a procurator for the 'vicesima libertatis' (tax on manumissions), likely from the Roman Imperial period.
